Summary: Incorrect simulation results for compartments when unit conversion is enabled
Description: SimBiology might return incorrect simulation results for compartments when all of the following conditions are met: Unit conversion is enabled. The model contains multiple compartments with different units. The model contains nested compartments.  In such cases, SimBiology might use the wrong unit multiplier to convert a compartment capacity from internal units to user-specified units. Internally, the simulation proceeds with the correct units, and the reported simulation results are correct for species and parameters.
Workaround: Work around the problem by doing any of the following:Disable unit conversion.Ensure all compartments have the same units.Convert all nested compartments to top-level compartments.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly show?Incompatible for models with arrays of complex numbers as output or input to function calls

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MinMax block might produce incorrect results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes with NaN input and zero-crossing detection
Description: The MinMax block produces incorrect results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes when the following conditions are true: Model solver is variable-step. MinMax block Enable zero crossing detection  is selected. MinMax block input has continuous sample time. Input to MinMax block has double data type. One of these is true: MinMax block has only one input and the first element of the input is NaN. For example, input [NaN 1] can output NaN, which is an incorrect result. MinMax block has multiple inputs and the first input has a NaN. For example, for a MinMax block with two inputs, the first input has an input of NaN and the second input has a 1, the resulting incorrect result is a NaN.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: HDL IP core generation using Xilinx Vivado fails as of January 1, 2022
Description: Since January 1, 2022, IP core generation fails when the synthesis tool is set to Xilinx Vivado. The following error message can be observed in the HDL Workflow Advisor Task 3.2:? Failed Task "Vivado IP Packager" unsuccessful. See log for details. Generated logfile: hdl_prj\hdlsrc\<myModel> \workflow_task_VivadoIPPackager.log  The log file at \hdl_prj\hdlsrc\<myModel>\workflow_task_VivadoIPPackager.log  reports an error message similar to the following: bad lexical cast: source type value could not be interpreted as target  ? ?while executing"rdi::set_property core_revision 2201031458 {component component_1}"  ? ?invoked from within  "set_property core_revision 2201031458 [ipx::current_core]"  ? ?(file "vivado_ip_package.tcl" line 57)
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log into an account with the appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.Locate the following file under your <MATLAB> folder and rename the file to "IPEmitterVivado_backup.p":<MATLAB>\toolbox\hdlcoder\hdlcommon\+hdlturnkey\+ip\IPEmitterVivado.pWindows Systems:?You might be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when renaming this file.  Download and unzip the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report.In the unzipped folder, navigate to the subfolder that matches the release you are using. For example, if you are using the R2021b release, open the R2021b subfolder.If you do not see a subfolder that matches the release you are using,?contact MathWorks Technical Support.In this folder, there is a newer version of the?IPEmitterVivado.p file. Copy this file and paste it in the location mentioned above.Windows Systems:?You might be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when pasting this file.Restart MATLAB.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation result might occur when using fixed point data type in S-Function Builder
Description: Simulink might produce incorrect simulation results when you use fixed point signals in your S-Function Builder block, and the data type override is set to a value other than off .
Workaround: First, manually edit the generated source file for the S-function.Open the generated source fileIn the mdlInitializeSizes method, update the data type override flag to 0. The override flag is the last field in function ssRegisterDataTypeFxpBinaryPoint or ssRegisterDataTypeFxpSlopeBias. For example:DTypeId DataTypeId_0 = ssRegisterDataTypeFxpBinaryPoint(?S,?OUT_0_ISSIGNED,?OUT_0_WORDLENGTH,?OUT_0_FRACTIONLENGTH,?0 );Then, recompile the source file before running the simulation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Possible simulation failure when a flexible body with three or more frames is part of a kinematic loop.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code might be generated for MATLAB Function blocks containing sub-functions with inplace specifications on inputs modeled in Simulink as buses
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a MATLAB Function block that meets these conditions: The MATLAB Function block includes a MATLAB function that contains a sub-function. The sub-function has an inplace specification (that is, the same input and output variable). The inplace input variable is modeled in Simulink as a bus.  For example, in the below representative code pattern, a MATLAB Function block is the function?top_function ?which contains the? sub_function . The?P1 ?input to?sub_function ?is an inplace variable which is modeled in Simulink as a bus. function [P1,P2] = top_function(In1)...[P1,P2] = sub_function(P1,In2);...end  function [P1,P2] = sub_function(P1,In2)  ...  end ? The code for the?sub_function ?is incorrectly generated as follows: sub_function(&P1,In2,&P1,P2);  Here,  P1  is passed as a pointer for the input and output, and the successive operations inside sub_function  on P1 ?overwrite the input value of P1 .? Instead, the generated code should be of this format:?? tmp = *P1  sub_function(&tmp,In2,&P1,P2);
Workaround: Incorrect inplace optimization can be eliminated by running the.p file attached to the workaround zip archive.

Summary: MATLAB can crash when calling getadjacencymatrix or when using the Model Debugger on a very large SimBiology model
Description: MATLAB can crash when calling?getadjacencymatrix  on a SimBiology model that contains hundreds of reactions using null as reactant or product. The?Model Debugger for SimBiology  also relies on getadjacencymatrix  and can crash when used with such a model.
Workaround: Replace null reactants and products with a dummy species. You can adapt the following code, which updates a SimBiology model stored in variable modelObj by replacing null with a dummy species named NULL, while ensuring the reaction rate remains the same.reactions = modelObj.Reactions;nullPattern = regexpPattern('\<null\>');for i = 1:numel(reactions)    reactionString = reactions(i).Reaction;    if contains(reactionString, nullPattern)        allSpecies = [reactions(i).Reactants; reactions(i).Products];        oneSpecies = allSpecies(1);        oneCompartment = oneSpecies.Parent;        nullSpecies = sbioselect(oneCompartment, 'Name', 'NULL');        if isempty(nullSpecies)            nullSpecies = oneCompartment.addspecies('NULL', 'Units', oneSpecies.Units);        end        nullSpeciesQualifiedName = nullSpecies.PartiallyQualifiedName;        reactionString = replace(reactionString, nullPattern, nullSpeciesQualifiedName);        rate = reactions(i).ReactionRate;        reactions(i).Reaction = reactionString;        reactions(i).ReactionRate = rate;    endend
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer for model with Outports using GetSet or AccessFunction storage class and a referenced model containing a Function-Call Subsystem block
Description: Code generation of a model might result in a silent wrong answer when all of these conditions are true: The model contains a Model block that is directly connected to an Outport block. The Outport block uses a GetSet  storage class or other storage class with Data Access  set to Function  and Access Mode  set to Value . The referenced model contains a Function-Call Subsystem block that is connected to the Outport block.  Code generation might also be incorrect if the output signals of the Model block in the parent model use a GetSet  storage class.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block in the parent model between the Model block and the Outports that are mapped to?GetSet or Function?storage class.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect signal might be logged by XCP-based external mode simulation
Description: ?An XCP-based external mode simulation might log an incorrect signal when, for a given block, all of these conditions hold: A signal from one of the block's output ports is marked for logging. Summary: Bulleted list formatting lost when importing requirements from Microsoft Word

Summary: Invalid evaluation of classifiers in sbiompgsa
Description: The evaluation of classifiers in sbiompgsa  can fail or yield wrong values.?? When a classifier in sbiompgsa  references two or more model components whose names are a mix of qualified and non-qualified names, then the function can error. The error says that the classifier is invalid, even though the classifier is valid. An example of a qualified name of a species Drug  in the compartment named?Central ?is Central.Drug . When a classifier in sbiompgsa  references both constant and non-constant model components, then the function can return the wrong sensitivities without any error indication.
Workaround: To avoid the incorrect error about invalid classifiers, reference model components either by names or by qualified names, not a mix of names and qualified names in classifiers.To avoid wrong sensitivities being reported by sbiompgsa, specify numeric values instead of names of constant model components in classifiers.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Sporadic MATLAB crashes during compilation of models with nonlinear terms and derivatives in constraint equations
Description: MATLAB might crash when you run or update a Simscape model where a constraint equation  contains a nonlinear term, which in turn contains a derivative of any variable and at least one more variable. Consider the following network equations: ??????? der(x) * u == u * u;? ? ? ? ? ? ? (1)??????? r == der(x);? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? (2)??????? y == der(r) + cos(u) * der(u);??? (3)??????? u == sin(time);? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?(4)  Equations (1), (2), and (4) are the constraint equations. When we differentiate equation (1) we get the following expression ? ? ?? der(der(x)) * u + der(x) * der(u) == 2.0 * u * der(u)   When applying derivative replacement method on this equation, der(x) * der(u)  term can cause the compilation to crash.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: DWARF parser fails to extract debugging information from ELF files compiled using Microchip's XC16 compiler
Description: Microchip's XC16 16-bit compiler generates binary ELF files that have a special structure in the .debug  sections. When a standard DWARF parser tries to extract debugging information from an ELF file, the structure causes the parser to fail. The ELF file appears as if it has been compiled without debugging information, which blocks XCP external mode workflows on Microchip 16-bit dsPIC target hardware. The workflows require the debugging information to infer memory addresses of global variables.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Double-Acting Servo Valve Actuator (IL) block incorrectly offsets spool position by half of the spool stroke
Description: The physical signal port S  reports a spool position of half the value of the?Spool stroke ?parameter when the spool is in the middle of the stroke. The value should be 0  at this point, to indicate the neutral position.
Workaround: Add a PS Subtract block and a PS Constant block to your model.Attach the?S?port of the Double-Acting Servo Valve Actuator to the +?port of the PS Subtract block.Attach the PS Constant block to the -?port of the PS Subtract block.Set the Constant?parameter for the PS Constant block to half of the value of the Spool stroke?parameter.The out port of the PS Subtract block is the correct spool position signal.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: Hex numbers are represented as integer literals in generated code
Description: Integer literals represented as hexadecimal in a Stateflow C Action Language chart might be represented as an equivalent decimal in generated code. This issue happens when a data type conversion is required in the statement containing the hexadecimal?literal.? For example, in a Stateflow chart containing a uint8_T  variable OutVal, ?the statement:OutVal = 0x01;  is generated as:OutVal = 1U;  instead of the expected:OutVal = (uint8_T)0x01;
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier might stall during the preprocessing stage for models with fixed-point 2-D Lookup Tables
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might experience stalls during the setup and compatibility stage for models that meet the following conditions:There is at least one 2-D Lookup Table with fixed-point breakpoints. The breakpoint ranges are close to the minimum and maximum values for that fixed-point type.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB crashes when?a classification experiment?uses?a large datastore for training or validation.
Description: When a classification experiment uses a large datastore (for handling more than around 2GB of data) for training or validation, a memory leak can cause MATLAB to crash. The memory leak happens when a trial is nearing completion.  Datastores are designed for working with collections of data that        are too large to fit in memory at one time. The problem here is caused by the readall  function incorrectly trying to load all the data in memory at once.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Data Type Propagation block does not support symbolic dimensions
Description: A Simulink model with Data Type Propagation  block?containing symbolic dimensions returns this error message:Modelname/Data Type Propagation does not support symbolic dimensions propagation.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect report of data dependency violation with asynchronous function-call initiator and Rate Transition block together with loop
Description: When both of the following conditions are met, you might see an incorrect report of a data dependency violation when updating or simulating your model: Either:?  The model contains an algebraic loop, and the model configuration parameter Diagnostics > Algebraic Loop  is set to none  or warning ; or  The model contains a loop formed by a graphical connection from the state port of a block with a state port, such as an Integrator block.   The model contains both an asynchronous function-call initiator and a Rate Transition block for that asynchronous rate in the same nonvirtual hierarchy as the loop.
Workaround: Separate the loop and the blocks with asynchronous rate into different nonvirtual hierarchies. For example, group the asynchronous function-call and corresponding Rate Transition block into an atomic subsystem.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink might use an outdated enum definition when simulating or generating code for a referenced model
Description: Simulink might use the previous definition of a dynamic enumerated data type when all of the following conditions are true: The enumerated type is defined by using the?Simulink.defineIntEnumType ?function. The model hierarchy contains at least one referenced model. The Rebuild ?configuration parameter of the top model is set to If any changes in known dependencies detected . The enumerated type definition has changed since the model reference simulation or code generation target was last built.
Workaround: Set the Rebuild configuration parameter of the top model to If any changes detected.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: pspectrum uses very low minimum threshold when plotting
Description: The pspectrum  function generates a plot with improper z -axis scaling when called without output arguments and without specifying the MinThreshold ?argument. The function chooses an unnecessarily small value for the minimum power, and as a result the power values do not occupy the entire range of the colormap. This code illustrates the bug: load batsignalpspectrum(batsignal,1/DT,'spectrogram')  The minimum spectral power displayed in the graph is lower than ¨C140 dB, as shown by the color bar.
Workaround: Use the MinThreshold?name-value argument to produce a spectrogram with a specified minimum value that does not display unwanted background content.This example uses pspectrum to plot a spectrogram with an adequate minimum threshold:https://www.mathworks.com/help/signal/ug/practical-introduction-to-time-frequency-analysis.html#TimeFrequencyAnalysisExample-2
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports decision objective for the last input index?of a Multiport switch block as Unsatisfiable.
Description: If a Simulink model contains a Multiport switch  block with following configuration:Number of data ports  set to 1 The data port is connected to the output of a?Mux ?block  then, Simulink Design Verifier always reports decision objective associated with the last input index?as Unsatisfiable  during the test generation analysis, even if it ?can potentially be covered by a simulation. Similarly, during dead logic analysis Simulink Design Verifier always reports this objective as Dead Logic .
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Error thrown from coder.mapping.internal.CodeMappingCopier.CacheRootIOCodeMappings
Description: When generating code for a subsystem, you may experience an error originating from  coder. mapping. internal. CodeMappingCopier. CacheRootIOCodeMappings.? The stack trace may appear similar to the following:  Error in coder. mapping. internal. CodeMappingCopier. CacheRootIOCodeMappings  Error in coder. internal. ss2mdl  Error in coder. internal. ss2mdl?  Error in rtwbuild - Show complete stack trace?

Summary: Simulink blocks with fixed-point input might report incorrect relational boundary coverage results
Description: If your model contains a block with fixed-point inputs, you might get incorrect coverage results. For example, consider a Switch block whose control input signal is fixdt(1,3,0) , with threshold u > 3 ,  and data input signals 1  and 4 , respectively. For this case, the expected result is trigger - threshold = -4 , but the coverage report shows that +LSB  (which is +1 ) is satisfied instead:  trigger - threshold? 33%  ? ? -LSB? 0/  2  ? ? ? 0 ? 0/  2  ? ? +LSB? 2/  2
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing Data Store Memory, Selector, and Assignment blocks
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Data Store Memory block, a Selector block, and an Assignment block. A Data Store Read block is connected to the index port of the Selector block. The same Data Store Read block is connected to the index port of the Assignment block. The output of the Selector block is connected to the data port (U ) of the Assignment block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink model containing a Demux block connected to a Stateflow chart might generate incorrect code
Description: Simulink model containing a Demux block connected to a Stateflow chart might generate incorrect code as shown below: RootModel_P.ConstantValue[-127]  This issue is caused due to integer overflow in the matrix index.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: SimBiology project saved in R2019b cannot be opened in later releases
Description: A SimBiology project saved in R2019b with empty plot matrix plots cannot be opened in later releases.
Workaround: To workaround this bug, re-open the project in R2019b, delete all empty plot matrix plots and re-save.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: remove method of SimData does not remove data
Description: For most types of queries, the remove  method does not remove any data. Instead, it returns a SimData  object with all the data present in the input object.
Workaround: Use a different SimData method to create the desired SimData object. For example, use the removebyname method to specify which data columns to be removed from SimData. Alternatively, invert the query and use the select method to specify the data to keep.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when logical variable is initialized using if-elseif-else containing a call to error()
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code if your MATLAB code satisfies all  of these conditions: A boolean variable is initialized using an if-elseif-else  tree. Two of the branches of the if-elseif-else  tree contain a single assignment of a literal true  or false  to the boolean variable. The other branch of the if-elseif-else  tree contains an unconditional call to error() . The boolean variable is read later in the program to determine the condition of another if  statement.  When the issue happens, the later if  statement that reads the boolean variable is incorrectly simplified assuming that the variable always takes on the value true  or false . For example, in the following program intermediate  is initialized in an if-elseif-else  tree: function out = issue_2492753(selector)    if selector == 1intermediate = false;    elseif selector == 2intermediate = true;    elseerror('Unknown selector');    end    temp = 100;    if ~intermediatetemp = 1000;    end    out = temp.^2;end When code is generated for the function issue_2492753  by running this command: codegen -config:lib -args 0?issue_2492753  In the generated code for?issue_2492753, the program is incorrectly simplified assuming intermediate always gets assigned the value false : double issue_2492753(double selector){  (void)selector;  return 10000.0;} The correct generated code is: double issue_2492753(double selector){  int a;  a = 100;  if (selector == 1.0) {    a = 1000;  }  return a * a;}
Workaround: Handle the error check and variable initialization in independent if trees. Here is a modification of the example program issue_2492753:function out = issue_2492753(selector)    if selector ~= 1 || selector ~= 2error('Unknown selector');    end    if selector == 1intermediate = false;    elseintermediate = true;    end    temp = 100;    if ~intermediatetemp = 1000;    end    out = temp.^2;endNote that the error check is performed before an if-else statement that initializes?intermediate.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Coverage simulation with a filter applied is very slow
Description: If you apply a coverage filter to a model using the Coverage filter filename  parameter or CovFilter   command line parameter and then simulate the model, the simulation is slower than normal. This performance regression is especially noticeable for very large models.
Workaround: Avoid using the CovFilter parameter. Instead, apply the coverage filter after analysis. For example, apply the filter to the cvdata object.set_param('MyModel', 'CovFilter', '');?cvd = cvsim('MyModel');?cvd.filter = 'MyFilter.cvf';?This approach is especially useful if you are attempting to collect cumulative coverage aggregated from the results of multiple simulations and only need to see filtering applied to this cumulative coverage result.? Rather than applying filtering (via the?CovFilter parameter) during every simulation, apply filtering only to the final cumulative cvdata object. This significantly reduces the number of coverage filtering operations that need to be performed.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB does not destroy MATLAB objects on the call stack after a runtime error occurs in models containing MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks
Description: When a run-time error stops the simulation of a model that contains MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks that use MATLAB as the action language, MATLAB does not destroy temporary objects on the function call stack.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Parameters are evaluated incorrectly when starting with double literal followed by more than approximately 4100 whitespace characters followed by additional operations
Description: When setting a block parameter to a value consisting of a double literal followed by more than approximately 4100 characters of whitespace (depending on the length of the double literal) followed by subsequent operations, the block ignores the subsequent operations.? This issue applies to any block numerical parameter.
Workaround: Ensure that the first 4096 characters of a block parameter include code in addition to the double literal and whitespace. ?For example, either of the following produces correct results:>> set_param(gcb, 'Value', ['0 + 1.5' repmat(' ', 1, 5000) '+ 7'])>> set_param(gcb, 'Value', ['1.5 + 0' repmat(' ', 1, 5000) '+ 7'])??
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Generating coverage report takes very long time
Description: It takes a very long time to generate a coverage report, especially when attempting to generate one report for multiple cvdata  objects, such as in this example: cvhtml('covReport', cvdata1, cvdata2, cvdata3, cvdata4);?
Workaround: To generate a report showing the total cumulative coverage result accumulated from multiple simulations, do not pass multiple cvdata as input arguments to cvhtml command. Instead, sum the cvdata objects from each individual simulation together and then, pass this cumulative result as an? input argument to cvhtml.?cvdataSum = cvdata1 + cvdata2 + cvdata3 + cvdata4;cvhtml('covReport_Cumulative', cvdataSum);?
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Check, Simulink Coverage
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier analysis on a subsystem fails if model parameter SolverPrmCheckMsg is disabled
Description: If SolverPrmCheckMsg  model parameter is disabled then Simulink Design Verifier analysis on any subsystem inside that model fails with the following error message: Changing property 'SolverPrmCheckMsg' is not allowed .
Workaround: This issue can be avoided by using either of the following two workarounds:Ensure that the?SolverPrmCheckMsg model parameter is enabled.Generate subsystem harness using Simulink Test and invoke Simulink Design Verifier analysis on that harness model.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur if grounded Function-Call Subsystem with IC specified on subsystem's outport is present inside model reference block
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: The Function-Call Subsystem is present inside model reference block.? Non-zero initial condition is specified on Function-Call Subsystem's outport.? The outport with initial condition is connected to model reference block's outport.? The Function-Call Subsystem is fed by Ground block.?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with local block outputs enabled and a Zero-Order Hold block with multiple rates inside a For Each subsystem
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The? Enable local block outputs  model?configuration parameter is selected. A Zero-Order Hold block is inside a For Each subsystem  The Zero-Order Hold block's input and output ports have different sample times. The Treat each discrete rate as a separate task  model?configuration parameter is selected.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Copy block with the Exclude this block from Block Reduction optimization?parameter selected between the Zero-Order Hold block and the For Each subsystem output.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Stateflow charts inside Simulink based states might execute during actions on entry
Description: Stateflow charts in models using variable step solvers might experience unexpected behavior if the chart contains a Simulink based state, and that Simulink based state models a subsystem with a Stateflow chart in it.? When the Stateflow chart inside the Simulink based state contains both?entry  and during  actions, and during this step the state becomes active,  the during  actions are unexpectedly executed on entry.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect HDL code generation for a subsystem in the presence of Bus signals with fixed-point datatypes configured with 'Slope and Bias'.
Description: For a subsystem that has Bus signals with the fixed-point datatypes, the generated HDL code might be incorrect when you specify the fixed-point datatype with 'Slope and Bias'.?? HDL code generation does not supports fixed-point datatype specification with 'Slope and Bias'. For more information on the fixed-point datatypes support in HDL Coder, open the following documentation link:?? https://www.mathworks.com/help/hdlcoder/ug/supported-data-types-and-matlab-operators-and-control-flow-statements.html
Workaround: Change the specified datatype to 'Word length' and 'Fraction length' format.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur if reusable subsystem is connected to Conditional Subsystem's outport through Selector block with storage class on it's inport.
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: Model contains a reusable subsystem. The model configuration parameter Buffer for reusable subsystems  (BufferReusableBoundary ) is selected.? Reusable Subsystem's outport is connected to a Conditional Subsystem's outport through Selector block.? Number of elements present in Selector block's output signal are less than number of elements present on it's input signal.? The input signal to Selector block has storage class with initial value specified on signal object.?
Workaround: Clear the Buffer for reusable subsystems (BufferReusableBoundary) model configuration parameter.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: PLC Coder could generate incorrectly typed matrix address expressions
Description: PLC Coder may generate code that has incorrect matrix address expressions when such addresses are not constants. This may lead to errors while compiling the code in the target IDE.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when building a model with the parameter?RollThreshold higher than the number of iterations of a For Each?Subsystem nested in a For Iterator?Subsystem
Description: Consider a model that has a For Each?Subsystem nested within a For Iterator?Subsystem. If the number of iterations of the nested For Each?Subsystem  is less than the RollThreshold  configuration setting of the model, then building such a model might result in a crash.? Note that not all models that meet the above conditions will result in a crash. The possibility of a crash is subject to the modeling semantics of the nested For Each?S ubsystem.
Workaround: Set the RollThreshold to a value less than or equal to the number of iterations of the nested For Each?Subsystem.For example, if the For Each?Subsystem is supposed to execute 4 times, then? set the RollThreshold to a value less than or equal to 4.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink might log wrong answer at root output port with automatic unit conversion
Description: Simulink automatic unit conversion might log the wrong answer at a root output port during simulation when all of the following are true: The logging SaveFormat  is set to Dataset .  The signal before the root output port is branched.  The root output port unit does not match the source signal unit and the units are convertible.  Simulink automatically converts units at the root output port.  At least one of the other branches has a different unit from this root output port.
Workaround: Manually insert a Unit Conversion block at the root output port instead of relying on automatic unit conversion.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Analysis terminates with an?Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier abstract interpretation back end message
Description: Simulink Design Verifier Test Generation analysis terminates with an Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier abstract interpretation back end  message when the following two conditions are met:The model has partial matrix references. The indices used in partial references are constant which are not used anywhere else.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model hierarchy in which one model has a single instance of a reusable library subsystem and another model has multiple instances of the same reusable library subsystem
Description: Incorrect generated code might occur for a model hierarchy containing two models that meet all of these conditions:?Model A references a reusable library subsystem once for a given specialization such as dimensions or data type. Model B references the same reusable library subsystem at least twice with the same specialization. The reusable library subsystem contains a block whose output is determined to be constant.  The generated code might have the wrong number of elements in the initialization code for the constant block outputs structure relating to the reusable library subsystems in Model B. The target compiler might not catch this incorrect code and produce an error. This code is an example of the constant block output structure for the reusable library subsystem:  typedef struct {? const int32_T Width;?????????? } ConstB_RLS_5QZ55YRG;  This code is an example of the constant block output structure for Model B:  /* Invariant block signals (default storage) */typedef struct {? ConstB_RLS_5QZ55YRG RLS1;????? ? ConstB_RLS_5QZ55YRG RLS2;????? } ConstB;   This code an example of the incorrect initialization of the model constant block output structure including the reusable library subsystem constant block output structure in Model B: const ConstB rtConstB = {? {??? 4,???????????????????????????? ??? 3????????????????????????????? ? }? ? {??? 4,????????????????????????????? ??? 3?????????????????????????????? ? }}; ?
Workaround: You can either make the reusable library subsystem a nonreusable library subsystem, or in all models that reference the reusable library subsystem, override the reusable library subsystem settings to break reuse with other models.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Code Generation Readiness tool (coder.screener) incorrectly identifies certain functions and classes as unsupported for code generation
Description: When the Code Generation Readiness tool analyzes MATLAB code, it attempts to identify toolbox functions and classes that are not supported for code generation. This tool incorrectly identifies certain MATLAB function and class names as unsupported. You can run the Code Generation Readiness tool at the command line by using the coder.screener  function, from the current folder browser by using the context menu, or in the MATLAB Coder app. This issue affects all of these workflows. Functions and classes that are misidentified for the affected releases (R2019b to R2021a) are listed below. These lists are cumulative such that all names that are misidentified in an earlier release are also misidentified in later releases. In R2019b, these names are incorrectly identified as unsupported for code generation: robotics.BinaryOccupancyGridrobotics.AimingConstraintrobotics.CartesianBoundsrobotics.JointPositionBoundsrobotics.OrientationTargetrobotics.PoseTargetrobotics.PositionTargetrobotics.PurePursuitrobotics.VectorFieldHistogramrobotics.DubinsConnectionrobotics.DubinsPathSegmentrobotics.GeneralizedInverseKinematicsfusion.AsyncMARGGPSFuserfusion.ErrorStateIMUGPSFuserfusion.MARGGPSFuserfusion.NHConstrainedIMUGPSFuserrobotics.InverseKinematicsrobotics.LidarSLAMrobotics.PRMrobotics.OccupancyGridrobotics.OdometryMotionModelcustomAntennaElementisotropicAntennaElementrobotics.PoseGraphrobotics.PoseGraph3Drobotics.Raterobotics.ReedsSheppConnectionrobotics.ReedsSheppPathSegmentrobotics.ResamplingPolicyrobotics.RigidBodyrobotics.Jointrobotics.RigidBodyTreerobotics.ParticleFilter  In R2020a, these additional names are incorrectly identified: phased.BackscatterPedestrianrobotics.LikelihoodFieldSensorModelrobotics.MonteCarloLocalization  In R2020b, these additional names are incorrectly identified: robotics.OccupancyMap3D  In R2021a, these additional names are incorrectly identified: phased.BarrageJammerphased.ConstantGammaClutterphased.gpu.ConstantGammaClutterphased.PulseCompressionLibraryphased.PulseWaveformLibraryphased.TwoRayChannelphased.WidebandTwoRayChannelrobotics.ros.Publisherrobotics.ros.Raterobotics.ros.Subscriber
Workaround: Depending on the context in which you use the Code Generation Readiness tool, do one of the following:?In the standalone Code Generation Readiness tool, ignore the misidentified function or class name.?In the Code Generation Readiness section of the MATLAB Coder App, dismiss the misidentified function or class names by clicking the Next button which advances the app to the next section.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0129?displays an incorrect warning for State transition tables
Description: The?Sub-ID d? of Model Advisor?check?db_0129 ( db_0129_d:?Check orientation of transition lines)?reports incorrect warning?transitions are not vertical or horizontal and/ or diagonal for flow chart loops ?for transitions in State Transition tables.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect Unsatisfiable objectives for Relational Operator blocks where vector inputs are compared to a minimum or maximum values for a particular data type
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect Unsatisfiable (or Dead Logic) objectives  when all the following conditions are met:Relational Operator block? Signal with non-scalar dimensions?? Exactly one of the inputs to the block is a constant value that is either the minimum or the maximum value for that data type, and this makes the condition always true or always false.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: Edit-time checks incorrectly flag blocks on non-English platforms
Description: Edit-time checks incorrectly flag blocks on non-English platforms.
Workaround: Turn off edit-time checking.

Summary: Failed model simulations result in incorrect scaling of first and total order indices reported by sbiosobol
Description: Failed model simulations result in incorrect scaling of the first and total order indices reported by?sbiosobol . If model simulations fail during the computation of Sobol indices, the reported Sobol indices are incorrectly scaled by the factor   s = number of non-failed model simulations / number of all model simulations .? If model simulations fail while executing sbiosobol , warnings about the failed model simulations are shown in the MATLAB command window. The relative importance ranking of Sobol indices is not affected by this bug, because all first and total order Sobol indices are scaled by the same factor.
Workaround: The scaling factor,?s, can be computed from the results, sobolResults, returned from sbiosobol as follows:  s = sum(all(sobolResults.SimulationInfo.ValidSample, 2)) / height(sobolResults.ParameterSamples);To correct the Sobol indices, divide the reported Sobol indices sobolResults.SobolIndices by s.However, this workaround does not correct the values of the Sobol indices stored on the?sobolResults. In particular, plotting the Sobol indices using the plot and bar methods still reports the incorrectly scaled Sobol indices.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Error when attempting to import a DOORS 9 requirements module
Description: When you attempt to import a module from IBM DOORS 9, an error message that says?Could not recognize the date/ time format...  might appear. ?The date and time string included in the error message shows "HH:mm:ss" inserted in between the module creation or modification date and time. The error prevents the requirements module from importing to Simulink Requirements. This only happens on some installations of DOORS. One known reported case was with DOORS version 9.5.2.4, but other versions may be affected.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when unit mismatch exists between?Simulink.Signal object and port
Description: When you update the diagram, MATLAB might crash while propagating data types and dimensions when:? A unit is specified on a Simulink.Signal  object assigned to a signal. The unit does not match the unit specified at the source or destination port of the signal.  The crash report might contain output similar to: [? 0] 0x00007ffc28512fe3 sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00143331 MLTerminate_sl_units_diagnostics+00122563[? 1] 0x00007ffc2850803d sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00098365 MLTerminate_sl_units_diagnostics+00077597[? 2] 0x00007ffc2851bf41 sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00180033 MLTerminate_sl_units_diagnostics+00159265[? 3] 0x00007ffc2853d9d1 sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00317905 slUnitTable::reportUnitMismatch+00000577
Workaround: Change the Unit?field of the Simulink.Signal object, or the source or destination port so that the units are the same.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Generated code contains usage of dynamic memory allocation when DynamicMemoryAllocation is disabled
Description: When generating code for a MATLAB function that contains: A binary operation where one of the operands is a concatenation expression. The concatenation expression contains bounded variable-size data.  MATLAB Coder might produce generated code using dynamic memory allocation even if the DynamicMemoryAllocation  configuration option is disabled. For example when generating code for the program:? function out = issue_2462856(val,n)    boundedData1 = generateBoundedData(val,n,2);    boundedData2 = generateBoundedData(2*val,n,1);    boundedData3 = generateBoundedData(4*val,n,1);    out = boundedData1 .* [boundedData2,boundedData3];endfunction out = generateBoundedData(val,n,dim2)    coder.inline('never');    assert(n <= 255);    out = val .* (1:n)';    out = repmat(out,[1,dim2]);end by using these MATLAB commands: cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.DynamicMemoryAllocation = 'off';codegen -config cfg issue_2462856 -args {1,1} the generated code incorrectly contains usages of emxArray  APIs that use dynamic memory allocation. See the call to?emxEnsureCapacity_real_T  in the following generated code snippet:[SNIP]  b_tmp_size = tmp_size[0];  loop_ub = tmp_size[0];  i = r->size[0] * r->size[1];  r->size[0] = tmp_size[0];  r->size[1] = 1;  emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(r, i);  for (i = 0; i < b_tmp_size; i++) {    r->data[i] = tmp_data[i];  }[SNIP]? See also Bug Report ID 2511529 ?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: Launching Model Slicer to debug or inspect a Simulink Design Verifier objective displays an error when the model has a?Referenced Configuration Set
Description: In a model with Referenced Configuration Set, setting up Model Slicer for debugging or inspecting Simulink Design Verifier?objectives (Design Error Detection, Property Proving or Enhanced MCDC) fails due to the following error:?? For example, a model links to adata dictionary, A.sldd , that links to data dictionary B.sldd .? The model uses a variable that is defined in the data dictionary hierarchy, but not in a dictionary that the model is directly linked to. For example, B.sldd .  ?Under these conditions, the silent wrong answer can occur when you take these actions:  Perform an update diagram or simulate the model. Close the data dictionaries by calling the function Simulink.data.dictionary.closeAll() . Modify entries defined in B.sldd  that are used by the model. Perform an update diagram or simulate the model again.

IF temporalCounter_i1.done THEN? ? ? ?result := TRUE;? ? END_IF;END_IF;  Case 2:  temporalCounter_i1(timerAction := 2, maxTime := 5000);IF temporalCounter_i1.done THEN?? ? IF condition <> 0.0 THEN? ? ? ? result := TRUE;? ? END_IF;END_IF;  The code generated in both these cases is different, however, the simulation behavior is same as case 2 code for both the Stateflow transitions.

Summary: Model configuration parameter Use memcpy for vector assignment is not respected
Description: To implement copy of vector assignments,?the code generator uses a?memcpy ?call if the number of bytes to copy exceeds the?Memcpy threshold (bytes) ?model configuration parameter and the?Use memcpy for vector assignment ?model configuration parameter is enabled. In rare circumstances,?the code generator emits?memcpy ?even though Use memcpy for vector assignment ?option is disabled.?Following is the generated code to expect when the?Use memcpy for vector assignment ?option is disabled. for (i = 0; i < 15; i++) { ? ?A[i] = B[i];  }? Instead, the code generator produces the following:? memcpy(&A[0],?&B[0],?15U * (sizeof(int32))); See also?Bug Report ID?2477020 .
Workaround: Set the?MemcpyThreshold (bytes)?parameter to a very high value such as?inf or intmax('int32'). For example:set_param(modelName, 'MemcpyThreshold', intmax('int32'));
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: RL agents train poorly if an observation or action?is an array of 3 or more dimensions
Description: When the observation or the action of an environment is an array of 3 or more dimensions (e.g., image width, height, color), agents incorrectly concatenate and reshape observations or actions during minibatch creation. As a consequence, the agents are not able to learn a good policy in such environments. This bug does not affect training in any environment in which actions and observations are arrays of one or two dimensions (vectors or matrices).
Workaround: Separate a 3D array observation into multiple 2D arrays in your environment. Use multiple input layers for neural networks and concatenate them along the third dimension. You must modify your environment and neural network architecture. For example, if the observation is an 80-by-80-by-3 image, you can then split the observation into three 80-by-80 images (Image1, Image2, and Image3). In this case, you can modify the 3D (80-by-80-by-3) observation into a cell array of 3 observations in step and reset functions if you use a MATLAB environment, as follows: Image1 = Observation(:,:,1);Image2 = Observation(:,:,2);Image3 = Observation(:,:,3);Observation = {Image1, Image2, Image3};Observation information for multiple image inputs can be defined as follows:ObservationInfo(1) = rlNumericSpec([80 80]);ObservationInfo(2) = rlNumericSpec([80 80]);ObservationInfo(3) = rlNumericSpec([80 80]);Then the multiple input layers can be concatenated as follows:lgraph = layerGraph();tempLayers = imageInputLayer([80 80 1],"Name","Image1",'Normalization' ,'none');lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,tempLayers);tempLayers = imageInputLayer([80 80 1],"Name","Image2",'Normalization' ,'none');lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,tempLayers);tempLayers = imageInputLayer([80 80 1],"Name","Image3",'Normalization' ,'none');lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,tempLayers);tempLayers = concatenationLayer(3,3,"Name","concat_1");lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,tempLayers);lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph,"Image1","concat_1/in1");lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph,"Image2","concat_1/in2");lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph,"Image3","concat_1/in3");The rest of the network is the same as the original network.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with an error Empty character vector corresponding to an enumerated value

Workaround: Remove Model Arguments from the Export Function model.

Summary: Unexpected indexing error when evaluating a SimFunction object
Description: Using a?SimFunction  object to simulate a model can fail with an unexpected indexing error of the form:   Index in position 2 exceeds array bounds. Index must not exceed 4. The numbers in the error message might be different. This error is thrown if The simulation fails, and The?SimFunction ?object?has an?Observable ?object as a logged state.  This error might also occur when you use other functionality that relies on a SimFunction  object, such as fitting, global sensitivity analysis, or scanning.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following strategies to avoid simulation failures:Avoid extreme parameter values.Turn off absolute tolerance scaling.Adjust solver tolerances.The?Model Debugger for SimBiology?might also help to identify unexpected behavior during simulation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: impzest?produces wrong answers when used with multichannel input
Description: The impzest  function swaps channels when used with a multichannel?recording. For example, the output channels in this example are swapped when the input is stereo. exc = sweeptone(2,100/44100);ir1 = impzest(exc,exc);ir2 = impzest(exc,0.5*exc);stereo = [exc 0.5*exc];ir = impzest(exc,stereo);subplot(2,1,1), stem([ir1 ir2])subplot(2,1,2), stem(ir)  This bug does not affect the Impulse Response Measurer  application because the app records only one channel at a time.
Workaround: Use impzest with single-channel inputs only. Process multichannel signals one channel at a time. (See example in Description of Problem.)
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: fwrite does not write UTF-8 character data correctly
Description: Using fwrite  with character or string data results in the wrong bytes written to a file with UTF-8 encoding. For example, this code returns 255 . The correct output is [207;168] . >> fid = fopen('file.txt','w','n','UTF-8');>> fwrite(fid,char(1000));>> fclose(fid);>> fid = fopen('file.txt','r','n','UTF-8');>> bytes = fread(fid,'uint8')>> fclose(fid);bytes =   255% But bytes should equal [207;168]
Workaround: Instead of fwrite(fid,textdata) use fprint(fid,'%s',textdata).For example:>> fid = fopen('file.txt','w','n','UTF-8');>> fprintf(fid,'%s',char(1000));>> fclose(fid);>> fid = fopen('file.txt','r','n','UTF-8');>> bytes = fread(fid,'uint8')>> fclose(fid);bytes =   207   168
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect answer may be observed with reusable subsystems receiving N-D signals in the row-major mode
Description: Incorrect code may be generated under the following conditions: Model configuration parameter Array layout  is set to Row-major . Multiple subsystems have Reshape block(s) inside and the subsystem block parameter Function packaging  is set to Reusable  or Auto . The subsystems receive different N-D signals that have the same size overall. For example, [4 x 3] ,  [3 x 4] ,  [6 x 2]  have different individual dimensions but the same overall size, which is 12 in this case.
Workaround: Use one of the following:Set model configuration parameter Array layout to Column-major.Set the subsystem block parameter Function packaging to anything other than Reusable or Auto.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect behavior for models that contain a Rate Transition block that is not configured to ensure data integrity
Description: Incorrect behavior during accelerated simulation and incorrect code generation might occur for a model when both of the following conditions exist: The model contains a Rate Transition block that supports specification of the parameter?Initial conditions ? and the value of that parameter is non-zero. The output of the Rate Transition block is connected to the input of a second Rate Transition block that has the parameter Ensure data integrity during data transfer  set to off .
Workaround: For the second Rate Transition block, set Ensure data integrity during data transfer to on.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Persistent data referenced in parfor loop might be initialized incorrectly
Description: MATLAB Coder might produce code that initializes persistent data incorrectly if your MATLAB code satisfies all  of these conditions: Your MATLAB code contains a parfor  loop The body of the parfor  loop contains a function call The definition of this function uses a persistent variable  In the generated code, the data corresponding to the persistent variable is implemented by using a global variable that is an OpenMP threadprivate. To match the behavior of the MATLAB code, the data corresponding to the persistent variable must be initialized in each thread. When this issue occurs, the generated code only initializes the data in the main thread. For example, define this MATLAB function: function y = issue_2437817(n)y = 0;parfor i = 1:n    y = y + pers();endend function y = pers()persistent p;if isempty(p)    p = 2;endy = p;end Generate code for the function issue_2437817  by running this command: codegen -c -config:exe issue_2437817 -args {10}  In the generated code the persistent data?p ?is implemented by a threadprivate global variable: static double p;#pragma omp threadprivate(p)  But the function generated to initialize this data only initializes p ?in the main thread: void pers_init(void){  omp_get_max_threads();  p = 2.0;} Instead, the initialization function should have been generated as: void pers_init(void){  int ub_loop;  int i;  ub_loop = omp_get_max_threads();#pragma omp parallel for schedule(static)\ num_threads(omp_get_max_threads())  for (i = 1; i <= ub_loop; i++) {    p = 2.0;  }  p = 2.0;} This function initializes p ?for each program thread.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect result when using a string operation in a Stateflow C chart that leads to string truncation
Description: When the?String truncation checking ?parameter is set to warning  or none  in the model configuration parameters, and a Stateflow chart with C action language uses a string operation that leads to string truncation, subsequent string operations can lead to incorrect results. For example, consider the following code:? y = strcat("Hello", " world!");?  y_len = strlen(y) ;? If y  has type specified as stringtype(10) , then the expected result for y_len  is 10. However, the result for y_len  is 12, which is the length of the string "Hello world!"
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Host example models for Hall sensor and quadrature encoder sensor offset calibration do not display calibrated offset value for single pole pair PMSM
Description: The host models mcb_pmsm_host_offsetComputation_f28069m.slx  and mcb_pmsm_host_offsetComputation_f28379d.slx  that are part of the examples ˇ°Hall Offset Calibration for PMSM Motorˇ± and ˇ°Quadrature Encoder Offset Calibration for PMSM Motorˇ± do not display the sensor offset value after calibration completes for a single pole pair PMSM. The problem occurs due to an incorrect speed check implemented in these models that verifies whether the motor is running.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Option Ensure outport is virtual?not honored in generated code for root Outport block when driven by a signal with storage class
Description: Incorrect code generated from a model when all of the following conditions are present: The model contains a root Outport block with option Ensure outport is virtual  selected. The signal driving the input port of the root Outport block resolves to a Simulink.Signal  object. The StorageClass ?property of the CoderInfo  property of the Simulink.Signal  object has a value other than Auto .   In this situation the generated code should combine the memory for the root Outport block and the memory specified by the Simulink.Signal  object. However,?the root Outport block incorrectly makes an extra copy of input signal.

The input signal lines to the Subsystem Reference  block have incorrect layout, and when the spacebar is pressed to autofit the same, the zooming turns to 0%.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for the rcond function might return NaN instead of 0 for some inputs
Description: For nearly-singular matrices, the code generated for?rcond ?might return NaN  in cases where MATLAB returns 0 . This behavior is caused by floating-point overflow in intermediate calculations.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Models containing Drive Cycle Source blocks with?Output sample period?set to non-zero values less than?1s can take from minutes to several hours to open
Description: If you set the Drive Cycle Source block Output sample period (0 for continuous)  parameter to a non-zero values less than 1s, models with the block can take from minutes to several hours to open. The opening time depends on the drive cycle length and sample period. The model does open, however, the wait time might be prohibitive.
Workaround: To control the output sample rate, consider using a Rate Transition blocks. Specifically, use Rate Transition blocks before the velocity input and immediately after the Drive Cycle Source outputs the signals. The Rate Transition blocks allow you to specify a sample time for the model that is different from the Drive Cycle Source block.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Error occurs when you create a comparison report in the Simulation Data Inspector
Description: The Simulation Data Inspector can successfully create one comparison report. Creating a subsequent comparison report in the same MATLAB session results in this error:? DiffSignalResult data invalid. ?
Workaround: When you create a comparison report, delete the current comparison results before performing another comparison. To delete the current comparison results, in the Compare?pane,?right-click the run or signal and select Delete?from the context menu. After you delete the current comparison results, you can perform additional comparisons and create another comparison report without error.

Summary: Computing model checksum during simulation and code generation takes a long time for some model configurations
Description: For some model configurations, simulation or code generation can take a long time and may appear to be stuck when the status in the lower left corner of the Simulink window displays Compiling:?Computing checksums: Started . For example, a model that contains several Model blocks might display this performance issue.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code may be generated in model with multiple n-D Lookup Table blocks with fixed-point Table data?data type and floating point Intermediate results data type
Description: Incorrect code may be generated if all of the following conditions are true: The model has multiple n-D Lookup Table blocks The individual n-D Lookup Table blocks have: Number of table dimensions  set to 1  or 2 . Same input and table data types. Table data  data type parameter is a fixed-point data type. Intermediate results  data type parameter is a floating point data type.  The multiple n-D Lookup Table blocks differ in their fixed-point slope values for the?Table data  data type.
Workaround: Replace each affected n-D Lookup Table block with Prelookup block(s) connected to an Interpolation Using Prelookup block.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible unexpected behavior for Stateflow local events sent from a transition reachable from several states
Description: A Stateflow chart might not enter the expected destination state when the following conditions are true:? A path from the currently active source state contains a transition whose action sends a local event The transition also belongs to path whose source is some other state. For example, transitions from different source states come together at a junction, and then a transition from that junction sends a local event  After the local event is resolved, Stateflow unexpectedly returns to the source state, instead of completing the transition to the destination state, and any exit actions of the source state are not executed.?

Summary: Error when updating or deleting tasks for common job scheduler (CJS) clusters
Description: After you use the vectorized?createTask() ?syntax to create multiple tasks at the same time on your local cluster or other common job scheduler (CJS) clusters, if you either update a task's?Function ?or?InputArguments , or delete one or more (but not all) of the tasks  then you can see a?'MATLAB:badsubscript' ?error or, in rare cases, can change a task's?InputArguments ?to incorrect values. In the latter case, the task might produce a silent wrong answer if the task runs successfully with the incorrect?InputArguments .
Workaround: As a workaround, you canAvoid updating the?Function or InputArguments?properties of tasks after you create them with the vectorized createTask() syntaxAvoid deleting some (but not all of) tasks created with the vectorized?createTask()?syntaxUse the non-vectorized createTask() syntax to create tasks in a loop

Summary: MATLAB may crash when using an S-function with tunable parameters that does not register run-time parameters
Description: MATLAB might occasionally crash when all of the following conditions are true: You are using an S-function with tunable parameters You do not register any run-time parameters through ssSetNumRunTimeParams , and  The parameters are n ot of type: double, single, integer, boolean, 1D char array or 2D char array?  For example, MATLAB might crash if the tunable parameters are fixed-point, struct, or char arrays of 3 or more dimensions, and no run-time parameter is registered.?
Workaround: Set the number of run-time parameters using ssSetNumRunTimeParams in your S-function with tunable parameters. Also, register the run-time parameters using?ssRegAllTunableParamsAsRunTimeParams, ssRegDlgParamAsRunTimeParam, or ssSetRunTimeParamInfo.?To detect if your Simulink model contains S-functions that could trigger this crash, set the?SfunCompatabilityCheckMsg?Configuration Parameter to?'warning'?or?'error'. Incompatible S-Functions will throw the error (or warning):"S-function "system" in "model" does not explicitly set the number of run-time parameters using?ssSetNumRunTimeParams. This compatibility diagnostic on the need for S-function upgrades can be set to?none,?warning, or?error?using the Configuration Parameters dialog".The stacktrace may contain lines similar to:[? 0] 0x00000000fe9ea270 libut.dll+00303728 ut_md5ChecksumRec::~ut_md5ChecksumRec+00000896[? 1] 0x00000000fe9eab7d libut.dll+00306045 ut_md5ChecksumRec::~ut_md5ChecksumRec+00003213[? 2] 0x00000001b825fdc7 sl_compile.dll+03210695 BusConnectionChecker::runChecks+00010407[? 3] 0x00000001b827626c sl_compile.dll+03301996 slcomp::slAddRTParamDataToChecksum+00000700[? 4] 0x00000001b8299a50 sl_compile.dll+03447376 slcomp::BdComputeChecksums+0002582??
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating models that contain masked Simscape blocks
Description: When you try to simulate a model containing Simscape blocks with masks put directly on them, MATLAB might crash.? In R2020a and R2020b, the crash report contains output similar to: ? ? ?0x00007fc779dc6a57 gl::sli::ModelTranslator::Impl::generateRuntimeParameterInfo(...)  In R2021a, MATLAB issues an error similar to:?? ? ? ?Error compiling Simscape network for model my_dcmotor.? ? ?Caused by:? ? ? ? ?['my_dcmotor/DC Motor/Rotor Resistance']: 'RTP_5F058808_R' is not defined in this scope.
Workaround: Using Simscape blocks as masked linked blocks is not supported. To mask a Simscape block, insert the block into a subsystem and mask that subsystem.

Summary: fplot?and related functions may be susceptible to low-precision problems
Description: When plotting symbolic expressions that?matlabFunction ?cannot handle,?fplot ,?fplot3 ,?fsurf ,?fmesh ,?fcontour ,?fimplicit , and?fimplicit3 ?perform function evaluation at low precision. In some cases, this may cause numerical instabilities, potentially including incorrect results that look reasonable. ex = str2sym("piecewise(x < 100, (pi^2-107607675/10902937)*x)");??fplot(ex)
Workaround: Avoid the low-precision evaluation by explicitly calling?subs. Note that this significantly degrades performance.syms xex = str2sym("piecewise(x < 100, (pi^2-107607675/10902937)*x)");?fplot(@(xval) subs(ex,x,xval))
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Greater than expressions that use the duration operator on the left hand side do not return true at the correct simulation step
Description: Stateflow incorrectly evaluates greater than expressions when all of the following conditions are true: The Stateflow chart uses a fixed sample time The expression includes a duration  operator on the left hand side The right side of the expression evaluates to a value that is not a whole multiple of the chart's sample time  When these conditions are met, Stateflow evaluates the expression as?true  one time step later than expected.
Workaround: For charts that include the duration operator on the left side of a greater than expression, ensure that the right side of the expression specifies time that matches sample time of the chart.Alternatively, you can change the sample time of the chart to accommodate all values of time that are used in the chart.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Discrepancy in value for?Override SIL or PIL Mode?between test case and expected report in Simulink Test
Description: In the IEC Certification Kit test suite for Simulink Test, the information section in test case?SLT_TIT_020? incorrectly specifies?Override SIL or PIL Mode  ?as?1 .? The correct value is?0 . As a result, the validation status fails due to a discrepancy between the?tTIR? baseline report and generated report.? Impacted files include:? <matlabroot>/toolbox/qualkits/iec/sltest/expected_results/tTIR.docx  <matlabroot>/toolbox/qualkits/iec/sltest/expected_results/tTIR.pdf  <matlabroot>/toolbox/qualkits/iec/sltest/expected_results/tTIR.zip

Summary: Discrepancy in value for Override SIL or PIL Mode between test case and expected report in Simulink Test
Description: In the DO Qualification Kit test suite for Simulink Test, the information section in test case SLT_TIT_020  incorrectly specifies Override SIL or PIL Mode   as 1 .? The correct value is 0 . As a result, the qualification status fails due to a discrepancy between the tTIR  baseline report and generated report.? Impacted files include:? <matlabroot>/toolbox/qualkits/do/sltest/expected_results/tTIR.docx  <matlabroot>/toolbox/qualkits/do/sltest/expected_results/tTIR.pdf  <matlabroot>/toolbox/qualkits/do/sltest/expected_results/tTIR.zip

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation when a Concatenate block is inside an Iterator Subsystem
Description: Incorrect code might be generated in some cases when an Assignment block feeds to a Concatenate block that is inside an Iterator Subsystem. This occurs while generating code from a Simulink model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a root Outport block that has a fixed-point data type with nonzero bias and an initial condition specified on its input signal
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: The Remove root level I/O zero initialization  configuration parameter is selected (that is,? ZeroExternalMemoryAtStartup ?is set to 'off '). The root Outport block has a fixed-point?data type with nonzero bias.? The root Outport block input signal connects to a subsystem Outport block with an initial condition.? The sum of the nonzero bias on the fixed-point signal and the initial condition on the subsystem Outport block is equal to zero.?
Workaround: Clear the Remove root level I/O zero initialization configuration parameter.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Normal mode simulation and code generation results might not match if the model is composed of an atomic subsystem containing only a Bus Selector block
Description: When a model is composed of an atomic subsystem that contains only a Bus Selector block, block reduction might remove the buffer synthesized in that subsystem incorrectly in code generation. This may lead to a simulation result that is different from the normal mode simulation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation results might be incorrect in the Accelerator and Rapid Accelerator mode for reusable subsystem outport connected to constant sample time signal
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all of the following conditions are met: Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator simulation mode is chosen. Reusable subsystem's outport is connect to block with constant (inf ) sample time? Configuration parameter BufferReusableBoundary  is checked
Workaround: Disable BufferReusableBoundary configuration parameter.

Summary: Uninstalling a previous version of a toolbox fails when the toolbox includes apps and multiple versions are installed
Description: If a toolbox includes apps and multiple versions of that toolbox are installed, uninstalling a previous version of the toolbox fails.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for Width block when output signal with a constant value is configured with default storage class
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a Width block when all of these conditions exist: The Width block has a constant value output. For example, a Width block with a fixed-dimension input signal that generates the signal dimension as output. The output signal is configured with the Model default  storage class through either individual mapping or a resolved signal object. The default storage class for internal data is Default.   In this case, the signal variable is not initialized in the generated code.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: RF instruments might generate distorted signal constellation and incorrect EVM
Description: The signal constellation generated by an RF signal generator instrument might be distorted, leading to larger than expected EVM values. This occurs when provided IQ data values are greater than 1. This affects the following functions and apps: rfsiggen  (Instrument Control Toolbox) Wireless Waveform Generator app (Communications Toolbox, since R2019a), see https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/details/2421208   This affects the output of RF instruments from all supported vendors, including: Rohde & Schwarz Keysight
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Unexpected error because of unresolved component names when simulating a?SimBiology.Scenarios?object using?SimFunction
Description: When simulating a?SimBiology.Scenarios ?object using?SimFunction , the simulation fails unexpectedly because component names are not resolved to model components in the SimBiology model. This happens when the names contain unnecessary square brackets. Examples of unnecessary brackets:  A species DrugTarget  is specified as [DrugTarget] . A parameter k ?is specified as?[k] . In a single compartment model, a species Drug 1 ?is specified as [Drug 1] .
Workaround: Remove the unnecessary brackets from quantity component names in the SimBiology.Scenarios object by using the?updateEntry?function to change their names.For example:Create a SimBiology.Scenarios object referencing species DrugTarget and Drug 1 with brackets:sObj = SimBiology.Scenarios("[DrugTarget]", [0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7]);?add(sObj, "cartesian", "[Drug 1]", [2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8, 2.9]);Change the names of the first and second entries in the SimBiology.Scenarios object:updateEntry(sObj, 1, "Name", "DrugTarget");??updateEntry(sObj, 2, "Name", "Drug 1");
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Exporting test-cases from Simulink Design Verifier to Simulink Test causes?Export operation aborted error
Description: Exporting test-cases from Simulink Design Verifier to Simulink Test could fail and display an?Export operation aborted  error dialog box under the following conditions:A Signal Builder harness is chosen for exporting the test-cases Simulink Design Verifier analysis is configured with parameter constraints
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Assertion error in?CG::SimpleConstructionFacade::structMemberRefE when generating code for Simscape models
Description: When generating code for a Simscape model, MATLAB crashes. The crash report contains output similar to: 0x00007ffb9504be43 CG::SimpleConstructionFacade::structMemberRefE at b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\facade\simpleconstructionfacade.cpp:1386 (in \\matlab\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll)0x00007ffba1ac0303 exec::cg::ExternalFunctions::translate at b:\matlab\toolbox\physmod\common\exec\cg\src\emitter\externalfunctions.cpp:393 (in \\matlab\bin\win64\physmod_common_exec_cg_emitter.dll)

Summary: Second calls to dlgradient do not return correct derivatives after using the lstm or gru functions with inputs containing a gpuArray.
Description: For models that use the lstm  or gru  functions for dlarray  inputs containing a gpuArray , calling dlgradient  more than once on values that depend on the outputs of the lstm ?or?gru  functions result in incorrect gradients in the second and subsequent calls. For example, in the following code snippet, the second, third and fourth calls to dlgradient  return incorrect results. dlY = lstm(dlX,H0,C0,weights,recurrentWeights,bias);  loss = mse(dlY,targets);  gradients1 = dlgradient(loss,dlX);  gradients2 = dlgradient(loss,H0);  gradients3 = dlgradient(loss,C0);  gradients4 = dlgradient(loss,weights);  ?

Summary: Incorrect storage class access function is used for custom TLC code
Description: Embedded Coder might use the value function of a storage class, even though the user authored TLC code requests the address.  For example, LibBlockInputSignalAddress ?returns the value function with a C address operator, if the custom block has a signal with a storage class that returns access function as its input.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 5, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for Mod block with AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains at least one Mod block whose two input types are of the same type. The input types are one of the following: int8  uint8  int16  uint16  int32  uint32   The AUTOSAR 4. 0 code replacement library is used to generate code. The? Mfx_Mod  function? is present in the generated code.  Because the block behavior is different than the AUTOSAR standard, numerical results from code generation will be incorrect when mod(x1, x2)  meets one of the following conditions:? ?x1 < 0  ?x2 = 0
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Poorly formatted Iteration names and Excel tab names when generated test cases are exported to Simulink Test
Description: Iteration names and tab names in the Excel file corresponding to the iterations are generated in the form Scenarios_1 , Scenarios_1_1 , Scenarios_1_1_1  instead of Scenarios_1 , Scenarios_2 , Scenarios_3  if test-cases generated by Simulink Design Verifier are exported to Simulink Test when:Using the Export test cases to Simulink Test  link in the Simulink Design Verifier Results Summary  Windows and selecting Excel  from the Test Data Format  drop-down selection in the Export Design Verifier Test Cases  dialog Using the Create Test for Component Wizard ?in Simulink Test Manager, select the Use Design Verifier to generate test input scenarios ?option in the Test Inputs ?page, and select EXCEL ?in the Specify the file format ?drop-down selection of the Generated Test ?page.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect markers for residuals plot in SimBiology Model Analyzer
Description: In the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, if you try to plot residuals for fit results generated using a mixed effects fitting algorithm, the plot markers for IWRES  and CWRES  are swapped from those indicated in the legend.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: For TQL4 qualifications - Incomplete list of MISRA C:2012 Directives and Rules in Polyspace Bug Finder Tool Requirements
Description: This issue affects TQL4 tool qualification only.??
