The following MISRA C:2012 directives and rules are covered in the Polyspace Bug Finder qualification tests, but they are missing from the table in section "2.8.2.1: MISRA C:2012 Rules or Directives" of the?Polyspace Bug Finder Tool Requirements? (qualkitdo_bugfinder_tr.docx ) artifact: Dir 1.1 Dir 4.7 Dir 4.14 Rule 12.5 Rule 21.13 Rule 21.14 Rule 21.15 Rule 21.16 Rule 21.17 Rule 21.18 Rule 21.19 Rule 21.20 Rule 22.7 Rule 22.8 Rule 22.9 Rule 22.10

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Normal?and Accelerator mode simulations may yield incorrect results for complex fixed-point types with word length greater than 64 bits
Description: Simulation results for?Normal ?and?Accelerator ?mode?may be inaccurate in models containing Simulink blocks with a complex parameter of fixed-point type which has word length greater than 64 bits and the block output datatype does not match the parameter type. For example, in a model containing a Constant block attached to an Outport block, if you specify the Constant value  as fi(1.5+2.5i,1,67,1) ?and specify the Output data type ?as fixdt(1,77,1) , an incorrect output is generated during simulation.? Observed in but not limited to the following blocks:  Constant block Gain block Unit Delay block Delay block
Workaround: The key condition which triggers this bug is that the block parameter undergoes data type conversion from its original fixed-point type to a different fixed-point type.Use alternative designs that avoid implicit data type conversions within the blocks for complex values which use fixed-point datatypes and have word length greater than 64 bits.This can be achieved by either one of these:Maintain data type consistency within the block by setting?Output data type?to?Inherit.Use a Data Type Conversion block when data type conversions are needed.Perform any data type conversions needed within the parameter or expression declaration in the block. For example, fi(fi(1.5+2.5i,1,67,1),1,77,1).
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: dlgradient can return incorrect results for models using the gru function when calculating derivatives on the CPU with respect to the GRU state.
Description: When the gradient computation depends on the state output of the gru  function (the second output), the derivatives are incorrectly computed as zero. This issue does not occur when the input data contains a gpuArray  object.
Workaround: Instead of using the state output of the gru function, use the last time-step of the first output. For example, for data where the third dimension of the output corresponds to time, replace instances of the code[dlY,hiddenState] = gru(dlX,H0,weights,recurrentWeights,bias)withdlY = gru(dlX,H0,weights,recurrentWeights,bias)hiddenState = dlY(:,:,end);
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: JMAAB check jc_0792 erroneously displays warning for enum types defined in the data dictionary
Description: The Model Advisor JMAAB check  Check unused data in Simulink Model ?(id:? mathworks.jmaab.jc_0792 ) erroneously displays?a warning for enum types used in the model that are defined in the data dictionary.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Unexpected error when using model arguments on a masked Model block
Description: Simulink will incorrectly error when you have a model hierarchy with three or more levels for which all of the following conditions are true: A referenced model has a model argument. Its parent model is referenced in normal mode. The Model block in this parent model is masked. The value of the model argument in this parent model is a model argument specified in the model workspace of this parent model.  The error will be similar to: The symbol 'X' resolved to a parameter object that was created by the MaskInitialization code of 'mid/ModelBlock'. Parameter objects should only be created in the base workspace, data dictionary, or model workspace.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Navigation to requirements in exported Web view file does not behave as expected
Description: When you generate a model Web view file that includes the model's requirements information, you can navigate to a requirement in two ways: Select a model element that is linked to a requirement. In the right pane, under Links , click the hyperlink to navigate to the listed requirement. In the Links ?tab, select a link. In the right pane, under Properties , a requirement might be listed as a?Destination ?item (or, rarely, as a Source? item). Click the hyperlink to navigate to the listed requirement.  When you try to navigate to a referenced requirement (slreq.Reference ?object), the navigation switches to the Requirements ?tab, but does not highlight or scroll to the referenced requirement. Additionally, this might happen sporadically for regular requirements ( slreq.Requirement ?objects).

Summary: Parallel build failure when execution profiling is enabled and generated code is up to date
Description: The build process for your model might fail under these conditions: Parallel building of referenced models is enabled. The generated code is up to date. The shared utility folder contains at least one .c  or .cpp  file. Execution profiling is enabled.  You might see an error message like this:? Error using loadUnable to read file 'C:\ Users\...\_sharedutils\ buildInfo. mat'. ?No such file or directory. Error in coder. internal. instrumentationStage
Workaround: Disable parallel building of referenced models. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box,? clear the?Enable parallel model reference builds check box.

Summary: JMAAB check?Consistency in model element names?incorrectly warns about Truth Table block
Description: The Model Advisor check?Consistency in model element names ? (ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0602)? produces an incorrect warning about the inconsistency in model element names for a Truth Table block.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Calling solve?in parallel on an EquationProblem?might fail to find a solution
Description: For some?EquationProblems , solve ?might not find a solution when the?UseParallel ?option is set to true . For example: ep = eqnproblem;x = optimvar('x', 1, 2);F = optimexpr(2, 1); F(1) = (-(x(1)+x(2))).^2 - x(2)*(1+x(1)^2);F(2) = x(1)*(x(2)-3).^2 + x(2)*(x(1)-2).^2 - 0.5;ep.Equations = F == 0;x0.x = [0 0];opts = optimoptions('fsolve', 'UseParallel',true);sol = solve(ep, x0, 'Options', opts); This code returns: No solution found.fsolve stopped because the problem appears regular as measured by the gradient,but the vector of function values is not near zero as measured by thevalue of the function tolerance. However, when run in serial, solve  finds a solution. sol = solve(ep, x0); When called with UseParallel  set to its default value (false ), solve  returns the following: Equation solved.fsolve completed because the vector of function values is near zeroas measured by the value of the function tolerance, andthe problem appears regular as measured by the gradient.
Workaround: Do not specify the UseParallel?option or set it to?false:sol = solve(ep, x0);

Summary: Unable to import requirements from IBM DOORS Next using direct import
Description: You can directly import requirements from IBM DOORS Next by opening the Requirements Editor and selecting File  > Import . In the Importing Requirements dialog, set Document type ?to IBM DOORS Next . Enter your server information and credentials in the DOORS Server dialog. The Document location ?drop-down is auto-populated with project names from the server that you entered. However, sometimes this drop-down? is not populated with DOORS Next project names, and it is impossible to select the project to import from. The malfunction happens when DOORS Next Server is configured for "Basic Authentication" (as opposed to the "Form-based Authentication"), and the IBM Jazz server version is 7.0.1 or newer.

Summary: NaNs appear at the output port of the Recursive Polynomial Model Estimator when system is overparameterized
Description: The problem occurs when the system is overparameterized (has too high an order) with respect to the variation in the data. The parameters of the identified system become insensitive to the data and this insensitivity drives the covariance to infinity.?  This results in Nans to appear at the output port of the Recursive Polynomial Model Estimator. Embedded processors may have difficulty in processing NaN values.

Summary: The Model Advisor check Check fundamental logical and numerical operations?displays an error when using?Simulink.AliasType
Description: The Model Advisor check Check fundamental logical and numerical operations ?(ID:mathworks.maab.na_0002 ) checks for appropriate usage of basic logical and numerical operations. Signals with Boolean alias type (Simulink.AliasType) connected to Enable subsystem or Trigger subsystem block is flagged by the check even though their base type is Boolean. For a similar issue see the following?bug report .
Fixed In: R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility checking fails for model containing AUTOSAR Blockset lookup table blocks
Description: If you check Simulink Design Verifier compatibility for a model that contains AUTOSAR Blockset lookup table blocks, the function fails with the following error message: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.Component:simulink | Category:Design Verifier compatibility error
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model reference targets may fail to rebuild when they have unsaved changes and parallel builds are used
Description: If a model references models that contain unsaved changes and parallel building is enabled for the referenced models, the parallel build process does not consider the unsaved changes. This can result in model reference simulation targets and code generation targets that are out of date. For example, if a model contains a Model block with Simulation mode  set to Accelerator  or? Software-in-the-loop (SIL) , simulation might produce incorrect numeric results.

Summary: Generated code may be incorrect if a mask parameter in reusable subsystems is used in multiple places
Description: In reusable subsystems if a mask parameter is used in multiple places and one of them has logical expression then the generated code may be incorrect. This is especially true if the value of mask parameter exceeds underlying data type of boolean  (which is unsigned char ). In such a case, overflow may occur and generated code may not produce correct result.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Code Generation:?GPU Coder generated code might produce incorrect output in the presence of gpucoder.reduce with large inputs
Description: GPU Coder generated code produces incorrect output when the following conditions are met: The design function contains gpucoder.reduce.?  The number of elements in the input array to gpucoder.reduce  is greater than 67107841 (?numel(inputArray) > 67107841 ).
Fixed In: R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Final state not restored for referenced model when a noncontinuous derivative output changes at the final state
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect if all the following conditions are true:? The top model in a model hierarchy references a model that does not reference other models. The referenced model outputs a noncontinuous derivative signal. For example, the referenced model might contain a Pulse Generator block that connects to an Integrator block that connects to a root output port. The referenced model simulates in accelerator mode. The referenced model Type ?configuration parameter is set to?Variable-step . The referenced model?Final states ?and?Initial state ?configuration parameters are selected.  Simulation does not restore the final state when the referenced model output changes at the final state.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when an export-function model contains a Constant block that feeds a block executing in a different task
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for an export-function model that meets all of these conditions: The export-function model contains a Constant block. The output of the Constant block feeds a block which executes in a different task. At least one of these blocks executes in a function-call subsystem that executes at an inherited rate.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multi-rate subsystem updates discrete state in an incorrect order
Description: When output and update code is in one function in the generated code, code generator may try to force the simulation execution order to be the same as the code generation order. The simulation execution order may not match the order in the generated code under all these conditions:? The model configuration parameter?"ForceCombineOutputUpdateInSim " is enabled.? The multi-rate subsystem contains Mux block. For the Mux block: Output is connected to a block with discrete state. One input is connected to Rate Transition block inside subsystem whereas other input is connected to Rate Transition block outside of subsystem.?  The simulation updates the buffer values in order such that output and update function for this subsystem cannot be combined.?
Workaround: Use Vector Concatenate block instead of Mux block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated from a combination of coder.const and coder.extrinsic?in MATLAB might be incorrect
Description: You can generate code from MATLAB code that simultaneously:? Declares a function foo ?as extrinsic by using  coder.extrinsic('foo')  Uses  coder.const  to constant-fold an expression that directly or indirectly invokes the extrinsic function  foo    This MATLAB code pattern might produce incorrect C/C++ code if the size of the return value of?foo  is different from what the caller of foo ?assumes (for example, based on a previous assignment). Specifically, this issue occurs if all  of these conditions are true about your MATLAB code:? The main function uses coder.const  to call a function Foo  Foo  contains a call to another function Bar  that is declared as extrinsic The extrinsic function Bar  returns an array A  The host function?Foo ?assigns this return value to a variable that has been assigned previously That variable is previously assigned from a scalar or from an array of size smaller than A   In such situations, the code generator drops the extra returned elements, and truncates the returned (larger) array so that it fits the size of the initially assigned array A . This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Changing a model argument value might not trigger rebuild of model reference target
Description: Simulation and code generation might use an out-of-date model reference target when a model argument value has changed and Simulink adds hidden Model blocks to the model. For example, Simulink adds hidden Model blocks when: The output of a Model block is a virtual bus. A Model block receives input from a?function-call input port.
Workaround: Delete the?slprj?folder and?.slxc?file from the previous build, then build the model again.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: undefined function error when you click Cancel Build in Build Status window
Description: If the Build Status window is open when parallel building of referenced models is in progress, clicking the Cancel Build  button can produce an?undefined function ?error.

Summary: SLibIfVariantConditionForm16b error for S-Function blocks using?LibAddToCommonIncludes
Description: Simulink model containing S-Function results in the build error Attempt to call a non-function value: SLibIfVariantConditionForm16b ?when the below conditions are TRUE :The S-Function includes a header(example TestHeader. h) using the LibAddToCommonIncludes . This header is also included by another parameter/ dwork/ signal which is variant.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Extraneous AUTOSAR array data types exported when shared base data type has min or max values
Description: Array data types exported from an AUTOSAR model might differ from the corresponding imported array data types if both of the following conditions exist: Imported ARXML descriptions contain AUTOSAR array data types and a ServerCallPoint  argument that reference the same base data type. The shared base data type specifies min or max values.  When you generate code, the exported ARXML contains additional unnecessary array data types.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Error while importing OpenDRIVE file into Driving Scenario Designer app
Description: If an OpenDRIVE file contains negative laneoffset values, and you import this file into the Driving Scenario Designer ?app,the app returns this error:'Index in position 1 exceeds arraybounds (must not exceed 1) '.

Summary: Generation of C++ code from MATLAB Code might take a long time
Description: For some large MATLAB designs, generation of C++  code from MATLAB code might take a long time, where generation of C  code takes significantly less time. If you encounter a very long code generation time, and switching the target language from C++  to C  speeds up code generation time significantly, consider updating to the release that contains the fix for this issue.

Summary: Generated code does not compile when an Intel SSE (Windows) code replacement library is used with a MinGW64 compiler
Description: The generated code does not compile on a Windows platform when all of the following conditions are true:?The Code replacement libraries  parameter is set to?Intel SSE(Windows) . ?The Toolchain  parameter is set to?MinGW64 ?or  Automatically locate an installed toolchain  with MinGW64 being the default toolchain.  ?The generated code contains SIMD code replacements for the floor  or ceil  functions, so the generated code contains _mm_floor_pd  or _mm_ceil_pd function s.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, set the Build configuration parameter to Specify.Click Toolchain details. In the table, if you are generating C code, for the?C Compiler?row, add -msse4 to the end of the statement in the Option column . If you are generating C++ code,?add -msse4 to the end of the statement in the?C++ Compiler?row.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: QoS settings for ROS 2 in Simulink get reset in code generation
Description: The Quality of Service (QoS) settings for ROS 2 Publish block and Subscribe block in Simulink are reset to default values in generated code.

Summary: MATLAB might crash while closing due to AUTOSAR mapping
Description: If an AUTOSAR model has a mapped block that resolves to a Simulink.Signal  object, MATLAB might crash with a fatal error when MATLAB is closing. The following types of blocks might cause the crash: Data Store Memory blocks Any block that has a state

Summary: MATLAB crashes when trying to access an empty sparse matrix returned from an out of process MEX function or an out of process C++ Engine application
Description: If an empty sparse matrix is either sent to MATLAB or returned from MATLAB using an out of process MEX function or an out of process C++ Engine application, MATLAB crashes.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: C Caller, C Function, MATLAB System, and Dataflow Subsystem blocks may generate wrong simulation results with bus type data
Description: The following blocks may yield wrong simulation results when using bus type data of any scope: C Caller C Function MATLAB System (when simulating using code generation) Dataflow Subsystem
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect answers in rapid accelerator mode when Stateflow charts call multi-instance model blocks in export-function mode
Description: Incorrect results are possible in rapid simulation mode fora model that meets the following conditions: The model contains two or more identical Stateflow charts. The Stateflow charts call multi-instance Model blocks.  The multi-instance Model blocks are in export-function mode.  For the above scenario, incorrect answers might occur at the model block outputs.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Rectangular images result in complex-valued shearlet coefficients for real transforms and NaNs in reconstruction
Description: Shearlet systems with images where the aspect ratio deviates significantly from 1:1 exhibit numerical instabilities.   The aspect ratio is the ratio of the image's? width to height. For example, an image with a width of 1200 and height of 600 has an aspect ratio of 2:1. You can create a shearlet system for this image size using code similar to this: shs = shearletSystem('ImageSize',[600 1200]);?  In the above shearlet system, the value of the?TransformType ?property? is 'real' , but because the image's aspect ratio deviates significantly?from 1:1, you might still obtain significantly large imaginary parts in the shearlet coefficients. Also, the inverse shearlet transform? might result in  NaN s?i n the reconstructed image.
Workaround: Pad the image to an aspect ratio closer to 1:1 as detailed in this example:https://www.mathworks.com/help/releases/R2020b/wavelet/ug/boundary-effects-in-real-valued-bandlimited-shearlet-systems.htmlYou can remove the padding after reconstruction or ignore the padded coefficients during analysis.?

Summary: A bug in 64-bit Windows 10 Version 2004 system math library can cause MathWorks products to return incorrect numerical?results.
Description: There is a bug in one Microsoft library included in the Windows 10 Version 2004. Some calculations performed using this library can cause later calculations to sporadically return NaN. For more background on this bug, see?this page on Microsoft's community developer forum .
Workaround: Unzip and run the attached MEX file ("ucrt_2004_fmod_patch.mexw64"). This MEX file modifies the system library in memory which should avoid the bug. You will need to run this file in each MATLAB session. You can automate this process by adding a call to this MEX-file in your?startup.m?file; see https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/ref/startup.html.This MEX file looks for the signature of the fmod error and if it does not recognize that signature, it prints this text (Actual Bytes might be different):INFO: ucrt_2004_fmod_patch was not applied.Expected signature of DD D8 DD D9 not recognized.Actual bytes:DD C0 DD C1?If you see this, it means you may have an older or newer version of the UCRT unaffected by this issue.

Summary: Incorrect ARXML data constraints exported for an AUTOSAR model containing multiple lookup tables
Description: Incorrect ARXML data constraints might be exported for an AUTOSAR model when all of the following conditions are true: The model contains multiple Simulink lookup tables. The lookup tables use the same field names for some of their table values or breakpoints. Table values or breakpoints that have the same field name specify different Simulink data constraints. For example, the table values or breakpoints use different min and max values or different data types.  In the exported ARXML for the model, the data constraints for table values and breakpoints that use the same field name are not correctly differentiated.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data might be incorrectly mapped when using atomic subcharts and either nested buses or an array of buses
Description: Data mapped to an atomic subchart in Stateflow might be incorrectly read, written, or assigned if the data comes from a bus with a nested bus or an array of buses.
Workaround: Create a local variable in the parent chart for the bus data that you want to mapMap the local variable to the atomic subchart variableWrite to the local variable as needed so its value always matches the bus data

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect number of bytes used for array of structures memcpy
Description: To implement a copy of an array of structures, MathWorks C/C++ code generators use a memcpy  call if the number of bytes to copy exceeds the Memcpy threshold (bytes)  model configuration parameter. Under rare circumstances the code generator might produce a memcpy  call for the array of structures with the incorrect number of bytes to copy. When this issue occurs the incorrect memcpy  corresponds to an array of structures copy with the following properties: Individual elements of the array are assigned as a sequence of statements A subset of array element copies are direct structure copies A subset of array element copies are composed of copies of the structure's fields  The following is a sample pattern where the code generator produces an incorrect memcpy . Note that element 0 and 1 of the array are assigned using structure's fields while elements 2 and 3 are direct copies: Str1[0].f1 = Str2[0].f1;Str1[0].f2 = Str2[0].f2;Str1[1].f1 = Str2[1].f1;Str1[1].f2 = Str2[1].f2;Str1[2]?= Str2[2];Str1[3]?= Str2[3]; When this issue occurs the code generator produces the memcpy : ? ? ?memcpy(Str1[0] , Str2[0], 2 * sizeof(struct));   Which only copies 2 structure elements, whereas the original code was copying 4 struct elements.  To check for an array of structures assignment as described above, the steps in the workaround section can be applied to disable memcpy  generation.? This issue affects the following workflows:? Simulink model to C/C++ code generation using Simulink Coder MATLAB to C/C++ code generation using MATLAB Coder
Workaround: Set?Use memcpy for vector assignment?to?Off, or, set the?Memcpy threshold (bytes)?parameter to a very high value such as?inf. Note that both of these approaches prevent the code generator from using memcpy for any array copy.

Summary: Simulation Deployment does not timeout after the specified time
Description: Even when the timeout parameter is specified for a simulation in deployed mode, the simulation continues to execute after the period specified by the timeout parameter.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model that contains a?Data Store Write block inside a conditional subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a conditional subsystem. There is a Data Store Write block inside the conditional subsystem. The input signal of the conditional subsystem connects to the Data Store Write block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect when Constant block output is used as the input to an exported Stateflow chart function
Description: The generated code might be incorrect when a Constant block output is used as an input to an exported Stateflow chart function.? As a consequence, the behavior of the code may not match the behavior of the simulation.
Workaround: You may configure the Constant block output signal as a test point.? Alternatively, you may assign a non-Auto storage class to that Constant block output signal.

Summary: Incorrect size of a printed Simulink model when using a print frame
Description: If print frames are used when printing a Simulink model, the frame and model content does not center and fill the printed area on the printed pages or in the exported PDF file.

Summary: serialport write operations might cause MATLAB to hang
Description: When calling a?write operation on a?serialport ?object from within the?BytesAvailableFcn  callback function, MATLAB hangs indefinitely. The write operations for the?serialport ?interface are?write ,?writeline ,?writebinblock , ?and?writeread . (The writebinblock  and writeread  functions require an Instrument Control Toolbox license.)

Summary: Flattening a Black Box subsystem generates incorrect HDL code
Description: HDL Coder incorrectly generates code when 'Flatten-Hierarchy' ?option is set on Black-Box subsystem that is configured to generate generics.
Workaround: Consider disabling Flatten Hierarchy on black-box subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model containing a triggered Model Reference block and a Data Store Write block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a triggered Model Reference block. The output of the triggered Model Reference block connects to a Data Store Write block. The initial condition of the output is not the same as the initial condition of the Data Store Write block.

Summary: Code generation compilation error when referenced model has S-Function block calling TLC function LibMdlStartCustomCode() for custom code
Description: Generated code might produce compilation errors when all of the following conditions are met: Model reference hierarchy is configured for code generation. The referenced model has the configuration parameter?Underspecified initialization detection  set to Simplified . The referenced model contains an S-Function block which uses .tlc  file for code generation. The S-Function block implements TLC function Start()  that?calls LibMdlStartCustomCode()  to insert custom code to the generated?Start() ?function.

Summary: getpdb fails to retrieve data files from PDB with old URLs
Description: RCSB PDB modified the download URLs for entries in the Protein Data Bank (PDB) in November 2020. Consequently, getpdb  fails when retrieving data files. For example, this line of code generates an error: pdbinfo = getpdb('4hhb')
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the PDB file from the updated URL using websave, and read the PDB information using pdbread. More details on the updated URLs can be found at http://www.rcsb.org/pages/download/http.websave('4hhb.pdb', 'https://files.rcsb.org/download/4hhb.pdb');pdbinfo = pdbread('4hhb.pdb')

Summary: Highlighted blocks might save their highlighting as foreground and background colors
Description: If a model is saved while blocks are highlighted, the highlighting might be saved as foreground and background colors for the blocks. For example, you might see this behavior when highlighting blocks with the?hilite_system ?function, Execution Order ?overlay, or Timing Legend.
Workaround: Clear the highlighting on all the blocks before saving a model by performing one of these actions:Right-click the canvas of the Simulink model and select?Remove Highlighting. Alternatively, press?Ctrl+Shift+H.In the MATLAB Command Window, enter this command, replacing modelName?with the name of your model:hilite_system(find_system(modelName,'FindAll','on','type','block'),'none');

Summary: ARXML exported for COM_AXIS lookup table in AUTOSAR model contains extra SwRecordLayoutGroup
Description: In the ARXML exported for a COM_AXIS lookup table in an AUTOSAR model, the SwRecordLayout  contains an extra SwRecordLayoutGroup .?The additional SwRecordLayoutGroup  is not required because the lookup table implementation data type is an array.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with Stateflow charts
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains Stateflow charts. One Stateflow chart has an exported function that is defined inside it. The exported function is reused in another Stateflow chart.? The model configuration parameter?Optimize block operation order in the generated code? parameter is set to?Improved code execution speed.
Workaround: In Configuration Parameters dialog box, set?Optimize block operation order in the generated code??to?None.

Summary: mdfVisualize crashes MATLAB when specifying an MDF-file name that is not in the current folder
Description: If you specify an MDF-file name without a full path for mdfVisualize , MATLAB crashes if the file is not in the current folder. This occurs even if the file is on the MATLAB search path.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, always specify a full path or make sure the file in the current folder.

Summary: Code generation for the?filtfilt?function errors out for constant inputs when dynamic memory allocation is turned?"Off"
Description: Standalone static library/dynamically linked library/Simulation-in-the-Loop (SIL)/Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL) code generated for the?filtfilt ? function returns an error when the input arguments are constants and the configuration settings satisfy these conditions: Dynamic Memory Allocation is turned?"Off"   For example, define a function?foo ?that calls?filtfilt : function y = foo() %#codegenx = rand(100,1);b = [0.9876 -0.9876];a = [1 0 -1];y = filtfilt(b,a,x);end Generate a standalone C static library for the function?foo ?using these commands: >> cfg = coder.config('lib');>> cfg.DynamicMemoryAllocation = "Off";?>> codegen foo -config cfg The commands result in this error message: ????Dynamic memory allocation must be enabled in order to create a sparse matrix.

Summary: Incorrect results from SimFunction objects that include both sensitivities and observables
Description: SimFunction  objects return incorrect simulation results if they include both sensitivities and observable objects in the simulation outputs. This can also lead to incorrect fitting results when using sbiofit  on a model that includes observables, if sensitivity analysis is enabled.
Workaround: If you need to simulate with sensitivities, create a SimFunction that computes sensitivities but not observables. Then, add observables to SimData results using addobservable.If you need to fit a model that contains observables, call sbiofit with the?SensitivityAnalysis name-value argument set to false.

Summary: Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block outputs incorrect pitch value
Description: In the Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block, the Orientation output port returns a pitch angle (Y -value) with a reversed sign. For example, an expected output of [10 5 0] is returned as [10 -5 0].
Workaround: To correct the sign of the pitch value, pass theoutput of the Orientation port through a Gain block that has a [1 -1 1] value.

Summary: Deep Network Designer errors when clicking the help button next to a layer name that contains parentheses
Description: When you click the help button next to a layer in Deep Network Designer, an error occurs if the layer name contains parentheses. For example, ˇ°maxPooling2dLayer (for unpooling)ˇ± or ˇ°resize2dLayer (for scale)ˇ±.

Summary: Calling mean on an empty?OptimizationExpression?returns an error
Description: mean(e)  returns an error when?e  is an empty?OptimizationExpression .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using memcpy function in a Stateflow chart generates incorrect code
Description: When you use the?memcpy ?C function in a Stateflow chart as a custom code C function, Stateflow writes the result of the function to a temporary variable. However, the temporary variable is not used to write to the intended destination variable, producing incorrect results in both simulation and generated code.

Summary: Breakpoints in MATLAB function Block might slow down simulation
Description: When MATLAB Function Blocks within a Simulink model have breakpoints, the simulation might significantly slow down after reaching the breakpoints. The simulation might remain slow after resuming, even if the breakpoints are never reached again.

Summary: Example models have incorrect speed measurementduring the transients due to incorrect Hall sensor A configurations
Description: The speed measurements for the models in the example "Field-Oriented Control of PMSM by Using Hall Sensor" (mcb_pmsm_foc_hall_f28069m.slx ?and?mcb_pmsm_foc_hall_f28379d.slx ) are incorrect during transients. This is due to an incorrect edge?detection configuration of the Hall sensor A interrupt. ?

Summary: Crash when copying SimBiology objects with?UserData storing other objects
Description: MATLAB might crash if you call?copyobj  on a SimBiology object that has its UserData  property storing other objects. Because SimBiology Scenarios  objects use copyobj , this crash can also occur when creating scenarios from dose objects that contain other objects in their?UserData ?properties.
Workaround: Instead of storing objects in the UserData property of SimBiology objects, consider using a struct that contains the needed information.

Summary: Simulink message blocks display an error when signal range checking is enabled
Description: Simulink message blocks do not support signal range checking. If the Signal Range Check  parameter is not set to none , message blocks display an error.
Workaround: Disable Signal Range Check:? In Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Data Validity, set Simulation range checking to none.

Summary: 'Normalization' option ignored for dlnetwork objects with multiple input layers.
Description: For dlnetwork  object objects with multiple input layers, the software applies the specified normalization of the first input layer to all other input layers. For example, create a dlnetwork  with two image input layers and specify different normalizations for each. branch1 = [  ? ?imageInputLayer([6 6 1],'Normalization','none','Name','in1')  ? ?fullyConnectedLayer(2,'Name','fc1')  ? ?concatenationLayer(1,2,'Name','cat')];?  lgraph = layerGraph(branch1);?  branch2 = [  ? ?imageInputLayer([6 6 1],'Normalization','zerocenter','Mean',1, 'Name','in2')?  ? ?fullyConnectedLayer(2,'Name','fc2')];  lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,branch2);?  lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph,'fc2','cat/in2');?  dlnet = dlnetwork(lgraph);  Make predictions using the network. dlX1 = dlarray(ones([6 6 1]),'SSCB');?  dlX2 = dlarray(ones([6 6 1]),'SSCB');? ?  [dlY1, dlY2] = predict(dlnet,dlX1,dlX2,'Outputs',["in1" "in2"]);  Because the software applies the normalization specified for the first input layer, the outputs dlY1? and?dlY2 ?are the same. The normalization specified by the second input layer?'in2' ?is not applied to the second input.
Workaround: Manually normalize the data before passing the data to the predict?and forward?functions. For example, to use zero-center normalization for the second input with mean mu. Subtract the mean from the input data manually before calling predict.dlX2 = dlX - mu; [dlY1, dlY2] = predict(dlnet,dlX1,dlX2,'Outputs',["in1" "in2"]);

Summary: NCO HDL Optimized block errors out when phase increment is zero and you are using frame-based output
Description: The NCO HDL Optimized block returns this simulation error message when you apply a phase increment of zero, and the Samples per frame  parameter is greater than 1.? Subscripted assignment dimension mismatch: [1][N ] ~= [1][0].?  N  is the value of the?Samples to frame  parameter.?? The same problem occurs with the dsp.HDLNCO System object(TM).?

Summary: Code generation fails with error "Unable to find Interface within the ModelReferenceInterfaces scope"
Description: Code generation might fail for a referenced model that only has event based rates with the following error message:"Unable to find Interface within the ModelReferenceInterfaces scope"

Summary: Incorrect parameter override values applied during test execution
Description: If a test case has a parameter override, and uses inputs from a Simulink Design Verifier MAT file that specifies the same parameter, then during simulation the MAT-file parameter value is applied, but the test case result shows that the parameter override is applied.

Summary: Compiling a model with an observer reference might crash MATLAB when the observer model has an asynchronous sample time
Description: When you compile an observer model that has an asynchronous sample time, MATLAB might crash instead of Simulink showing an error that the sample time is unsupported.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for MATLAB code in which the loop bounds are unknown at compile time and the lower bound is greater than the upper bound
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for MATLAB code in which the loop bounds are unknown at compile time and the lower bound is greater than the upper bound. In the incorrect code, SIMD optimized code incorrectly computes the scalar loop bounds. ?Consider this MATLAB function: ?function out = dynamicBounds(a, b, start, stop)  ??? assert(isa(start, 'int32'));   ??? assert(isa(stop, 'int32'));   ??? out = zeros(size(a), 'like', a);  ??? for i = start:stop  ??????? out(i) = a(i) * a(i) + b(i) * b(i);  ??? end  ?end  When start > stop +1, the loop should execute zero iterations, but in the incorrect generated code, SIMD optimizations might result in one or more iterations executing at run time.
Workaround: To obtain correct behavior without disabling SIMD optimizations, you can modify the bounds of loops exhibiting this issue. For the preceding example, review the problematic case and alter the loop bounds to ensure that there is zero iteration.?if start > stop+1? ? start = int32(1); ? ? stop = int32(0); ?end ?for i = start:stop ? ? ... ?endThis modification ensures zero iterations of the loop without encountering the error condition start > stop+1.

Summary: Import from IBM DOORS Next not working when configuration management is enabled for project
Description: When attempting to import an IBM DOORS Next module from Jazz server version 6.0.6.1 or later, and when Configuration Management is enabled for the DOORS Next project, the? Importing Requirements dialog in Simulink Requirements fails to display the list of modules. After selecting the project and selecting?Full module hierarchy ?in the Importing Requirements dialog box, the UI takes a while to refresh the DNG Module ?selector, but no entries appear in the drop-down list.

Summary: Analysis may terminate with an?Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end?error message.
Description: Analysis might terminate without producing the results due to floating-point computations in the model that produce very large values. When you run the analysis with a user interface, it displays the following error message: Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end.  When you run the analysis in the?Test Generation ?mode, the error message displayed at the MATLAB Command Window is: Test generation produced errors  In releases beginning in R2018a, the below stack trace is produced: src/data_graph/graph_rat_prop-new.c:475 err=data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double    src/data_graph/graph_rat_prop-new.c:110 err=data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double    src/tempo/tempo_safe_induction.c:1343 tempo_error=tempo_err_unknown  When this issue is hit, the stack trace will contain data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Block LoadFcn callbacks do not execute in test harnesses
Description: When blocks inside an internal test harness contain a LoadFcn callback, they might not execute when a test harness is opened or loaded.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using a context menu in Simulink or Stateflow on macOS Big Sur
Description: On macOS Big Sur (11.0), using a context menu in any Simulink or Stateflow window might cause MATLAB to crash. For example, control +clicking a block in the Simulink Editor, an element in the Model Explorer, or a parameter in a block dialog box might cause the crash.

Summary: Incorrect adjacency matrix for some SimBiology models using reversible reactions
Description: The adjacency matrix is incorrectly constructed when calling getadjacencymatrix  on a SimBiology model containing reversible reactions that have the same species on both the left-hand and right-hand sides of the reaction, for example "null <-> a + null " or "x + y <-> x + z ".
Workaround: Replace each reversible reaction that has the same model species on both sides of the reaction with two equivalent irreversible reactions. For example, replace "x + y <-> x + z" with reactions "x + y -> x + z" and "x + z -> x + y".

Summary: Labeler apps render a blank dialog box when you add a new scene label definition.
Description: The Define new scene label ? dialog renders a blank dialog box when you try to add a new scene label definition in the Ground Truth Labeler , Image Labeler , Video Labeler,  or the Lidar Labeler . The problem occurs after loading a saved labeler session that contains 60 or more scene label definitions. The?Label Name ,?Color, Group , and?Description? fields are missing from the dialog box, and only the?OK? and?Cancel? buttons are shown. When you click?OK  , the labeler returns the following error: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.    Error in vision.internal.labeler.tool.FrameLabelDefinitionDialog/onOK (line 89)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? isValidName = this.SessionLabelSet.validateLabelName(newLabelName) ...    Error in vision.internal.uitools.OkCancelDlg>@(varargin)this.onOK(varargin{:}) (line 39)  ? ? ? ? ? ? this.OkButton = uicontrol('Parent', this.Dlg, 'Callback', @this.onOK,...  ?  Error while evaluating UIControl Callback.

Summary: Persistence: mps.cache.DataCache put?function fails silently when attempting to write objects larger than 512 MB to Redis cache
Description: Redis cache does not support writing objects larger then 512 MB. The?mps. cache. DataCache ?put ?function fails silently when you configure MATLAB to use Redis for data persistence and use the?put ?function to write to the Redis?cache an object larger than 512 MB. The large object is not written to the Redis cache, and MATLAB does not throw an error to indicate so. This issue does not affect MATLAB variables and objects smaller than 512 MB, which the?put ?function can successfully write to the cache.

Summary: The Model Advisor check?Check usage of floating-point expressions in Stateflow charts does not check ~= and != operators
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check usage of floating-point expressions in Stateflow charts? (ID: mathworks.maab.jc_0481) does not check ~=  and !=  operations as expected by jc_0481 MAB guideline. The check is meant to analyze equality and inequality operations (==, ~=, !=) on floating-point data types as per the MAB guideline jc_0481.?

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier ignores certain justification rules in filter files
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may ignore certain justification rules for individual objective outcomes while running Test Generation or Dead Logic Detection.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 2, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model that contains a Model block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a referenced model. An output signal of the referenced model connects to a block that has an update method, such as a Unit Delay block.

Summary: Model advisor S-function checks might incorrectly flag the S-functions generated by the Legacy Code Tool
Description: Model Advisor S-function checks incorrectly flag S-functions generated from the Legacy Code Tool, even if the the S-function does not have state data or multiple sample times. The checks can also incorrectly flag user-defined S-functions where SS_OPTION_CAN_BE_CALLED_CONDITIONALLY  is true.

Summary: The WindXYZ inport of the Vehicle Body 3DOF Longitudinal?block can become wrongly connected internally to the external forces port when External suspension is selected
Description: When the External suspension  option of the block is selected, the WindXYZ inport can be incorrectly connected internally to the external forces port Fext, causing a units propagation error to occur.
Workaround: Check the External forces checkbox, then uncheck it. This will fix the miswiring issue.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Real-time test cases in Test Manager for models with Stateflow chart using verify keyword errors
Description: When you run real-time test cases in Test Manager and the?models or harnesses in the system-under-test contains Stateflow charts that use the verify  keyword, then this error occurs when processing data from the target: Not enough input arguments  The test case fails and no verify signals are shown in the results.

Summary: Serialization fails for objects that are serialized as structs
Description: It is possible for class definitions that overload the loadobj ? method, which generates the serialized representation of objects of that class, to represent the object as a different data type in the serialized form.? Before the final serialization (to JSON or Protobuf) takes place, the object is converted to MATLAB data array representation.? A bug in that conversion results in an exception being thrown for these types of objects.

Summary: Custom template makefile on Windows does not fully honor?'MAKEFILE_FILESEP = /'?macro
Description: On Windows, the file separator is by default the  \ ? (backslash) character. If you use a custom template makefile that defines the file separator with this macro:  'MAKEFILE_FILESEP = /'??   then, the source-to-object rules in the generated makefile can contain paths with mixed file separators. For example:?  C:\MATLAB\R2019a\toolbox\coder\rtiostream\src\utils/rtiostream_utils.c   Only the last file separator is set to the? /  (forward slash) character, which results in compilation errors: . ../llvm-9.0.1.1/WIN64/bin/clang -c -D__VXWORKS__ ....... -O2 C:\MATLAB\R2019a\toolbox\coder\rtiostream\src\utils/rtiostream_utils.c  clang: error: no such file or directory: 'C:MATLABR2019atoolboxcoderrtiostreamsrcutils/rtiostream_utils.c'  clang: error: no input files  make.exe: *** [rtiostream_utils.o] Error 1
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a

Summary: Compiling a model containing a subsystem reference block with physical ports and unsaved changes may crash Simulink
Description: Compilation of a model containing a subsystem reference block may crash if the following conditions are met  The subsystem reference block contains physical port(s) as part of its interface? There are unsaved changes inside the subsystem reference block?

Summary: Closed data dictionary might cause unnecessary rebuild of model reference simulation or code generation target
Description: Simulating, generating code, or updating the diagram of a top model in a model hierarchy might cause an unnecessary rebuild of a model reference simulation or code generation target when all of the following conditions are true: The referenced model is configured to simulate in accelerator mode. The referenced model is?not loaded. The referenced model uses a data dictionary that is not loaded. The referenced model contains a MATLAB Function block or a Stateflow block that uses a data type defined in the data dictionary. The first build of the model hierarchy successfully completed.  The rebuild message looks like: ### Model reference simulation target (aModelRef_msf.mexa64) for model aModelRef is out of date because a MATLAB file used by a Stateflow block or a MATLAB Function block has changed.
Workaround: Load the referenced model or the linked data dictionary.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 2, R2021a

Summary: Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar does not use?Detection range parameter to detect objects
Description: The Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar does not use the?Detection range ?parameter value to detect objects.??Instead, if you specify a Detection Range  parameter value, the block incorrectly returns detections within the default 1 m to 150 m range.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Coverage might return incorrect information when run on a Stateflow chart that contains an enumeration of a custom base type
Description: Coverage results might be incorrect for Stateflow charts for which all of the following conditions are true:? The chart contains a graphical, MATLAB, or Simulink function At least one input argument to the function is defined as an enumeration The chart passes an integer or other base type when calling the function

Summary: The command window password prompt for Matlab Job Scheduler does not correctly wait for input
Description: When using a Matlab Job Scheduler configured with security level greater than zero, certain operations require the user to interact with a password prompt to confirm their identity. If the MATLAB client is running in nodisplay mode, this prompt switches from an interactive window to a running in the command window. For some operations, this command window prompt does not correctly wait for the user input. This means the prompt cannot be correctly completed, and instead the user input is executed as a command in the MATLAB console.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect documentation for configuration parameter?Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout?(UseRowMajorAlgorithm)
Description: The documentation for configuration parameter?Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout ?(UseRowMajorAlgorithm ) contains this incorrect statement: "You can enable the Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout  configuration parameter to enable efficient algorithms, which might result in numeric differences between simulation and generated code."
Workaround: Refer the updated documentation link -?https://www.mathworks.com/help/simulink/gui/use-algorithms-optimized-for-row-major-array-layout.html

Summary: Crash might occur when a function in MATLAB Compiler SDK Python package is called repeatedly
Description: If a function in a MATLAB Compiler SDK Python package is called repeatedly in a tight loop for thousands of iterations, a crash might eventually occur.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Real-time kernel fails to install on macOS Big Sur
Description: The Simulink Desktop Real-time kernel fails to install on an Intel-based macOS Big Sur (11.0) computer. The installation ends with an error message and the real-time kernel may not be installed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect outputs from Simulink based state in discrete-time Stateflow charts
Description: Outputs from blocks in a Simulink based state may be delayed by one step if all of the following conditions are true: The Stateflow chart has a discrete sample time The Simulink based state contains a block that does not require direct feedthrough, that is,?outputs in a given time step depend only on the state of the block At least one input of the chart is mapped to the input of the Simulink based state

Summary: NCO HDL Optimized HDL simulation doesn't match Simulink simulation when reset is applied at reset input port
Description: This problem occurs with an NCO HDL Optimized block configured to have a reset input port and a phase increment input port.?? When you simulate the generated HDL code, the behavior when the reset signal is applied in the HDL simulation doesn't match the Simulink simulation.? The same problem also occurs with the dsp.HDLNCO System object when it is configured to use a reset input argument and a phase increment input argument.??

Summary: Incorrect simulation results when referencing a function-call model in accelerator mode
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect when all of these conditions apply: The model hierarchy contains at least three levels of models. The top model of the model hierarchy simulates in normal mode. A referenced model contains a Model block that has a function-call or trigger port and a triggered sample time type. That Model block's?Simulation mode  is Accelerator . The number of sample times varies among the models in that Model block's path. For example, the top model might have three discrete sample times, and the model that references that Model block might have two discrete sample times.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when extending manually generated test cases using Simulink Design Verifier
Description: Simulink Design Verifier supports the extension of manually generated test cases?to aid in test generation using a .mat file.However, a crash is observed when: TestCases.paramValues  field is missing in the given .mat file.?? TestCases.paramValues = [];   The crash occurs with an error log shown below:? Abnormal termination:  Access violation  Snippet from Stack Trace (from fault): [? 0] 0x00000000fe13ab89 matrix::detail::noninlined::mx_array_api::mxIsEmpty at b:\matlab\foundation\matrix\src\matrix\array.cpp:793  [? 1] 0x00000002a8cc5cf2 DV::MX::CGTestCaseFromMx::initParamsFromMxArray at b:\matlab\toolbox\sldv\src\sldv\analyzer\mxresults.cpp:721  [? 2] 0x00000002a8cbf9ef DV::MX::ResultsFromMx::addResults at b:\matlab\toolbox\sldv\src\sldv\analyzer\mxresults.cpp:1578  [? 3] 0x00000002a8cbc57f DV::ExternalLoader::Analyzer::load at b:\matlab\toolbox\sldv\src\sldv\analyzer\loadexternaldataanalyzer.cpp:50  [? 4] 0x00000002a8c79c8c DV::DvInternal::loadTestCases at b:\matlab\toolbox\sldv\src\sldv\analyzer\dvinternal.cpp:1309  [? 5] 0x00000002a8eed489 TestComponent::loadTestCases at b:\matlab\toolbox\sldv\src\sldv\udd\methods\avt_testcomponent_methods.cpp:84  [? 6] 0x00000002a8ee0e3f TestComponentloadTestCasesMI::invokeMethod at b:\matlab\toolbox\sldv\src\sldv\udd\autogen\avt_testcomponent.cpp:247  [? 7] 0x000000001f355a36 UDMethodInfo::invokeMethodE at b:\matlab\src\udd\udd_util\udmethodinfo.cpp:103
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: dateshift returns the wrong answer for the nearest end of a time unit
Description: The calculation of the nearest end of a time unit in dateshift  is incorrect, adding an extra increment to the result. For example, this call to dateshift  returns a datetime  value for the end of September 2019 (30-Sep-2019 )?when it should return the end of August 2019 (31-Aug-2019 ). >> dateshift(datetime(2019, 8, 17),'end','month','nearest')ans =   datetime   30-Sep-2019 Furthermore, for units of 'week' , 'month' , 'quarter' , and 'year' , the nearest value is determined based on the beginning of the week, month, quarter, or year rather than on the end of the specified time unit. This issue has an impact on results within a day of the midpoint of the specified time unit.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Getter function for bus arrays, which return by value, used as a pointer return in the algorithm code
Description: The return type of getter function in the algorithm code is treated as pointer in the algorithm code for bus arrays in which the bus has arrays as fields. The invalid get function usage appears when an element of the field array is read directly within algorithm code.

Summary: MATLAB might crash if an error occurs when simulating models that contain Stateflow charts or S-Function blocks
Description: If an error occurs during initialization of a model containing Stateflow charts or S-Function blocks, the error might cause MATLAB to crash.The crash report may contain output similar to this: +09050739 slAccRegPrmChangeFcn+00041411  +02241817 SLExecBD::modelCleanupRuntimeResources+00000105

Summary: Custom del.BAT?file on the Windows path  may cause model build error
Description: The model build process on Windows systems uses the system del ?command to remove out-of-date generated files. When the system has a custom del.BAT  file on the Windows path, this file may cause a model build error such as: NMAKE : fatal error U1077: 'c:\windows\del.BAT' : return code '0x1'.Stop.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Models with booleans in nested bus inputs exported as Tool-coupling FMUs might send incorrect data to remote MATLAB sessions
Description: When a model is exported as a tool-coupling FMU, it might send incorrect data to the remote session during simulation if the following conditions are satisfied: The FMU block accepts as its input a bus signal. The input bus signal is a nested bus that contains a boolean and a sub-bus.  For example, if an FMU has as input the following signals in a non-virtual input bus: --Subbus----double----int--boolean--double--double--double. . . The number after the boolean received by the remote MATLAB session differs from the number sent by the originating MATLAB session.??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model that contains a Unit Delay block inside a reusable conditionally executed subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem whose outport requires an initial condition. The Subsystem block parameter?Function packaging ?is set to Reusable function . There is a Unit Delay block inside the conditionally executed subsystem. The Unit Delay block connects to the signal that feeds the outport of the conditionally executed subsystem. The initial condition of the Unit Delay block specified by using the block parameter Initial condition  is different than the initial condition of the outport of the conditionally executed subsystem. In Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Buffer for reusable subsystems  parameter?is selected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model that contains a Simulink Function block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a Simulink Function block. The Simulink Function block has more than one caller block such as a Stateflow chart, a Function Caller block, a Function-Call Generator block, and so on. The caller blocks are on different levels of the model hierarchy. The model configuration parameter?Optimize block operation order in the generated code  (OptimizeBlockOrder )?is set to?Improved code execution speed.
Workaround: Set the model configuration parameter?Optimize block operation order in the generated code?(OptimizeBlockOrder) to Off.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model that contains a?Unit Delay block connected to Stateflow chart output
Description: The generated code might contain incorrect initialization of a Unit Delay block state for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a Stateflow chart. A Unit Delay block has an input that directly connects to the Stateflow chart output.? The Unit Delay block has a nonzero initialization value.

Summary: The Capacity vector and Head vector?parameters?in the Centrifugal Pump (TL) block cannot be set to Run-time
Description: In the Centrifugal Pump (TL) block, the?Capacity vector ?and?Head vector? parameters?do not currently support run-time capabilities. However, the block dialog incorrectly provides the option to choose between the?Compile-time ?or?Run-time ?settings. Selecting?Run-time ?and simulating the model results in a compilation error: Function 'fluids_functions. polynomial_fit' must be an unprotected MATLAB function. ?In fluids. thermal_liquid. pumps_motors. centrifugal_pump
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Compilation error when Dynamic compressibility is set to Off?for heat exchanger sample variants in the?thermal liquid domain
Description: This issue occurs if Dynamic compressibility ?is set to Off  in: Heat Exchanger (TL), Simple Variant Heat Exchanger (TL-TL), Simple Variant Heat Exchanger (TL-G), Simple Variant? Simple Heat Exchanger Interface (TL)  In such situations, simulating the model generates this error message: Number of equations exceeds number of variables .?
Workaround: Set Dynamic compressibility?to On.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Calling the write function for characteristic objects without specifying the response type fails to write data to the BLE device
Description: Some BLE devices have characteristics that support the "Write"  response type, but not "WriteWithoutResponse" . For these characteristics, calling the write  function without the optional response type input argument results in a failed write operation, but might not return an error. For example, the following command might fail to write to the BLE device.? >> write(c,1:5)
Workaround: If your characteristic object supports "Write" but not "WriteWithoutResponse", specify?"withresponse"?as an input argument for write. You can check whether your characteristic object supports "WriteWithoutResponse" by examining its Attributes property. For example, use the following commands to view a characteristic object's attributes and complete a write operation:>> c.Attributesans =   1ˇÁ2 string array    "Read"    "Write">> write(c,1:5,"withresponse")

Summary: When computing AUTOSAR C++14 or other C++ rules, analysis gets stuck on the message?Verifying sources ...
Description: When the AUTOSAR C++14 coding rules are computed with the option -autosar-cpp14 , the analysis might be stuck on messages such as the following: Verifying sources ...file1.c (1/100)file2.c (2/100) The issue? might also happen with the options?-misra-cpp  or -jsf-coding-rules . The issue occurs because of a bug in how data types in template instantiations are?internally?constructed.
Workaround: If you are checking for defects along with AUTOSAR C++14 rules and encounter this issue, check for defects only. If you are checking for AUTOSAR C++14 rules only and encounter the issue, there are no workarounds.

Summary: Aggregated coverage data description missing from coverage report
Description: When a description is applied to aggregated coverage data, the aggregated coverage report does not display this description.  Descriptions applied to coverage data for individual test runs included in the aggregated results will be shown in the report, in the Aggregated Tests table next to the corresponding test.? The description for the overall aggregated coverage result is not shown in the report.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Dependency Analyzer incorrectly displays a file path with Unicode characters on machines with non-UTF8 locales
Description: In a project, the Dependency Analyzer incorrectly reports the path of a file  if both   of these conditions are true:The file is stored in a folder with Unicode characters The machine uses non-UTF8  locale settings

Summary: The input parameter settings and corresponding modify actions for sub-ids B & D of the JMAAB check?Check Model font settings?(ID: mathworks.jmaab.db_0043)?do not work as expected?
Description: The input parameters for sub-id B and D for check Check Model font settings  (ID: mathworks.jmaab.db_0043) on the Model Advisor Configuration editor are not being considered correctly by the check. Due to this, it is possible that the check gives incorrect results when these input parameters are set differently in Model Advisor Configuration Editor. As a result of this issue, the modify action of the check also fails to modify the model correctly.

Summary: JMAAB check Check usage of internal transition incorrectly flags transitions
Description: The Model Advisor check Check usage of internal transition  (ID: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0760 ) incorrectly flags an internal transition starting from the left edge in a subchart.

Summary: JMAAB check Prohibition of logical value comparison in Stateflow?incorrectly flags transitions where no logical constants are used
Description: The Model Advisor check?Prohibition of logical value comparison in Stateflow ?(ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0655 )?incorrectly flags transitions where no comparisons between logical variable and logical constants are made.

Summary: Simulations take longer and more time steps than expected
Description: SimBiology models that contain repeated assignment rules might take significantly longer to simulate than they did in previous releases. The differential equation solver might take more time steps than necessary.

Summary: Incompatibility reported for models with custom block replacement rules to stub Initialize, Reset or Terminate Function blocks
Description: Simulink Design Verifier reports incompatibility with model consisting of Initialize, Reset or Terminate Function blocks even if the user has provided custom block replacement rule to stub such blocks. An example of such block replacement rule can be the one that replaces these blocks with empty subsystems.
Workaround: The incompatibility can be worked around with following two step approach:Generate replacement model using sldvblockreplacement?Run Simulink Design Verifier analysis on the replacement model generated in step 1.

Summary: AUTOSAR model build fails for lookup table with unmapped breakpoints
Description: Code generation for an AUTOSAR model might fail when the following conditions are met: The model contains a lookup table block with Data specification ?set to?Table and breakpoints . At least one of the following conditions is true: There is a mismatch between the input (operating point) data type and the breakpoint data type. The breakpoints are not mapped to an AUTOSAR parameter.   Simulink reports the following error: Unrecognized method, property, or field 'Identifier' for class 'coder.descriptor.StructExpression'

Summary: Error when creating link with IBM DOORS Next (DNG)
Description: When you create a link from an item in MATLAB or Simulink to an item in IBM DOORS Next (formerly known as IBM DOORS Next Generation, and often abbreviated as DNG), a dialog box might appear with an error that says "Failed to parse ProjectID ". This error might occur if the IBM DOORS Next service is hosted on Jazz server 6.0.6.1 or later version. As a result of this error, you cannot create links between items in MATLAB or Simulink and items in IBM DOORS Next.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: slvnvmakeharness and sldvmakeharness error out and pauses the simulation when the Bus element port is of inherit:auto data type
Description: Simulink Design Verifier requires every Bus element port to have a bus data type associated with it. Functions slvnvmakeharness  and sldvmakeharness? errors out on models and pauses the simulation when the model has an In Bus element  port with Inherit: auto data type. It is expected that the model errors out in the above scenario, however the functions slvnvmakeharness  and sldvmakeharness? pause the simulation which is an unexpected behavior.?

Summary: If you set the Vehicle Body 3DOF Dual Track block Axle force?parameter to?External forces, the block incorrectly implements the lateral force signal input to the front right tire
Description: If you set the Vehicle Body 3DOF Dual Track block?Axle force? parameter to?External forces , the block incorrectly implements the signal array connected to the input port?FwF . Specifically, the block does not correctly use the array element for lateral force on the right front tire. The issue does not affect the Vehicle Body 3DOF Single Track block.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Viewing a Word document using rptview or docview might produce an error related to the .NET version
Description: Viewing a Word document using rptview   or docview ?on a system with an older version of .NET might result in the following error:  'system.diagnostics.process' could not be found in the .NET Global Assembly Cache (GAC).  Error in mlreportgen.utils.internal/WordController/createApp (line 28)  ? ? ? ? ? ? hApp = mlreportgen.utils.WordApp();  Error in mlreportgen.utils.internal/OfficeController/start (line 59)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? hApp = this.createApp();  Error in mlreportgen.utils.WordApp.instance (line 39)  ? ? ? ? ? ? start(wc);

Summary: Simulink compiler execution will crash due to reregistering existing dynamic enum definitions.
Description: If a Simulink model uses dynamic?enum data types, ?reregistering the same dynamic enum definition will cause the deployed application to crash.

Summary: Add-On Manager does not show?available support package updates in the Updates?tab
Description: When checking for updates in the?Add-On Manager, the Updates  tab does not show any available support package updates even when the matlabshared.supportpkg.checkForUpdate   ?command indicates that there are available updates. To open the Add-On Manager Updates ?tab, go to the?Home ?tab and click?Help > Check for Updates .??

Summary: Embedded Coder replacement files may have incorrect encoding
Description: Embedded Coder replacement files may have incorrect encoding. For example, if encoding is Shift_JIS , these files were being inadvertently written out using UTF-8 .

Summary: MATLAB crashes when capturing baseline to Excel file if the file already exists and is open
Description: Clicking the Capture  button in the Baseline Criteria  section for a test case created from an external Microsoft Excel file causes MATLAB to crash if the file already exists and is open in the Microsoft Excel application.

Summary: Check for model elements that do not link to requirement?results in an abnormal exit
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for model elements that do not link to requirement ?(Check ID:?mathworks.hism.hisl_0070 ) results in abnormal exit when the number of violations of either "Maximum number of requirement links per model elements" or "Maximum number of child objects per linked component" are greater than two.

Summary: Error?Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application on?Red Hat based distributions
Description: This issue is applicable to the following Linux distributions:  Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.2 and later CentOS 8.2 and later Fedora 32 and later  The following error can be observed while installing MATLAB or while running certain features in MATLAB (such as Simulink Start Page, Help Browser, Live Editor, UIFigure, App Designer, Simulink Data Inspector, etc.): terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::runtime_error'??  ????? what():? Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application ??? Aborted    You might also see: MATLABWindow application failed to launch. Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application.  Or if you enter this command in MATLAB: system(fullfile(matlabroot, '/bin/glnxa64/MATLABWindow'))   MATLAB displays: ./MATLABWindow: symbol lookup error: /lib64/libk5crypto.so.3: undefined symbol: EVP_KDF_ctrl, version OPENSSL_1_1_1b
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Code View fails to display code generated using Embedded Coder
Description: For some models that use custom code, when you generate code using Embedded Coder, the Code View fails to show the generated code.

Summary: Delays while editing Simulink model with Simscape blocks
Description: Simulink models that contain Simscape blocks might experience a delay when moving and connecting blocks that have multiple ports.
Workaround: Connect at least one of the ports on the block before moving the block.

Summary: Performance problems with experiments?when showing training plot
Description: Experiment Manager training is slower when showing the training plot. The training time of a trial can be up to 20 times longer depending on the trials. For example, using smaller networks and small numbers of images with large numbers of iterations can result in a large slow down.
Workaround: To work around this issue, turn off the training plot when running the experiment.

Summary: Block replacement fails for models containing Saturation Dynamic block
Description: Simulink Design Verifier block replacement fails when the block replacement rules are set to FactoryDefaultRules ?for a model containing Saturation Dynamic  block. The following error is reported in the Diagnostic Viewer: Sldv Switch Replacement block (mask) does not have a parameter named 'DoSatur'.

Summary: PIL:pil:ModelBlockLUTTableIsInput error from Model block SIL/PIL simulations with lookup table objects that are mapped to non-Auto storage class ?
Description: If you run a Model block software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation that uses lookup table objects that are mapped to non-Auto storage classes, Simulink incorrectly produces this error message:  Model 'modelname' contains one or more Lookup Table blocks where the 'TableIsInput' parameter is set to 'on'. Model block SIL/PIL simulations do not support this configuration. Consider running a top-model SIL/PIL simulation instead.   This error should occur only when the model contains a Direct Lookup Table (n-D) block where parameter TableIsInput  is 'on' .

Summary: Simulation of linear models with doses using SimFunction does not log output times in dose intervals at late simulation times
Description: Simulation of linear models with doses using?SimFunction ?does not log output times in dose intervals at late simulation times. All reported simulation results are correct. However, if a dose is applied at late simulation times, the simulated model response might not be logged. When you use SimFunction  to simulate models that meet certain conditions, such as all reactions being zero- or first-order with MassAction kinetic laws, SimBiology uses a linear solver, and you may encounter this bug. However, this issue does not happen when SimBiology is using a nonlinear solver.
Workaround: If your model is linear, change the kinetic law from MassAction to Unknown.?SimBiology then simulates the model using a nonlinear solver which reports appropriate output times for the solution.

Summary: lstm and gru?provide output in incorrect order if the first input is unformatted and permuted
Description: The lstm  and gru  functions can return the output with dimensions in the wrong order. This behavior occurs when the following conditions are met:? ?The first input (data) is unformatted, i.e., its dimensions are not labeled. ?The first input has been permuted using the?permute ?function at least once.  The output is returned with a dimension ordering that is different from that specified by?DataFormat .? The following example illustrates the incorrect ordering behavior. numFeatures = 2;  numObservations = 5;  sequenceLength = 10;  % Construct data as TBC  X = dlarray(randn(sequenceLength,numObservations,numFeatures));  % Permute to get CBT  X = permute(X,[3 2 1]);   The array X  has size 2 x5 x10  which corresponds to the dimensions 'CBT' .  % Create the lstm parameters with three hidden units.  numHiddenUnits = 3;  H0 = dlarray(randn(numHiddenUnits,numObservations),'CB');  C0 = dlarray(randn(numHiddenUnits,numObservations),'CB');  weights = dlarray(randn(4*numHiddenUnits,numFeatures),'CU');  recurrent = dlarray(randn(4*numHiddenUnits,numHiddenUnits),'CU');  bias = dlarray(randn(4*numHiddenUnits,1),'C');    % Apply lstm  Y = lstm(X,H0,C0,weights,recurrent,bias,'DataFormat','CBT');   The array Y  has size 10 x5 x3 , matching the format 'TBC' .  The expected size is size 3 x5 x10 , to match the input format 'CBT' .
