Workaround: To avoid the incorrect ordering, you can use the?reshape?function after permuting the array but before the lstm or gru operations. Reshape the array to the permuted shape by providing the size of X as the required output size.X = reshape(X,size(X));?This does not modify the array X. The internal state of the array is updated and so works around the permutation issue.For the example given above, using the reshape function provides output in the correct order.??% Construct data as TBCX = dlarray(randn(sequenceLength,numObservations,numFeatures));% Permute to get CBTX = permute(X,[3 2 1]);% Call reshapeX = reshape(X,size(X));% Apply lstmY = lstm(X,H0,C0,weights,recurrent,bias,'DataFormat','CBT');The array Y has size??3x5x10, which matches the input format?'CBT'.

Summary: TLC error when code is generated for an AUTOSAR model that contains a calibration parameter within a private scoped Simulink Function
Description: If an AUTOSAR model contains a calibration parameter within a private scoped Simulink Function, code generation might fail with a TLC error similar to the following: Error: File: C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\autosarsup.tlc Line: 540 Column: 34Unable to find CallSites within the System scopeMain program:==> [00] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\autosarsup.tlc:SLibAutosarFcnAddRunnableAccessInfoViaSystem(540)    [01] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\autosarsup.tlc:SLibAutosarFcnAddRunnableAccessInfoViaSystem(554)    [02] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\autosarsup.tlc:FcnAddAccessInfoForAutosarCalibrationHelper(602)    [03] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibCGIR_AUTOSAR_TrackCalPrm(2325)    [04] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:FcnGenRateGroupedCodeFromModule(5765)    [05] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:FcnGenerateCodeFromModule(5647)    [06] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodySysFcn(292)    [07] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodyFcnCache(509)    [08] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodySysFcnEntry(667)    [09] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc:FcnGenBodySysCache(1790)    [10] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc:SLibGenBodyCache(6288)    [11] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\formatwide.tlc:(70)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when a scalar signal that uses a custom storage class authored in TLC selects an element of a bus array
Description: The generated code is incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:? The model uses a multi-level bus object that contains an array of buses as one of its elements. The top level bus uses a custom storage class authored in TLC such as "ImportFromFile" . The model uses a scalar signal to select an element of the bus array. The scalar signal must use a custom storage class authored in TLC such as "ImportFromFile ". A block that uses TLC to generate code, such as S-Function blocks or Model Reference blocks, must use the selected signal.

Summary: Error when trying to enter a numeric DNG ID into the Location field of Outgoing Links dialog
Description: When you create a link to an IBM DOORS Next (DNG) requirement in the Simulink Requirements Outgoing Links dialog, you can select DNG Requirement  from the Document Type ?drop down menu, and then click Browse ?to select the DNG project for linking. When you input the numeric DNG ID into the Location ?field, an error might occur and an error dialog box might appear.? The error dialog might say "Dot indexing not supported" or "Index out of bounds".

Summary: The vaderSentimentScores and ratioSentimentScores functions throw unexpected warnings and errors when using custom sentiment lexicons containing character vectors.
Description: The vaderSentimentScores  function throws a warning when using custom sentiment lexicons containing unigrams specified as character vectors. For example: lexicon = table; lexicon.Token = {'goood'; 'baaad'};lexicon.SentimentScore = [1; -1];documents = tokenizedDocument(["This film is goood!"; "This film is baaad..."]); compoundScores = vaderSentimentScores(documents,'SentimentLexicon',lexicon);  Warning: The 'rows' input is not supported for cell array inputs. > In cell/unique>celluniqueR2012a (line 236)In cell/unique (line 148)In textanalytics.internal.validateSentimentLexicon (line 40)In vaderSentimentScores>validateVaderScoringParameters (line 148)In vaderSentimentScores (line 67)  The ratioSentimentScores  function throws a warning when using custom sentiment lexicons containing unigrams specified as character vectors. Summary: SimFunction errors on a SimBiology.Scenarios object that contains doses with empty dose tables
Description: The simulation of a?SimBiology.Scenarios ?object using?SimFunction ?errors unexpectedly if the Scenarios  object contains doses with empty dose tables. Empty dose tables can occur if all properties of the dose are parameterized.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: dsp.Channelizer and Channelizer Block will generate incorrect channel data if the 'OversamplingRatio' property is greater than 2
Description: dsp.Channelizer and Channelizer Block will generate incorrect channel data if the 'OversamplingRatio' property is greater than 2.

Summary: Using Java Builder or similar code to print a variable can crash MATLAB
Description: Running on a non-interpreter thread and using Java Builder or similar code to print a MATLAB variable can cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Creating a subsystem from a selection that contains an area that is connected to an annotation might crash MATLAB
Description: Creating a subsystem from a selection might crash MATLAB when the selection contains an area that is connected to an annotation with a line. For example, dragging a selection box around the area, right-clicking a selected element, and selecting Create Subsystem from Selection ?might crash MATLAB.
Workaround: Create a subsystem from only the area. For example, right-click the area, then select Create Subsystem from Area. Alternatively, delete the annotation connector before creating a subsystem from the selection. Then, re-create the connector.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier might fail to complete normally when performing Out of bound array access detection on models containing matrix-typed data with an InitialValue
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might fail to complete normally when all of the following conditions are true: Design Error Detection is performed with?Out of bound array access   enabled A data object of matrix type has a non-empty InitialValue   specification (such as through the Model Workspace).  In some cases, analysis might fail with an "Error detection produced errors" message. In all cases, the return status of sldvrun will be 0 when this issue is applicable.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Viewing PowerPoint presentation more than once using rptview or pptview results in error
Description: Viewing a PowerPoint presentation more than once using rptview  or pptview  results in this error: Error using mlreportgen.utils.internal/executeRPC (line 60)  Retry calling function "@()showNETObj(hNETObj)".    Error in mlreportgen.utils.PPTPres/show (line 54)  ? ? ? ? ? ? mlreportgen.utils.internal.executeRPC(@()showNETObj(hNETObj));    Error in mlreportgen.utils.internal/rptview (line 33)  ? ? ? ? ? ? show(viewer);    Error in rptview (line 65)  mlreportgen.utils.internal.rptview(fileName, varargin{:})    Error in test1 (line 9)  rptview(ppt);    Caused by:  ? ? Error using mlreportgen.utils.PPTPres>showNETObj (line 237)  ? ? Message: Presentation (unknown member) : Object does not exist.  ? ? Source: Microsoft PowerPoint  ? ? HelpLink:

Summary: Use of nargout in function with arguments block might return incorrect value
Description: Calls to nargout ?with empty parentheses "nargout() " ?in functions that contain arguments blocks might incorrectly return the value of nargin .? For example, define the MATLAB function foo :? function [a,b] = foo(x)arguments    xendnargout()end Call the function foo ?as follows :? >> foo(1); MATLAB might incorrectly return the value of nargout  as 1. ?T he correct nargout  value for this call is?0 .??
Workaround: Remove the empty parentheses from the call to?nargout:?function [a,b] = foo(x)arguments    xendnargoutendIf you make the same call to foo as before,??>> foo(1);MATLAB returns the correct value 0 of nargout.??

Summary: Connections inside a Subsystem Reference block appears broken when a model is loaded
Description: In certain situations, when you load a model that contains a Subsystem Reference block, the blocks inside the Subsystem Reference block might not get rendered correctly. Also, the connections inside the Subsystem Reference block might be broken. This issue might happen if you try loading a model with a Subsystem Reference block immediately after performing?any  of these actions: Copy and paste to the Subsystem Reference block in which the paste operation fails and throws an error? Converting a Subsystem block to a Subsystem Reference block in which the conversion fails and throws an error
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when generating code from a Simulink model containing a Function Caller block
Description: Generating code from a Simulink model with one or more Function Caller blocks may cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Renaming the root node in the Blockset Tree panel in Simulink Blockset Designer causes an error
Description: Changing the name of the root node in the Blockset Tree panel in Simulink Blockset Designer produces the error "Unrecognized method, property, or field 'BLOCKSET_LIBRARY' for class'Simulink.BlocksetDesigner.internal.MetaDataManager'".

Summary: MATLAB warns about being disconnected from the license server after connection has been restored
Description: If you are using MATLAB with a network license and you have enabled idle timeout, you might encounter this issue. When the MATLAB connection to the license server times out and is idle a long time, MATLAB continues to issue warnings about not being connected even after the connection is restored. This commonly happens when a VPN connection is stopped and MATLAB is left idle overnight. Then, in the morning after the VPN connection is restored, MATLAB issues these warnings.
Workaround: To clear the messages, restart MATLAB.

Summary: Error when linking MATLAB Function block code to selection in IBM DOORS Next
Description: When you link a requirement from IBM DOORS Next to code in a MATLAB Function block by right-clicking the code selection and selecting Link to Selected Item(s) in DOORS Next , this error occurs:  Selection linking failed: index exceeds number of array elements (0) .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur if reusable subchart contains exported graphical function with return value
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions:The Stateflow chart contains a graphical function from custom Simulink library. This exported graphical function has output data. This graphical function is called from different subcharts present within the same model.
Workaround: Set the graphical function's Function Inline Option?property to Inline.

Summary: Project does not automatically rename usages of data dictionaries inside internal test harnesses
Description: Project automatic renaming does not update data dictionary names  inside internal test harnesses saved in a model. When renaming a data dictionary, in the Rename  dialog box, Project offers to automatically update all links to the data dictionary. However, the automatic renaming does not complete the expected actions :?  When the user expands the impacted model and double-clicks the Data Dictionary  line in the Rename  dialog box, Project highlights the data dictionary of the containing model instead. When the user confirms renaming the data dictionary in the Rename  dialog box, Project does not update the link in the internal test harness.
Workaround: Manually update the data dictionary name in the internal test harness.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: MATLAB throws an out of memory error or crashes when calling h5disp or h5info on an HDF5 file containing a scalar string
Description: When returning information from an HDF5 file containing HDF5 scalar strings, h5disp  and  h5info  fail with an out of memory error or crash MATLAB.

Summary: Low average precision value when using trainSSDObjectDetector to train an SSD object detection network
Description: When you use the?trainSSDObjectDetector ?function to train an SSD object detection network, the detections are poor and the average precision value is low. As a result, the average precision value obtained in the Object Detection Using SSD Deep Learning example decreases from 0.8 to 0.4, when you set the doTraining  variable to true .

Summary: 3-D convolutions on a GPU can cause MATLAB to crash when running Windows 10.
Description: On Microsoft Windows 10 platforms, 3-D convolutions on a GPU can cause MATLAB to crash. This includes: Training a network containing a convolution3DLayer  object using the trainNetwork  function Training a dlnetwork  object containing a convolution3DLayer  object using a custom training loop. Training a network defined as a model function that uses the dlconv  function on data with three spatial dimensions. Making predictions with SeriesNetwork or DAGNetwork objects containing     a convolution3DLayer  object using the classify, predict, and activations functions.           Making predictions with a dlnetwork object containing             a convolution3DLayer  object using the predict and forward functions.                 Making predictions with a network defined as a model function that uses the                 dlconv  function on data with three spatial dimensions.

Summary: Synchronous Machine block displays incorrect unsaturated characteristic
Description: For the Synchronous Machine block,?if you select the Electrical > Plot Open-Circuit Saturation (pu)  option on the block context menu, the displayed unsaturated characteristic is incorrect.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: SIMD code generation results in incorrect answers for min/max operations operating on NaN inputs
Description: When you enable SIMD code generation through an Intel SSE/AVX  or ARM NEON CRL  table, the optimized loops for min/max operations may produce incorrect answers for NaN  inputs. The SIMD optimized implementations of min/max operations do not preserve the expected behavior of a non-NaN  input being returned when only one input of min/max operations is a NaN . The precise behavior depends on the particular SIMD instruction set being targeted, but neither of the supported vendor's SIMD instruction sets matches simulation behavior for min/max operations.

Summary: The Define New ROI Label?dialog appears blank when you try to add a new ROI label definition in any of the Labeler apps
Description: The Define New ROI Label ? dialog renders a blank dialog box when you try to add a new ROI label definition. The problem occurs after loading a saved labeler session that contains 60 or more label definitions into a Labeler app.  The Label Name , Color, Group , and Description  fields are missing from the dialog box, and only the OK  and Cancel  buttons are shown. When you click OK  , the labeler returns the following error: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type. Error in vision.internal.labeler.tool.ROILabelDefinitionDialog/onOK (line 139)  ? ? ? ? ? ? isValid = this.SessionLabelSet.validateLabelName(newLabelName)...  Error in vision.internal.uitools.OkCancelDlg>@(varargin)this.onOK(varargin{:}) (line 39)  ? ? ? ? ? ? this.OkButton = uicontrol('Parent', this.Dlg, 'Callback', @this.onOK,...?  Error while evaluating UIControl Callback.?
Workaround: If you wish to add additional label definitions to a loaded session with 60 or more label definitions, then perform the following steps:In a new labeler session, recreate the label definitions and additional new "spare" label definitions to save for future modification.In a later session, you can load the previously saved definitions and modify the labels.The additional labels created in step 1 were created as placeholders for future sessions.

Summary: Unable to import an SMBL file containing reaction modifiers
Description: When importing an SBML file with sbmlimport , SimBiology incorrectly handles reaction modifiers if the model contains multiple compartments or the name of the reaction modifier is not a valid MATLAB variable name. In some cases, the file will import without error, but the model incorrectly import species and reactions. In other cases, the import will fail with an error message such as the following: Invalid Reaction value. Spaces are required before and after species names and stoichiometric values, for example, 2 A + B -> 2 C. Stoichiometric values must be greater than zero. Type 'help SimBiology.Model.addreaction' for more information.Error in privatesbmlio
Workaround: Replace each modifier in the SBML file with a corresponding reactant and product with the same stoichiometric coefficient. For example, consider an SBML file containing the following definition for reactants, products, and modifiers in a reaction:<listOfReactants>  <speciesReference species="reactant" stoichiometry="2" constant="true"/></listOfReactants><listOfProducts>  <speciesReference species="product" stoichiometry="1" constant="true"/></listOfProducts><listOfModifiers>  <modifierSpeciesReference species="modifier"/></listOfModifiers>Replace the above SBML with the following:<listOfReactants>  <speciesReference species="reactant" stoichiometry="2" constant="true"/>  <speciesReference species="modifier" stoichiometry="1" constant="true"/></listOfReactants><listOfProducts>  <speciesReference species="product" stoichiometry="1" constant="true"/>  <speciesReference species="modifier" stoichiometry="1" constant="true"/></listOfProducts>

Summary: SVN cleanup removes unversioned files, ignored files, and stale transactions and locks
Description: In releases prior to R2020a Update 5, SVN cleanup removes stale locks,                    unfinished transactions, and unversioned or ignored files.  Carefully use this functionality as it might delete unversioned files that you need for your design.

Summary: On Windows 10 May 2020 Update, a computer restart can occur when model simulation ends
Description: On Windows 10 May 2020 Update   and later operating systems, a computer restart can occur when Simulink Desktop Real-Time model simulation in external mode ends. The computer restart occurs more often when the model uses a large value for the ExtModeTrigDuration  parameter. This parameter specifies the size of external mode signal buffers.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model containing a?Data Store Write block inside a reusable subsystem that interacts with another subsystem that has an initialization function
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: ?The model contains a subsystem that has an initialization function. ?The output of this subsystem connects to the input of another subsystem.? ?For the latter subsystem, in the Subsystem block parameters dialog box,? the?Function packaging ?parameter is set to? Reusable function  The latter subsystem has a Data Store Write block that directly connects to its subsystem input.

Summary: Calling the imaqhwinfo function for hamamatsu and qimaging adaptors might return a misleading error message
Description: When calling imaqhwinfo("hamamatsu")  or imaqhwinfo("qimaging") , MATLAB might return an error message containing a link to the Add-On Explorer to install the support packages for these adaptors. However, the link is broken since native support for Hamamtsu and Qimaging have been removed from MATLAB in R2020a. For example, the old error message is as follows. >> imaqhwinfo("qimaging")   Error using imaqhwinfo (line 131)   To use the 'qimaging' adaptor, install the QImaging support?package via Add-On Explorer.     The updated errors no longer link to the Add-On Explorer and have new messages. For example, the new error message is as follows.? >> imaqhwinfo("qimaging")   Error using imaqhwinfo (line 143)   QImaging is no longer natively supported in MATLAB. For more information, see QImaging Camera Support from Image?Acquisition Toolbox.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: err output port of NR Polar Decoder gives wrong answer
Description: Simulating NR Polar Decoder block in accelerator or rapid accelerator modes produces a wrong answer for the?err  output port.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for the cast?function might return a real matrix instead of a complex matrix with zero imaginary part
Description: If you use the 'like' ?argument of the?cast ?function in the generated code , the result might have incorrect complexity if all ?these conditions are true: The first input A  (the input array that is to be cast) is sparse, real, and numeric (not logical) The last input p  (the prototype) is complex
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Autorename fails for a Simulink bus object containing multiple bus elements.
Description: The autorename fails for a Simulink bus object that meets the following conditions: The bus object contains two or more bus elements. One of the bus elements is based on the type that is being renamed.  MATLAB stack trace: Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BaseWorkspaceFileHandler/modifyObject  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler/refactor  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.VariableRefactoringHandler/refactor  Error in dependencies.internal.action.refactor  Error in dependencies.internal.action.refactor  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Dependency.refactor?
Workaround: Manually modify the remaining instances of the bus usages.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Polyspace on Windows crashes in the C to intermediate language translation phase
Description: When running Polyspace Code Prover, you might see an error message in the?C to intermediate language translation   phase. The message refers to a?ps_c_me.exe ?executable. For instance: System error Invalid argument (inval: Invalid argument)?Error: unable to execute C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\bin\win64\ps_c_me.exe (errno=22).? The issue occurs because the internal?ps_c_me.exe  executable crashes on Windows if the full command is too long (32k characters limit). This might happen if you use a very large number of options when running Polyspace Code Prover.
Products: Polyspace Code Prover, Polyspace Code Prover Server

Summary: A large number of global data shows up for some Schedule Editor models in the Model Comparison Tool
Description: Global data property for Schedule Editor models shows up in the Model Comparison Tool, which is not useful information and causes confusion.

Summary: Imported assessment results are empty in a new MATLAB session
Description: If you export a Simulink Test results file and then imported it into a new MATLAB session, the assessment results in the result set are empty and invalid.

Summary: MATLAB Web App Server on Linux produces an error when using the?uigetfile?function in a web app
Description: When? you use the uigetfile  function in a web app that is running on the MATLAB Web App Server in Linux, the server produces the following error in the session log:  "Permission denied"

Summary: Failure to login to IBM DOORS Next when performing oslc.configure() procedure
Description: When logging in to IBM DOORS Next (previously known as IBM DOORS Next Generation or DNG), after the correct server name, user name, and password are provided by user, MATLAB prompts for the password again, and again, and then prompts to confirm server name and/or user name, followed by more password prompts, but login never succeeds after entering all correct information.

Summary: Simulink might crash when multiple models simulate simultaneously, but the models finishing last contain string signals
Description: Simulink allows multiple unrelated models to simulate at the same time. Simulink might crash if one model finishes simulation and the remaining models contain string signals.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Code View hangs after post-processing generated code
Description: When you customize the generated code after it is written to the disk, the Code View might hang when you try to view the modified code files.?

Summary: Simulink models with calls to functions utilizing sparse matrices may produce an unexpected error during simulation and C++ code generation
Description: Simulation and generating code for models containing calls to functions utilizing sparse matrices (e.g, filtfilt and sparse) will result in an unexpected error under the following conditions: ?The function is called from a MATLAB Function Block or Stateflow-Based Block (e.g, Truth Table) ?The model Configuration has C++ set as its Simulation Target Language   The error will contain text "undefined reference to", for example: Copy_of_mg2282394_20a_sfun.mexa64 @Copy_of_mg2282394_20a_sfun.mol????? c1_Copy_of_mg2282394_20a.o: In function `c1_chartstep_c1_Copy_of_mg2282394_20a(SFc1_Copy_of_mg2282394_20aInstanceStruct*)':c1_Copy_of_mg2282394_20a.cpp:(.text+0x17a5): undefined reference to `cs_di_sqr(int, cs_di_sparse const*, int)'c1_Copy_of_mg2282394_20a.cpp:(.text+0x17bf): undefined reference to `cs_di_lu(cs_di_sparse const*, cs_di_symbolic const*, double)'

Summary: SimBiology Model Analyzer can hang when loading saved projects with unfinished sampling runs
Description: If you add a generate samples step to a simulation program and then stop the program in the middle of execution, plots might be left in a broken state. ?If you save the project and tries to reopen it, the app might hang.

Summary: The spectrum plot is displayed in linear units.
Description: The spectrum plot of radarWaveformAnalyzer?is displayed in linear units but should be display in dB to agree with the y-axis label.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar sensor mounted under wheel of vehicle causes vehicle to tilt
Description: If you mount a Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar sensor block under the one of the wheels of a Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block, then the vehicle becomes tilted. The tilt of the vehicle is relative to the height of the sensor above the ground.

Summary: A timer TasksToExecute property can affect a running TimerFcn
Description: Changing the TasksToExecute  property while a multirate timerFcn  is running should cause the timer to react.  This assumes that the TasksToExecute  property gets reset to a new value before the timer finishes scheduling timerFcn , based on the previous value of the TasksToExecute  property.? Otherwise, a race condition can occur. For example:   import matlab.unittest.TestCase;  testCase = TestCase.forInteractiveUse;  changeTasksToExecuteMidCallTo = 20;  timer1 = timer('Period',1,'ExecutionMode','FixedRate','TimerFcn','disp(''Running'')','TasksToExecute',40);  start(timer1);  pause(1);  timer1.TasksToExecute = changeTasksToExecuteMidCallTo;   wait(timer1); % wait for the timer to stop Note : If the timer has already scheduled 40 tasks before execution of timer1.TasksToExecute = changeTasksToExecuteMidCallTo; , the timer will not be affected. Use this kind of workflow sparingly, as it has race condition issues. Ensure that the timer ran 20 times, changing the TaskstoExecute  affected the number of times that the timer ran. Assuming no race condition:  testCase.verifyEqual(timer1.TasksExecuted, changeTasksToExecuteMidCallTo);

Summary: The function factormay return wrong results for polynomial expressions when called with the option'FactorMode', 'Full'
Description: The function factor might return a wrong factorization of polynomial expressions when called withthe option 'FactorMode', 'Full' . The product of all factors and the expressionmay differ by a constant factor.?

Summary: Signals exported to OpenDRIVE might reference a different road than the one specified in RoadRunner scene
Description: In scenes exported to OpenDRIVE, some junctions might have incorrect associations between the gates and the physical signs or signals. In these cases, the signal entry might appear on a different OpenDRIVE road than the signal entry on the corresponding RoadRunner road.

Summary: Code generation for the?resample?function errors out for constant inputs when variable sizing is enabled and dynamic memory allocation is turned "Off"
Description: Standalone code (static library/dynamically linked library/Simulation-in-the-Loop (SIL)/Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL)) generated for the?resample ? function returns an error when the input arguments are constants and the configuration settings satisfy these conditions: Variable Sizing is enabled Dynamic Memory Allocation is turned "Off"   For example, define a function?foo ?that calls?resample : function y = foo(x) %#codegenx = rand(100,1);y = resample(x,4,5);end Generate a standalone C static library for the function?foo ?using the following commands: >> cfg = coder.config('lib');>> cfg.EnableVariableSizing = true;>> cfg.DynamicMemoryAllocation = "Off";?>> codegen foo -config cfg The commands result in this error message: ??? Computed maximum size of the output of function 'resample' is not bounded.  Static memory allocation requires all sizes to be bounded.  The computed size is [:? x 1].  Please consider enabling dynamic memory allocation to allow unbounded sizes.  Error in ==> foo Line: 3 Column: 5  Code generation failed: View Error Report  Error using codegen (line 210)?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: sum(x,dim) might return incorrect answers for?single?and?half?inputs?in generated code that uses row-major layout
Description: Code generated for the function  sum(x,dim) , where?dim ?is the dimension to operate along,?might return incorrect answers if all  the following conditions are true: class(x)  is 'single ' or 'half ' The generated code stores  x ?in row-major order x  is not a vector x  is not empty The dim  argument is supplied size(x,dim)  is greater than 1024  size(x,k)~=1 ?for some?k < dim   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: fmincon?code generated with?MATLAB Coder can return an incorrect result
Description: Because of an error in the code generation version of the fmincon  'sqp'  algorithm, the internal line search can sometimes cause the algorithm to converge to an infeasible point or a point that is not a local minimum. You can observe when the internal line search operates in the Step Length column of iterative display (Display  option = 'iter' ). When any reported step length is less than 1, the line search operates, and the code generation version of fmincon  can sometimes give an incorrect result. For example, consider the following nonlinear programming problem. objective = @(x)? x(1)*x(2) + x(2)*x(3);  nonlcon = @(x) deal( [x(1)^2 - x(2)^2 + x(3)^2 - 2; x(1)^2 + x(2)^2 + x(3)^2 - 10], []);  opts = optimoptions('fmincon','Algorithm','sqp', 'Display', 'iter');?  [x,fval,exitflag,output] = fmincon(@objective,ones(3,1),[],[],[],[],[],[],@nonlcon,opts);   When run in MATLAB, fmincon  returns?exitflag = 1? and the display shows that the Step Length at iteration 3 is less than 1. ?Iter? Func-count? ? ? ? ? ? Fval? ?Feasibility? ?Step Length? ? ? ?Norm of? ? Summary: Nonlinear index reduction may not work correctly for models involving linear cancellation of variables
Description: In models that have a high differential index and equations involving linear cancellation of some variables (for example, y == x-x ), the nonlinear index reduction?algorithm may either fail to reduce the differential index, or may?produce a suboptimal index-reduced system.

Summary: Incorrect analysis results on certain models containing Sqrt blocks with fixed-point input signal
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect derived ranges and analysis results under the following conditions: The model contains a?Sqrt? block with function?parameter set to sqrt  or  rSqrt  The input signal type of the Sqrt  block is?fixed-point with more than 32 bits wordlength and/or non-zero fractionlength

Summary: Using the?memcpy function in a Stateflow chart throws assertion failure errors during simulation
Description: When you run a model that uses memcpy ?as a custom code C function in a Stateflow chart and the "Import Custom Code" configuration parameter is enabled, Simulink throws an error similar to: failed assertion in b:\matlab\toolbox\shared\cgxe\src\cgxe\engine\jitengineimpl.cpp at line 883:?In sanityCheckFunctionCalls: VM Internal Error: Found a function 'memcpy' with an unknown input type

Summary: Running the Similarity-Based Remaining Useful Life Estimation example causes an error
Description: After you manually download and unzip the data from the NASA PHM data repository, then run the first step of the example script, the Similarity-Based Remaining Useful Life Estimation example generates the following error message:  ?Error using helperLoadData (line 21)  The VariableNames property must contain one name for each variable in the table.
Workaround: Use the following steps to work around this error:Open the helperLoadData.m file:>> edit helperLoadData Comment Line 11, so that it looks like this:% t(:,end) = []; %Discard last column of loaded data as all are empty ''

Summary: Navigation broken from imported DOORS Next item to the original item in DOORS Next project
Description: An error may be displayed when Configuration Management is enabled for the DOORS Next project, and you perform the below actions in sequence: An imported IBM DOORS Next (also referred to as DNG) item is selected in the Simulink Requirements Editor. In the right pane, click Show in document  to navigate from the imported reference to the original item in IBM DOORS Next.  Depending on MATLAB session configuration, there may be an Unknown configuration  error on the MATLAB side, or the web browser may display a page that states: Unknown or deleted configuration .

Summary: Requirements imported directly from IBM DOORS Next have incomplete contents
Description: When you directly import requirements from IBM DOORS Next (also known as IBM DOORS Next Generation or DNG), the Description ?property for the? imported referenced requirements does not contain the full requirement text that is listed in IBM DOORS Next.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when deleting or modifying a file that is part of a class
Description: MATLAB can crash when using a class and you delete or modify a file that either contains the class definition or implements a method of the class.

Summary: nufftn can cause MATLAB??to crash when operating on a single sample point or query point
Description: Calling the nufftn  function can result in a crash when the inputs are:  A single sample point and multiple, non-uniform, non-gridded query points Multiple sample points and a single query point
Workaround: ?Several workarounds are possible depending on the inputs to the function.When there is a single query point f, a "dummy" second query point can be used to avoid the crash.? For example:x = randn(100);tx = 1:100;ty = 1:100;?[Tx,Ty] = ndgrid(tx,ty);f = [0 0];?y = nufftn(x, [Tx(:) Ty(:)], [f; f]);When there is a single sample point t, a "dummy" second sample point and input value can be used to avoid the crash.? For example:x = randn;?t = [0 0];fx = 1:100;fy = 1:100;[Fx,Fy] = ndgrid(fx,fy);y = nufftn([x; 0], [t; t], [Fx(:) Fy(:)]);In cases where the non-uniform points (whether sample points or query points) can be separated into grid vectors, the crash can be avoided by instead specifying the non-uniformly spaced grid through the grid vector syntax.? For example:% Replacing y = nufftn(x, [Tx(:) Ty(:)], f);y?= nufftn(x, {tx, ty}, f);or% Replacing y = nufftn(x, t, [Fx(:) Fy(:)]);y = nufftn(x, t, {fx, fy});?

Summary: Import from DOORS Next module or query does not work with port number 443
Description: If you click Import in the Simulink Requirements menu, and select "IBM DOORS Next Generation" for the document type, Simulink Requirements will prompt you for server login information, and will allow you to select a Project to import from, but the rest will not work.  For example, if you select the option to import a specified Module, Simulink Requirements will try to populate the Module selector combo-box with the list of available modules, but the list remains empty. This happens when DOORS Next server is configured to use the default HTTPS port number 443. The malfunction does not happen with any other port. For example, if server is configured to use IBM's default port number 9443, import works as expected.

Summary: MATLAB web browser might not work behind a proxy server
Description: The MATLAB web browser might fail to load external web pages when a proxy server is being used. For example, the following command might fail to load the mathworks.com page when a proxy server is in use. web('http://mathworks.com')

Summary: removedose?causes MATLAB to crash
Description: When you call?removedose ?on a dose object that is not on a model, MATLAB crashes. To check whether the dose you are trying to remove is on a model, use getdose .

Summary: Generating a coverage report in the Test Manager for subsystem harnesses at different levels in a library causes an assertion failure
Description: Generating a coverage report in the Test Manager for subsystem harnesses at different levels in a library causes an assertion failure in cvi.TopModelCov.moveHarnessTest: failed assertion in b:\matlab\?toolbox\?slcoverage\src\?slvnv\?utils\?object.?cpp at line 2472: id>0 && id<pool_capacity && object[id]!=NULL[??0] 0x00007ffe7a7a775f Q:\?matlab\?bin\?win64\?slvnv.dll+00423775 modelcov_root_relatives_and_consistency+00000239[??1] 0x00007ffe7a7a6ec1 Q:\?matlab\?bin\win64\?slvnv.?dll+00421569 modelcov_merge_data+00000385..?.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 7, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening AUTOSAR model in which blocks are linked to a library
Description: MATLAB might crash when opening an AUTOSAR model if both of these conditions exist in the model: Simulink states, signals, or data stores are mapped to AUTOSAR variables. The mapped states, signals, or data stores are associated with blocks that are linked to a library.

Summary: Overwritten Embedded Coder Dictionary in Simulink data dictionary
Description: When you open the Embedded Coder app or the Embedded Coder Dictionary dialog for a model, if the model uses a configuration set that you store in a Simulink data dictionary, the Embedded Coder Dictionary in the Simulink data dictionary might be overwritten.

Summary: ASAP2 files describe lookup table blocks as VAL_BLK instead of STD_AXIS?when multiple lookup table blocks?share the same lookup table object or parameters
Description: When multiple lookup table blocks share the same lookup table object or parameter, the generated A2L file describes the blocks as?VAL_BLK  instead of STD_AXIS .

Summary: Unconnected Stateflow message input port without an internal receiving queue may crash MATLAB when you generate code
Description: MATLAB might crash when you generate code for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model contains a Stateflow chart with a message input For message input Use internal queue ?check box is not selected The message input port is unconnected

Summary: intlinprog returns an incorrect solution when the problem contains identical integer variables
Description: intlinprog  can return an infeasible solution, a suboptimal solution, or an incorrect objective function value under thefollowing circumstances. There is a set of indices of integervariables S, with S having at least two elements, satisfying all of the followingconditions. (For example, S = [1,3].) All elements of theobjective vector f(S) are equal. (For example, f(1) = f(3).)? All elements of theinequality matrix A(:,S) are equal also. (For example, A(1,1) = A(1,3) = A(2,1) = A(2,3) = ... for all rows of A.) All elements of theequality constraints Aeq(:,S) are equal also. (For example, Aeq(1,1) = Aeq(1,3) = Aeq(2,1) = Aeq(2,3) = ... for all rows of Aeq.) The Heuristics  optionis set to 'basic'  (default), 'intermediate' , or 'advanced' .  The? LPPreprocess  option is set to? 'basic'  (default).   The error is due to an inconsistency between LP preprocessing and the 1-opt heuristic. The 1-opt heuristic runs for? 'basic' ,?'intermediate' , or?'advanced' ?heuristics.
Workaround: There are two workarounds:Set the Heuristics option to 'rss', which has similar behavior to 'basic', but does not call 1-opt heuristic. options = optimoptions('intlinprog','Heuristics','rss');Set the LPPreprocess option to 'none'. options = optimoptions('intlinprog','LPPreprocess','none');

Summary: dlnetwork objects might have nonpositive TrainedVariance state values for batch normalization layers
Description: Converting small numbers to single precision can result in positive numbers being converted to nonpositive numbers with single precision. This can result in nonpositive values for batch normalization variance state when training or assembling deep learning networks. For non-gpuArray ?input, the?predict ?function for?dlnetwork ?objects throws an error when a batch normalization layer has a nonpositive value in the?TrainedVariance ?state parameter.?For example: net = resnet50;lgraph = layerGraph(net);lgraph = removeLayers(lgraph,'ClassificationLayer_fc1000');dlnet = dlnetwork(lgraph);trainedVariance = zeros([1 1 64]) + 1e-64;dlnet.State{2,3} = {single(trainedVariance)};dlX = dlarray(rand(224,224,3,'single'),'SSCB');dlYPred = predict(dlnet,dlX);Error using nnet.internal.cnn.dlnetwork/predict (line 258)Layer 'bn_conv1': Invalid input data. The value of 'Variance' is invalid. Expected input to be positive.
Workaround: For each batch normalization layer in the dlnetwork object, set the TrainedVariance state to eps('single') using the dlupdate function. For example:idx = dlnet.State.Parameter == "TrainedVariance";boundAwayFromZero = @(X) max(X, eps('single'));dlnet.State(idx,:) = dlupdate(boundAwayFromZero, dlnet.State(idx,:));

Summary: Misleading error when opening a project that uses a new format in older MATLAB releases
Description: Loading a project in releases prior to R2020b fails if all  of these conditions are true:The project is saved in R2020b The project uses the default project format  In such situations, MATLAB issues a misleading error:No project found in root folder.   The actual cause of the error is that MATLAB releases prior to R2020b do not recognize the new default project format.

Summary: Video Labeler app returns an error when you export data from a loaded custom source.
Description: The Video Labeler  app returns an error when you export labeled data from a custom data source.  The app returns the following error: Error using groundTruthDataSource/parseAndPopulateInputs (line 490)   Input folders or files contain non-standard file extensions.   Use FileExtensions Name-Value pair to include the non-standard file extensions.   Warning: Error occurred while evaluating listener callback.?

Summary: mlreportgen.utils.html2dom.prepHTMLFile incorrectly prepares HTML file with special characters
Description: If you use mlreportgen.utils.html2dom.prepHTMLFile ?with? an HTML file that contains special characters, such as? < ,?> ,? or?&,  and then pass the prepared HTML to? mlreportgen.dom.HTML ?or? mlreportgen.dom.HTMLFile , an error occurs with this message: >> s = mlreportgen.utils.html2dom.prepHTMLFile('example.html'); >> html = mlreportgen.dom.HTML(s); Error using mlreportgen.dom.HTML  HTML error: expected entity name for reference

Summary: Labeler custom reader returns an error while readingdata source.
Description: The custom reader used to read custom files and other datasources for labeler applications, returns an error related to incorrecttimestamps. The reader incorrectly sets the start time to zero producing an error similar to, or starting with:?  Error using groundTruthDataSource/ parseAndPopulateInputs

Summary: Unable to plot data from imported datasets with columns whose names only differ by case in the SimBiology Model Analyzer app
Description: When you?plot data from imported datasets with columns whose names only differ by case in the?SimBiology Model Analyzer ?app, the app?shows the following error message: "Plot Error: Unable to perform assignment because the left and right sides have a different number of elements."

Summary: Empty file and segmentation fault message when generating RTF documents on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 or CentOS 7.8
Description: If you generate an RTF document on a Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 or CentOS 7.8 platform, the size of the resulting RTF document is 0 bytes and a segmentation fault occurs.

Summary: Compilation error when simulating model containing certain Simscape blocks
Description: When the underlying equations?for a Simscape block use the prod  function applied to a single-element vector or matrix, simulating a model containing such block may generate a compilation error similar to the following: Error compiling Simscape network for model m1.  Caused by:  ? ? Passing empty expression container where non-empty container is expected.

Summary: Lookup Table blocks unable to perform numerical perturbation when using Cubic spline or Akima spline interpolation method
Description: 1-D, 2-D, and n-D Lookup Table blocks are unable to perform numerical perturbation when the Interpolation Method  parameter is set to Cubic spline  or Akima spline .

Summary: Compilation error occurs if For Each Subsystem is present within a graphical function of a resusable Stateflow chart
Description: Compilation error might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: Model contains a reusable Stateflow chart with graphical function. Graphical function contains For Each Subsystem.

Summary: Saving a symfun object and loading it in a different MATLAB version can result in the loaded object having class sym
Description: Saving a symfun  object in R2019b or earlier and loading the mat file in R2020a can result in the loaded object having class sym  instead of symfun . No warning is printed.
Workaround: Convert the loaded object into a symfun:load data.matf(x) = f;?orload data.matf = symfun(f,x);

Summary: MATLAB might stop responding with a drawnow timeout
Description: In some situations involving uifigures, MATLAB might stop responding. ?After 15 minutes, a drawnow timeout might occur with one of the following: the warning MATLAB:graphics:drawnowTimeout  the message Client may not be connected .

Summary: Simulink Test Manager might crash when running tests that collect coverage
Description: If you add coverage after running multiple test cases, Simulink Test Manager might crash.

Summary: Scoping coverage to requirements-based tests causes 0% coverage for subsystem test harnesses
Description: Scoping model coverage results to requirements-based tests in the Simulink Test Manager incorrectly causes the aggregated coverage results to show as 0% achieved when the component-under-test is a subsystem test harness. The scoped coverage results are reported as if no requirements were linked to elements in the component-under-test.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB functions that recursively call themselves might produce incorrect code
Description: C/C++ code generation from MATLAB might produce incorrect code if all  of these conditions are true: The source MATLAB code contains a function that (directly or indirectly) calls itself? The first call to the recursive function (or the function that starts the recursion) passes a constant input to that function During recursion, the subsequent calls to that function pass different values for that input parameter?  For example, define a MATLAB function simple2 ?that calls the recursive function? recursive_fcn ? with a constant input?5 : function out = simple2 %#codegenout = recursive_fcn(5);end The function recursive_fcn ? recursively calls itself with a different input value for each call:function out = recursive_fcn(n)if n > 0    m = n - 1;    out = 0;    out = recursive_fcn(m);    out = out + do_something(n);else    out = 0;endend function out = do_something(n)out = cumsum(1,n);end The generated C code contains an infinite recursion and does not terminate. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: RoadRunner scenes imported from OpenDRIVE might fail to export to OpenDRIVE due to an unsupported imported U-turn.
Description: U-turns that connect to a singly-digitized road in RoadRunner would fail to export to OpenDRIVE if the u-turn started and ended at the same distance at one end of the incoming road. Certain OpenDRIVE files may choose to define u-turns in this way; RoadRunner will maintain the layout during import.

Summary: signalDatastore errors out when reading files from a remote location.
Description: signalDatastore  is unable to read data from a remote location, such as cloud storage in Amazon S3? (Simple Storage Service), Microsoft? Azure? Storage Blob, or Hadoop? Distributed File System (HDFS?).

Summary: Drive Cycle Source block produces an incompatible file error
Description: The Drive Cycle Source block produces an incompatible file error dialogue message when importing files with non-uniformly sampled time data.
Workaround: Click?OK?to accept the error. You will then see the correct error message regarding the issue with non-uniformly sampled data.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation might ignore AUTOSAR per-instance properties of mapped signal, state, or data store
Description: AUTOSAR code generation might ignore the per-instance properties of a mapped signal, state, or Data Store Memory block after the corresponding block is resolved to a library. For example, in the generated C code, the ShortName ?value specified for a signal mapped to AUTOSAR ArTypedPerInstanceMemory  might be absent.
Workaround: After a block corresponding to a mapped signal, state, or data store resolves to a library, and before generating code, re-specify the per-instance properties in the AUTOSAR mapping.?Repeat each time the model is re-opened.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Check trigger signal names?flags Simulink functions nested in Stateflow charts
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check trigger signal names ?(Check ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0281 ) incorrectly flags the Simulink functions nested in the Stateflow charts.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor run might run incorrectly when filter input setting used for Simulink to physical signal converter blocks
Description: If the Simscape model contains Simulink-PS Converter blocks, in the Block Parameters dialog box for the blocks, on the Input Handling  tab, the Filtering and derivatives ?parameter must be set to Zero derivatives (piecewise constant)  or Provide Signals  and the Provided signals  parameter must be set to Input only. Using a different setting can cause the converter blocks in the HDL implementation model generated by running the Simscape to HDL Workflow Advisor to not preserve the filtering behavior. This can result in a numeric mismatch between the Simscape model and the implementation model. In R2018b and R2019a these filtering options are not caught by the Simscape to HDL Workflow advisor and the generated model does not have the filtering behavior that is set on the Simulink-PS Converter. In? R2019b_update6, the error in the Simscape to HDL advisor will be: Unrecognized property 'SPSFilterBlocks' for class  ????????????'ssccodegenworkflow. SwitchedLinearWorkflow  In R2020a_update4 onward the error will read:  Only the ''Provide signals'' and ''Input only'' settings on S-SP conveters are supported with the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor.
Workaround: Add a Discrete Filter block before the Simulink-PS Converter block to capture the filter behavior and change the Block Parameters of the Simulink-PS Converter block to use Provide Signals and Input only.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Multiple CAN FD  channel connections cannot run on the same MathWorks virtual channel
Description: You cannot simultaneously run more than one CAN FD channel connected to the same MathWorks virtual device channel.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Bus signal connecting Unit delay block to MATLAB function block may generate wrong code
Description: Incorrect PLC Code might be generated if all of the following conditions are true in the Simulink subsystem for which code is being generated:A signal connects a Unit-delay block to a MATLAB function block The 'auto ' option is set in Block Parameters (Subsystem)  -> Code Generation  pane.
Workaround: Change the Function packaging option to 'Reusable function' instead of 'Auto' in Block Parameters (Subsystem) -> Code Generation pane of the MATLAB function block in question.

Summary: MATLAB Production Server Compiler App missing icon resulting in blank image
Description: On Linux, the image icon of the MATLAB Production Server Compiler application is missing in the app gallery. This is solely a graphical issue that does not affect the functionality of the MATLAB Production Server Compiler.?

Summary: When using Signal Editor interface,?duplicating or using copy and paste on signal plotted inside dataset might cause non-stop spinner
Description: When?using the Signal Editor interface, a non-stop spinner might appear when the Signal Editor interface contains a signal plotted inside a dataset/scenario. If you then duplicate or copy and past the signal, a persistent non-stop spinner might appear.?

Summary: Generating tests based on existing coverage data may fail to complete normally for models with Logical Operator blocks having unsatisfiable objectives
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may fail to complete normally when all of the following conditions are met: 1) Test Generation performed based on existing coverage data. 2) At least one logical operator block has all of the following properties:?? a) At least one objective covered in the existing coverage data?? b) At least one Unsatisfiable objective?? c) No vector inputs

Summary: tabularTextDatastore errors when it encounters prefixed hex and binary literals
Description: An error is encountered when constructing a tabularTextDatastore for data which has variables that are prefixed hex literals such as 0xabc943 or prefixed binary literals such as 0b10101111. For example:  ?>>?ttds = tabularTextDatastore(filename);  ?Error using datastore (line 175)  Output argument "formatString" (and maybe others) not assigned during call to  "matlab.io.internal.text.determineFormatString>convertDatatypeToFormatString".?
Workaround: Supply the appropriate hex / binary textscan format specifier via the 'TextscanFormats' name-value pair of TabularTextDatastore. For example, if the third variable has prefixed hex values such as?0xabc943?and the fifth variable has prefixed binary values such as?0b10101111 use:?>> ttds = tabularTextDatastore(filename,'TextscanFormats',{'%q','%f','%x','%f','%b'});?

Summary: Code generation assertion for AUTOSAR model with corrupt signal, state, or data store mapping
Description: AUTOSAR code generation might fail with an assertion if the model has a corrupt signal, state, or data store mapping. While this corruption can happen for multiple reasons, it usually happens when an AUTOSAR model contains a Stateflow chart with atomic subcharts. The build displays an error message containing this text: In getIndividualDSMGroupPropertyFromMapping: Assertion failed: aBlk != nullptr

Summary: Pressure-Compensated Flow Control Valve might incorrectly calculate the Pressure Compensator valve and Variable Orifice areas
Description: The Pressure-Compensated Flow Control Valve incorrectly calculates the Variable Orifice opening fornonzero Initial opening  values and might use an incorrect value for the Pressure Compensator maximum passage area.
Workaround: If the block has a nonzero value for Initial Opening, you can remedy this with the following steps:Set the?InitialOpening?parameter to 0 m.Add a PS Add block and connect its output port to the Pressure-Compensated Flow Control Valveblock port?C.?Connect a PS Constant block to one of the PS Add block input ports. Set the Constant parameter equal to the desired?Initial Opening?value.?Connect the control signal originally connected to port?C?to the other PS Add block input port.Additionally, if the Valve parameterization setting is?By area vs. opening table, set theOrifice maximum area?equal to the lastelement of Tabulated orifice area. Whilethe?Orifice maximum area?is not used in the?By area vs. opening table setting, the parameter is used to set the maximum passage area of the Pressure Compensator valve.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect output from IC block when using Parameter Writer?block?to set IC block's initial value parameter
Description: When an IC block is placed inside a Model block, and the IC block's initial value  parameter uses an instance parameter of the Model block, Simulink improperly allows this instance parameter to be set by a Parameter Writer block outside the Model block.? In this situation, the IC block's output might be incorrect, causing incorrect results in simulation and generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Discrepancy between results for normal vs. SIL/PIL simulation for export-function Model block with Initialize port
Description: Software-in-the-loop (SIL) or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation results differ from normal simulation results for a model when all of the following conditions are met:  The top model or test harness contains a Model block with?Show model initialize port ?selected.  In the same Model block, Simulation mode  is set as Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL).  In the referenced model, the configuration parameter?Code Generation ?>  Optimization?> ?Remove internal data zero initialization ?is selected. The initial value for any internal data in the referenced model is zero.  The difference occurs when the Initialize port is called a second time during simulation. The normal mode simulation reinitializes the internal data of the referenced model to zero, while the SIL/PIL simulation does not.

Summary: Example model mcb_pmsm_foc_sim.slx depends on Fixed-Point Designer product license for single datatype
Description: The example model, mcb_pmsm_foc_sim.slx (which is explained in the Code Verification and Profiling Using Processor-In-the-Loop Testing example), errors when datatype is changed to single if Fixed-Point Designer product is not present.

Summary: Some stereotypes and stereotype properties are missing from the dialog for creating a view
Description: There are stereotypes and stereotype properties missing in the filter builder section of the create view dialog. This can occur when profiles are linked to referenced models and not to the model that the view is being created for.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink.CoderInfo?object that has Identifier property set causes missing initial values in generated PLC code
Description: Simulink.CoderInfo ?object has an Identifier  property that can be used to change the name of the generated variable. This can result in the variable tr  having?a different name than that of the original object.  ? For example,  >> K1 = Simulink.Parameter?  >> K1.CoderInfo.StorageClass = 'ExportedGlobal'  >> K1.CoderInfo.Identifier = 'gainTune'  The generated code in the above case contains a variable named gainTune  instead of K1 . The initial value for this variable might be missing in the generated PLC code.?

Summary: Profile does not have the correct name in System Composer after the profile file is renamed in the file system
Description: If a profile file is renamed through the MATLAB File Browser or in the OS native file system, then when the profile is loaded in System Composer, the name of the profile is incorrect and shows the previous name.?

Summary: dlgradient can return incorrect results for models using the crosschannelnorm function with even channel window size and the execution occurs?on a GPU
Description: There is a bug in the NVIDIA library function cudnnLRNCrossChannelBackward  used by the crosschannelnorm  function to compute the cross-channel normalization backward function on a GPU.Because of this,  dlgradient can return incorrect results for models using the crosschannelnorm function with even channel window size     and  the execution occurs?on a GPU.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, gather data from the GPU before calling the crosschannelnorm function.For example, replace instances of the code dlY = crosschannelnorm(dlX,windowSize);with?dlX = gather(dlX);?dlY = crosschannelnorm(dlX,windowSize);?After the operation, do not cast the data to the GPU as this can cause the issue to persist.

Summary: "Unrecognized function getTypeFromPackageInfoUrl" error displays in the Command Window on startup.
Description: When MATLAB starts up, if add-ons are installed that have updates available, the error "Unrecognized function getTypeFromPackageInfoUrl" displays in the Command Window. The error also displays when logging into MATLAB Online. After MATLAB starts up, all add-ons updates fail to install.
Workaround: No workaround available.

Summary: Using VideoReader to read WebM files crashes MATLAB
Description: On Windows platforms, reading a WebM video file using VideoReader  crashes MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Reciprocal block produces wrong answer for zero-valued fixed-point inputs
Description: When the following conditions are true, the simulation behavior and generated code behavior for the HDL Reciprocal block do not match: The input value is 0 . The input data type is signed fixed-point with negative fraction length. Number of iterations ?is set to 1 .   The generated code produces the expected result while the simulation does not.?

Summary: Spectrogram plot in radarWaveformAnalyzer app reverted to 3D from 2D
Description: The Spectrogram plot in radarWaveformAnalyzer app is shown as a 3D plot instead of a 2D plot

Summary: MATLAB might crash when deleting Stateflow events, data, or messages
Description: MATLAB might crash due to an interaction between the Symbols window and deleting objects in the Stateflow chart.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model with a MATLAB Function block with a nontunable parameter bus
Description: The generated C/C++ code might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions:  The model contains a MATLAB Function block. The MATLAB Function block has a nontunable parameter of bus type that is a bus array. Each element of the bus array contains an array of numeric values. Some of the nested arrays of numeric values contain only real values while others contain values with a non-zero imaginary part.  For a model that meets the above conditions, the generated code might contain incorrect numeric values for the nontunable parameter. Specifically, the imaginary part of some parameter values (that is, those that should be equal to 0) are set to wrong values. This issue manifests itself as a subtle change in the generated code from run to run. One way to detect the presence of this issue is to re-generate C/C++ from a model two times in a row, on different computers or after rebooting the computer. If the model is affected by this issue, you will likely see different numeric values in the generated code in those two builds.?

Summary: Diagnostic Service Component internal error during simulation of AUTOSAR Basic Software service calls
Description: Simulating Diagnostic Event Manager (Dem) caller blocks that invoke AUTOSAR services implemented in a Diagnostic Service Component block can generate an error that references Diagnostic Service Component block internals.? For example: A definition for the function 'Dem_GetIUMPRDenCondition' called by Function Caller block'TestHarness/Diagnostic Service Component/RTE Service Connector/Port_GetIUMPRDenCondition/Dem_GetIUMPRDenCondition(caller)',could not be found.

Description: Reading MDF data from certain byte array channels might crash MATLAB. The issue can occur when the byte arrays have variable length. Summary: readtable errors if the SpreadsheetImportOptions property VariableNamesRange is specified as a positive scalar integer
Description: readtable ?errors if the?SpreadsheetImportOptions ?property?VariableNamesRange ?is specified as a positive scalar integer, which is a valid VariableNamesRange  value. >> opts = spreadsheetImportOptions("VariableNamesRange", 1);?>> readtable('patients.xls', opts)Error using matlab.io.ImportOptions/readtable (line 499)Comparison between double and string is not supported.
Workaround: To prevent readtable from throwing an error, specify the VariableNamesRange?property as a character vector that represents the starting cell for the data.>> opts = spreadsheetImportOptions("VariableNamesRange", "A1");?>> readtable('patients.xls', opts);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Prelookup block might output wrong results in normal mode simulation with in-range index linear search method and mixed double, single, and built-in integer types
Description: The Prelookup block might output wrong results in normal mode simulation when these conditions are all true: Index search method ?is set Linear search .  The input value to the Prelookup?block is within the range of the specified breakpoints. The input, breakpoint, and fraction have double or single precision data type. The index has a built-in integer type.?  Code generation and simulation targets like accelerator and rapid accelerator modes output correct results.?

Summary: Performance degradation when simulating nesting export-function models with run time check
Description: When simulating a multitasking model referencing nested export-function models that have multiple exported functions invoked in different sample times, there is a performance degradation when model parameter Enable strict scheduling checks for referenced models  is selected.
Workaround: Go to Model Settings (Configuration Parameters) > Model Referencing and uncheck Enable strict scheduling checks for referenced models.

Summary: MATLAB stalls during test execution
Description: During test execution using the Test Manager, MATLAB might stall.
Workaround: Force MATLAB to close and restart it. Then, open the Test Manager and rerun the test.?

Summary: The input function might block callback processing in compiled applications and libraries on Windows
Description: Compiled libraries and applications that call input  might not process graphics or timer callbacks until user input is received. This issue is encountered only  on Windows.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when generating code from a Simulink model with a Function Caller block in an inactive variant
Description: Generating code from a Simulink model causes MATLAB to crash when all the following conditions are true. The Function Caller block is in an inactive variant. The Function Caller block invokes a Simulink Function block that is defined in a referenced model. The Function visibility ?parameter of the Simulink Function block is set to scoped , and the C/C++ Function Interface of the block is configured.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes after deleting a dose from a SimBiology model
Description: SimBiology dose objects maintain references to targeted species as well as parameters in parameterized dose properties. Those references become invalid if you delete the dose object using the delete  function, or if you move a target species from one compartment to another in the SimBiology app. MATLAB crashes if you: Rename a species or a parameter referenced by the deleted dose object. Save a project containing invalid references in the SimBiology app.
Workaround: In the MATLAB command lineUse the method removedose?to safely remove doses from the model. For more information on how to use this method, type:help SimBiology.Model.removedose The dose object returned by removedose can be deleted with the delete function.For example, to delete a dose object?dObj with the name 'doseName' from a SimBiology model?mObj, enter:delete(removedose(mObj,'doseName'));? % instead of: delete(getdose(mObj,'doseName'));?ordelete(removedose(mObj,dObj));? % instead of: delete(dObj);??In the SimBiology appClear the TargetName property of the dose object before deleting a dose, or before moving a species that is targeted by a dose from one compartment to another.To clear the TargetName?property, right-click the dose and select Properties. Delete the TargetName property in the Block Property Editor.

Summary: Code spacing is larger than 1 LSB for unimpaired Binary Weighted DAC
Description: The output of a unipolar DAC should range over [0,2^N-1]  LSB.The output of a bipolar DAC? should range over [-2^(N-1),2^(N-1)-1]  LSB. In both cases, 1 LSB = Vref/2^N .  But the Binary Weighted DAC ranges over [0,2^N]  LSB for a unipolar DAC and [-2^(N-1),2^(N-1)]  LSB for a bipolar DAC. However, the DAC does not have the extra code such ranges imply. This results in a code spacing slightly larger than 1  LSB for unimpaired converters.

Description: MATLAB might crash while generating GPU code with the stack trace containing the following errors:? Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: movmax and movmin?can return partially incorrect results for some inputs
Description: The movmax  and movmin  functions can return partially incorrect results when both of these conditions are present: No sample points are supplied, or the supplied sample points are uniformly spaced The input is longer than twice the window length  In cases where incorrect output is generated, if the total window size is k , then the incorrect values only occur in the final (k+2)  elements of the output.
Workaround: A workaround for this issue is to provide non-uniformly spaced sample points that do not change the output.For example, consider a call to movmax with a window size of [kb kf], where?kb?and?kf?are integers:y = movmax(x,[kb kf]);You can replace this call to movmax?with the commands:n = length(x);y = movmax(x,[kb kf],'SamplePoints',[1.1 2:n]);The modified call to movmax adds non-uniform sample points that do not change the output. Any window that should include the sample point 1 will also include the sample point 1.1, while any window that should not include the sample point will similarly not include 1.1.

Summary: Model Metrics Dashboard crashes MATLAB when collecting metric data for a demo model
Description: Collecting metric data for a demo model using the Model Metrics Dashboard may crash MATLAB when the current simulation cache folder was generated by an older version of MATLAB.
Workaround: Delete the simulation cache folder generated by an older release prior to collecting metric data on a demo model.

Summary: Training RNNs with trainNetwork?and datastore input silently ignores "Shuffle" option
Description: When training a recurrent neural network (RNN), such as long short-term memory (LSTM) or gated recurrent unit (GRU) networks, using?trainNetwork ? with an input datastore, the data is not shuffled.?? Under the following conditions, trainNetwork   does not shuffle the input data during training: Network layers contain sequenceInputLayer , lstmLayer , bilstmLayer , or gruLayer  Input training data is a datastore  This behavior occurs even when using training options  that specify the "Shuffle"  option as one of the following values:? "once"  "every-epoch"   The "Shuffle"  option is silently ignored by trainNetwork . The training result is equivalent to setting "Shuffle","never" .?
Workaround: To obtain the "Shuffle","once" behavior, call shuffle on the input datastore before calling trainNetwork. For example:layers = [ ...    sequenceInputLayer(numFeatures)    lstmLayer(numHiddenUnits1,'OutputMode','last')    fullyConnectedLayer(numClasses)    softmaxLayer     classificationLayer];myDatastore = sequenceDatastore(filepath);opts = trainingOptions('sgdm');?myDatastore = shuffle(myDatastore);?net = trainNetwork(myDatastore,?layers, opts);?There is no workaround to obtain the "Shuffle","every-epoch" behavior.?

Summary: Binary Weighted DAC requires 15 conversion clock cycles to settle
Description: Ideally, a DAC should require less than one conversion clock cycle to settle, but the Binary Weighted DAC requires 15 conversion cycles.

Summary: Adapter loses ports and connections when mapping multiple input interfaces to a single output interface
Description: When you try to map multiple input interfaces to an output interface in the adapter block, the adapter block loses its existing ports and connections. If you then open the adapter dialog, the missing interface elements are highlighted in red and previous interface mappings are lost. Follow these steps to reproduce this behavior:? From three components, connect to an Adapter block with two input ports and one output port. Assign interfaces to the ports such that there is an interface with two elements on one input port and another interface with two elements on the second input port and an interface with four elements on the output port. Double-click the Adapter block to launch the Interface Adapter. Map the input port interface elements to all the interface elements in the output port. Apply and save. Re-open the Interface Adapter and notice that missing mappings are highlighted in red.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table(n-D) block with fixed point inputs containing negative fraction length generates incorrect HDL code
Description: The generated HDL code for a Simulink model might be incorrect when the following settings are applied: The model contains a Lookup Table(n-D) block with Flat? Interpolation method The inputs are of fixed-point type with negative fractional length?, such as  sfix6_E3 numerictype(0, 6, -3)?   The incorrect HDL code can result in simulation failures in ModelSim when verifying the generated code.
Workaround: Avoid negative fraction lengths as input type to the Lookup Table(n-D) block.

Summary: Loading externally saved test harness using load_system might cause MATLAB to crash.
Description: If an externally saved test harness has?RebuildOnOpen  set to true  and the rebuild operation fails during test harness load, using load_system  to load that test harness might cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Entity Find block deletes impending events associated with the found entity during entity modification without extraction
Description: If the Entity Fin d block finds an entity in the model, and the following two criteria are met: The found entity is being modified in place without extraction The found entity has any impending event on the Event Calendar  at the same simulation time   The impending event is inadvertently deleted from the Event Calendar  and this causes incorrect simulation results.

Summary: Performance regression in reading char data with low-level file I/O functions
Description: Low-level file I/O functions such as fscanf , fgets , fgetl , and fread  show a marked regression in performance. This regression occurs when opening a file with fopen  without specifying the encoding.

Summary: DAC Testbench results deviate further than specified Error Tolerance
Description: The values from the DAC Testbench block might deviate further from nominal than specified?Error tolerance  parameter.

Summary: The allanvar function returns zeroes for matrix input of large dimensions
Description: When matrices of large dimensions are passed into the allanvar  function, some output columns might contain all zeros instead of the expected data.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by individually passing each column of the input matrix into the allanvar function.

Summary: MATLAB stops responding after updating previously imported requirements in Requirements Editor
Description: When you have apreviously imported requirement set loaded in Requirements Editor,?and ?you use the Update  button?under Requirement Interchange  pane pull-in changes from the original external document,?MATLAB may become unresponsive for a long time after the update is finished. Depending on the number of changes received in the update, the unresponsiveness might continue for over an hour. If MATLAB eventually starts responding, any action that requires a refresh of the Import node property pane might lead to another prolong period of unresponsiveness. Alternatively, if the update is performed using the command-line API, MATLAB stops responding when opening the updated?.slreqx  file in the Requirements Editor, for the same reason.?

Summary: Error when starting MATLAB with a custom preferences folder location
Description: If the MATLAB preferences folder location is set to a custom location that is less than two levels from the root folder of a drive (for example?C:\prefdir ), MATLAB displays an error when starting up. The error is a null pointer exception with these lines: java.lang.NullPointerException  com.mathworks.addons_common.util.FolderNameUtils.getDefaultAddonInstallLocation
Workaround: Specify a preferences folder location that is at least two levels below the root folder of a drive. For example,??C:\folder\subfolder\prefdir.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when collecting model metrics on a model that references a protected model
Description: Collecting metrics using the Model Metrics dashboard on a model that references a protected model causes MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Persistent lateral drift for vehicle models with solid axle suspensions and non-zero initial roll angles
Description: If your vehicle model has a non-zero initial roll angle and a solid axle suspension, the vehicle can have a persistent lateral drift during simulation.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b
