Summary: Edits in Signal Editor interface might not be saved when editing a signal value in table with duplicate time points
Description: In Signal Editor interface, edits might not be saved when adding a row and then editing the time value for a signal in the table editing area.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Memory allocated for a dynamic array in the generated code might be?freed before last use of the array
Description: In the generated code, memory allocated for a dynamic array might be?freed before the last use of the array if all  these conditions are true: Your MATLAB code contains an array for which the code generator allocates memory dynamically on the heap. (Such arrays must be variable-size and unbounded, or variable-size with bound that is greater than a threshold.) In your MATLAB code, the last reference of the dynamic array is used inside a function call. This function call appears in the condition of an if  or while ?statement.  For example, consider this MATLAB function: function y = example(n1,n2) assert(isa(n1, 'double'));assert(n1 <= 100);assert(isa(n2, 'double')); % Create a 1x100 upper bounded struct array that contains an inner unbounded arrays1 = repmat(struct('x', zeros(1,n2)), 1, n1);% Create an unbounded struct array that contains an inner unbounded arrays2 = repmat(struct('x', zeros(1,n2)), 1, n2);for i = 1:numel(s1)    s1(i).x(10) = n1;end a2 = subfcn(s2);if subfcn(s1) == 42    y = 123 + s2(10).x(2);else    y = 456 + a2;end function y = subfcn(s)coder.inline('never');y = 0;for i = 1:numel(s)    y = y + s(i).x(1);end Generate MEX code for this function by running:? codegen example?  The condition "if  subfcn(s1) == 42 " ?contains the last reference to s1 . This last reference ?occurs inside a call to the function subfcn . In the generated code,?s1  contains dynamic matrices that are heap allocated and need to be freed after the last use of s1 . (The reason for the prior call subfcn(s2)  is to make sure that the code generated for the function subfcn ? can handle arbitrary array sizes. The second call subfcn(s1) ?reuses this generated code.)  The generated code looks like: b_s1.data = &s1.data[0];b_s1.size = &s1.size[0];b_s1.allocatedSize = 100;b_s1.numDimensions = 2;b_s1.canFreeData = false;emxFree_struct_T_1x100(&s1); /* The free happens too early because s1 is indirectly used below via b_s1 */st.site = &d_emlrtRSI;if (subfcn(&st, &b_s1) == 42.0) { Calling the generated MEX function example_mex  might result in a MATLAB crash and/or an incorrect answer. For example, example_mex(10,10)?  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Create a new temporary variable that holds the last reference to the dynamic array instead of putting that reference directly in the condition of the if or while statement. For example, in the previous MATLAB function example, change:?if subfcn(s1) == 42?to:t = subfcn(s1);if t == 42
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Error loading Simulink operating point
Description: Starting simulation from a saved Simulink operating point, even without any changes to the model, might result in an error similar to the following: Simulink cannot load the initial operating point because the model, 'm1', was changed after the operating point was saved. Run?the simulation again and resave the operating point.

Summary: Project Upgrade fails for Simulink libraries with nested library blocks
Description: Project Upgrade fails if all  of these conditions are true:The project contains Simulink libraries with nested library blocks The nested library blocks names start with the same character  In such situations, the upgrade checks do not complete and MATLAB issues the following error:Invalid Simulink object name.
Workaround: Disable the library links, then run the Project Upgrade. Restore the library links once the upgrade is complete.

Summary: Iterations configured for Fast Restart mode run in Normal mode
Description: In the Test Manager, if the system under test in a test case is a test harness with iterations configured to run in Fast Restart simulation mode, the iterations instead run in Normal mode.

Summary: Arrays exchanged between MATLAB and OPC UA servers are written or read in the wrong order
Description: When reading an array from an OPC UA server node, the array in MATLAB is the correct size but the elements are in the wrong order. Similarly, when writing arrays to an OPC UA server node, the elements of the MATLAB array are written to the OPC UA server in the wrong order.? For example, the MATLAB array [11,12,13; 21,22,23]  is written to the server as [11,21,12; 22,13,23] .?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table block with linear interpolation and output word length less than or equal to table word length causes HDL mismatch
Description: When calculating the interval of two consecutive points in a Lookup Table block for linear interpolation, overflow might occur if the result cannot be represented by the output type set on the block. This can cause a numeric mismatch in the generated HDL code.
Workaround: Increase the output data type to be 1 bit larger in word length than the data type of the table data.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: ReqIF ID values might change between revisions when exporting to ReqIF
Description: When you make changes to a requirement set in Simulink Requirements that has already been exported to a ReqIF file and then export it again, some existing ReqIF ID values might change. For example, in the first export, the ReqIF?replicationID  might have some value "a", and, when changes are made to the requirement set and exported a second time, the  replicationID ?might change to some value "b".  This might happen for requirement sets that are created in or imported to Simulink Requirements.

Summary: Cannot start desktop MATLAB COM server when MATLAB is registered using comserver function
Description: If you register MATLAB using the comserver   function with one of these syntaxes: comserver('register', 'user', 'all')  comserver('register')?  then the following command does not start a desktop MATLAB:? actxserver('matlab.desktop.application') ; Instead, MATLAB opens in nondesktop mode with a warning message: Warning: Unrecognized command line option: Desktop.  You might get the same warning using this PROGID to invoke MATLAB desktop server from any application, such as Spreadsheet Link.?
Workaround: In a R2020a MATLAB with admin rights:?%clear registry entriescomserver('unregister','User','all')comserver('unregister');%register MATLABregmatlabserver

Summary: Starting MATLAB on Windows with the option -nodesktop can later crash
Description: If you start MATLAB on Windows with the -nodesktop  option, MATLAB crashes when you type:   >> clear session

Summary: Vector CAN FD channels and NI CAN or CAN FD channels might experience large memory consumption
Description: The following vendor channels might cause larger than expected memory consumption in MATLAB if messages are left unreceived in the channel, resulting in a possible slowdown in system performance: Vector CAN FD NI CAN FD NI CAN

Summary: MATLAB might crash when continuing from a breakpoint in a MATLAB function block
Description: When continuing Simulink simulationfrom a breakpoint in a MATLAB function block, MATLAB may crash if the SimulinkProfiler is enabled simultaneously. The first line of the generated stack trace will contain a reference to "sletrace::ExecNode::leave", for example:? [? 0] 0x00007ffaf3b3c820 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libmwsl_etrace.dll+00116768 sletrace::ExecNode::leave+00000000
Workaround: Ensure that the Simulink Profiler is disabled while using breakpoints in a MATLAB function block. Simulink will not enable the profiler unless the simulation is started from the Profile tab, but the profiler may also be enabled from the command line by setting the configset parameter "Profile" to "on." Ensure your model was not saved with "Profile" set to "on" using get_param:>> get_param('ModelName', 'Profile')If get_param returns 'on' set the parameter to off, and resave the model:>> set_param('ModelName', 'Profile', 'off')>> save_system('ModelName');?

Summary: Runtime errors indicating incompatible vector lengths when using the Combined Slip Wheel 2DOF block with vector inputs?
Description: IIf you configure the Combined Slip Wheel 2DOF block with vector inputs to model more than one wheel, simulation can result in runtime errors. If the sidewall compression of one wheel approaches zero (for example, during lift-off) when another wheel is still in compression, simulation runtime errors indicate incompatible vector lengths.??

Summary: Blockset Designer project takes a long time to load
Description: Blockset Designer project takes a long time to load during blockset project loading when there is a large number of blocks present in the blockset. Load time can be several minutes with 50 blocks.

Summary: Running a test file containing test cases with external test harnesses that contain a Signal Builder block might error
Description: If you run a test file that has test cases with different externally saved test?harnesses and there is a Signal Builder block in one of those harnesses, this error might occur: Unable to close the test harness '<harness name>' since it has unsaved changes.

Summary: Test result report that includes Signal Editor block data values produces an error
Description: When you create a Simulink Test report for a test result that uses a Signal Editor block and includes Signal Editor data values, this error occurs: Reference to non-existent field 'SignalBuilderGroup'

Summary: Comparing Simulink models that contain one or more Subsystem Reference blocks might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you compare two Simulink models that contain Subsystem Reference blocks, MATLAB might crash, leading to possible loss of unsaved data.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with Reset Function block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a Reset Function block. There is nonvirtual Outport block inside the Reset Function block. The?Initial output ?parameter of the nonvirtual Outport block is set to a nonzero value.

Summary: Analog Output Generator app displays incorrect error information in the MATLAB command window
Description: When an error occurs during signal generation in the Analog Output Generator app, instead of the error information appearing in the app, an unrelated stack trace appears in the MATLAB Command Window.
Workaround: To recover from this error state, do any of the following:Select a different device by clicking it in the Devices List.Refresh the device list by clicking Refresh in the Devices tab.Close and reopen the Analog Output Generator app.

Summary: Induction machine blocks display incorrect unsaturated characteristic
Description: Forthe Induction Machine Wound Rotor and Induction Machine Squirrel Cageblocks,?if you set the Parameterization unit  parameter to?SI and you select the?Electrical > Plot Open-Circuit Saturation ?option on the blocks context menu, the displayed unsaturated characteristic is incorrect.

Summary: Test For Model Component wizard errors when generating tests for models with configuration set references
Description: When you generate tests using the Test for Model Component wizard and the model to be tested uses configuration set references, this error occurs:  Parameter update is not supported for a configuration reference. Update parameter in the configuration set that it references.

Summary: MATLAB occasionally crashes when opening a library block attached to a Bus Selector block that contains out-of-date bus information
Description: MATLAB might crash when navigating through library blocks on the canvas. If the library block is connected to a Bus Selector block that outputs a non-virtual bus and the following is true:? The Simulink.BusObject contains bus information that does not match the signals selected in the Bus Selector block. The Bus Selector block is inside a library subsystem.  MATLAB crashes with a stack trace similar to: [? 0] 0x00007fff69b47579? ? ? ? ? ? C:\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\sl_types.dll+00357753 SLTypeSys::ImmediateElementAccess::getUnitId+00000041  [? 1] 0x00007fff69b41747? ? ? ? ? ? C:\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\sl_types.dll+00333639 DtGetDataTypeElementUnitId+00000039  [? 2] 0x00007fff74c4bf28? ? ? ?C:\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+04308776 DiscreteBlockExecWrapper::getAdaptorTerminate+00019768  [? 3] 0x00007fff7510f156? ? ? ?C:\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+09302358 SetupToQueryCompiledInfo+00146870  [? 4] 0x00007fff757a668d? ? ? ?C:\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+16213645 LineTracer::getNumPortsForConnTokenBlock+00000045  [? 5] 0x00007fff757a8b15? ? ? ?C:\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+16222997 LineTracer::walkAcrossBlockToNextLine+00000021
Workaround: To update the bus signal information, resave the library model containing the Bus Selector block.

Summary: The?yolov2ReorgLayer?of an deep network produces incorrect output on a GPU, if the layer has either?inputs with different height and width values or different stride values along the height and width dimensions.
Description: When you run an deep learning network with reorganization layer on a GPU, the reorganization layer added by using the yolov2ReorgLayer  function produces incorrect output if the size of the input to reorganization layer has different values for the height and width dimensions. stride value of the reorganization layer is different along the height and width dimensions.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model containing a?Data Store Memory block interacting with a reusable subsystem configured to minimize algebraic loop occurrences
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a subsystem.?  In the Subsystem block parameters dialog box,? the?Function packaging ?parameter is set to? Reusable function and the? Minimize algebraic loop occurrences ?is selected. The model contains a Data Store Memory block that interacts with the subsystem such that a D ata Store Read block connects to a subsystem input or the subsystem contains a Data Store Write block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing Data Store Memory block and a For Iterator Subsystem or a While Iterator Subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Data Store Memory block and a For Iterator Subsystem or a While Iterator Subsystem block. The read or the write operation to the Data Store Memory block occurs inside the For Iterator Subsystem or a While Iterator  Subsystem block.

Summary: Testing a component in a library when simulation mode is overridden to not use model settings might fail
Description: When these two conditions occur,?  The system under test in the test case is a test harness for a component in a library.? The SimulationMode override is set to something other than [Model Settings].   a test might fail and produce this error during during test execution:?? ?non-existent field 'functionInterfaceName'

Summary: resample?function does not support signals with?NaNs for uniform resampling
Description: Input with NaN  values throws this error for the resample  function with uniform sampling: Error using upfirdn>validateinput (line 101)The input signal X must be a double-precision vector.  For example, this code generates the error message: y = resample([1 NaN NaN 1],2,1)

Summary: Traceability Matrix does not render link icons correctly
Description: If the left artifact column of the traceability matrix contains too many rows to view all of the artifacts when first opened, then the traceability matrix does not render link icons correctly. For example, if only the first 25 rows are visible and the artifact corresponding to row 26 has a link, then when you scroll to the cell that contains the link in row 26, there is no icon. This affects link icons as well as the expand icons that appear when an artifact hierarchy is collapsed.

Summary: Errors in ADC Testbench and ADC DC Measurement blocks and inldnl function when using a data converter with impairments
Description: When using a data converter with impairments, ADC Testbench results in errors due to several issues:? 1. If the analog size of the transfer curve of the ADC with impairment is larger than the nominally specified dynamic range, ADC Testbench does not properly evaluate measurement results. This happens when the converter has a negative offset error impairment, among other cases.2. ADC Testbench does not eliminate samples derived from initial conditions. This causes the analysis to conclude that the most impaired transfer curves are nonmonotonic.3. The DC analysis results plot from ADC Testbench and ADC DC Measurement blocks shows points for missing start and end codes. The plot contains an INL point for the first code, even if no data has been collected for that code.4. The Bestfit plot from ADC Testbench and ADC DC Measurement blocks is not fitted for converters with missing codes. The plotted best fit line's x-values does not match the ones the y-values were generated from.5. The inldnl function does not account for bipolar converters with the maximum measured code equal to 2^NBits . This is common on custom bipolar converters that saturate at the same point for positive and negative codes.

Summary: An error occurs in the Simulink Test Manager while aggregating coverage data for a Subsystem Harness if the subsystem contains a call to an external MATLAB file
Description: The following error occurs during subsystem coverage aggregation if the subsystem contains a call to an external MATLAB file:?  >> Error using stm.internal.Coverage.getMetrics  Input argument #1 is an invalid cvdata object. CVDATA objects  become invalid when their associated models are closed or  modified  Error in stm.internal.Coverage.addAndReturnFile>aggregateSubsystem  Error in stm.internal.Coverage.addAndReturnFile

Summary: Failure to connect with IBM DOORS Next (DNG) when importing requirements
Description: When you use?File > Import  in the Requirements Editor, select DOORS Next Generation  as the Document Type , and enter the password, the password prompt displays again, and then nothing happens. This?might be?caused by an incompatibility in the server configuration.?When you log in using system browser, if the password prompt has the title "Jazz AUTHORIZATION SERVER" you will not be able to use the? "DOORS Next Generation" document type option in the Simulink Requirements Import dialog.

Summary: bandwidth function cannot be overloaded
Description: In R2019b and prior releases, you can overload the bandwidth  function by adding a new function with the same name (bandwidth.m ) on the path. In R2020a, MATLAB? calls the built-in version of bandwidth  even when an overloaded version is present on the path.

Summary: lp2lp, lp2hp, lp2bp, and lp2bs functions error out when given a complex-valued?filter prototype as input
Description: lp2lp ,?lp2hp ,?lp2bp , and?lp2bs ?functions?error out when given complex-valued transferfunctions or state-space matrices?as input.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initial value for block output inside reusable subsystem
Description: The initial value for block output in a reusable subsystem may be incorrectly computed under all these conditions:?The model configuration parameter?Buffer for reusable subsystems ?is selected. The reusable subsystem's outport is multi-rate (for example, a Mux block combines a Inf  sample time signal and a discrete sample time signal feeds into the subsystem's outport).

Summary: Passing ReadRowNames to detectImportOptions for spreadsheet files might cause an error
Description: When detecting import options for spreadsheet files, detectImportOptions  errors if both of the following conditions exist: The name-value pair ReadRowNames  is passed in as true . The spreadsheet file has not more than 2 non-empty columns.  For example: >> opts = detectImportOptions(filename,"ReadRowNames",true,"Range","A:B");Error using detectImportOptions (line 140)Invalid 'VariableNamesRange'. The column size must match the number of variables.
Workaround: Instead of passing ReadRowNames to detectImportOptions, you can workaround this issue by setting the RowNamesRange and SelectedVariableNames properties on the SpreadsheetImportOptions object returned by detectImportOptions:>> opts = detectImportOptions(filename, "Range", "A:B");>> opts.SelectedVariableNames = opts.SelectedVariableNames(2:end)>> opts.RowNamesRange = "A2";

Summary: Using Start simulation from steady state increases compilation time?for some models
Description: When the?Start simulation from steady state  check box in the Solver Configuration block is selected, models that include nested intermediate expressions compile slowly. These nested?intermediate expressions?can be caused by?the use of intermediates  sections or nested let  statements in the block source, or by variable elimination using linear equations.

Summary: Index exceeds array dimensions error thrown while running HDL implementation model generated from Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor
Description: Running the HDL implementation model generated by the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor can throw the following error: Index exceeds array dimensions   The error occurs in the MATLAB Function block HDL Subsystem/HDL Algorithm/Mode Selection/Generate Mode Vector  inside the HDL implementation model . This error might occur under the following conditions:  Non-scalar inputs are provided to the Simscape network. In this case, the error is thrown for array u . Non-scalar states are present in the Simscape network. In this case, the error is thrown for array x .
Workaround: Modify the line throwing the error message by reducing the second operand of the colon operator by one. For example, if the error is thrown on the following line:tmp1 = u(1:4);?Change it to:?tmp1 = u(1:3);Alternatively, when the error occurs due to non-scalar inputs, splitting them into multiple scalar inputs(one Simulink-PS Converter block per scalar input), and re-running the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor checks also resolves the issue.

Summary: dsp.SpectrumAnalyzer or Spectrum Analyzer block that uses the Filter bank spectrum estimation method can cause MATLAB crash
Description: dsp.SpectrumAnalyzer or Spectrum Analyzer block might crash MATLAB on Windows if all these conditions are true: Spectrum Estimation 'Method' is set to 'Filter bank' Frequency Span set to either 'Start and stop frequency' or 'Span and center frequency'? Input is complex

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code might be generated for a model containing a MATLAB Function block with similar expressions over struct type variables
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a MATLAB Function block The MATLAB Function block uses the same expression more than once with different operands. One or more of the operands to the expression has a?struct  data type.  In the attached example m2244678.slx, the MATLAB Function block contains two uses of the sqrt  expression with different input variables of struct  type: function var = bar(var)  ? coder.inline('never');  ? ?P11 = var;  ? ?var(1).f1(1,1) = (var(1).f2(1:1:1,1:1:1)) - (P11(1).f1(1,1));  ? ?var(1).f2(1,1) = floor(mod(sqrt(abs(var(1).f1(1:1:1,1:1:1))),sqrt(abs(P11(1).f1))));  end?   The code generator generates incorrect code containing invalid reuse of the return value of sqrt? in the function?m2244678_bar  in?m2244678.c : static void m2244678_bar(IA1_busObject *var)  {  ? ?real32_T tmp;  ? ?var->f1 = var->f2 - var->f1;  ? ?tmp = (real32_T)sqrt((real32_T)fabs(var->f1));  ? ?var->f2 = (real32_T)floor(m2244678_mod(tmp, tmp));  }   The expected correct code without the invalid reuse is: static void m2244678_bar(IA1_busObject *var)  {  ? real32_T P11_f1;  ? P11_f1 = var->f1;  ? var->f1 = var->f2 - var->f1;  ? var->f2 = (real32_T)floor(m2244678_mod((real32_T)sqrt((real32_T)fabs(var->f1)),(real32_T)sqrt((real32_T)fabs(P11_f1))));  } ?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 3, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Adding new IBIS-AMI parameters and clicking Refresh Init?may corrupt the code in the Custom User Code Area of Init function
Description: After adding custom IBIS-AMI parameters or changing the connectivity of parameters within a Pass-Through block in a Simulink? model, you need to click the Refresh Init  button to add or update the new parameter references in the Custom User Code area of the Initialize (Init) function. If you do not add code to the Custom User Code Area of Init function, this process works as expected. But if any previous code is present, the newly generated code is added to the bottom of the Custom User Code area instead of the top. This may override intended behavior of the previous custom code. Additionally, the Refresh Init process attempts to find previously generated stale code to comment out with user permission. Only generated code should be affected, but all references are currently commented out.

Summary: diric?returns incorrect output for some input values equal or close to multiples of 2*pi.?
Description: diric  returns incorrect output for some input values equal or close to multiples of 2*pi . The inaccuracy occurs for a wider range of input values than in previous versions due to a change of the tolerance value that prevents division by zero.?

Summary: Signal logging might cause issues with clock rate pipelining and delay balancing
Description: If signal logging is enabled in the original model for signals that are affected by clock rate pipelining and delay balancing, the optimizations might fail. Messages about failed delay balancing or an Oversampling factor  that is not sufficient might be generated during code generation.
Workaround: If these messages are generated, try disabling signal logging on the appropriate signals or for the entire model. Disabling signal logging in the Configuration Parameters dialog box does not work around the issue. Logging needs to be disabled on the signals themselves to where the logging symbol no longer appears.>> mdlsignals = find_system(bdroot,'FindAll','on','LookUnderMasks','all','FollowLinks','on','type','line','SegmentType','trunk');>> ph = get_param(mdlsignals,'SrcPortHandle');>> cellfun(@(x) set_param(x, 'datalogging', 'off'), ph);

Summary: JMAAB check?Clarification of connections between structural subsystems results in abnormal exit
Description: The Model Advisor check?Clarification of connections between structural subsystems ? (ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0171)? results in an abnormal exit when From block is connected to multiple ports in a model with the following message:   Abnormal exit: Non-scalar in Uniform output, at index 1, output 1. Set 'UniformOutput' to false.

Summary: Running R2015a test cases with mapped inputs in R2020a might fail in Test Manager
Description: If you map inputs to model elements, running a test case in R2015a might fail when run from R2020a using the Run Tests in Multiple Releases workflow. Simulink Test throws the following error: Undefined function 'join' for input arguments of type 'cell'

Summary: Column classifications are not honored when performing a single simulation of a model with a dose from a dataset
Description: In the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, the columns classified as "group" and "independent" are not honored when creating a dose from a specified group in a dataset. MATLAB throws an error when you try to run the simulation.?

Summary: Error about invalid amount units when trying to simulate a model using doses specified from an imported data set
Description: When you simulate a model using doses specified in an imported data set?in the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, the app does not use the units from the dose column in the data set. Consequently, when unit conversion is on, you get an error message about invalid (i.e., empty) amount units on the doses.?
Workaround: Add the "AmountUnits" property to the Parameter Set dose configuration table, and manually set the desired units in the program.?

Summary: bilinear function outputs only the real part of the transfer function coefficients
Description: bilinear  function should output complex-valued coefficients if the input continuous-time transfer function has complex-valued coefficients. For example:? [numd,dend] = bilinear([0 1],[1 1+1j],50); % numd and dend should be complex-valued

Summary: RoadRunner image assets locked after exporting
Description: When you export scenes containing RoadRunner Asset Library image assets to visual formats such as DXF, DAE, glTF, FBX, OSG, USD and OBJ, the image files remain locked and are unusable by third-party applications.

Summary: Live Editor and Help Browser can fail with MATLAB proxy settings
Description: If you set proxy information in MATLAB preferences, the Live Editor and Help Browser might load with a blank screen or generate the error ERR_TUNNEL_CONNECTION_FAILED .

Summary: XCP master blocks do not recognize EV_SESSION_TERMINATED event
Description: The XCP master blocks do not recognize the EV_SESSION_TERMINATED  event. If an XCP slave decides to autonomously disconnect from the master, the slave sends an EV_SESSION_TERMINATED  event. Currently, the XCP master blocks ignore EV_SESSION_TERMINATED  event and leave the connection open.?The status appears connected, but no new data appears on the DAQ lists.

Summary: sbiofit with scattersearch uses incorrect bounds for the response-specific combined error model
Description: sbiofit  uses incorrect bounds for the error model parameters if you specify 'scattersearch' ? as the estimation function and?'combined'  as response-specific error models. This can result in an error exception about invalid upper bounds for the estimation.?

Summary: Simulation results differ between Normal mode and code generation
Description: When all the following conditions exist, simulation results obtained with generated code may differ from results of Normal mode simulation: The model contains Simscape blocks with parameters designated runtime  or variables configured to override default initial values. The Simscape network is configured to use the local solver and a Simulink fixed-step solver is used for simulation.?? The code is generated with support for multitasking and tunable parameters.

Summary: Output argument?error when trying to simulate a Truth Table after upgrading
Description: If you save a model that contains a Truth Table and then try to run the model in a newer release, Simulink may throw an error similar to:  "Output argument is not assigned on some execution paths"
Workaround: In the release you are using, make a change to the model,then save the model.

Summary: Test execution compiles model multiple times
Description: If a test case has iterations and parameter overrides, then executing the test case compiles the model multiple times for each iteration.

Summary: Unable to install updates to?JSONLab: a toolbox to encode/decode JSON files?using Add-On Manager
Description: Add-ons with updates that are packaged differently than previous versions of the add-on cannot be installed using the Add-On Manager. For example, the add-on?JSONLab: a toolbox to encode/decode JSON files ?was originally created as a toolbox (.mltbx file). The latest version of this add-on is a collection (ZIP file). Therefore, users who installed a previous version of the add-on (when it was a toolbox) are unable to update the add-on in the Add-On Manager.

Summary: Mismatch between impulse response sample interval and simulation sample interval in custom channel impulse response results in inconsistency between the shape of and metrics derived from the Simulink and Statistical Analysis eye diagrams.
Description: When you are using a custom channel impulse response, if the impulse response sample interval does not match the simulation sample interval, it results in inconsistency between Simulink eye diagram and Statistical analysis eye diagram, including the eye shape, eye width metric and eye height metrics.?

Summary: Error when reading text files with 'Range' specified beyond 'XFD1048576'
Description: When using the readtable , readmatrix , readcell , readtimetable  or readvars  functions to read a text file by specifying a value for 'Range'  beyond 'XFD1048576' , the following error is encountered: >> m = readmatrix('filename.txt',"Range", "1:1048577");Error using readmatrix (line 149)Unable to determine range. Range must be a named range in the sheet or a single cell within 'XFD1048576'.>> m = readtable('filename.txt',"Range", "A:XFE");Error using readtable (line 198)Unable to determine range. Range must be a named range in the sheet or a single cell within 'XFD1048576'.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Simulation plots in the Simulink Test expected results are incorrect
Description: When multiple Simulink Test test cases generate simulation plots, the plot that is generated during the last executed test case is incorrectly inserted for all test cases in the results report.?

Summary: Error when calling TLC library function LibBlockInputSignalAllowScalarExpandedExpr
Description: When LibBlockInputSignalAllowScalarExpandedExpr  is invoked from a custom TLC file, Simulink code generation will fail with the following error message:Argument 4 to 'Block. SetInputAllowScalarExpandedExpr' must be a 'boolean'

Summary: Using?fcn2optimexpr?with the?ReuseEvaluation?flag can lead to errors
Description: This error relates to solving an optimization problem using the problem-based approach. When you convert a function to an optimization expression using?fcn2optimexpr ?with 'ReuseEvaluation',true , the?solve  function might issue an error. For example, this script has a function for?fcn2optimexpr ?at the end: x = optimvar('x',4);[f,c] = fcn2optimexpr(@computeall,x,'ReuseEvaluation',true,'OutputSize',[1 1]);obj = f + 20*(x(3) - x(4)^2)^2 + 5*(1 - x(4))^2;cons = c <= 0;prob = optimproblem('Objective',obj);prob.Constraints.cons = cons;x0.x = [-1;1;1;2];x0.x = x0.x/norm(x0.x);[sol,fval,exitflag,output] = solve(prob,x0);function [f1,c1] = computeall(x)c1 = norm(x)^2 - 1;f1 = 100*(x(2) - x(1)^2)^2 + (1 - x(1))^2;end Running the script leads to the following error:? Error using optim.problemdef.OptimizationProblem/solveIndex exceeds the number of array elements (0).Caused by:Failure in initial objective function evaluation. FMINCON cannot continue.
Workaround: Save the function passed as the first input to fcn2optimexpr as a named function rather than a nested, scoped, or anonymous function.For the example, save computeall.m as a separate file on the MATLAB path, and delete the computeall function definition in the script. Then the script runs without error.

Summary: Shared utility code folder error for modified Vehicle Dynamics Blockset and Powertrain Blockset example
Description: Simulating or updating the diagram of a Powertrain Blockset or Vehicle Dynamics Blockset example model might produce an unexpected error if you have edited a model in the model hierarchy. The error might look like: ? Error while bringing model references of model SiCiPtReferenceApplication up to date: Error????building model 'SiEngineController'.  The current model configuration differs from the model????configuration used to generate the shared utility code folder, ????'<myPath>\ slprj\ sim\ _sharedutils'.
Workaround: Delete the slprj folder and .slxc files, then try again.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Changed ports in an observer model do not highlight correctly in Manager Observer dialog box
Description: If you create a new observer model with a given port configuration, save the model, and then change the port wiring before closing the observer model, the port links do not appear to be updated in the Manage Observer dialog box. Although the observer connections are updated and appear correctly in the model, both the old and new connections are highlighted in the Manage Observer dialog box.

Summary: Dragging ports of a subsystem interface might cause lost connections in associated test harnesses
Description: For a subsystem with one or more test harnesses associated with it, reordering ports in the subsystem block by dragging and dropping them will disconnect the component under test within the test harnesses.

Summary: Simscape Variable Viewer and Statistics Viewer missing?from the Debug tab of the Simulink Toolstrip
Description: When a model uses a reference configuration set, the Simscape button is not shown in the Diagnostics  section of the Debug  tab in the Simulink Toolstrip. This makes it impossible to access the Statistics Viewer and the Variable Viewer.
Workaround: Use attachConfigSetCopy and setActiveConfigSet to?attach a copy of the referenced configuration set to the model and activate it.

Summary: nufft and nufftn can return incorrect results for sufficiently large input sizes when both the sample and query points are nonuniformly spaced
Description: For input data with at least 32 sample points, and where both the sample points and ?query points are nonuniformly spaced, nufft  and nufftn  can return incorrect results in the following cases: For nufft , the issue is present only for complex-valued input data.  For nufftn , the issue is present either for complex-valued input data or when both the sample and query points are specified using cell arrays.

Summary: Generated model after HDL code generation might fail to compile for Constant blocks with complex values
Description: If the Simulink model contains Constant blocks that have complex values, then the generated model after HDL code generation might fail to compile.

Summary: Recovered Stateflow Charts block inserted in Subsystem Reference test harness
Description: When creating test harnesses for Subsystem Reference models that contain Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function Blocks, you might see a Recovered Stateflow Charts block inserted after you reopen the harness. If you delete the corrupted charts manually and then simulate the test harness, MATLAB might crash.
Workaround: A corrupted test harness created before R2020a update 5 cannot be recovered automatically, even if you see a Recovered Stateflow Charts block in it. To replace the corrupted harness:Create a new test harness in release R2020aU5 or later.Copy any modifications from the previously corrupted harness to the new harness.?

Summary: Error when running or building the definition file generated with clibgen.generateLibraryDefinition for C++ libraries with certain template classes
Description: If you run or build the definition file generated with clibgen.generateLibraryDefinition  for C++ libraries with certain template classes, MATLAB displays an error. Template classes with template argument as a class with template argument as template type/non-type parameter results in an error. For example, if header contains a class type like the parameter type in function foo  or bar  below, the definition file will error. The parameter in function?foo ?is a template class Myclass<Myclass<T>>  with template argument Myclass<T>  which is a template class with template argument as template parameter T . template<typename T>class Myclass {};?  template <int N>class Myclass2{};?  template <typename T>void foo(Myclass<Myclass<T>> in){}  template <int N>void bar(Myclass<Myclass2<N>> in){}

Summary: Development Version of MATLAB Web App Server will be running at 100% CPU, timing out launching sessions when using "localhost" for server address
Description: When user launches Development version of MATLAB Web App Server via management UI home button, browser might be pointed to the "localhost" server address. This causes slower interactions with web app server, leading to eventual server performance degradation.
Workaround: Locate in the logs folder "webapps_service_start.out" file, and navigate to "Home Page" url printed there. Use this url to access server.

Summary: Test using?sltest.testmanager.run?on models with fast restart fail, but pass when using Test Manager
Description: If you have a model that is set to use fast restart and you run that test using sltest. testmanager. run ?, it might produce a?Simulation stopped before end time ? error, but if you run the same test in the Test Manager, it passes.

Summary: Drive Cycle Source block Output acceleration units?parameter not saved
Description: If you select the Drive Cycle Source block Output acceleration ?parameter, the model does not retain the Output acceleration units ?parameter when you save the model.
Workaround: You can either:?Re-enter the?Output acceleration units?parameter when you open the model.Add?set_param(blockname,'outAccUnit','m/s/s')?in the the model post-load function

Summary: h5disp incorrectly errors out on HDF5 files containing fixed-length UTF-8 encoded strings
Description: Attempting to display the contents of an HDF5 file containing fixed-length UTF-8 encoded strings results in an unexpected error in MATLAB. For example, the following code   h5disp('myHDF5FileWithFixedLenUTF8Strings.h5') returns this error: Error using h5infocUTF-8 encoding is only supported for variable length strings.Error in h5info (line 108)hinfo = h5infoc(filename,location, useUtf8);Error in h5disp>display_hdf5 (line 121)hinfo = h5info(options.Filename,options.Location);Error in h5disp (line 99)display_hdf5(options);

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code from a model with a C Caller block
Description: MATLAB might crash while generating code from a model that contains a C Caller block containing a struct with a field with no identifier. The following stack trace occurs with the crash: [ 0] 0x00007ff822a29ad3 D:\ relarchive\ R2020a\ executables\ GR\ win64\ bin\ win64\ cg_ir. dll+00367315 CG::AbstractTypeIterator<CG::TypeIteratorElement>::internalEnterField+00000067[ 1] 0x00007ff822a187dd D:\ relarchive\ R2020a\ executables\ GR\ win64\ bin\ win64\ cg_ir. dll+00296925 CG::AbstractTypeIterator<CG::TypeIteratorElement>::advanceAggregate+00000477[ 2] 0x00007ff8185ac0b9 D:\ relarchive\ R2020a\ executables\ GR\ win64\ bin\ win64\ cgir_cpp_emitter. dll+00311481 CG::domCpp::NewExpr::setBaseType+00033721[ 3] 0x00007ff8185a8f9a D:\ relarchive\ R2020a\ executables\ GR\ win64\ bin\ win64\ cgir_cpp_emitter. dll+00298906 CG::domCpp::NewExpr::setBaseType+00021146[ 4] 0x00007ff8185abcf9 D:\ relarchive\ R2020a\ executables\ GR\ win64\ bin\ win64\ cgir_cpp_emitter. dll+00310521 CG::domCpp::NewExpr::setBaseType+00032761[ 5] 0x00007ff8185ac853 D:\ relarchive\ R2020a\ executables\ GR\ win64\ bin\ win64\ cgir_cpp_emitter. dll+00313427 CG::domCpp::NewExpr::setBaseType+00035667[ 6] 0x00007ff8185ab551 D:\ relarchive\ R2020a\ executables\ GR\ win64\ bin\ win64\ cgir_cpp_emitter. dll+00308561 CG::domCpp::NewExpr::setBaseType+00030801

Summary: FIR Rate Conversion block might crash MATLAB
Description: The FIR Rate Conversion block might crash MATLAB during simulation, when both of these conditions are true: Rate options is set to "Allow multirate processing" The input is multichannel
Workaround: Reducing the frame size or the filter length might eliminate the crash.

Summary: Error with behavior specification options in Polyspace analysis in client-server mode
Description: When you using either of the following options with a Polyspace analysis in client-server mode: In R2019b: -function-behavior-specifications filePath   In R2020a: -code-behavior-specifications filePath    filePath   must be the same on the client and server side. For instance, the path can be a network path accessible both from client and server. Otherwise, an error like this results:     I/O error on BinIo.openIn (file=/path/to/behavior-specifications-file.xml). Exiting.    System error No such file or directory (noent: No such file or directory)    exception Io(Io) raised.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating or generating code for dataflow domains that contain virtual subsystems
Description: MATLAB might crash when simulating or generating code for dataflow domains that contain virtual subsystems, including masked subsystems. The generated stack trace contains lines similar to: [? 0] 0x00007ff9923de86c bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+07268460 slcomp::BlockShouldNotHavePriority+00003612  [? 1] 0x00007ff9923dee5e bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+07269982 slcomp::PartitionGraphsInModel+00000238  [? 2] 0x00007ff992a8e7b2 bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+14280626 slcomp::MoveBlocksIntoSimulinkFunction+00068658
Workaround: Expand virtual subsystems within the dataflow domains, or turn off dataflow domains.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation for deep learning network might produce incorrect answer if a dropoutLayer is followed by?an activation layer
Description: Code generation for deep learning networks might produce an incorrect answer if they satisfy both these conditions: A layer has at least two branches feeding to other layers. At least one branch has a dropoutLayer  or an nnet.onnx.layer.FlattenLayer  followed by an activation layer, such as reluLayer , leakyReluLayer , clippedReluLayer  or a batchNormalizationLayer.   ?An example pattern is:? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? convolution2dLayer? ? ? ? ? ? ?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ?___________| __________?? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? |? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? |???convolution2dLayer? ? ?dropoutLayer ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? |?? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? |?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?reluLayer ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? |?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? |?

Summary: Displayed column widths in Requirements Editor might be reset
Description: Displayed column widths in Requirements Editor might be shrunk to default width suddenly. This issue can occur when you navigate to a requirement or link from an other editor, or switch between Requirements and Links view. The issue also occurs when Implementation or Verification Status is displayed from the menu action on the Requirements Editor.

Summary: Experiment Manager gives unreproducible results if setup function calls rng
Description: If you try to set the random number generator by calling rng  in your experiment setup function, then Experiment Manager ignores the specified random number generator setting for the first trial of the experiment. In contrast, subsequent trials use the random number generator setting specified by the setup function and produce the expected results. This means you see different?"Training Accuracy"?or?"Training RMSE" ?results for the first trial each time you rerun the experiment, but they should produce the same results if you set?rng .

Summary: applyToModel method on SimulationInput object may error out for certain model parameters
Description: Calling applyToModel  method on a Simulink.SimulationInput? object which contains certain model parameters may cause an error. For example, DataTypeOverride , errors out when the model is using a configuration reference?with the following error message: Property 'DataTypeOverride' does not exist?

Summary: Join Tables live task does not update the output table when the + or - buttons are clicked
Description: In the Join Tables live editor task, when you click the +  or -  buttons to add or remove a "merging variable" pair, the script that produces the output table is not updated. As a result, the output table does not take the new selections for "merging variable" into account.
Workaround: Toggle any other control within the live task to update the generated script. For example, you can change join types and then change back to your selection to get the correct script and output.

Summary: Sporadic MATLAB crash while training a reinforcement learning agent in parallel with ten or more workers
Description: MATLAB might crash while attempting to train a reinforcement learning agent in parallel with ten or more workers. The crash is due to a communication race condition between the client and worker processes. The report for this crash contains a line similar to the following: [104] 0x00007ff95d837348 mexFunction at b:\matlab\toolbox\parallel\src\mex\distcomp\distcompserialize64.cpp:28
Workaround: To bypass the communication race condition for PG, DQN, DDPG, TD3, and PPO agents, use synchronous parallel training and configure the workers to wait until the end of the episode before sending data to the host. To do so, configure your?rlTrainingOptions?object as shown in the following code:? ?trainOptions = rlTrainingOptions;trainOptions.UseParallel = true;trainOptions.ParallelizationOptions.Mode = "sync";trainOptions.ParallelizationOptions.StepsUntilDataIsSent = -1;Using StepsUntilDataIsSent = -1?is not supported for AC agents. To avoid a communication race condition for these agents, consider using a PPO agent with experience-based parallel training or a PG agent with gradient-based parallel training.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing logged signals from Stateflow charts when Simulink.fileGenControl sets the CacheFolder to a directory other than pwd
Description: Signals inside Stateflow charts that are inside a model block (set to Accelerator mode simulation) can be logged using the Data Import/Export  pane. When Simulink.fileGenControl  is set to generate code and cache files in a directory other than pwd  using the following command:? Simulink.fileGenControl('set','CacheFolder','C:\otherDirectory')?  Such signals may not appear in the final logged data.?
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant sample time output signal in referenced model might lead to incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated if all these conditions are true:Signals, states and internal data is default mapped to a built-in storage class. Model contains Model reference block. Referenced model has both constant and non-constant sample time output signals.

Summary: The Simulink model comparison tool produces an error on Windows for models containing Schedule Editor information
Description: On Windows, for models containing Schedule Editor information, the comparison tool produces an error.

Summary: importFromBaseWorkspace?does not import Simulink.Breakpoint objects to a data dictionary
Description: The importFromBaseWorkspace  function does not import variables that are Simulink.Breakpoint  objects to a data dictionary. In this case, Simulink does not report an error.

Summary: Model Advisor check Data type selection for index signals?produces an error
Description: The Model Advisor check Data type selection for index signals ?(ID: mathworks.hism.hisl_0022 ) produces an error with the following message for Stateflow models that have Japanese comments: Index exceeds the number of array elements (0)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Precision error in double-typed initial values in RAM blocks inferred during HDL code generation from persistent variables in MATLAB function block
Description: When the initial values are in double precision, more specifically, for values such as pi, 1/3, 0.1, only ?15 digits are preserved by default during HDL code generation. The initial value is therefore not bit-accurate and has lower precision than the original input value, which results in a numeric mismatch.

Summary: Cannot load app with grid that has row height or column width of zero
Description: If you create an app with a grid layout and set any row or column to have a size of zero, then you will not be able to load the app.

Summary: Incorrect error message for vector quantity in the Pipe (IL) block
Description: In the Pipe (IL) block, when the Initial liquid pressure ?parameter is a vector, the block may incorrectly return the following message:  "Invalid type for predicate expression. Predicate expressions must be scalar types."
Workaround: Replace the vector quantity with a series of pipes with scalar values. For the n number of elements in the?Initial liquid pressure parameter,?connect n Pipe (IL) blocks in series with the associated scalar value of Initial liquid pressure. Adjust the following parameters in each of the Pipe (IL) blocks for consistency:Divide Pipe length?by n.If the?Elevation gain specification?is?Constant, divide the?Elevation gain from port A to port B?by n.if the?Elevation gain specification?is?Variable,?divide the PS input signal EL?by?n.If the?Viscous friction parameterization?is set to?Nominal pressure drop vs. nominal mass flow rate, divide?Nominal pressure drop?by?n.If the?Viscous friction parameterization?is?set to?Haaland correlation?and the?Local resistances specification?is?set to?Aggregate equivalent length, divide the?Aggregate equivalent length of local resistances?by n.If the?Viscous friction parameterization?is?set to?Haaland correlation?and the?Local resistances specification?is?set to?Local loss coefficient, divide the?Total local loss coefficient?by n.

Summary: Image Labeler session returns an error after importing? a modified  groundTruth object.
Description: The Image Labeler  app returns an error after importing? a groundTruth  object which was previously modified. After using the Image Labeler  app to remove a labeled image, the app exports the  groundTruth  with LabelData  as structs rather than double arrays.? Importing this groundTruth  object into an?Image Labeler  in a later session returns the error: Error using vision.internal.imageLabeler.tool.Session/addLabelData (line 308)  Unrecognized table variable name 'Hierarchy'.  Warning: Error occurred while evaluating listener callback.?

Summary: Single scan operations using multiple counter input channels return data only for the final channel
Description: Single scan operations containing multiple counter input channels return incorrect data (0 ) for all counters except for the channel with the highest index. For example, the following code with two EdgeCount  measurement channels, returns data from only only the higher: s = daq.createSession("ni");ch0 = addAnalogInputChannel(s, "Dev1", "ai0", "Voltage");ch2 = addCounterInputChannel(s, "Dev1", "ctr2", "EdgeCount");ch3 = addCounterInputChannel(s, "Dev1", "ctr3", "EdgeCount");inputSingleScan(s)ans =   -0.1495         0  125.0000 In this case, the EdgeCount  value for the channel ctr2  returns a false value of 0 .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unexpected result or error when using a tabulated data parameterization of the Pressure Relief Valve
Description: In the Pressure Relief Valve block, when the Opening area parameterization  is set to Tabulated data - Area vs. pressure , and the pressure drop between port A  and port B  exceeds the last element of the?Pressure differential vector , one of the following may occur: The orifice area may incorrectly change to an unexpected value. The block may erroneously generate the following message: "Nonlinear solver: failed to converge, ?residual norm too large."
Workaround: To avoid these errors, either:Replace the Pressure Relief Valve with a Pressure Compensator block.Set the?Opening area parameterization?to?Tabulated data - Area vs. pressure.Connect the pressure sensor port X to port B. Connect the sensor port Y to port A.Reverse the order of the elements in the?Opening area vector. For example, if the existing vector is [0.25, 0.8, 1.3], adjust it to [1.3, 0.8, 0.25]. This can be achieved by setting it to?fliplr([0.25, 0.8, 1.3]).Reverse?the order of the elements in the Pressure differential vector and flip the element signs. For example, if the existing vector is [-10, 5, 13], adjust it to [-13, -5, 10]. This can be achieved by setting it to?-fliplr([-10, 5, 13]).All other parameters and port connections should match the original Pressure Relief Valve block settings.?In the Pressure Relief Valve block, use a linear parameterization by setting the?Opening area parameterization?to Linear area-pressure relationship.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: Signal Editor prematurely indicates that a save operation is complete
Description: When the Signal Editor is saving data, the indicator that the save is occurring does not appear.? You might notice a delay when saving large data files.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for deep learning networks using TensorRT might produce incorrect answer if the design file calls?predict and activations for?the same network
Description: Code generated for deep learning networks using TensorRT library might produce an incorrect answer if all of the following conditions are met: Design file contains calls to?predict  and activations? for the same network. The size of the input passed to?predict  and activations  is the same .   ?An example design file is as follows:? function [out1, out2] = predict_activations(in)  %#codegen  persistent mynet;  if isempty(mynet)?  ?mynet = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('network.mat');  end  out1 = predict(mynet, in);  out2 = activations(mynet, in, 'layername');  end?
Workaround: Replace the call to the?predict?function with the?activations function by specifying the output layer name of the network.For example, if the name of the output layer is 'classification_output', then the example design file can be rewritten as:?function [out1, out2] = predict_activations(in)%#codegenpersistent mynet;if isempty(mynet)??mynet = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('network.mat');end% Replace 'predict(mynet,in)' with an activation call?out1 = activations(mynet, in, 'classification_output');out2 = activations(mynet, in, 'layername');end?

Summary: Simscape model compilation fails when using legacy random number generator
Description: When you are using a legacy random number generator (for which RandStream.getGlobalStream.Type  returns 'legacy' ), running or compiling a Simscape model results in the following error:   Conversion to double from cell is not possible.
Workaround: Upgrade your code to use a nonlegacy random number generator, as described in https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/math/updating-your-random-number-generator-syntax.html, then recompile the Simscape model.

Summary: Setting gain on graphicEQ object after closing parameterTuner returns an error
Description: The following steps return an error:? Create a graphicEQ  object: h = graphicEQ ; Open parameterTuner  on the object:? parameterTuner(h)  Close the parameterTuner  UI. Set graphicEQ  gain property programmatically: h.Gains(1) = 1 ;  The error message looks similar to:     Invalid or deleted object.    Error in graphicEQ/createParameterTunerWidgets (line 763)                        f.Position(3) = 80 * 11;    Error in graphicEQ/updatePlot (line 717)                        createParameterTunerWidgets(obj);    Error in graphicEQ/set.pGains (line 361)                updatePlot(obj);    Error in graphicEQ/set.Gains (line 353)                obj.pGains = gainVec;
Workaround: To avoid this issue, set graphicEQ gains either programmatically or with the parameterTuner user interface. Avoid using both with the same object.To continue working after encountering the issue, reopen the parameterTuner UI by calling parameterTuner on your graphicEQ object and keep it open. While the parameterTuner UI is open, you can set gain values programmatically or using the UI.

Summary: Model Advisor check Check usage of Merge block flags Initialize Function block
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check usage of Merge block ?(ID: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0659  and mathworks.hism.hisl_0015 ) wrongly flags the usage of Initialize Function blocks with Merge blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Index outputs of union, setdiff, and setxor might refer to the last instances of repeated elements instead of the first instances
Description: The generated code for setxor  and union  might return the indices corresponding to the last occurrence of repeated elements in its second and third outputs rather than the first occurrences. For example, with?[y,ia,ib] = x_union([2,2],1) ,  we should have?ia = 1 , but code generation returns?ia = 2 .?  Similarly, the generated code for setdiff ?might return the last occurrence of repeated elements in its second output rather than the first occurrence. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when System Composer functions have invalid or missing stereotypes as input
Description: When creating a System Composer architecture model, if the following functions are called with an invalid or missing stereotype, it can cause the model to get corrupted and might lead to a MATLAB crash: addComponent  addPort  connect   When the corrupted model is saved or loaded and edited, the Simulink Diagnostic Viewer might display an error that indicates the System Composer model is inconsistent, and then result in a MATLAB crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Streaming and sharing of resources that are clock-rate pipelined can cause HDL mismatch
Description: If streaming and sharing are both requested when Clock Rate Pipelining  is enabled, the generated HDL code might have a numeric mismatch when compared to the original model. The mismatch occurs because the scheduling logic inside the clock-rate pipelined region does not correctly handle resources participating in multiple optimizations that require scheduling such as streaming and sharing. This can happen if streaming is requested on a subsystem which contains another subsystem requesting sharing.
Workaround: Remove the sharing request from the lower subsystem, or avoid using Clock Rate Pipelining when requesting the streaming and sharing optimizations on the same resources.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Simulink crashes while saving a model containing a Subsystem Reference block
Description: Simulink may crash while saving, for a model containing a Subsystem Reference  block with the following stack:[ 0]slPrmValue::isStringPrmValue at b:\ matlab\ src\ simulinkblock\ blk_obj\ slprmvalue. cpp:47?[ 1]SLRootBlock::getGrParamUValue at b:\ matlab\ src\ simulinkblock\ blk_core\ slrootblock. cpp:982?[ 2]slsr::util::referenceModelParamValueIsEmptyOrDefault at b:\ matlab\ src\ sl_lang_blocks\ subsystem_reference\ subsystemreferencehelper. cpp:55 [ 3]slsr::util::getSRComponentBD at b:\ matlab\ src\ sl_lang_blocks\ subsystem_reference\ subsystemreferencehelper. cpp:372[ 4]Simulink::CornerLineDrawer::paint at b:\ matlab\ toolbox\ simulink\ src\ sl_editor\ glyph\ slsubsystemreferencenodedetails. cpp:60?

Summary: S-Function Builder may fail to build when specifying non-ASCII include paths
Description: S-Function Builder wrote C and C++ wrapper files in UTF-8 encoding instead of the Simulink system encoding. When using non-ASCII characters in the source file, compilation might fail with an error similar to: test_wrapper.cC:\temp\test_wrapper.c: warning C4819: The file contains a character that cannot be represented in the current code page (932). Save the file in Unicode format to prevent data lossC:\temp\test_wrapper.c(17): fatal error C1083: Cannot open include file: '.\ĄĆĄąĄČ\testheader.h': No such file or directory

Summary: Poor alignment between slip and master roads where banking is applied
Description: When a slip road and its corresponding master road both have banking applied to their cross sections, the boundary at which these two roads meet might have elevation differences and be misaligned.?

Summary: Simulation 3D Scene Configuration block only loads scenes in the Unreal Engine??project?/Game/Maps folder
Description: In the Simulation 3D Scene Configuration block, if you set Scene source  to Unreal Executable ,? the block only loads scenes that are in the?Unreal Engine??project?/Game/Maps ?folder. If you set Scene  to a scene path that is not in /Game/Maps , the block does not load the scene.

Summary: Error in Code view after running SIL/PIL simulation
Description: The Code view displays the error message "index exceed number of array elements" after you generate the code for a model and run a software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation with the verification configuration parameter Measure task execution time ?selected.

Summary: Model Advisor check Model object names error on clicking "What's this?"
Description: The Model Advisor check Model object names  (ID: mathworks.hism.hisl_0032 ) errors out on clicking "What's this?" with the following error: Error using helpUtils.csh.helpviewMLFactory/checkForMapFile (line 38)Specified topic id (mathworks.do178b.hisl_0032) does not exist in map file Error in helpview (line 213)??? help_path = factory.checkForMapFile(mapfilename, topic_id);Error in ModelAdvisor.Node.opencsh

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for multiple persistent variable initializations across multiple if(isempty(p)) regions might be incorrect
Description: Suppose that your MATLAB code contains multiple instances of this code pattern: persistent p;?  x = <constant_value>; if(isempty(p))? ? ?p = ... x ...end?  If all of the following conditions are true, the code generator might produce incorrect code for your MATLAB code: Two instances of this code pattern (that declare and initialize two different persistent variables) use the same variable x .  The values that? x ?is assigned to are constant values  The assignment of the constant values to x happens outside the? if(isempty(p)) ?regions.  The value of  x ?changes between the? if(isempty(p)) ?regions.   Taking these conditions into account, the MATLAB code pattern that might produce incorrect code is: persistent p1, p2;x?= <constant_value>;??...?if(isempty(p))? p1 = ... x ...end ?  x = <different_constant_value>;?...if(isempty(p))??p2 = ... x ...end  This bug might not affect all situations that use this code pattern. One situation in which the bug might occur is when x is assigned to a structure constant.? Here is an example MATLAB function that causes this bug to occur in the generated code. Although this example uses coder.ceval, this bug might also occur in code that does not use coder.ceval. function out = foo()  persistent p;persistent p2;  X = coder.const(struct('prop', 1));if(~coder.target('MATLAB'))?? ? coder.ceval('externalFcn', X);end  if(isempty(p))?? ? p = X;end  X = coder.const(struct('prop', 2));if(~coder.target('MATLAB'))?? ? coder.ceval('externalFcn', X);end?  if(isempty(p2))? ???p2 = X;end  out = p.prop + p2.prop;end   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Workaround: Directly assign the persistent variables to the constant values inside the if(isempty(p)) region. That is, change the code pattern in the previous section to:persistent p1, p2;...? if(isempty(p))  ? ?p1 = ... <constant_value> ...end...? if(isempty(p))  ? ?p2 = ... <different_constant_value>...end

Summary: readtable might misclassify char data as metadata
Description: In text files where the datatype of all columns is char , the presence of a numeric data entry causes detectImportOptions  to classify all previous rows as metadata. As a result, calling readtable  on the text file with default import options skips the initial rows of data. For example:     >> students = ["Sally"; "Brian"; "Andrew"; "Ashley"; "Norman"; "Ellie"; "Linda"];    >> grades = ["A-"; "B"; "91"; "C+"; "87"; "A+"; "B-"];    >> gradesTable = table(students, grades);    >> writetable(gradesTable, "studentGrades.csv");     >> readtable("studentGrades.csv")    ans =      5ˇÁ2 table         students     grades        __________    ______        {'Andrew'}    {'91'}        {'Ashley'}    {'C+'}        {'Norman'}    {'87'}        {'Ellie' }    {'A+'}        {'Linda' }    {'B-'} When you pass the file to detectImportOptions , the DataLines  property of the ImportOptions  object excludes the first two lines of data:     >> ops = detectImportOptions("studentGrades.csv");    >> ops.DataLines    ans =         4   Inf
Workaround: To work around this issue, compute the ImportOptions object using detectImportOptions and set the DataLines property to the desired data range.?For example:    >> ops = detectImportOptions("studentGrades.csv");    >> ops.DataLines = [2 Inf];    >> readtable("studentGrades.csv", ops)    ans =      7ˇÁ2 table         students     grades        __________    ______        {'Sally' }    {'A-'}        {'Brian' }    {'B' }        {'Andrew'}    {'91'}        {'Ashley'}    {'C+'}        {'Norman'}    {'87'}        {'Ellie' }    {'A+'}        {'Linda' }    {'B-'}

Summary: The Pressure Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve may default to an incorrect value for the maximum orifice area.
Description: In the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve block, when?Orifice parameterization ?is set to By orifice area vs. orifice opening table , and the sum of the signal at port C  and the Orifice initial opening  ?exceeds a certain threshold, the orifice area may equal a value that is unrelated to the values in?Tabulated orifice area .   In R2018b and earlier, this threshold is the value of the?Orifice maximum opening , which is visible when the?Orifice parameterization  is By maximum opening and area .?In R2019a and later, this threshold is 0.005 m, the default value of?Orifice maximum opening .
Workaround: To avoid these errors, either:Replace the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve with a Variable Orifice block and Pressure Relief Valve block.Connect the Variable Orifice S port to the node that was previously connected to the original block C port.Connect the Variable Orifice port A and Pressure Relief Valve A to the node that was previously connected to the original block A port.Connect the Variable Orifice port B to the node that was previously connected to the original block B port.Connected the Pressure Relief Valve B to the node that was previously connected to the original block R port.Set the Variable Orifice?Model parameterization?to?By area vs. opening table.Leave the Variable Orifice Orifice orientation?as?Opens in positive direction.Set all other Variable Orifice parameters to match the parameters on the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve?block Variable orifice tab.Leave the Pressure Relief Valve Opening area parameterization??as Linear area-pressure relationship.?Set all other?Pressure Relief Valve parameters to match the parameters on the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve?block Pressure compensator tab.?In the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve block, use a linear parameterization by setting the?Orifice parameterization?to?By maximum opening and area.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB crashes when dragging ports on?an Enabled Synchronous Subsystem block
Description: Dragging a port on an Enabled Synchronous Subsystem block with a mouse causes MATLAB to crash.Snippet from the MATLAB crash:  [??0] 0x00007ff9a135e410???C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ mf0. dll+00451600 mf::zero::ModelElement::Impl::isProxy+00000000  [??1] 0x00007ff9a11fac01 C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ mm_interface. dll+00568321 sl::mfzero::interfaceModel::Port::Impl::setIndexOne+00000049  [??2] 0x00007ff9866ef822 C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_lang_blocks. dll+07796770 PortInterface::CopyHandler::willUseCopyInfoForAdd+00088242  [??3] 0x00007ff98671a71c C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_lang_blocks. dll+07972636 sl::interfaceModel::modify::transferMastershipTo+00142156  [??4] 0x00007ff9866f78bf C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_lang_blocks. dll+07829695 sl::interfaceModel::modify::renumberPort+00000063  [??5] 0x00007ff987f34e92 C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ libmwsimulink. dll+11554450 SLGlue::renumberPort+00000082  [??6] 0x00007ff9775dec13 C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_editor. dll+08186899 Simulink::FlexiblePortPlacement::doesSimulinkSupportFlexiblePortPlacement+00206307  [??7] 0x00007ff9775c11ad C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_editor. dll+08065453 Simulink::FlexiblePortPlacement::doesSimulinkSupportFlexiblePortPlacement+00084861

Summary: Audio Test Bench hangs if MATLAB Debugger is stopped during a test bench simulation.
Description: Audio Test Bench enters an unusable state when the following actions occur:? You add a break point in the source code of the Object Under Test used in Audio Test Bench. You start a simulation in Audio Test Bench.? The Audio Test Bench begins initializing, and the break point is hit in MATLAB Editor / Debugger.? If you select "Quit Debugging", Audio Test Bench will get stuck in an initializing state.? All buttons in Audio Test Bench are disabled in this state, including the ability to close the app.  The only way to recover the app when these actions occur is to restart your MATLAB session.
Workaround: Do not stop debugging in MATLAB debugger while Audio Test Bench is running. Instead, stop the test bench simulation.

Summary: Simulating models with variant blocks might crash or generate different (but equivalent) preprocessor conditions
Description: When variant blocks are used in a Simulink model, one of the following cases might occur: Model might crash during simulation or code generation on certain platforms such as Mac. Code generation of the model might generate different (but equivalent) preprocessor conditions across multiple runs and on different platforms.  For example, code generation might generate different (but equivalent) conditions on Linux and Windows platforms as shown below: Linux:  '(NN == 1 && O == 1) || (NN == 1 && P == 1) || (NN == 2 && O == 1) || (NN == 2 && PP == 1)'     Windows:  '(NN == 1 && O == 1 && P ~= 1) || (NN == 1 && P == 1) || (NN == 2 && O == 1 && PP ~= 1) || (NN == 2 && PP == 1)'

Summary: Signal Analyzer copy display functionality does not work
Description: Users of the Signal Analyzer  app are not able to copy one or more displays to the clipboard and paste them into another application.

Summary: Cannot override logging for data store defined in data dictionary using Test Manager.
Description: When you create a logged signal set in the Test Manager Simulation Outputs section, a data store defined in?data dictionary does not appear in the signal selection dialog,

Summary: diary?function in a MATLAB Production Server deployable archive?does not log text to file.
Description: Executing a MATLAB Production Server deployable archive (CTF file) that contains the?diary ? function creates a log file but does not write to the file.

Summary: Simulate with Embedded Types button remains disabled in the Fixed-Point Tool after applying data types
Description: When using the Iterative Fixed-Point Conversion  workflow in the Fixed-Point Tool, if a subset of data type proposals are deselected in the Accept ?column of the Results  spreadsheet and then Apply Data Types ?is selected, then the Simulate with Embedded Types ?button remains disabled.

Summary: Operations are performed on the wrong columns when editing timetables with non-ASCII variable names in the Variables editor
Description: In the Variables editor, if you try to perform an operation on one or more selected columns in a timetable with non-ASCII variable names, the Variables editor might perform these operations on the wrong columns. Examples of operations include deleting or sorting columns using the context menu or toolstrip, or plotting using the?Plots? tab.
Workaround: To workaround the problem, you can either:Change the variable names to be ASCII variable names.Perform the operations in the Command Window using the appropriate numeric indexing.?

Summary: Traceability link from Requirement to Simulink Test Case appears unresolved
Description: A traceability link from a requirement to a Simulink Test Case may appear as unresolved (yellow triangle warning icon) in the Requirements Editor even if the linked Test Case is opened in the Simulink Test Manager. This may?happen immediately after creating a link. The warning icon appears in the Requirements Editor in the?Label? column or in the right pane under?Properties  when, in the?View ?drop-down menu,?Links ?is selected. Navigation to the Test Case from the Requirements Editor might work if the .mldatx? file is on MATLAB path, but does not work if the .mldatx  file is not on MATLAB path, even if the Test File is already loaded in the Simulink Test Manager.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when using Boolean vector or matrix with C Function block
Description: Using a Boolean vector or matrix with a C Function block and indexing into it could cause MATLAB to crash when you simulate your model.? You may see a message like the following: CGIR assertion 'In moveToParentAndMapScriptInputs: Assertion failed: isCGTypeMatched(matrixBase,  pointerBase)' failed in 'matlab\ toolbox\ shared\ slcc\ src\ slcc_blocks\ src\ cfunctionblockrtwcg. cpp'[??0] 0x00007ffdb80ca932 libmwslcc_blocks. dll+00567602 SLCC::SymbolLocation::setLength+00354386?

Summary: The h5writeatt function errors when setting the parameter TextEncoding to 'system'
Description: Using the HDF5 high level function h5writeatt  to write an attribute to the H5 file with the parameter TextEncoding  set to 'system' , the function fails with an error.? For example: h5create('myfile.h5','/g1/g2/myDataset',[100 200 300]);date = datestr(now);h5writeatt('myfile.h5','/','creation_date', 'junk', 'TextEncoding', 'system'); Unrecognized function or variable 'attvalue_size'.Error in h5writeatt>createDatatypeId (line 186)        elseif attvalue_size > 0Error in h5writeatt (line 86)datatypeId  = createDatatypeId(Attvalue, useUtf8);

Summary: emd function throws unexpected error and gaussdesign?function throws incorrect error
Description: The emd  function throws an error when the maximum number of intrinsic mode functions specified with 'MaxNumIMF'  is reached and 'Display'  is set to true . The error can also occur if 'MaxNumIMF'  is not specified and emd  uses the default maximum value. The error message is:Incorrect number of parameters supplied for 'shared_signalwavelet:emd:general:MaxNumIMFHit'.?Expected 0 but found 1.   The gaussdesign  function throws an incorrect error when the filter order is odd. The filter order is specified as the product of the?number of symbols and the number of samples per symbol period. The error message is: Incorrect number of parameters supplied for 'signal:rcosdesign:OddFilterOrder'. Expected 0 but?found 2.

Summary: Multi-testbench signal group time range check may cause multi-testbench code generation workflow to error
Description: Generating multi-testbench code from a model throws a conformance check error if: ????? * the model contains one or more Signal Builder blocks, and ????? * the Signal Builder blocks create signal groups with different time ranges
