Description: Suppose your model has a subsystem that contains a Stateflow chart and function profiling is enabled through the?Measure function execution times  configuration parameter. When you generate code through a right-click subsystem build, you might see an error. For example:   Error at element 4 of first input to get_param:Invalid Simulink object name: <SID>

Summary: ARXML import for AUTOSAR adaptive software component validates existing enumerations against header file requirements for AUTOSAR Classic Platform instead of AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform
Description: When importing ARXML for an AUTOSAR adaptive software component, imported enumeration data types could match those that already exist in the model's scope (base workspace) and can be reused. These existing enumerated data types are validated against the header file name requirements for the AUTOSAR Classic Platform ('Rte_Type.h') instead of the requirements for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform ('impl_type_<symbol>.h'). A false error similar to the following is thrown:? Error using arxml.importer/createComponentAsModel     You are defining enumerated type ENUMNAME.    However, a corresponding enumeration class already exists. The header file    for the class is not set to Rte_Type.h. Either implement the static method    getHeaderFile and configure it to return Rte_Type.h, or remove the class from the path.?
Workaround: Removeexisting enumerations from the path and workspace to avoid this error.

Summary: Simulink Diagram and ElementDiagram reporters error for models with custom background color
Description: When generating a snapshot of a model that has a custom background color, t he slreportgen.report.Diagram  and slreportgen.report.ElementDiagram  reporters throw an error with the following message: Error using double Conversion to double from cell is not possible.?
Workaround: Before using the Diagram or ElementDiagram reporters, change the background color of the model to a named color ('white', 'red', etc.)mdl = "vdp"; open_system(mdl);? set_param(mdl, "ScreenColor", "[0.5, 0.25, 0.25]") rpt = slreportgen.report.Report("output"); diag = slreportgen.report.Diagram(mdl);oldColor = get_param(mdl,"ScreenColor");set_param(mdl, "ScreenColor","white"); add(rpt, diag); set_param(mdl, "ScreenColor",oldColor); close(rpt); rptview(rpt)

Summary: Extract Equations step in Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor does not terminate when Stop time of a model is Inf
Description: The Extract Equations  step of the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor simulates the original model is extract the differential algebraic equations. If the Stop time  of the model is set to Inf , the model simulation doesn't terminate and the Extract Equations  step doesn't get completed. Change the Stop time  of the model to capture the relevant simulation behavior in a short amount of time.

Summary: Launching an analysis from MATLAB generates the error: Product required for 'pslinkrunImpl' not installed
Description: Launching a Polyspace analysis from MATLAB on a model reference with a configuration set reference with a command like this:   pslinkrun(modelName,? psCfg,? true); generates the following error: Error using pslinkrunImplProduct required for 'pslinkrunImpl' not installedError in pslinkrun (line 34)[resultDir,? sysResultDir] = pslinkrunImpl(varargin{:});Error in myfunc (line 90)    [~,? resDir] = pslinkrun(modelName,? psCfg,? true);
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using?polarscatter?or polarplot with markers
Description: When using the?polarscatter ?or?polarplot ?function with markers, MATLAB might crash and generate a stack trace that includes the term 'createSizes' .
Workaround: There is a workaround for polarlscatter, but not for polarplot.If you are using the polarscatter function, specify the size data for the markers.? If you want the markers to be the same size, specify one of the marker sizes to be a slightly different size than the others.For example, this code crashes MATLAB:?polarscatter(rand(1,20000), rand(1,20000))The default marker size is 36 for polarscatter. You can avoid the crash by setting one of the marker sizes to 36.001, which is a visually imperceptible difference:s = 36*ones(1, 20000);s(1) = 36.001;polarscatter(rand(1, 20000), rand(1, 20000), s)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Absolute-time temporal logic operators before and after may result in incorrect behavior when used with constant thresholds and with milliseconds and microseconds as time units
Description: Absolute temporal logic operators before(n,time_unit)  and after(n,time_unit)  may result in an incorrect behavior during simulation and code generation if all of the following conditions are true: The chart executes with a fixed sample time step of size sample_time . n  is a numeric literal constant (e.g, 1.2). time_unit  is msec  or usec . round(n/sample_time)  is equal to 2^k - 1 for some integer k>0. n  is slightly larger than an integer multiple of sample_time . More precisely, this condition is satisfied if: time_unit ?is?msec ?and? ceil(n/(sample_time*1000) - (sample_time*1e-8)) != ceil(n/(sample_time*1000) - (sample_time* 1e-5))  time_unit ?is?usec ?and? ceil(n/  (sample_time*1000000) - (sample_time*1e-8) ) != ceil(n/(sample_time*1000000) -  ( sample_time* 1e-2) )    ?????????? where the arithmetic operations are performed according to IEEE 754 standard.  For example, when the sample time is 1 second,  the expression after(1000002.5, usec) will never be true. the expression before(1000002.5,?usec)?will be true at all time steps.
Workaround: Either:Change the constant threshold to an integer multiple of?sample_timeUse sec as the?time_unit

Summary: Model Advisor check for an AUTOSAR adaptive model with message root I/O crashes MATLAB
Description: Model Advisor check for an AUTOSAR adaptive model crashes MATLAB if all of these conditions are true: The model contains a root Inport or Outport connected to a Message Receive or Message Send block. The root Inport or Outport is mapped to an event. Model advisor check  Check integer word length ?is enabled.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit Conversion block returns incorrect generated code for conversions with offsets and fixed-point data types with bias
Description: The Unit Conversion block might return incorrect answers for accelerator mode, rapid accelerator mode simulation, or code generation when the following conditions are true:The Output data type  parameter is set to Inherit: Inherit via back propagation , The input or output data type is a fixed-point data type with a bias, The unit conversion has an offset, for example, ˇăC  to ˇăF  or ˇăC  to K .
Workaround: Set the Output data type parameter of the Unit Conversion block to Inherit: Inherit via internal rule and insert a Data Type Conversion block after the Unit Conversion block.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: trajectoryOptimalFrenet throws error if no feasible trajectory is found
Description: If you use the trajectoryOptimalFrenet ?function for local trajectory generation and no feasible trajectory is found, the trajectory generator?throws an error.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Rte read function call generated for AUTOSAR model with bus ports and ErrorStatus inport
Description: An incorrect Rte  read function call is generated for anAUTOSAR model in which all  of the following conditions exist: A bus inport and signal inport are mapped to the sameAUTOSAR data element. The bus inport is mapped to AUTOSAR data access modeExplicitReceive . The signal inport is mapped to AUTOSAR data access modeErrorStatus .  The generated code stores the error status and the received data in the same variable, rather than different variables.

Summary: Incorrect error message with variants and mask workspace variables during Fast Restart mode
Description: Incorrect error message is shown when all the following conditions are true:The model uses Variant Subsystem and masked workspace variables in the variant choices. The active choice of the Variant Subsystem is switched in between two simulations. The second simulation uses Fast Restart mode.?  The error message Invalid Simulink Object name  is displayed for content in the previously active variant choice.
Workaround: Close and reopen the model in between simulations.

Summary: Ignored values for model diagnostic parameters?NonCurrentReleaseOperatingPointMsg and?OperatingPointInterfaceChecksumMismatchMsg
Description: Setting the values for the model diagnostic parameters NonCurrentReleaseOperatingPointMsg  and?OperatingPointInterfaceChecksumMismatchMsg ?has no effect on the behavior controlled by these parameters.
Workaround: Use the alternative command-line names for these parameters to change the model settings:For NonCurrentReleaseOperatingPointMsg?use SimStateOlderReleaseMsg.For OperatingPointInterfaceChecksumMismatchMsg use SimStateInterfaceChecksumMismatchMsg.

Summary: polyspace-configure could not open options file
Description: Running?polyspace-configure ?on a build command results in this message after the build command is complete: ERROR: Could not open options file optionsFile   This error indicates that during compilation, a compiler options file was accessed using a relative path.? polyspace-configure ?fails to locate options files with a relative path. Failure to locate and read the options file can lead to the generated project being incomplete. For instance, some options or source files might be missing.
Workaround: If possible, in your build system files, change the options file path to an absolute one. This workaround is not always possible because the options files are often temporary files generated by the build tools.

Summary: ARXML import of enumeration data type for AUTOSAR adaptive software component does not create Simulink enumeration data type
Description: When importing ARXML that contains enumeration data types for an AUTOSAR adaptive software component, import does not create a Simulink enumeration data type. Instead, import creates data type objects based on the enumeration base type (for example, uint8).?
Workaround: Manually create enumerationdata types in Simulink. Identify enumerations in the ARXML and createthe corresponding enumeration types in Simulink.?

Summary: Reading string data in MDF-files might generate the error: Result buffer too small
Description: Although the ASAM MDF standard states that strings stored in MDF-files should be zero-terminated, in practice some tools generate files that do not comply.?When attempting to read strings that are not zero-terminated from MDF-files, the following error might occur: ? Result buffer too small

Summary: Generated VHDL code fails to compile when HDL RAM System blocks have initial value of data type?fixdt(0,1, 0)
Description: When the design has HDL RAM System block of fi(0, 1, 0) input data type and initial value specified, the generated VHDL code fails to compile. This erroneous behavior is specific to VHDL target language for HDL RAM's that support initial value.

Summary: Simulink Compiler does not support configuration references
Description: Simulink compiler cannot create standalone applications from models that use configuration references. Attempting to do so will produce an error: Warning: The following error was caught while executing 'onCleanup' class destructor:Error using buildRacTarget>locCleanupPostParameter update is not supported for a configuration reference. Update parameter in the configuration set that it references.?

Summary: Models exported from SerDes Designer app toSimulink? results in a hard crash for some combinations of Symbol Time andSamples per Symbol
Description: In the SerDes Desinger  app, if you set Symbol Time  to 32.25  ps and Samples per Symbol  to greater than 8 , then export the modelto Simulink?, the exported model experiences a hard crash for Simulink?.This is due to the improper storage of a parameter in the serdes.ChannelImpulse ?mask . The improper storage of the parameter leads to an operation that requestsa matrix of size much larger than the computer is physically able to handle andmakes the system crash.
Workaround: SetSamples per Symbol to 8 while designing the model in the SerDes Designer app.Once you export the model to Simulink?, you can change the Samples per Symbol parameter toyour desired value.

Summary: Generating code for Bus Creator with bus inputs can lead to assertion indicating Empty records are present
Description: When using buses in a Simulink model, an internal assertion might be generated if a bus composed of other buses is used. This is typically modeled by using a Bus Creator block with bus inputs. Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/createCGIR  Assertion failed:    matlab\src\cgir_hdl\dom_pir_core\recordlowercomponent.cpp:142:Empty records are present

Summary: Images from exportgraphics and copygraphics might be incorrect
Description: The graphics captured by the exportgraphics  or copygraphics  functions might be shifted to the upper-left corner of the image, or it might be clipped in when any of these conditions are true:  The Renderer  property of the figure is set to 'painters'   You call the exportgraphics  or copygraphics  function inside a parfor loop You start your MATLAB session with the -nodisplay  option?

Summary: ARXML import for AUTOSAR adaptive software component ignores data types specified as STD-CPP-IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE of category TYPE_REFERENCE
Description: When importing ARXML foran AUTOSAR adaptive software component, import ignores data types specified asSTD-CPP-IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE  of category TYPE_REFERENCE . As a result, you might see errors when updating the diagram, validating AUTOSAR properties, or generating code due to the missing data types.

Summary: Error occurs when AliasType used in a C Function block with no external typedef is provided
Description: A parsing error occurs when an AliasType defined on either the: C Function block Symbol Specification table incoming input signal   without any external typedef defined in the header files of the C Function block custom code or in the?Configuration Parameters  > Simulation Target  > Header file  of the model.
Workaround: Specify the typedef in the custom block header file or include a file that has the typedef for the AliasType in the Configuration Parameters > Simulation Target > Header file.

Summary: AUTOSAR XML import fails with Unrecognized method, property, or field 'IsApplication' for class 'M3I.Object'
Description: AUTOSAR import fails when reading ARXML that contains fixed-axis BaseType s that use float  or single  primitive types.? The importer generates the following message: Error using arxml.importer/createComponentAsModel (line 115)Unrecognized method, property, or field 'IsApplication' for class 'M3I.Object'.

Summary: Using cvhtml to generate a report for aggregated SIL coverage results exported to the MATLAB workspace can cause an error
Description: Calling cvhtml  on aggregated SIL coverage results exported from the Simulink Test Manager to the MATLAB workspace causes an error when your model contains a MATLAB Function inside a Stateflow chart.  cvi.  ReportUtils.  checkHarnessData>checkBDHarness(line 76)??????????errmsg =??????????message('Slvnv:simcoverage:cvhtml:IncompatibleModel',  ??????????harnessModel,  ?thisModelName);Error in cvi.  ReportUtils.  checkHarnessData (line36)????????[topModelName,  ?rootId,  ?errmsg] =????????checkBDHarness(covdata,  ?modelcovId,  ????????harnessModel);Error in cvi.  ReportUtils.  loadTopModelAndRefModels(line 16)????????[isHarnessData,  ?tTopModelName,  ?~,  ?errmsg,  ????????ownerModel] =????????cvi.  ReportUtils.  checkHarnessData(topHarnessCovData,  ????????[]);
Workaround: Replace MATLAB Functions inside Stateflow charts with a Simulink function and MATLAB function block.

Summary: AUTOSAR mappings for blocks within referenced subsystem do not persist when model is reopened
Description: For an AUTOSAR software component model configured for the Classic Platform, the AUTOSAR mappings for Rate Transition, Simulink Function, or Function Caller blocks that are located within a referenced subsystem do not persist when the model is closed and reopened.

Summary: OptimizationExpressions with multiple power or mpower operators can return incorrect results
Description: When creating an OptimizationExpression  in the Problem-Based workflow, using multiple successive power  or mpower  operators can return an incorrect result. For example: x = optimvar('x');  p = x^2;  expr = p + p^2   yields: expr =?  ? Nonlinear OptimizationExpression  ? ? (x.^4 + x.^4)   The correct expression is (x.^2 + x.^4) .
Workaround: Avoid writing successive power or mpower operations on the same expression.For the example above, instead write:expr = p + x^4;orp = x^2;q = x^4;expr = p + q;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Customized step function prototype?with custom storage class on Root-level Outport might generate incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all the following conditions are met:Model has customized step function prototype. Step function is configured to have return argument. Root-level Outport corresponding to this return argument has custom storage class.  The generated code might have uninitialized return value in the step function.

Summary: ARXML export from an AUTOSAR adaptive model generates incorrect tag,IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE, for Enumeration data types and references insideDataTypeMaps
Description: Whenexporting ARXML from an AUTOSAR adaptive model, theAUTOSAR classic tag, IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE , incorrectly generates for Enumeration data types and references insideDataTypeMaps. The correct tag that should be generated is the AUTOSAR adaptivetag, STD-CPP-IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE .
Workaround: Manuallyedit the exported ARXML to fix the incorrect tags.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with Stateflow chart
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a Stateflow chart. The Stateflow chart has an exported function defined inside it. The exported function uses an input or output variable from a signal which is not defined in the arguments of the exported function. The exported function is reused in another Stateflow chart?in the same model.? In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Optimize block operation order in the generated code? parameter is set to?Improved code execution speed.
Workaround: In Configuration Parameters dialog box, set?Optimize block operation order in the generated code??to?None.

Summary: Simulink Requirements exported ReqIF file has wrong attribute definition references
Description: The ReqIF file has incorrect attribute definition references which cause an incorrect mapping of the data when the ReqIF file is exported from Simulink Requirements and imported in a third-party requirements management tool. In exported ReqIF file, the <ATTRIBUTE-VALUE> elements within the <SPECIFICATION> elements are referencing the <ATTRIBUTE-DEFINITION> elements of a <SPEC-OBJECT-TYPE>. According to the specification, the value element should reference the definition in the <SPECIFICATION-TYPE>.

Summary: XCP-based external mode fails for binaries with debug symbols for empty compilation units
Description: XCP based external mode uses debug symbols to compute the addresses of global variables.Some compilers (including ARM Compiler 6.12 and Clang 3.8) generate DWARF debug information for empty compilation units. When parsing such debug information, the DWARF parser used by external mode may fail, and the following error may be displayed on the command line: std::exception
Workaround: If an empty source file is being compiled and linked, a fix would be to either remove it from the build or insert a dummy global variable.

Summary: Windows user names with non-English characters can cause issues when opening some MATLAB features
Description: MATLAB installed on a Windows system with a user name containing non-English characters might run into issues and be unable to open the Add-Ons Explorer, Add-Ons Manager, App Designer, and others.MATLAB stores preferences in the user's home folder on Windows, for example, C:\Users\username \AppData\Roaming\MathWorks\MATLAB\R2020a . When the username  is composed of non-English characters, MATLAB runs into issues while creating and modifying files that are needed during startup from the folder where preferences are stored.
Workaround: To avoid these issues:When installing MATLAB, do not use a username that contains non-English characters.?Set the environment variable MATLAB_PREFDIR to a preferences folder that does not have any non-English characters in the path.

Summary: Requirements links lost in round trip workflow when exporting with ReqIF
Description: When a requirement set is imported from a third party tool using a ReqIF file format, any links added in Simulink Requirements will fail to export unless a link of the same type existed in the original ReqIF file. For example, if you import a requirements document from Jama, and some items had "Related to" links between them, and then you add more "Related to" links in a Simulink Requirements session and export the updated Requirement Set to a ReqIF file, the added links will export successfully. However, if the original import did not have any "Related to" links, the newly added links will not be present in the exported content. This Import > Modify > Export workflow using ReqIF is referred to as a round trip workflow.

Summary: The Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) block may not calculate the same NTU factor when the two-phase fluid flow is reversed.
Description: When the NTU factor is small, the factor calculated by the Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) block does not have the same value when the two-phase fluid flows from port B1  to port A1  as from port A1  to port B1 .
Workaround: Ensure that the two-phase fluid always flows from port A1 to port?B1.

Summary: Subsystem Reference block's port properties are lost on reloading the model or if the contents of the Subysystem Reference block are refreshed
Description: Port properties (such as tag, description, signal info etc.) specified for a Subsystem Reference block are lost if you either reload the model or refresh the Subsystem Reference block's contents. A refresh happens if you modify and save the contents of the block. These properties are also lost for a Subsystem block when it is converted to a Subsystem Reference block.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: ARXML import for AUTOSARadaptive software component errors out when an ApplicationDataType is mapped to anAUTOSAR Adaptive Platform type
Description: When importing ARXML that contains an ApplicationDataType mapped to an AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform type, import errors out with a message similar to the following: Error using Simulink. metamodel. arplatform. ArxmlImporter/ readAssertion failed: Invalid property Type for element of type Simulink. metamodel. types. FixedPoint in file / tmp/somepath/mMaxShortNames_autosar_adaptive/ mMaxShortNames_interface. arxml line 275 at arxml/ ArxmlReadTransformer4p0. cpp:13512?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Tunable parameters in non-inlined S-function might lead to incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated if all these conditions are true:?Model uses a non-inlined S-function with parameters. Model configuration parameter Default parameter behavior  is set to Tunable . Model parameters are default mapped to a structured storage class.

Summary: Interactions in the Live Editor become sluggish when displaying a large number of variables
Description: The Live Editor becomes sluggish over time if one or more live scripts are open and displaying a large number of variables. For example, interactions such as rerunning a live script take longer after some time.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing Data Store Memory and MATLAB System blocks
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Data Store Memory block and a MATLAB System block. The read and writes to the Data Store Memory block occur inside the MATLAB System block.

Summary: Code generation error for AUTOSAR model that uses AUTOSAR.Parameter in Simulink.Variant object
Description: When an AUTOSAR model uses a Simulink.Variant  object, for which the condition references an AUTOSAR.Parameter ?object,? generating code might result in the following error: The call to autosar_make_rtw_hook,?during the after_tlc hook generated the following error:    Unrecognized function or variable 'Rte_SysCon_<SystemConstantName>'.

Summary: The Dependency Analyzer is stuck when opened or running an analysis
Description: If a project exists in the root of the current file system, the Dependency Analyzer is stuck in the following cases: When you try to open the Dependency Analyzer, MATLAB stays busy and the application does not show. The Dependency Analyzer is already open. You try to run an analysis, MATLAB stays busy and the analysis is stuck at the "Analyzing: Finishing analysis" stage.
Workaround: To continue the loading process, in the Command Window, press ctrl+c (or control+c on Mac OS X). The Dependency Analyzer will start working again. Some of the decoration may not be correct.To resolve the issue without upgrading, delete the project from the root directory or move it to a different location. Alternatively, on Windows, create a network map for a folder that contains the file or project you want to analyze. Make sure it does not contain a project in the top level root. Run the analysis from inside the newly created network drive.

Summary: Unexpected error message in the 4-Way Directional Valve (IL) block
Description: In the 4-Way Directional Valve (IL) block, when the?Area characteristics  parameter is setto?Identical for all flow paths  and the?Orificeparameterization  parameter is set to?Linear ¨C Area vs. spool travel, right-clicking on the block and selecting Fluids > Plot valvecharacteristics  erroneously generates the following message: Reference to non-existent field 'smoothing_factor_prompt.'
Workaround: To work around this issue, make either of these settings:Set the Area characteristics parameter to?Different for each flowpath, orSet the?Orifice parameterization parameter to?Tabulateddata ¨C Area vs. spool travel or Tabulated data ¨C Volumetric flow ratevs. spool travel and pressure drop.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results for models containing model reference hierarchy using?Simulink.LookupTableObject?as model argument with inconsistent LUT object struct order settings between parent and child models
Description: Incorrect simulation results for a model with these settings: Top-level model contains: Certain setting for?LUT object struct order for explicit value specification  and?LUT object struct order for even spacing specification  parameters (either  Size,Breakpoints,Table  or  Size,Table,Breakpoints )  A? Model reference block with these characteristics: Refers to a model containing an n-D Lookup Table block with these configurations Different setting from parent model in either?LUT object struct order for explicit value specification? or?LUT object struct order for even spacing specification ?configuration parameters. n-D Lookup Table block contains a Simulink.LookupTableObject  with these characteristics Data Specification ?parameter set to?Lookup table object .  Lookup table object configured to be passed as an argument to the Model reference block.      This is an invalid combination and will cause errors.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation results may be incorrect in the?Accelerator and Rapid Accelerator mode for models with Assignment blocks inside a For Iterator subsystem
Description: Simulation?might produce incorrect results when a model meets the following conditions:Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator simulation mode is chosen. An Assignment block is inside a For Iterator subsystem.? The Assignment block has Y0  port enabled. The propagated sample time for the Assignment block is [inf 0] (that is, a constant sample time).

Summary: Variable change of IC block?parameter ignored for referenced model running in accelerator mode with fast restart
Description: For a referenced model simulated in accelerator mode with fast restart, Simulink incorrectly ignores a change to the Initial value  parameter of the IC block between simulations.

Summary: Unexpected error in the 3-Way Directional Valve (IL) block and Cartridge Valve Insert (IL) block
Description: The following blocks might erroneously generate the message: "Each column of Volumetric flow rate table, q(ds,dp) must be arranged in ascending or descending order." In the 3-Way DirectionalValve (IL) block, this might occur when the?Area characteristics  parameter is setto?Identical for all flow paths , and the?Orificeparameterization  parameter is set to?Tabulated data ¨C Volumetric flowrate vs. spool travel and pressure drop . In the Cartridge Valve Insert (IL) block, this might occur when the?Valve seat specification ?parameter is set to Custom ?and the?Orifice parameterization? parameter is set to?Tabulated data ¨C Volumetric flow rate vs. spool travel and pressure drop .
Workaround: To work around this issue, do one of the following:?Set the?Pressure drop vector, dp?parameter so that all vector elements are equal to or greater than 0 when the?Orifice parameterization parameter is?Tabulated data - Volumetric flow rate vs. control member position and pressure drop.Set the?Orifice parameterization parameter to?Linear¨C Area vs. spool travel or Tabulated data ¨C Area vs. spool travel.?For the 3-Way Directional Valve (IL) block, set the?Area characteristics?parameter to?Different for each flow path.

Summary: macOS users might receive a warning for GPU support in a future MATLAB release, without explicitly attempting to use a GPU
Description: macOS users might receive the following warning message without explicitly working on a GPU:? Support for GPU acceleration with Parallel Computing Toolbox on macOS will be removed in a future MATLAB release.  For example, users of the Deep Learning Toolbox might receive the warning message when calling trainNetwork   without specifying the? 'ExecutionEnvironment'  option or when setting it to 'auto' .?
Workaround: If you do not intend to use a GPU, you can ignore the warning message. To suppress the warning message, execute the following command:warning off parallel:gpu:device:MacOSGPUSupportToBeRemoved;?If you are using the trainNetwork function from Deep Learning Toolbox and you do not intend to use a GPU, you can set the?'ExecutionEnvironment'?option to 'cpu' to avoid the warning.

Summary: Subsystem code reuse regression in R2019a update 5 and R2019b for subsystems containing different units
Description: If two subsystems have these characteristics: They are identical with respect to their interface and contents. The only difference is the 'Unit' property of the signals feeding the subsystems. The subsystems do not contain any Unit Conversion blocks along the signal path from the input signal in (2).  The functions for the two subsystems might not be not reused on the generated code.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Linking errors when using code generation with Computer Vision Toolbox OpenCV functions for non-MATLAB targets
Description: Computer Vision Toolbox OpenCV functions do not recognizeproduct hardware types for code generation. This error results in the softwareusing MATLAB instead of non-MATLAB as thetarget, which produces library linking errors. Specifically, the generated code errors out during the build? process with library dependencies.
Workaround: To avoid the linking errors due to MATLAB built OpenCV library files, follow these steps:Locate the makefile in the generated code archive,? (<function name>_rtw.mk).Identify the variable that links the OpenCV binaries,? (LIBS = $(MATLAB_ROOT)/bin/glnxa64/libopencv_calib3d.so.4.2 ...) .Modify the variable that? links the OpenCV binaries built on the target (Eg: LIBS = `pkg-config --libs opencv` or LIBS = /usr/local/lib), with the location of the Opencv libs for the correct target.Build using the modified makefile on the target.?

Summary: Error during compilation of C++ file: stl_tree.h, line 2142: error: no instance of constructor
Description: When verifying a C++ application using option -compiler gnu7.x  or option -cpp-version cpp14 , the following error can appear during the Polyspace compilation phase: .../stl_tree.h, line 2142: error: no instance of constructor "std::pair<_T1, _T2>::pair [with _T1=std::_Rb_tree_node_base *, _T2=std::_Rb_tree_node_base *]" matches the argument list...

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing a Selector block that connects to a Unit Delay block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Selector block that directly connects to the input of a Unit Delay block. The input to the Selector block branches to another block aside from the Selector block.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initial value for block output inside reusable subsystem
Description: The initial value for block output in a reusable subsystem may be incorrectly computed under all these conditions:?The model configuration parameter?Buffer for reusable subsystems  is selected. The reusable subsystem's inport is multi-rate (For example, a Mux block combines a Inf sample time signal and a discrete sample time signal feeds into the subsystem's inport).

Summary: Spectrum Analyzer block and dsp.SpectrumAnalyzer show wrong samples per update if overlap percentage is not 0%
Description: Spectrum Analyzer block and dsp.SpectrumAnalyzer show wrong samples per update if both these conditions are true: Overlap % is not 0 Spectrum estimation method is 'Filter bank'?

Summary: Changes in Connection Port block?or Simscape Bus block connections across subsystem boundaries might be ignored when made between simulation runs
Description: After simulating a model containing Simscape blocks inside of a subsystem, if you disconnect Connection Port blocks and reconnect them to different blocks within the subsystem, the change in connections might be ignored on subsequent runs. Similarly, changing Simscape Bus connections by modifying Simscape Bus port identifiers when the Simscape Bus line crosses a subsystem boundary can also be ignored on subsequent runs.?
Workaround: After changing the Connection Port of Subsystem Bus connections, save the model and?close it. Then reopen the model to have Simscape recognize the changes in connectivity.

Summary: Slow comparison and merge for Simulink models with a large number of signal line changes.
Description: When comparing and merging Simulink models that have thousands of signal line changes, the comparison can take a long time to complete.

Summary: Incorrect results when using sbiofit with nlinfit and proportional?or combined error model
Description: sbiofit  returns incorrect results when?using nlinfit ?as the estimation function and proportional  or combined  as the error model.?nlinfit ?returns residuals that are weighted, but?sbiofit ?does not correctly account for this weighting.

Summary: Invalid simulation output in failed test case result
Description: If a test case fails due to an error in model simulation, then the simulation output from the previous model simulation might appear in the test case results section.

Summary: Model update hangs at "Configuring subsystem output ports"
Description: If a model has a large virtual bus with an uninterrupted feedback loop, updating the model might hang at "Configuring subsystem output ports" and the memory usage might increase when the model is compiling.
Workaround: Interrupt the feedback loop with a Unit Delay block or convert the large virtual bus into several smaller buses.

Summary: Rapid accelerator simulation fails on Windows with generic error
Description: A rapid accelerator simulation might fail on Windows with the following error message if you have previous MATLAB version DLLs on your system path: Error occurred while simulating the model model_name in rapid accelerator mode. Rerun the simulation in normal mode to diagnose this error.
Workaround: Clean up your system path of any MATLAB-related items before installing R2020a or invoke the attached winraccelworkaround.p each time you start MATLAB, such as in a startup.m file.

Summary: Estimated motor parameters are inaccurate if the binary files are manually downloaded to the target hardware?
Description: Motor Control Blockset provides binary files (*.out) that you can use as part of an alternate workflow for motor parameter estimation. If you manually download and run these files on the target hardware, and then perform motor parameter estimation, the estimated parameters may be inaccurate.?

Summary: Discover Clusters dialog doesn't open in MATLAB Online Server
Description: In MATLAB Online Server, the Discover Clusters dialog does not open when you select Parallel  > Discover Clusters... . The Discover Clusters dialog also does not open when you select Discover Clusters  from the Cluster Profile Manager dialog.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Function block that performs comparisons with constant value in LHS generates incorrect HDL code with?MATLAB Datapath architecture
Description: The generated code for a Simulink model is incorrect if the following conditions are met: The model contains a MATLAB function block with Architecture  set to MATLAB Datapath  The MATLAB code contains constant expressions with the constant value on the left hand side of the relational operator. This occurs for the relational operators <, <=, >,  and >= .  For example, the following expression generates incorrect code: uint8(1) >= x ?
Workaround: The constant expression can appear on the right hand side of a comparison operation. For example:x <= uint8(1)

Summary: sldvisactive may incorrectly return false when translating a model with model blocks
Description: sldvisactive  may incorrectly return false  when Simulink Design Verifier is run on a model that contains one or more model  blocks.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Xilinx System Generator and HDL Coder integration broken due to Vivado incompatible upgrades
Description: The workflow described in?this document ?is broken in R2019a release and generates this error: Error: Evaluation of elaborate function on class hdldefaults.XilinxSystemGeneratorSubsystem failed with the error message: Xilinx:Environment: Unknown path type identifier.?
Workaround: An updated version of this example is available in R2020a. To see the example, refer to?this document.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Motor currents measured using example host models might be inaccurate
Description: The example host models (mcb_*host*.slx ) in Motor Control Blockset contain various signals that you can use to measure current from the motor. These measured values might deviate by about 10% when compared with the actual values from the motor.?

Summary: AUTOSAR adaptive model fails to generate code when root inport message data type is an array of fixed-point data
Description: AUTOSAR adaptive model fails to generate code when the following conditions are true: The model contains at least one root inport. The message payload for at least one root inport is an n-dimension matrix of fixed-point data.   Generating code for such a model results in the following error: CGIR assertion 'In constructGetNewSamplesCallbackFcnBody: Assertion failed: msgDataVar' failed in 'rtwcg_utils/message_functions/MsgAccessFcnTxAdpt1903Schema.cpp:192'

Summary: AUTOSAR adaptive model fails to generate code when root inport message data type is an enumeration
Description: Code generation for an AUTOSAR adaptive model throws an error if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a root inport connected to a Message Receive block. The root inport is mapped to an event. The data type for the message payload is an enumeration.

Summary: On macOS and certain distributions of Linux such as CentOS, MATLAB Web App Server might stop rendering icons for the web apps on the apps home page
Description: When MATLAB Web App Server has been running for a while on macOS and certain distributions of Linux such as CentOS, the server might stop rendering icons for the web apps on the apps home page.?? On macOS and Linux systems, the server stores some temporary data in the file system's /tmp  folder. When that data gets cleaned up, you might notice the app icons on the apps home page missing. On macOS systems, ?you might notice this happening after 3 days, and on Linux distributions such as CentOS, ?you might notice it after 10 days. This issue is not present on Debain distributions of Linux.
Workaround: You might need to restart web apps services (mw-webapps-launcher-R2020a, mw-webapps-R2020a) either using webapps-stop & webapps-start commands or using operating system tools ("systemctl stop" on Linux and "launchctl unload" on macOS) to resolve the issue, or tweak how often /tmp directory is being cleaned up.

Summary: mldivide or mrdivide with parpool('threads') can generate inconsistent warnings for some matrices
Description: When running a parfor  loop on a parallel pool using the parpool('threads')  mode, a call to mldivide  from within this loop might not be thread-safe. Specifically, calling x = A\b  with a sparse real symmetric indefinite matrix A  might generate incorrect warnings about the condition number of A . The output?x  is still correct in this case. This bug also affects the mrdivide  function x = b/A .

Summary: SimBiology projects created with unconfigured math lines in releases before R2019b error when loaded in R2019b or later
Description: SimBiology projects created prior to R2019b may use math lines to create reference lines in plots. ?The SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, introduced in R2019b, converts these math lines into observables. ?Loading a project containing math lines with invalid configurations into the app in R2019b or later generates the following error: Unable to convert result object to new format for SimBiology Model Analyzer: Unrecognized function or variable 'expressionResults'.
Workaround: Open the project in R2019a, remove the unconfigured math lines and resave before opening in R2019b.

Summary: Automated Driving Toolbox examples for monoCamera and birdsEyeView classes do not display expected results when installed with Vehicle Dynamics Blockset.
Description: The results returned by these examples are incorrect when the Vehicle Dynamics Blockset is installed.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Reading an MDF channel containing only empty strings might generate the error:?Invalid argument
Description: When reading MDF data from a channel that contains only empty strings, the following error might occur: ? Invalid argument

Summary: Packaging a custom toolbox fails to include additional software entries
Description: When you package a custom toolbox that contains additional software, the packaging might fail and produce this error:  File '<toolboxName>.mltbx' was not found  If the toolbox has already been packaged and the mltbx ?files exists, an additional packaging process completes without an error. However, when the toolbox is installed on another machine, the additional software is not installed.

Summary: Cannot set?Number of Newton's iterations or Extrapolation order parameters with odeN solver and implicit integration method
Description: An unexpected error occurs when trying to set the?Number of Newton's iterations or? ?Extrapolation order  parameters in the Solver pane of a model's Configuration Parameters when the following conditions are met: Solver  is set to odeN (Nonadaptive) Integration Method  is set to either ode14x (extrapolation)  or ode1be (Backward Euler)
Workaround: At the MATLAB command line, use set_param?to set the?NumberNewtonIterations?and?ExtrapolationOrder?parameter values.?You do not need to change the solver.orIn the top model's Configuration Parameters - Solver pane:??Set the?Solver?in the Solver selection section to?ode14x (extrapolation).in the Solver details section, set the desired values for the?Number of Newton's iterations?and?Extrapolation order?parameters.?In the Solver?selection section, change the Solver back to odeN (Nonadaptive).

Summary: When restoring a SerDes Designer App session from a saved .mat file, references to workspace variables for the CTLE GPZ or custom channel impulse responses are broken and cause the app to hang.
Description: When restoring a SerDes Designer App session from a saved .mat file, references to workspace variables for the CTLE GPZ or custom channel impulse responses are broken and cause the app to hang.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results for some simulation modes for models containing model reference hierarchy?with particular LUT object struct order settings using Simulink.LookupTableObject as model argument
Description: Incorrect simulation results for a model with these settings: Top-level model contains: Model configuration parameter LUT object struct order for explicit value specification  set to 'Size,Table,Breakpoints'  A? Model reference block with these characteristics: Refers to a model containing an n-D Lookup Table block with these configurations LUT object struct order for explicit value specification ?set to? 'Size,Table,Breakpoints'  n-D Lookup Table block contains a Simulink.LookupTableObject with these characteristics Data Specification  parameter set to Lookup table object  Lookup table object BreakpointsSpecification? parameter set to  Explict values  Lookup table object configured to be passed as an argument to the Model reference block      Incorrect simulation results might also occur when one of these is true for the model: The simulation mode  of the model is set to Normal ?and the simulation mode?of the Model reference block is set to Accelerator .    The simulation mode?of the model is set to Accelerator   and the simulation mode  of the Model reference block is set to Software-in-the-loop (SIL)
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB? might crash while generating code for a model containing a Bitwise operator block on a Mac platform
Description: MATLAB might crash while generating code on a Mac platform for a model that meets the following conditions:The model a contains Bitwise operator block. The model configuration parameter Operator to represent Bitwise and Logical operator blocks  is set to a value other than Logical operator .

Summary: MATLAB crashes when generating code for a model that receives a message with an Enumeration or bus data type
Description: MATLAB crashes when generating code for a model with these conditions:  The model has a root Inport connected to a Message Receive block. The Inport data type is either an Enumeration or bus. The model is not a referenced model.

Summary: AUTOSAR createEnumeration function does not group imported BITFIELD_TEXTTABLE elements by short label
Description: After importing BITFIELD_TEXTTABLE elements from ARXML, if the imported elements are ordered by short-label, calling the createEnumeration  function generates Simulink enumerations with incorrect elements.

Summary: FOC example that uses Simscape Electrical converter throws 'Sample time mismatch' error during simulation
Description: The mcb_ee_pmsm_foc ?example model provides three options for inverter selection. If you select either Simscape Electrical 3 phase converter ?or Simscape Electrical Modular Multilevel converter , the model throws a Sample time mismatch ?error during simulation. This model may also display incorrect results when simulated with above two options, due to other issues related to plant model configuration.  However, if you select the option that does not use Simscape Electrical converter (Motor Control Blockset average inverter ), this example works as expected.

Summary: Standalone code generated for fftn or ifftn?produces incorrect output when the input array X is two-dimensional and and the size vector sz?has length greater than two
Description: Standalone code (static library/dynamically linked library/SIL/PIL) generated for the?fftn ?or the? ifftn ?function produces incorrect output when the input arguments satisfy all of these conditions: The input array X  is two-dimensional The length of the size vector sz ?(that contains the lengths of transform dimensions) is three or greater than three ?The size vector sz ?has the pattern? [1 a b c...] ?where:  The second element  a  is any positive integer greater than  2  The subsequent elements ( b, c,...)  are any nonzero positive integers?    For example, define a function foo  that calls fftn:  function y = foo(x) %#codegeny = fftn(x,[1 3 4]);end Generate a SIL MEX function for foo  by running these commands: cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.VerificationMode = "SIL";codegen foo -args {ones(5,5)} -config cfg Execute the generated SIL MEX function foo_sil  with the two-dimensional run-time input ones(5,5) : ysil = foo_sil(ones(5,5))?  Execute the original MATLAB function foo  with the same input: yml = foo(ones(5,5))?  Observe that the two outputs ysil  and yml  are different. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when?linearizing Interpolation Using Prelookup block with lookup table object
Description: MATLAB might crash when linearizing the Interpolation Using Prelookup  block when the following conditions are true: ?The TableSpecification  parameter is set to Lookup table object  ?The algorithm used for linearization is set to blockbyblock.  ?For example via MATLAB command: linOption = linearizeOptions('LinearizationAlgorithm','blockbyblock');   ?A linear approximation of an?Interpolation Using Prelookup? block in model?mdl ?is obtained. For example by executing MATLAB command: linearize   (mdl  ,blockpath ,linOption)

Summary: Generating a model coverage report using an exported object can cause an error
Description: If a model has a custom library block and you export the coverage data object from a Simulink Test run, regenerating the report causes an error: Error using cvi.? TopModelCov.? updateModelHandles (line 23)?  Attempt to modify 'MyLibrary' which is a locked (read-only) library
Fixed In: R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Blocks that use elapsed sample time (such as Discrete Time Integrator or Weighted Sample Time Math) might produce wrong answers if placed inside an aperiodic partition
Description: Blocks that use elapsed sample time (such as Discrete Time Integrator or Weighted Sample Time Math) might behave incorrectly under the following conditions: The block is inside a partitioned atomic subsystem The subsystem has the?Schedule as ? parameter set to?? Aperiodic partition ?  Simulink might produce incorrect numerical results in Normal, Accelerator, and Rapid Accelerator simulation?modes as well as in generated code.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when opening a file with an extremely long expression.
Description: An extremely long expression is composed of hundreds of sub-expressions. These expressions are usually from machine-generated code. MATLAB might crash when you either: Use the editor to open a file that contains an extremely long?expression. Open a directory that contains a file with an extremely long?expression.

Summary: Requirements Editor might become frozen after deleting multiple objects
Description: Requirements Editor might become frozen after requirements or links are deleted with multi-selection with the following error message in a pop up dialog:  Dot index is not supported for variables of this type.   ?The error occurs when the following conditions are met: Loaded requirements or links has comments Multiple objects selected in the requirements browser are deleted or objects are deleted by API operation The objects being deleted with comments were never displayed in the Property Inspector.
Workaround: For deleting requirements or links, use single selection and delete them one by one using the requirements browser.Workaround for saving data?If the error happens, unsaved data can be saved by the following script:reqSet = slreq.find('type','ReqSet','Dirty',true);arrayfun(@(x) save(x), reqSet);linkSet = slreq.find('type','LinkSet','Dirty',true);arrayfun(@(x) save(x), linkSet);The following command will clear requirements and links from memory and close the frozen requirements editor:slreq.clear?

Summary: MATLAB renders the project root incorrectly and throws NullPointerException when creating a project from a Simulink Model Manifest
Description: When you create a project from a Simulink Model Manifest, the New Project dialog renders the project root incorrectly and throws a NullPointerException in the Command Window.
Workaround: The Model Manifest Report automatically attempts creating a project using the project root reported in Files to include section. If that is acceptable, you can safely ignore the issue. The project creation proceeds successfully.If a different folder is required, create a project from a folder: In MATLAB, on the Home tab, select New > Project > From Folder and specify the right folder. The project includes all files in the folder, not only the ones listed in the manifest report.

Summary: MATLAB sporadically crashes when editing a transition in a Stateflow chart
Description: MATLAB might crash when you edit a transition in a Stateflow chart. The crash report refers to buildTransition. On Windows, the call stack is similar to: [ 16] 0x... CxxThrowException ? (in VCRUNTIME140.dll)[ 17] 0x... std::_Xout_of_range ? (in MSVCP140.dll)[ 18] 0x... std::_String_val::_Xran ? (in stateflow.dll)[ 19] 0x... SF::ir::TransitionAdapter::getConditionString ? (in stateflow.dll)[ 20] 0x... SF::ir::IrBuilder::buildTransition ? (in stateflow.dll)

Summary: Missing header files for enumerations used on root inports and outports for AUTOSAR adaptive models
Description: When models configured for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform use enumeration data types on root inports and outports the header files for the enumerated types are not generated. The generated code cannot be compiled and linked into an executable application.
Workaround: Manually add header files for enumeration data types after inspecting the code and compilation errors. Information for the enumerated types can be taken from the generated ARXML.

Summary: writeall on TransformedDatastore throws an error when the underlying datastore is a CombinedDatastore
Description: Calling the writeall  function on a TransformedDatastore  can generate an error if the underlying datastore is a CombinedDatastore  with multiple underlying datastores.? For example: imds = imageDatastore(repmat("peppers.png", 10, 1)); cds = combine(imds, imds); tds = cds.transform(@(img) img{1});tds.writeall("outputimages2", "OutputFormat", "png")Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values. Error in matlab.io.datastore.FileWritable/writeSerial (line 31)         if cachedFilename == "" || ~currentFileIndexComparator(dsCopy, numFilesRead) Error in matlab.io.datastore.FileWritable/writeParallel (line 41)             writeSerial(ds, location, compressStr, nvStruct); Error in matlab.io.datastore.TransformedDatastore/writeall (line 379)                writeParallel(ds, location, files, nvStruct);

Summary: Unable to compile a Stateflow chart (.sfx) file using MATLAB Compiler
Description: When you compile a Stateflow chart (.sfx) file using the mcc command or the apps in MATLAB Compiler, your compilation will fail with an error message.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data mismatch between Simulink and HDL simulation for partly serial architecture of Discrete FIR Filter HDL Optimized block
Description: A data mismatch between Simulink and HDL simulation can happen for partly serial architecture of Discrete FIR Filter HDL Optimized block and for the dsp.HDLFIRFilter  System object in either of these cases: When casting the coefficients to the specified data type produces different values depending on the Saturate on integer overflow  parameter. ?When the coefficients are only partly symmetric/anti-symmetric.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Model Transformer tool generates an error while refactoring a model to eliminate Data Store Memory blocks
Description: The Model Transformer tool generates an error saying Unexpected Exception caught,  when the following conditions are met: Model refactoring tries to eliminate Data Store Memory blocks. The model has a subsystem reading from a Data Store Memory block that?has been written to outside the subsystem.

Summary: MATLAB crashes in Test Extension workflow when the configured parameters result in dead logic
Description: Simulink Design Verifier supports extension of testcases when the parameters in the model are configured for analysis.?However, MATLAB crashes when the configured parameters result in dead logic, with the following crash log:?Abnormal termination:  Access violation    Snippet from the Stack Trace (from fault):  [ 0] 0x0000000147913ed7 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll+05127895 CG::BoolValue::BoolValue+00000039  [ 1] 0x000000014792995a C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll+05216602 CG::Value::createDirectReferenceValueRef+00002138  [ 2] 0x00000001479289c3 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll+05212611 CG::Value::createDirectReferenceValueCRef+00000051  [ 3] 0x0000000147921366 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll+05182310 CG::StaticMatrixValueCG::elementReference+00000198  [ 4] 0x00000001d30f7744 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00620356 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00198932  [ 5] 0x00000001d30f6e17 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00618007 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00196583  [ 6] 0x00000001d30f72ad C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00619181 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00197757  [ 7] 0x00000001d30f75c5 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00619973 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00198549  [ 8] 0x00000001d30fe9fa C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00649722 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00228298  [ 9] 0x00000001d30ff5e4 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00652772 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00231348  [ 10] 0x00000001d30c9a60 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00432736 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00011312  [ 11] 0x00000001d333d2e3 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+03003107 DV::StructExplosionCb::updateInterface+00451491

Summary: Update diagram might crash for model reference hierarchy in accelerator mode using data dictionaries and enumerated types
Description: Update diagram might crash for model reference hierarchy running in accelerator mode if all the following conditions are satisfied: Enumerated types are defined in a Simulink data dictionary or as Simulink classes. For example, Enum.sldd  or Enum.m . Design data in a data dictionary refers to these enumerated types. Design data could be defined in: Enum.sldd . A data dictionary referring to Enum.sldd . For example, Param.sldd .  One or more submodels in a model reference hierarchy: Simulate in accelerator mode. Use design data defined in Enum.sldd  or Param.sldd .  You perform an update diagram on the top model with the submodels closed.
Workaround: Keep submodels that meet the crash criteria open during update diagram on the top model.

Summary: Header file name for enumeration data types is not validated against the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform standard
Description: The AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform standard prescribes that header file names for data types conform to the following pattern: impl_type_<symbol>.h. The AUTOSAR Blockset does not enforce this pattern for header file names for enumeration data types.?
Workaround: Write a Model Advisor check to find enumeration data types used in the model and verify the header file naming convention to match the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform standard.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Block reduction might result in incorrect simulation results for a model containing a Signal Conversion block
Description: The simulation results might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:? A Signal Conversion block is inside an enabled subsystem and it connects directly to a Root Outport block. The input signal for the Signal Conversion block has a rate of infinity.  The block reduction optimization could reduce the Signal Conversion block resulting in a constant, incorrect initial value from the enabled subsystem in simulation.
Workaround: Exclude the signal conversion block from the reduction by selecting the option Exclude this block from block reduction optimization.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect cuboid rotation angle returned by the bboxwarp function
Description: The bboxwarp  function returns incorrect rotation angles for cuboids. The function incorrectly returns counter-clockwise rotations (with respect to the positive direction of the rotation axis), for transforms with positive rotation angles. The correct rotation should be clockwise with respect to the positive direction of the rotation axis.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, you can negate the rotation angle returned by bboxwarp.?

Summary: Simulation 3D Scene Configuration block only loads scenes in the Unreal Engine??project?/Game/Maps folder
Description: In the Simulation 3D Scene Configuration block, if you set Scene source  to Unreal Executable ,? the block only loads scenes that are in the?Unreal Engine??project?/Game/Maps ?folder. If you set Scene  to a scene path that is not in /Game/Maps , the block does not load the scene.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing directly connected Bus Creator blocks
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains two Bus Creator blocks and two Bus Selector blocks. The outputs of the Bus Creator blocks are of the same type. One Bus Selector block chooses an element from the first Bus Creator block and inputs it to the second Bus Creator block. The other Bus Selector block chooses an element from the second Bus Creator block. The index of this element is higher than the index of the element chosen by the first Bus Selector block.  In the generated code, the same local variable gets incorrectly reused for both Bus Creator block outputs causing an incorrect value for an element of the second Bus Creator block output. Here is an example of the incorrect code pattern:? Bus1.a=1 Bus1.b=2 Bus1.c=3?  Bus1.a=5??Bus1.b=20??Bus1.c=Bus1.b?  In the incorrect code, the three elements of Bus1  are overwritten, so that the third element gets an incorrect value of 20 .?

Summary: MATLAB Datapath architecture for MATLAB Function blocks incorrectly throws error about unsupported functions using complex integer?types
Description: When using complex integer types in a MATLAB Function block that has HDL architecture set to?MATLAB Datapath,  HDL code generation might ? throw confusing error messages about unsupported functions when they may actually be supported. For instance, an error can be seen if a zeros  function is used with a 'like ' declaration of a complex integer variable:v = zeros(1, 1, 'like', var); ?
Workaround: Change the integer type to an equivalent fi type.

Summary: Incorrect indenting and keyword matching when using?end?to indicate last array index
Description: If you use the?end ?keyword to indicate the last index of an array within a block of code, indenting within the block of code is incorrect. In addition, MATLAB indicates incorrect language keyword pairs within the block of code. For example, this code produces incorrect indenting and keyword matching:? if 1 ? ? x = 1:10;? disp(x(1:end));end?

Summary: In batch mode, MATLAB incorrectly opens a dialog box.
Description: MATLAB can hang by incorrectly opening a modal dialog box in batch mode.  When you open MATLAB in batch mode, modal dialog boxes should not be allowed. However, when you close a model in the Simulink Editor (in batch mode), the "Save model before closing?" dialog box appears. This dialog box waits for the user to click Yes, No or Cancel.

Summary: Simulink PLC Coder does not support the Simulink.LookupTable, Simulink.Breakpoint, and Simulink.DualScaledParameter objects for code generation
Description: The Simulink.Lookuptable , Simulink.Breakpoint , and Simulink.DualScaled  parameter objects use the coderInfo  property for tunability purposes, that might result in the generated code containing variables linked to these objects having no initial values.? For this reason, Simulink PLC coder does not support these objects for code generation.?

Summary: Fixed-Point Designer > Matrix Operations?Library blocks throw confusing errors for MATLAB Function blocks during HDL code generation with floating-point types
Description: When generating HDL code for Fixed-Point Designer library blocks that perform QR decomposition, various failure messages might be seen around MATLAB Function blocks when the block is configured to use floating-point types. Floating point is not supported for these blocks using HDL code generation.

Summary: int(besselj(a, b*r)^2*r, r, 0, Inf) produces incorrect result
Description: The definite integral over integrands of the form besselj(a, ?b*r)^2*r  and the positive reals as the range of integration return an incorrect result. The integral does not converge and no finite result should be reported.?

Summary: PreserveDimensions check box is available for supported storage classes in Code Mappings > Data Defaults but has no effect for Data Stores.
Description: The PreserveDimensions  check box is available for Shared local data stores , Global data stores  and Internal data  in Code Mappings  > Data Defaults  tab when the storage class is set to any one of these: Const  Volatile  ConstVolatile  ExportToFile  ImportFromFile  FileScope   This check box has not effect because there is no support for Shared local data stores , Global data stores  and Internal data  until R2020a.

Summary: SIL simulation with Microsoft Visual C++ compiler option /TP produces compiler error
Description: The??Microsoft Visual C++ compiler option /TP  specifies that its filename argument is a C source file, even if the filename does not have a .c  extension. If you run a software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation and use the compiler option /TP , the simulation produces a compiler error.

Summary: Saving a model with Subsystem Reference having Simulink Function block fails
Description: A model with Subsystem Reference containing Simulink Function block may fail save operation with the below error message:  An error occured during a callback while saving "C:\localPath\Subsystem.slx" The previously version of this file (if any) has not been changed.  All the below conditions must be true for unsuccessful save of the edited top model:  The Subsystem Reference is edited in the context of top model  The Subsystem Reference have a Simulink Function block inside a Stateflow chart
Workaround: Directly modify and save the Subsystem file?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Product block configured with ShiftAdd architecture can generate incorrect HDL code for unsupported multiplication operations
Description: The ShiftAdd  architecture of the Product block is supported only for multiplication operations '*/', '/',  and Element-wise '.*'.   ?When?ShiftAdd  architecture is selected for any operation other than these supported operations it can lead to incorrect generated HDL code.

Summary: Induction Machine Squirrel Cage and Induction Machine Wound Rotor blocks do not include the?magnetic flux due to the leakage inductance in the saturation characteristic
Description: If saturation is enabled for the Induction Machine Squirrel Cage or Induction Machine Wound Rotor blocks, the characteristic does not include the?magnetic flux due to the leakage inductance. When saturation is enabled, the saturated voltage is not calculated correctly. The saturation characteristic obtained from simulation is different to the saturation data provided in the block parameterization.

Summary: When you create a copy of a model that includes a panel with dashboard blocks, the panel in the original model or copy model might appear to contain duplicate dashboard blocks when you open both models in the same MATLAB session
Description: When you create a copy of a model that includes a panel with dashboard blocks using copy-paste or by saving the original model with a different name, the panel in the original model or copy model might contain duplicate dashboard blocks when the models are open at the same time in the same MATLAB session.  The duplicate blocks are associated with the first model you opened, whether that model is the original or the copy, and appear in the second model you open. The duplicate blocks might be hard to see because they are positioned directly on top of the copy of the blocks associated with the second model. To detect the duplicate blocks, you can do one of the following: Change the size of the panel in the first model you opened without entering panel edit mode. The blocks inside the panel also resize, and so might the duplicate blocks in the second model you opened. Simulate the first model you opened and watch the panel in the second model you opened. The duplicate blocks in the second model update with values from the simulation of the first model.
Workaround: To avoid issues related to duplicate blocks, open only the original model or only the copy model, or open each model in a separate instance of MATLAB.

Summary: Dataflow execution domains?might produce incorrect results when?AdvancedOptControl parameter is set to -SLCI
Description: Normal mode simulation of dataflow execution domains might produce incorrect results when the AdvancedOptControl  parameter is set to -SLCI .
Workaround: Remove -SLCI from the AdvancedOptControl parameter.? For example, at the command-line enter:>> set_param(bdroot, 'AdvancedOptControl', '');

Summary: mlreportgen.report.Equation reporter might not produce correct image of equation
Description: An mlreportgen.report.Equation ?reporter might not produce the correct image of an equation.? For example, the following equation reporter produces an image where the underscore covers part of the equation: pt = mlreportgen.report.Report('eq','pdf');eqObj = mlreportgen.report.Equation('${T}_{egr\_wall}$');eqObj.FontSize = 10;add(rpt,eqObj);close(rpt);rptview(rpt);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Output variables not updated for sub-function block calls related to initialization
Description: Output variables are usually updated in SS_STEP/SS_UPDATE  case statements. In R2017a, Simulink PLC Coder stopped updating the output variables for function block calls related to initialization. For example, for the function block call: i0_IfActionSubsystem(ssMethodType := SS_INITIALIZE,  In1 := 1. 0);  b := i0_IfActionSubsystem. Out1; ? is not generated based on the assumption that?there will be corresponding SS_UPDATE/SS_OUTPUT call . Updating the outputs for function block calls related to initialization requires the caller variables to be updated as well, leading to special cases where generating?b := i0_IfActionSubsystem. Out1  might cause wrong answers.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated C code for divisions with floor or ceil rounding modes might be incorrect
Description: When all the following conditions are true, the generated C code for divisions might return incorrect results: There is a signed division by constant or signed modulo by constant The division rounds to 'Floor'  or 'Ceil'  (rounding is not specified on modulo)? The generated code contains functions with names that are a variant of the following (where the number following the? s ?is? 8 ,? 16 ,? 32 , or? 64 ) :? div_nde_s32_ceiling ? div_nde_s32_floor  Inside the above function definitions, the following code sequence exists:? (boolean_T)(numerator % denominator)?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model?containing a bus data type across a reusable atomic subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:?? A bus data type is specified on the input and output signals of a reusable atomic subsystem. In the Configuration Parameter dialog box, the?Eliminate superfluous local variables (Expression folding) ?parameter is selected.?  In the generated code, one or more temporary local variables might be incorrectly eliminated.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameter dialog box, clear the?Eliminate superfluous local variables (Expression folding)?parameter.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit Conversion block gives incorrect answer when converting data types during Normal mode simulation
Description: The Unit Conversion block might return incorrect answers for Normal mode simulation when the following conditions are true: The?Output data type ?parameter is set to Inherit: Inherit via back propagation , The input or output data type is a double or single, The output data type and input data type are different.  or The?Output data type ?parameter is set to?Inherit: Inherit via back propagation , The input or output data type is integer or fixed-point, The input signal is nonscalar.
Workaround: Set the Output data type parameter of the Unit Conversion block to?Inherit: Inherit via internal rule and?insert a Data Type Conversion block after the Unit Conversion block.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a

Summary: Error message generated for reals in Stateflow?Chart does not show location of unsupported code when Native Floating Point is used
Description: HDL Coder generates an error in presence of unsupported reals used in Stateflow?charts. However, the error message does not show the location of unsupported floating-point code. This can make fixing the error difficult especially for large models.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Changing value of global variable used in non-tunable block parameter does not trigger rebuild of model reference code
Description: In a referenced model, if a variable in the base workspace or a data dictionary is used in a non-tunable block parameter (for example, Sample time ), changing the value of the variable does not automatically cause the code for the referenced model to be regenerated. This affects accelerated simulation code and production code.
Workaround: Use one of these alternatives:Define the variable as a Simulink.Parameter object with non-Auto storage class.>> MyVariable = Simulink.Parameter(MyVariable);>> MyVariable.CoderInfo.StorageClass = 'ExportedGlobal';Simulate referenced models in normal mode instead of accelerator mode.Delete the generated files to force them to be regenerated.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Coder might return incorrect results while using?monotonic?direction for?issorted and issortedrows functions
Description: When all the following conditions are true, the generated code might return incorrect results: The MATLAB code uses? issorted ?or?issortedrows.  The input to? issorted ?and?issortedrows? is correctly sorted in descending order.  The direction input is  monotonic . The  MissingPlacement  option is specified as  first .  The input contains? NaN values. ? ?   When these conditions are true,?generated code? for?issorted ?and?issortedrows  functions can return false,? even though the vector input is correctly sorted.? Also, when all the following conditions are true, the generated code for?issortedrows?might return incorrect results:? The MATLAB code uses? issortedrows.  The direction input is? monotonic . The expected result from? issortedrows? is false.  The elements used to check the row ordering are arranged in ascending order in some columns and in descending order in others.   When these conditions are true, the?generated code? for the?issortedrows? function can return true instead of false.  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function Blocks or MATLAB System Blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.??

Summary: getgeodata fails due to changes in the FTP structure by NCBI
Description: NCBI reorganized the Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) data structure in February of 2020. Consequently, getgeodata  fails when retrieving series matrix files or SOFT data files.
Workaround: To work around this issue in R2018b or earlier, follow these steps.For accessing series (GSE) accession numbers:1. Connect to NCBI FTP server.ftpConnection = ftp('ftp.ncbi.nih.gov'); 2. The series matrix file is in the directory: /geo/series/GSE<X>nnn/<accessionnum>/matrix/, where <X> is the numeric portion of the accession number divided by 1000.gseAccessnum = 'GSE11287';cd(ftpConnection, '/geo/series/GSE11nnn/GSE11287/matrix/');seriesFileGz = sprintf('%s_series_matrix.txt.gz', gseAccessnum);seriesFile = sprintf('%s_series_matrix.txt', gseAccessnum);3. Download the file and unzip it.mget(ftpConnection, seriesFileGz);gunzip(seriesFileGz); 4. Read the unzipped file with geoseriesread.gsmGeoData = geoseriesread(seriesFile); For accessing DataSet (GDS) accession numbers:1. Connect to NCBI FTP server.ftpConnection = ftp('ftp.ncbi.nih.gov'); 2. The SOFT file is in the directory: /geo/datasets/GDS<X>nnn/<accessionnum>/soft/, where <X> is the numeric portion of the accession number divided by 1000. gdsAccessnum = 'GDS2602';cd(ftpConnection, '/geo/datasets/GDS2nnn/GDS2602/soft/');softFileGz = sprintf('%s.soft.gz', gdsAccessnum);softFile = sprintf('%s.soft', gdsAccessnum);3. Download the file and unzip it.mget(ftpConnection, softFileGz);gunzip(softFileGz);4. Read the unzipped file.gdsGeoData = geosoftread(softFile);

Summary: Error when starting a real-time model on system that uses Windows 10 May 2020 Update
Description: When starting a real-time model on a system that uses the Windows 10 May 2020 Update  operating system, the model does not start. MATLAB reports the error: Hardware timer cannot be allocated. Real-time kernel cannot run.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 5, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Assertion when string inputs are used for Stateflow custom code along with custom storage classes specified on arguments
Description: Assertion is encountered and code generation does not finish when Stateflow custom code functions have these elements: String inputs. Custom storage classes are applied on other arguments in the function. Custom storage classes are applied on inputs/outputs to the Stateflow charts.?

Summary: App containing tab group does not resize properly?when AutoResizeChildren?property is set to 'on'
