Description: If an app contains one or more tab groups and the AutoResizeChildren  property is set to 'on', then resizing the app can result in an unexpected layout.?For example, some components might be larger or smaller than expected, or some components might be clipped. The first resize might work as expected, but subsequent resizes might result in an unexpected layout.
Workaround: Set the Resize?property of the Figure?object to 'off' so that the app does not resize.If you are creating the app in AppDesigner:Select the uifigureIn the Inspector, go to the Position section and uncheck ResizeIf you are creating the app programmatically outside AppDesigner:f.Resize = 'off';where f is the handle to the Figure object.

Summary: systemIdentification app is not supported on MATLAB Online and should not launch
Description: systemIdentification app can be launched on MATLAB Online but using it to manage data or identify models throws errors.

Summary: Bullet points not imported correctly from DOORS 9
Description: When performing direct import of requirements?from a DOORS module, and when Object Text for some items has bullet points, and the RichText option is enabled, Simulink Requirements fails to display bullet points. Instead, the Description area in the UI displays "Bullet point" text.? When examined using command-line API, the Description value contains image tags like this:

Summary: Table data from Lookup Table blocks might be missing in generated HDL code in the presence of model references
Description: In the presence of model references, the table data from a Lookup Table block might be incorrectly removed in the generated HDL code. This results in HDL compilation or synthesis errors.

Summary: A model with string data with non-Auto custom storage class can generate code that does not compile
Description: String data with any of the following custom storage classes might generate code that does not compile: Volatile  ExportToFile  ImportFromFile  FileScope  Localizable  Struct  Reusable      The noncompilable code contains an incomplete character literal, such as the following: MyTestStruct.A[7] = '  For R2018b, only Update 5 and later are affected by this issue. For R2019a, only Update 5 and later are affected by this issue.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Links to imported DOORS Next items not listed in browser popup window on DOORS Next side
Description: The Simulink Requirements ?widget in the IBM DOORS Next (previously known as IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation, sometimes referred to as DNG) interface allows users to Query Links from SL  (Simulink), but the displayed table of links only includes direct links from Simulink to DNG items, and does not include links from Simulink objects to imported DNG items in Simulink Requirements sets.

Summary: The change in enabled status of Proof Objective is not considered while rerunning property proving analysis
Description: Simulink Design Verifier fails to consider the change in enabled  status of a Proof Objective  in following scenario:  1. RebuildModelRepresentation  option is set to IfChangeIsDetected   2. Simulink Design Verifier generates replacement model during compatibility check  3. R ebuilding of model representation for Property proving analysis is triggered by : Changing  enabled   status of any of the Proof Objective  blocks without making any changes to model or Design Verifier options.
Workaround: Modify value of RebuildModelRepresentation option to Always

Summary: Reaction expression in SimBiology models are not updated to use compartment-qualified species names after copying a compartment into the model
Description: Reactions referencing species by names in a one-compartment SimBiology model are not updated to use species names that are qualified by their compartment name. As a result, the resolution of the species in the reaction might be ambiguous.
Workaround: Update references to species names in reactions to names that are qualified with their compartment names. For example:radiodecayModel = sbmlimport('radiodecay');   decayReaction =  radiodecayModel.Reaction;m = sbiomodel('source model');comp = addcompartment(m, 'another compartment');addspecies(comp, 'x');copyobj(comp, radiodecayModel); disp(decayReaction.Reaction); % update references to species names to use names that are qualified with their compartment namedecayReaction.Reaction = 'unnamed.x -> unnamed.z';

Summary: Executing command-line scripts results in a "Unable to read the server info file" error
Description: When your system restarts and you execute command-line scripts for MATLAB Web App Server, you get the following error message: "Unable to read the server info file" . Despite the error message, the server remains operational and the web apps home page continues to be accessible.? The issue occurs due to corruption of the the file server.info  located in UserStorageRoot .
Workaround: To resolve this issue, restart the following services associated with MATLAB Web App Server using operating system commands:mw-webapps-launcher-R2020amw-webapps-R2020aWindows(Admin) Command Shell: SC STOP <service_name>Linux(sudo) Terminal: systemctl stop <service_name>?macOS(sudo) Terminal:?launchctl unload /Library/LaunchDaemons/com.mathworks.<service_name>.plist?After that you should be able to use webapps-start command to start MATLAB Web App Server again.?

Summary: Incorrect warning with Check use of default variants(mathworks.maab.na_0036) for?Label?variant control mode
Description: Model Advisor MAAB check for  na_0036 : Default variant  reports  No Default variant  for Variant Subsystem using Label  variant control mode.?  This warning message is incorrect as default variant is required only for Variant Subsystems using Expression  variant control mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Runtime error not reported when switch expression is non-finite valued
Description: When generating code for a program containing a switch statement, MATLAB Coder requires that the value of the switch expression is representable by the C type int . This is enforced through runtime checks in the generated code. When generating code for a MATLAB program containing: A double or single precision switch expression The expression is multiplied by a literal 0  The code generator might omit the necessary runtime check on the switch expression. This can lead to incorrect runtime behavior when the switch expression is non-finite valued. A simple example can be seen below in example_2172393 function y = example_2172393(x)   switch(x*0)      case 0         y = 11;      case 1         y = 22;      case 2         y = 33;      otherwise         y = 44     endend Generating code for this function with a double input argument and executing with a value of nan  or inf  should result in a runtime error.? >> codegen example_2172393 -args {0}>> example_2172393_mex(inf)Expected a value representable in the C type 'int'.  Found -nan instead.Error in example_2172393 (line 2)   switch(x*0) When this issue occurs, a runtime error is not thrown. Instead the generated code behaves as if the switch expression is valued as 0 >> example_2172393_mex(inf)ans =     11? See also Bug Report ID 2204299 ?

Summary: Code generated from model configured for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform does not honor function interface configuration settings for Function Caller blocks
Description: Codegenerated from model configured for an AUTOSAR adaptive system target does not honorthe Configure C/C++ Function Interface dialog box settings for Function Callerblocks. The generated code remains the same and does not reflect changes to the settings.

Summary: Creating an arduino object fails on macOS 10.15 Catalina
Description: When using MATLAB Support Package for Arduino Hardware version 19.2.1 on macOS 10.15 Catalina, creating an arduino object fails with the following error: "Failed to program the Arduino board, Click the following link to see the error log. Contact Technical Support if you need more help" .
Workaround: For the following MATLAB releases, a fix is provided as an update to the "MATLAB Support Package for Arduino Hardware" support package.To install the updated version, start Add-On Explorer:On the MATLAB Home tab, in the Resources section, click Add-Ons > Get Hardware Support Packages , orExecute?supportPackageInstaller?command in MATLAB command window.?MATLAB R2019bOpen Support Package Installer and update the "MATLAB Support Package for Arduino Hardware" support package to version 19.2.2 by uninstalling the support package and reinstalling it.

Summary: The Simulink Design Verifier generated harness model does not simulate with design model having Out Bus Elements of heterogeneous types
Description: If the design model has Out Bus Elements  of the same bus but, with mixed data types, Design verifier generated harness model will throw errors on simulation. You may notice a sample error message as below:? Input port cannot accept a virtual bus of mixed data types. 'Input Port 1' of 'model_harness/Out1' expects a signal with unique data type. However, it is driven by a virtual bus with elements of differing data types. ?One solution is to convert the virtual bus to a nonvirtual bus by placing a Signal Conversion block prior to the input port, with its 'Output' parameter set to 'Nonvirtual bus'. ?Alternatively, if this is an input port of a function-call subsystem, consider checking the 'Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs' option. This message is related to a hidden Outport block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code when using parameter, internal signal or state with default mapped pointer type storage class
Description: Incorrect code is generated when all the following conditions are true: A parameter, internal signal or state is set to use "Model default " storage class. The default setting in the Code Mappings editor for its category is a pointer type storage class ("ImportedExternPointer " or a pointer type storage class created by using CSC Designer). The parameter, internal signal or state is passed as an input to a model reference block function in the generated code.  Such a parameter, internal signal or state in the model reference function call is passed by pointer again, which results in a wrong answer in the generated C code. For example: If param  uses "Model default " storage class and the default specification in Code Mappings editor is "ImportedExternPointer ", param  should be a pointer to double. The correct function call should be ref(param) .  The incorrect one generated is ref(&param) . The ref()  function expects a pointer to double, but actually a pointer to pointer to double is passed into it.
Workaround: Directly choose the pointer type storage class in the individual modeling element, instead of using "Model default".

Summary: Requirements Editor becomes slow when opening requirement sets with large number of incoming links
Description: When loading a Simulink Requirements requirement set with a large number of incoming links, especially spread over a large number of link set (.slmx) files, the Requirements Editor might become slow to respond and refresh.

Summary: Name conflict between RS232 blocks and some DSP filters causes a compile error when building a model containing both
Description: The RS232 drivers for the legacy serial ports use an identifier for the Interrupt Identification Register (IIR) that conflicts with a variable name for some Infinite Impulse Response (IIR) filters. ?If both of these are used in the same model, there is a compile error when the model is built for the target.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mapped SI Engine block does not use engine temperature to determine engine torque when you select Input engine temperature
Description: If you select the Mapped SI Engine block parameter?Input engine temperature , the block does not use a lookup table that is a function of engine temperature, commanded torque, and engine speed. Instead, to determine the engine torque, the block incorrectly uses a lookup table that is a function of only commanded torque and engine speed.

Summary: System Design Description report generation fails when including links in linked libraries and dictionaries
Description: When a Simulink model is using a Data Dictionary, and the "Include links in linked libraries and dictionaries" ?preference is?enabled for the Requirements Report, the System Design Description report generation might fail. This error message is displayed when generating the report: A simulation of block diagram <DIAGRAM NAME>  is currently running. Consider using the cached search method option.

Summary: The Signal Labeler app's Import from Workspace?option allows importing signals with time information after importing labeledSignalSet objects in samples
Description: In?Signal Labeler  app, if you import a labeledSignalSet  object in samples using the?Import from Workspace ?option, you can then also import a signal with time information. The app should disable that capability in the import dialog box.

Summary: Error during integration of AUTOSAR Adaptive artifacts from an existing model into another one
Description: The following error is thrown when you set Instance Identifier  for a port in AUTOSAR Dictionary.  "Error occurred when evaluating apply callback"?  This issue only arises when a model for which the mapping was already created is integrated to a new model for which the mapping was not created.

Summary: AUTOSAR Component Quick Start for adaptive models does not honor specified component package
Description: When creating an adaptive component configuration, AUTOSAR Component Quick Start does not honor the Component package  path setting. The component is placed in the default package.
Workaround: Move the component to the desired package manually using the AUTOSAR Dictionary or the autosar.api.getAUTOSARProperties function.

Summary: MATLAB Datapath architecture of MATLAB Function block generates internal error when coder pragma is used as an input to a function
Description: Internal errors about unknown type or logical conditions might be generated during HDL code generation for MATLAB Datapath  architecture of the MATLAB Function block ? if pragmas are used as inputs to functions. An example is shown below. y = max( coder.const(2), x); ?
Workaround: Assign the pragma argument to an intermediate variable and pass that into the function instead.

Summary: Incorrect references to Polyspace Bug Finder?qualification reports for SEI CERT C: 2016 Coding Standards
Description: The DO Qualification Kit does not provide tests to qualify Polyspace Bug Finder checks for SEI CERT C:2016 Coding Standards. However, several artifacts inaccurately include references to these qualification reports: qualkitdo_bugfinder_qualificationreport_certc_2016.txt  qualkitdo_bugfinder_server_qualificationreport_certc_2016.txt?   Artifacts that reference the qualification reports include: qualkitdo_bugfinder_run.mlx  qualkitdo_bugfinder_criteria2_tqp.docx/pdf  qualkitdo_bugfinder_criteria3_tqp.docx/pdf  qualkitdo_bugfinder_tcp.docx/pdf

Summary: Incorrect references to Polyspace Bug Finder?qualification reports for SEI CERT C: 2016 Coding Standards?
Description: The IEC Certification Kit?does not provide tests to qualify Polyspace Bug Finder checks for SEI CERT C:2016 Coding Standards. However, several artifacts inaccurately include references to these qualification reports:? certkitiec_bugfinder_qualificationreport_certc_2016.txt  certkitiec_bugfinder_server_qualificationreport_certc_2016.txt?   Artifacts that reference the qualification reports include: certkitiec_bugfinder_run.mlx  certkitiec_bugfinder_tqp.docx/pdf  certkitiec_bugfinder_ug.pdf

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: C Caller block might generate incorrect results after?changing port specification
Description: After the first simulation or code generation of a model with a C Caller block, subsequent?changes to?the C Caller block's port specification might not trigger?necessary updates to the model's simulation or code generation results. The unchanged results might be incorrect. You may?encounter this issue in these two scenarios: Changing port dimension shape, for example changing size from [2 3] to [3 2] or from [6 1] to [2 3],?when the function being called?is configured to row-major Swapping the scope of two pass-by-pointer?function arguments?that are configured to input and output ports and that are of the same data type

Summary: Simulink models might fail to open
Description: A Simulink model might sometimes fail to open with an error similar to the following:    Error using open (line 146), File 'modelName.slx' does not contain a valid Simulink model in SLX format. Could not open source package. When this problem occurs, the size of the SLX seems normal and it can be opened in a Zip utility such as 7-Zip, however is cannot be opened in Simulink. This problem can occur with any of the newer MATLAB or Simulink file formats, but it is most likely to occur in larger files such as SLX, SLXC, MLTBX, CTF, and MLAPPINSTALL files.

Summary: Crash when calling codegen on coder.const of disp of coder.PrimitiveType
Description: Calling codegen  on a function which calls coder.const  on the disp  method of a coder.PrimitiveType  object, can cause MATLAB to crash. For example, given the function:  function foo     x = coder.const(feval("coder.typeof", 1));     coder.const(@disp, x);  end Running the following command causes MATLAB to crash:   codegen foo

Summary: Incorrect results for the Relational operator block when the block input is a nonscalar complex signal
Description: Incorrect objective status is reported by Simulink Design Verifier during the test-generation analysis when the objective belongs to the Relational operator  block whose input is a nonscalar signal of complex type.

Summary: Incorrect error message during code generation with variant S-Function
Description: TLC Errors are encountered when all the following conditions are met :  S-Function includes custom header files  The S-Function has a propagated variant condition  SystemTargetFile  is set to ert.tlc   Error message: Attempt to call a non-function value: SLibIfVariantConditionForm16b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect HDL Code might be generated when sharing or streaming optimizations are requested in the presence of clock-rate pipelining
Description: If sharing or streaming optimization is requested on a design with an Oversampling factor  set to? greater than 1, an unnecessary output delay might be added which generates incorrect code. This occurs when the number of delays that are allocated by the clock-rate pipelining optimization for the sharing or streaming region exactly matches the number of delays needed to perform the streaming or sharing calculation.
Workaround: If sharing or streaming is requested, always run the validation model to insure there is no mismatch between the original model and the generated model. If a mismatch occurs increase the oversampling factor by 1.?
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Crash when referencing an export-function model with Initialize/Reset/Terminate functions and scheduling rates with Schedule Editor
Description: When a model has the Model block that references an export-function model with Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Functions, MATLAB will crash when updating the model if the Model has its block parameter 'Schedule rates with:' set to 'Schedule Editor'.

Summary: Incorrect error message with sldvlogsignals when its?first argument?refers to a model with bus element port blocks
Description: sldvlogsignals  can take two arguments, where the first argument can be a 'model block' or a 'harness model' and the second is testcase index.? You may encounter an error message?Dot indexing is not supported  when the first argument in the above scenario happens to be a model block and that model block has In Bus Element  port blocks.

Summary: JSON data returned in response to a RESTful client that requests no data
Description: MATLAB Production Server incorrectly returns exactly one JSON element in the response from the server when both the following conditions occur: To execute a MATLAB function deployed to the server, the client uses RESTful API and JSON, and sets the request parameter?nargout ?to 0. The MATLAB function deployed to the server returns at least one data element.

Summary: Missing line break between memory section post-statement and pre-statement
Description: When all the following conditions are true, a line break is missed between memory section post-statement and pre-statement:The model generates shared utilities. At least two different memory sections are used.
Workaround: Manually add a new line character (\n) at the end of the user-specified string of the post-statement.

Summary: SIL/PIL simulation fails if model contains Reset Function block and model step function uses function prototype control
Description: If a model contains a Reset Function block and you provide function prototype control that uses a return argument, a software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation produces a compiler error for the generated file, xil_interface.c . The message indicates that the generated function accesses a return argument that does not exist.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized variable used when loop control variable passed to a function as an in-place argument
Description: For MATLAB code that meets the following conditions:? ?A for loop with the number of iterations below the specified LoopUnrollThreshold  value  The loop control variable is passed to a function as an in-place argument (that is, a = foo(a))   MATLAB Coder might incorrectly generate code for the unrolled loop using an uninitialized variable for the in-place argument corresponding to the loop control variable. This can lead to unpredictable behavior at runtime as the function called in the loop reads an uninitialized variable. For example,? function example_2166816()    for k = uint8(2):uint8(3)k = foo(k);    endendfunction io = foo(io)    coder.inline('never');    global out;    out = io;end Produces the following code which reads an uninitialized variable k void example_2166816(void){  unsigned char k;  if (!isInitialized_example_2166816) {    example_2166816_initialize();  }  foo(&k);  foo(&k);} The correct code can be seen below:? void example_2166816(void){  unsigned char k;  if (!isInitialized_example_2166816) {    example_2166816_initialize();  }  k = 2U;  foo(&k);  k = 3U;  foo(&k);}

Summary: Code generation may fail when combining?datetime arrays created by the NaT function with other datetime functionality supported for code generation
Description: Calling codegen  on a function may fail when both of these conditions are met: The function uses datetime  values created by the NaT  function The function also uses other supported datetime  functionality for code generation  When code generation fails in this manner, MATLAB? displays the error: ??? The left-hand side has been constrained to be non-complex, but the right-hand side is complex. Tocorrect this problem, make the right-hand side real using the function REAL, or change the initialassignment to the left-hand side variable to be a complex value using the COMPLEX function. For example, calling codegen  on the following function results in the above error. function x = exampleFun()   x = [NaT NaT NaT];   x(2) = datetime(2015,1,1,10,0,0);end
Workaround: Instead of creating NaT values with the NaT function, use the datetime constructor with NaN inputs.% Create a 1 by 3 NaT array via the datetime constructor?x = datetime(nan(1,3),0,0);

Summary: Setting GenerateMakefile to false might cause an error if using MinGW compiler
Description: If you set the configuration setting GenerateMakefile  to false: cfg = coder.config('lib');?cfg.GenerateMakefile = false;?codegen example.m -config cfg ?  and try to generate code with MinGW selected as your C/C++ compiler, you might get an error like: ??? Unknown compiler 'mingw64
Workaround: To work around this issue, set?GenerateMakefile to true:cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.GenerateMakefile = true;codegen example.m -config cfg??

Summary: Model simulation is slow or hangs if certain UI components are open during simulation
Description: Simulating models with Model Data Editor or Model Explorer open, causes increased simulation time and for large models may cause the model to hang at the termination step of sim.
Workaround: Ensure that Model Explorer and Model Data Editor are closed before simulating the model.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0602?displays an incorrect warning for Outports connected to Bus Selector signals
Description: Model Advisor JMAAB check for  jc_0602 :?Consistency in model element names ?reports the warning? The following model elements are not consistent with the connected signal name?for Outports  connected to signals from Bus Selector  block. This warning message is incorrect as? the signal name from Bus Selector block is resolved as "<signalName>" and the check includes "<", ">" symbols in the signal name for comparison with the Outport block names.

Summary: Unable to view confusion matrix for completed trials in Experiment Manager
Description: When trying to view the confusion matrix for a completed trial, the following is displayed in Experiment Manager:?? No data is available to generate the confusion matrix   Also, the following warning is shown in the MATLAB command window when running the experiment:? Warning: Variable 'trainingData' was not saved. For variables larger than 2GB use MAT-file version 7.3 or later   An alternative symptom of this problem is that the Results   directory in the project is unexpectedly large (up to 2GB per trial).? ?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when running Simulink Design Verifier on an empty subsystem
Description: MATLAB crashes if you run Simulink Design Verifier on a subsystem that contains no non-virtual blocks.?

Summary: uisetcolor dialog box appears behind UI figure?windows
Description: When uisetcolor  is executed from an App Designer or uifigure -based callback, the uisetcolor  dialog box appears behind the existing UI figure window. This issue appears only on a Windows platform.

Summary: Unexpected error when specifying a cross-section with very short edges in the General Flexible Beam block
Description: The following error may unexpectedly occur when the cross-section specification in the General Flexible Beam block includes some very short edges: The sectional properties computation is ill-conditioned for the specified cross-section. This might be due to overlapping or very close edges. Resolve this issue in order to simulate the model.   In particular, this can happen when the first and last vertices in any boundary of the specified cross-section are repeated. Due to the floating-point representation of numbers, the coordinates of these vertices may not be exactly equal, which leads to an extremely short edge between them. This particular issue exists in the sm_sailboat  example.

Summary: Detections from the Simulation 3D Probabilistic?Radar block are offset by one timestep.
Description: The detections output by the Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar block are offset by one timestep forward. All other blocks that communicate with the Unreal Engine do not have this offset.

Summary: Testcases are not extended when the configured parameters are of fixed-point type
Description: In Test Extension workflow, if the parameters are of fixed-point type, the provided test cases are not extended and the corresponding objectives are analyzed again.

Summary: Unable to filter datetime arrays in live script tables
Description: In a live script table that contains datetime  arrays, when you expand the table view dropdown to filter by dates, you cannot select a date using the date picker. You also cannot enter a date into the date text field.
Workaround: When filtering tables with datetimes in them, the filtering can be done by dragging the sliders in the plot to limit the datetime values.

Summary: Non-deterministic ordering of Rte_Read API calls for SenderReceiver ports in generated AUTOSAR code
Description: Non-deterministic ordering of?Rte_Read ?API calls in the generated code might occur if your AUTOSAR model meets the following conditions: At least two root inports are mapped to SenderReceiver  ports. The AUTOSAR data access mode of the root inports is set to ExplicitReceive .  Each time you generate code from the model, the locations of the Rte_Read  calls corresponding to the SenderReceiver  ports might change.

Summary: Error when generating HDL code for Product block set to matrix multiply using serial multiply-accumulate?DotProductStrategy
Description: If a Product block is configured to perform a matrix multiplication where the row-times-column operation is a scalar operation (i.e. 2x1 * 1x2 = 2x2 ), and the DotProductStrategy  in the HDL Block Properties dialog box is set to serial multiply-accumulate , an assertion can occur: Assertion failed:  b:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_transforms\hardwaremodepostprocessor.cpp:101:latency
Workaround: Use the default DotProductStrategy, that is,?fully parallel.

Summary: Reading Byte Array data in MDF-files might generate the error: Result buffer too small
Description: When reading MDF data from a channel that contains any Byte Array greater than 256 bytes in size, the following error might occur: ? Result buffer too small

Summary: MATLAB crash for AUTOSAR model if AUTOSAR mapping deletion affects library blocks
Description: An AUTOSAR model might crash MATLAB if all of these conditions exist: The model includes blocks that are defined in a library. The Simulink to AUTOSAR mapping of the model was deleted. The deleted AUTOSAR mapping included mapped signals associated with the library blocks.  The actions that can crash MATLAB include updating the block library, saving the model, or loading the model.
Workaround: Deleting the library-based blocks from the model and adding new block instances might resolve the issue.

Summary: Test Specification Report generation errors if Description section has a LineBreak
Description: If the description section of a test has a LineBreak  in it, an error occurs when you create a Test Specification Report for that test.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator can produce incorrect C/C++ code for concatenation of entry-point function inputs, if the inputs are categorical arrays with different sets of categories
Description: The generated C/C++ code might read uninitialized memory if the source MATLAB code concatenates two or more categorical arrays and if all of the following statements are true: The categorical arrays have different sets of categories? The categorical arrays are provided as inputs to the entry-point functions?  For example, consider this MATLAB function test : function out = test(c1,c2)out = cat(c1,c2);end Generate code for test ?by running these commands:  c1 = categorical({'Female';'Male'});c2 = categorical({'Male';'MissingInfo';'1'});codegen test -args {c1,c2} The generated code incorrectly assumes that both input arguments c1  and c2  have three categories each, so it reads further into memory than it should. This behavior might result in MATLAB crash or might produce an incorrect answer.?

Summary: Sharing a project by archive file or template fails with error "File system entry already exists"
Description: Creating an archive or a template from a project that contains a referenced project fails when all of the following conditions are true: The project contains a referenced project in the project subfolder. One of the referenced project folders starts with a number or a capital letter. You are working in a Windows environment.
Workaround: To share your project as an archive file, you can either:Save the archive as a Zip file.Exclude referenced projects from the archive.Note: Saving the archive as a Zip file may?have limitations if the project contains long paths.To share your project as a template, exclude referenced projects from the template.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: n-D Lookup table block might produce incorrect simulation results in accelerator mode for row-major algorithms with tunable table size
Description: n-D Lookup table block might produce incorrect simulation results when all of these conditions are true: Model:  Simulation mode?is set to 'Accelerator'  Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout  configuration parameter check box is  selected   n-D Lookup Table block: Number of table dimensions ?parameter is 2 or greater  Data specification ? parameter? is set to  'Table and breakpoints'  Support tunable table size in code generation  check box is selected

Summary: systemcomposer.exportModel() API returns error when exporting architecture models with identical interface element and interface names
Description: Using the systemcomposer.exportModel()  API with System Composer models returns an error when mapped interfaces to ports have elements with names that are the same as any interface name in the model dictionary. Error details : ? Error using systemcomposer.utils.exportModelClass/addPort2table Unable to perform assignment because the left and right sides have a different number of elements. Error in systemcomposer.utils.exportModelClass/addComp2table Error in systemcomposer.utils.exportModelClass Error in systemcomposer.exportModel?

Summary: HERE HD Live Map features do not support HERE Marketplace service
Description: HERE Technologies has migrated data catalogs for HERE HD Live Map from the legacy DataStore service to the Marketplace service. If you migrated to the Marketplace service for HD Live Map, using your credentials for the HD Live Map features (hereHDLMReader, importing maps into driving scenarios) results in the following error: "Invalid App ID and App Code combination."

Summary: Editing link in Requirements Editor might be lost when large number of links are loaded
Description: If you have a large number of links (For example, 10000 links) that are loaded in Requirements Editor and modify property of links quickly, you might loose the modifications that are applied while the Editor is processing the first modification. If you see a progress indicator displayed at the top of Requirements Browser, it means that the Requirements Editor is processing the modification. Any edits that are made while the progress indicator is shown might not be applied.

Summary: Blocks in Automated Driving Toolbox may not be accessible without Vehicle Dynamics Blockset license
Description: Opening a model containing simulation 3D scene configuration , camera block , and vehicle blocks  from Automated Driving Toolbox without a Vehicle Dynamics Blockset license shows the following warning:  In instantiating linked block 'TestModel/Simulation 3D Lidar' : Invalid setting in Simulation 3D Lidar block (mask) 'Simulation 3D Lidar' for parameter 'vehTag'

Summary: Running code that repeatedly expands a complex vector into a complex matrix might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when you perform either of these tasks many times: (1) Expand a complex row vector into a complex matrix by adding rows to it. (2) Expand a complex column vector into a complex matrix by adding columns to it.?
Workaround: Instead of increasing the size of your complex row or column vector by adding rows or columns to it, preallocate the maximum space required for the complex array. This workaround should prevent the crash.

Summary: Undefined function handling of macros that are aliases of other custom defined functions
Description: When importing custom code into Simulink, if there are macro functions defined in custom code which are aliases of other custom defined functions, these macro functions are marked as undefined functions. The auto-stubbing behavior proceeds only if the Undefined Function Handling  parameter is set to Use interface only .

Summary: Diary for MATLAB Job Scheduler jobs and tasks can be missing the first 256 characters
Description: When accessing diary of MATLAB Job Scheduler jobs and tasks using: >> job.diary();?  >> task.Diary?  the first 256 characters are missing if the diary grows from a non-zero size of less than or equal to 256 characters to more than 256 characters.? In this case, the first 256 characters of diary is lost, but all remaining diary is kept.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 5, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Missing requirement links for Stateflow objects in library after resolve-push
Description: If you have a Stateflow chart in a referenced library, and objects inside the chart have requirements traceability links, these links might break after resolving the link to this library and using the "push" option to brings updates from model to library. When viewed in the Requirements Editor, the links appear with a warning, and the source is unresolved. In the Requirements Perspective view,?the chart does not display requirement link badges, as if there?are no links.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 5, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect datatypes in generated test cases for models containing variants and model blocks
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might have incorrect datatypes for the models containing model blocks and variants when generating test cases/counterexamples.This behavior is seen when all the below conditions are TRUE : The model blocks are inactive due to variant propagation. The option?Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  is selected, which ?influences the data types of the model.

Summary: Error when trying to link with IBM DOORS Next artifact by manually entering the link target ID
Description: When linking with requirements in IBM DOORS Next (previously known as IBM DOORS Next Generation or DNG), the manual input option is provided for users who cannot use the "Simulink Requirements" widget in the DNG Mini Dashboard. Ideally, when a DNG artifact is selected in the browser page, MATLAB is notified, and links can be created using the "Link to Selected Item(s) in DNG" context menu shortcut. However, when the Simulink Requirements widget in the DNG Mini Dashboard is not installed, or is unable to communicate with MATLAB due to server configuration limitations, Simulink Requirements displays a warning that "Nothing is selected in DNG", and?allows to enter the link target ID manually. When the manual ID is provided, there is an error dialog box: "Browser context not known".
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Generating audio plugins might fail with a supported third-party compiler
Description: In some cases, plugin generation might fail to complete when using a supported compiler. This issue affects the Microsoft Visual C++ 2019 product family and Mac Xcode 11.x.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use a different compiler setup for your platform:?On Windows, select VS2017, or include the VS2017 toolset in the VS2019 installation.On Mac, select Xcode 9.x or 10.x.

Summary: Font not honored when HTML that is imported contains a font-family value enclosed in double quotes
Description:
When you use one of the DOM API objects or methods that import HTML and the source HTML includes a font-family attribute value enclosed in double quotes, the imported value incorrectly includes the double quotes. The imported font family value that includes the double quotes is invalid and ignored in a report. For example, with this HTML, the Noto Sans font is not honored:

Hello Word

With this HTML, the Noto Sans font is honored:

Hello Word

?These are the DOM API objects or methods that you can use to import HTML: mlreportgen.dom.HTML addHTML mlreportgen.dom.HTMLFile addHTMLFile?
Workaround: In the source HTML, if a font-family attribute value is enclosed in double quotes, change the double quotes to single quotes.?

Summary: XCP CAN Data Stimulation block code generation error
Description: Code generation for the XCP CAN Data Stimulation block results in the following error:Error: Errors occurred - aborting

Summary: Import or Update from Microsoft Word fails with an error popup
Description: When importing requirements from Microsoft Word, or when attempting to update a Requirement Set that was previously imported from Microsoft Word, the process may fail with an error popup:  Error getting data from Word: RPC call rejected by the callee.   This malfunction might be more frequent on machines using Italian locale settings.
Workaround: * When performing original import, do not enable the "RichText" option, and import as plain text.* When updating an existing RichText import, minimize the document before you click the "Update" button.? If the document window pops-up to the foreground at the start of the update process, minimize it again immediately.

Summary: Running the EV and HEV reference applications with default settings results in solver errors
Description: The EV and HEV reference applications are unable to simulate the entire drive cycle with the default ode23tb  solver settings. A numerical error stops the simulation. This issue affects these reference applications: HEV Multimode HEV Input Power-Split HEV P0 HEV P1 HEV P2 HEV P3 HEV P4 EV  The conventional vehicle reference application is not impacted.
Workaround: With the reference application loaded, set the model solver to?ode23.?From the MATLAB command-line, enter:>>set_param(bdroot,'Solver','ode23')Run the simulation to complete the entire drive cycle.

Summary: Discrepancies when comparing Simulink Code Inspector test results
Description: These discrepancies exist when comparing the expected test results against the actual results in the Simulink Code Inspector Tool Qualification Report.?  Test Case : MATLABFcn_pos_6 ? Expected results summary: Overall Status, PASSED Verification Status, VERIFIED Traceability Status, TRACED   Actual results summary:? Overall Status, FAILED Verification Status, FAILED_TO_VERIFY Traceability Status, FAILED_TO_TRACE    Test Case : SharedUtil_C_pos ?? Expected results summary:? Overall Status, PASSED) Verification Status, VERIFIED) Traceability Status, TRACED? Utils Verification Status, VERIFIED   Actual results summary:? Overall Status, FAILED_TO_VERIFY? Verification Status, VERIFIED Traceability Status, TRACED Utils Verification Status, FAILED_TO_VERIFY

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In a rate-based model with periodic event ports, if a function-call initiator?is in a nonvirtual subsystem, the sorted order might be incorrect
Description: Simulink might produce an incorrect sorted order for a rate-based model that meets the following conditions:The Model block parameter Schedule rates  is selected, with the Schedule rates with:  option set to Ports . Both of the following are in a different nonvirtual subsystem from the Model block: A function-call initiator to a periodic event port. Source blocks for data input ports of the corresponding discrete rate.   In such a case, the data source block might be incorrectly sorted after the function-call initiator, which might lead to incorrect numerical results.
Workaround: Avoid using function-call initiator and data source blocks in a different non-virtual hierarchy from the Model block or use block priority to make data source blocks sort before the function-call initiator.

Summary: Observer port moved to new signal shows link to original signal
Description: If you link an observer port to a signal in an Observer model, save and reopen the model, and then, move that observer to another signal, the signal highlighting does not fully update. Although the observer port is linked to the new signal, the original signal still shows as also being connected to the observer port.

Summary: Java exception error and incorrect visualization for some CAD geometry files when using Reduced Order Flexible Solid block
Description: When simulating a model using the Reduced Order Flexible Solid block with its Graphic Type  set to Partitioned Geometry , a Java exception error and an incorrect Mechanics Explorer visualization might occur for some CAD geometry files. As a result, the flexible body does not appear in the visualization.

Summary: Error generating Test Specification Report
Description: In Test Manager, if a Test Case with iterations is used for Test Specification Report generation, an error message about an undefined function or variable might occur.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating HDL code for models configured for IP Core Generation workflow that introduce blocks with unused ports and have critical path estimation enabled
Description: Sometimes, generating HDL code for models configured for?IP Core Generation ?workflow that has?Generate high-level timing critical path report ?enabled.might cause a MATLAB crash when the model introduces certain blocks with unused ports.

Summary: MATLAB crashes during code generation for an AUTOSAR model that has mapped signals, states, or data stores
Description: MATLAB crashes during code generation for an AUTOSAR model if all of these conditions exist: Model includes blocks that are defined in a custom library and that library is not accessible. Model includes signals, block states, or data stores that are mapped to AUTOSAR variables. Model was saved using MATLAB R2019b.   The model gets corrupted during the save operation. The model corruption leads to MATLAB crashing during code generation.
Workaround: Use this MATLAB command, where model is the name of the AUTOSAR model,? to delete the AUTOSAR mapping for the model:autosar.api.delete(model);Then, create a new mapping, save the model, and generate code.?

Summary: Compilation and simulation hang when Test Sequence editor is open
Description: For a model with model references that contain a large number of buses, if there are Test Sequence blocks in the model and the Test Sequence editor is open, when you update or simulate the model, the compilation and simulation hang at "Updating" or "Initializing."

Summary: Crash when tab-completing the properties of an invalid class in the workspace
Description: When editing MATLAB source code for a class definition while instances of that class exist in the workspace, it is possible to crash MATLAB. The crash can occur while attempting to tab-complete properties of a class when the source code that defines the class is saved in an invalid state. Specifically, this sequence of events can cause a crash: ?1. The source code for a MATLAB class is created or modified and then saved while it is in a syntactically good state. ?2. An instance of the class is created in workspace. ?3. The file is then edited and saved while in a syntactically bad state. The saved version of the class invalidates the instance created in step 2. ?4. While typing input arguments to a function or method call, tab-completing the properties of that invalid class instance can result in a crash.
Workaround: To avoid this crash:Avoid saving the source code file when the file is in a syntactically bad state.If a class instance is in such a state, then avoid tab-completing it.

Summary: MATLAB and Simulink might appear to hang while loading some CAD geometry files in the Reduced Order Flexible Solid block
Description: When using the Reduced Order Flexible Solid block with CAD geometry files which take some time to load, MATLAB and Simulink might appear to hang every time you open the block dialog or refresh the dialog's graphics pane. Eventually, control returns. Closing the dialog does not return control immediately.

Summary: Assertion detected in generated HDL code from IFFT/FFT HDL Optimized block
Description: Simulink simulation and HDL simulation doesn't match for EndOut signal if only EndOut output port is selected. This can be detected by running cosim simulation or stand alone HDL test bench.
Workaround: To avoid assertion, check/uncheck both StartOut and EndOut option on the mask.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Math Function block mod and rem functions might produce incorrect results in generated code for large inputs due to overflow
Description: The Math Function block might produce incorrect results in generated code when all the following conditions are true:? The Function  parameter is set to mod ?or  rem  Block input ports 1 (a) and 2 (b) are of floating-point data types    The quotient of a/b  overflows to?Inf ?or?-Inf   The result in simulation is correct (zero ), but the result in generated code is incorrect (non-zero ). ?Note: This bug also affects MATLAB Coder (Bug report ).

Summary: Extra paragraph when a block hole in a?Word template table entry is filled with a table
Description: To create a block hole in a table entry in a Word template, you can insert a paragraph in the table entry, select the paragraph, and add a rich text content control that includes the paragraph. If the report generation program fills the hole with a table, an extra paragraph appears in the table entry in the generated report.

Summary: MATLAB warns if axes toolbar has multiple drop-down menus
Description: If you create an axes toolbar with multiple drop-down menus, then the axes toolbar does not work and MATLAB throws a warning such as: Warning: Error updating AxesToolbar.Dimensions of arrays being concatenated are not consistent.

Summary: Check boxes in GUIDE Menu Editor do not show checked state.
Description: When viewing the properties of a menu in the GUIDE Menu Editor, the following check boxes always show unchecked regardless of their true state: Separator above this item  Check mark this item  Enable this item   Selecting the check box toggles the state but always shows it as unchecked.
Workaround: In the Menu Editor, select a menu. Then click on the?More Properties...?button to view or edit the menu properties using the property inspector.

Summary: decomposition returns incorrect results when repeatedly solving some complex sparse linear systems
Description: When decomposition  solves a complex sparse linear system with a full real right-hand side, subsequent solves of the same system with sparse right-hand sides produce incorrect results. The following example creates a non-Hermitian complex matrix A and uses decomposition  to solve a few linear systems: one with a full real right-hand side, and then one with a sparse right-hand side that has some structural zeros.?The first solution is correct while the second solution, and any subsequent solutions, give incorrect results. rng default;n = 100;A = sprand(n,n,0.1) + 1i*speye(n);dA = decomposition(A);f = rand(n,1); % Full real RHSs = double(sparse(rand(n,1) > 0.5)); % Sparse RHSx = dA \ f; % Correct initial solvenorm(f - A*x)ans =   3.6490e-15y = dA \ s; %?INCORRECT second solvenorm(s - A*y,'fro')ans =    4.1527z = A \ s; %?Compare second solve to backslasherr = norm(y-z,'fro') %?Should be exactly 0err =   15.4031
Workaround: A workaround is to always make the right-hand side full by using the full function.rng default;n = 100;A = sprand(n,n,0.1) + 1i*speye(n);dA = decomposition(A);f = rand(n,1); % Full real RHSs = double(sparse(rand(n,1) > 0.5)); % Sparse RHSx = dA \ f; %?Correct initial solvenorm(f - A*x)ans =   3.6490e-15y = dA \ full(s); %?Correct second solve if you use full(s) to make RHS fullnorm(s - A*y,'fro')ans =   5.5542e-15z = A \ full(s); %?Compare with backslasherr = norm(y-z, 'fro') %?Should be exactly 0err =     0

Summary: Error when using MATLAB Compiler with standalone charts
Description: When compiling an executable that involves a standalone Stateflow chart, MATLAB Compiler throws an error.?  In R2019b, the runtime error says:   Cannot get debuggable line numbers from <path to chart> . This is not a valid operation for this file type.    In R2020a, the compile error says:   Failed to build CTF file: <path to CTF file> . Details: fl:filesystem:PathNotFound

Summary: Custom toolbox fails to install with the error "Something unexpected occurred"
Description: If you try to install a custom toolbox (.mltbx  file) and get this error message?"Something unexpected occurred" , then it might be because?the toolbox name contains invalid characters for your file system. Characters such as < , > , and :  are reserved and cannot be used in file or folder names on some system types.
Workaround: Ask the author of the toolbox to complete both of these actions:Change the toolbox name so that it does not include invalid characters.Republish a new .mltbx installation file.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Enumeration as variant control variable causes build errors during c++ code generation
Description: Code generation might lead to build errors when all the following conditions are met : Model contains a Variant subsystem using an enumeration as a variant control  The Variant Subsystem has the option?Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditional? selected  The SystemTargetFile? is ert.tlc   and the TargetLang ?is C++

Summary: In Test Manager, capturing baseline for iterations? with names that include special characters causes MATLAB to crash
Description: If an iteration name in Test Manager contains any special characters such as? < , > , : , " , / , \ , | , ? , or *,?then capturing baseline for that iteration causes MATLAB to crash on Windows platforms.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect C/C++ code generation from MATLAB code that has a structure that contains handle objects or system objects and is returned from a function
Description: Code generation from MATLAB might produce incorrect C/C++ code if your MATLAB code satisfies all of these conditions:  One of your MATLAB functions uses a structure. One of the fields of this structure is a handle object or a system object. After  the structure is copied from another variable, a new field is introduced into the structure by using a field assignment operation. The structure is returned from the function in which the object was created. In the calling function, the object is used (for example, its properties are accessed).?  Cause:  The combination of adding a field after a structure copy and a handle object that escapes from the function that created it causes the generated code to allocate the handle object in the wrong stack frame. In such situations, the handle object is allocated in the stack frame of the callee instead of the caller.  Here is an example MATLAB function that exhibits this issue: function result = xg2156502(arg)x = local(arg);result = x.f.p1;function result = local(arg)coder.inline('never')x.f = MyHandleClass;x.f.p1 = arg;result = x;result.g = arg; Here, MyHandleClass  is a class that inherits from handle :classdef MyHandleClass < handle    properties        p1    endend This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: mod?and rem functions might return nonzero values when there is overflow
Description: For standalone targets, the code generated for?mod(a,b) ?and?rem(a,b)  might return nonzero values if the inputs a  and b  are of floating-point type and the quotient a/b ?overflows to Inf ?or -Inf . In such cases, MATLAB returns zero. This issue affects standalone code generated by using MATLAB Coder or Simulink Coder.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 5, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: ?Height and Width properties not honored for a DOM Image object with a ScaleToFit format object
Description: An mlreportgen.dom.Image  object does not honor the Width  and Height  properties if the Style property contains an mlreportgen.dom.ScaleToFi t format object.? Instead, the image is always scaled to fit the page in the generated report.
Workaround: Remove the mlreportgen.dom.ScaleToFit object from the Style property of the mlreportgen.dom.Image object.

Summary: Short equations in a report might be cropped
Description: Short equations in a report might be cropped if the Windows display scaling is set to a value other than 100%.
Workaround: Open the Windows Settings app.??Search for "Make everything bigger".Set? "Change the size of apps and text on the main display" to 100%.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Error might prevent generating code for a multirate subsystem
Description: Right-clicking a subsystem and selecting?C/C++ code > Build This Subsystem ?might produce an error when all of the following conditions apply: The subsystem you right-click is an atomic subsystem that contains another atomic subsystem. The subsystem it contains has both infinite and discrete sample times. The subsystem it contains directly connects an input port to an output port.  The error message is: Undefined function 'eq' for input arguments of type 'cell'.

Summary: Unexpected ReqIF file XML namespace handling
Description: When importing requirements from a ReqIF file, the?XML namespace handling is unexpected / incorrect. For example, importing a ReqIF file with a locally declared namespace can result in an error message such as:?ReqIF import error: Type 'reqifxhtml:div' has not been found in the registry . A local namespace is in the form of : <reqif-xhtml:div xmlns:reqif-xhtml="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">?   reqif-xhtml  and reqifxhtml  can differ between ReqIF files. Also, exporting requirements to a ReqIF file can result in an omitted declaration of XHTML namespace. If this happens, third-party tools might be unable import the ReqIF file.
Workaround: If Simulink Requirement fails to import ReqIF file with the error message mentioned in the description:Open the ReqIF file in text editorLocate the opening <REQ-IF> tag at the beginning of the fileDefine the following attribute at the end of the tag: xmlns:reqif-xhtml="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml"For example, the starting tag may be edited to become:<REQ-IF xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xml:lang="en" xmlns="http://www.omg.org/spec/ReqIF/20110401/reqif.xsd" xmlns:reqif-xhtml="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated from two or more dsp.LMSFilter objectsproduces uninitialized output data or memory corruption (wrong answers,crashes, etc).
Description: When code is generated from a function that contains two or more dsp.LMSFilter objects, the generated code for one of the objects produces uninitialized output data or causes memory corruption when all of the following is true:  The ˇ®OutputWeightsˇŻ property for one or more of the objects is set to ˇ®AllˇŻ. The ˇ®OutputWeightsˇŻ property for one or more of the other objects is set to ˇ®LastˇŻ. The multiple objects are configured identically except for the ˇ®OutputWeightsˇŻ property.?  In the generated code, both the LMS filter objects call the same generated function. The two objects have different ˇ®OutputWeightsˇŻ property values (ˇ®LastˇŻ and ˇ®AllˇŻ) and therefore should call different generated functions. This behavior causes issues such as uninitialized output data or memory corruption. Memory corruption may cause the generated code to crash, produce wrong answers, or otherwise behave unpredictably.??
Workaround: Change the ˇ®OutputWeightsˇŻ property of the object that was set to ˇ®LastˇŻto ˇ®AllˇŻ instead, so that the objects are configured identically. Then,explicitly extract the last vector from the matrix of the output weights forthat object.

Summary: Calling the serialport or arduino function on Linux might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: When calling the serialport ?or  arduino ?function on Linux, MATLAB might crash. ?This occurs if MATLAB is launched from a user account that does not have write permissions for the /var/lock/  folder. The MATLAB crash log might include the following line: Stack Trace (from fault):  [? 0] 0x00007f2e6bf7db44 fileno_unlocked+00000004 at /usr/lib/libc.so.6+494399 (file not found)
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following steps to allow write permissions for the?/var/lock/?folder.Log in to the user account that you will be launching MATLAB from.Open a Terminal window.In the Terminal window, use the?chmod command to grant permission to write to the?/var/lock/?folder.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when saving AUTOSAR model
Description: Saving an AUTOSAR software component model might cause MATLAB to crash, with a stack trace that contains logs like the following: mcos::factory::Marshaler<sl::mi::HandleVectorPolicy>::toMForPtr<std::vector<autosar::StateMapping *,std::allocator<autosar::StateMapping *> > *> at b:\matlab\src\sl_services_mi\export\include\sl_services_mi\slsv_mcos.hpp:1041

Summary: logsout property of sltest_simout object is empty for parallel tests
Description: When running tests in parallel, the sltest_simout.logsout  property of the test results is empty in the Custom Criteria  section of the Test Manager.

Summary: Run with Stepper does not return simulation output for models with SaveFormat set to Structure with time
Description: When using Run with Stepper on a model that uses Structure with time  format, the simulation output of the model is not captured with the test case result.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Real matrix input to Hermitian block with output type set to complex leads to mismatch in generated HDL code
Description: Generated code for the Math Function block is incorrect when the following conditions are true:  The Function  parameter set to  hermitian   Input to the block is a real fixed-point matrix  The Output signal type  on the block is set to  complex   The generated code performs only a 'complex' operation on the input instead of a complex and  transpose operation.

Summary: Run with Stepper might cause test case failures for test harness models
Description: Using Run with Stepper on a test harness model might error unexpectedly and fail the test case.

Summary: Missing source files or compiler options in Polyspace project when?polyspace-configure?fails to read compiler options file
Description: In some situations, the?polyspace-configure ?command?fails to open the cached version of a compiler options file and falls back to the version of the options file in the file system. In these situations, if the options file was modified during the build, polyspace-configure  will only use the last version of the options file, leading to missing source files or compiler options in the generated project.
Workaround: If possible, do not edit the compiler options file during the compilation. Reusing the same file name should be also avoided.

Summary: Properties for hosts in Admin Center do not show
Description: Ifyou right-click on any host in the Hosts pane in Admin Center and select Properties... , then the hostproperties dialog does not show.

Summary: HDL Coder generates incorrect code for Gain block with complex? gain values
Description: When the Gain  value is a? complex number whose absolute value (abs) is a power of 2, HDL Coder incorrectly generates code for the design.For instance, complex gain value of?0.7071 - 0.7071i  is incorrectly marked as a power of 2.

Summary: Compatibility check may fail for models containing Subsystem Reference blocks
Description: For a model containing?Subsystem Reference? blocks, Simulink Design Verifier may fail during the preprocessing phase of compatibility check with the following message: Checking Compatibility Failed. ? Simulink Design Verifier failed due to errors.  Additionally, the following error message may get reported in the Diagnostic Viewer window:Subsystem reference block <Block Path> has unapplied changes.  You may also encounter the above error message when you execute sldvblockreplacement ?on the model ?

Summary: Nested uigridlayout components do not transform mouse coordinates properly.
Description: A uigridlayout parented to another uigridlayout does not properly transform mouse coordinates, resulting in an incorrect value for the figure's CurrentPoint property.

Summary: Inaccurate time in MATLAB Profiler for the first function call recorded
Description: In the MATLAB? Profiler, the reported time for the first function call is greater than it should be. This problem only affects the first function listed in the Profiler window. It does not affect any subsequent calls or their recorded times.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Evaluating a fistree object silently produces incorrect results when multiple inputs use same values
Description: Evaluating a fistree   object?can silently produce incorrect results when both of the following conditions are true: The fistree  object contains more than two FISs One fistree  input is connected to the inputs of multiple FISs within the fistree   This issue can produce incorrect results when evaluating a?fistree  object using the?evalfis ? function. Also, the issue can produce incorrect parameter values when tuning a fistree  object using the tunefis  function.
Workaround: To workaround the problem, first remove any input-to-input connections from the fistree?object by modifying the Connections?property of the fistree object. Then, specify the same input data for all previously connected inputs when evaluating the fistree?object.For example, suppose your?fistree?object?fisT?contains four FISs with the following configuration.The first input of each of the first three FISs are connected.The second input of each of the first three FISs are connected.The outputs of the first three FISs are connected to the three inputs of the fourth FIS.fisT?has the following Connections property value.fisT.Connections = ["fis1/input1" "fis2/input1";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis1/input1" "fis3/input1";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis1/input2" "fis2/input2";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis1/input2" "fis3/input2";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis1/output1" "fis4/input1";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis2/output1" "fis4/input2";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis3/output1" "fis4/input3"];Evaluating fisT as follows can silently produce incorrect results. Here, input is a two-column array of input data.?output = evalfis(fisT,input);To remove the input-to-input connections, set?Connections?as follows.fisT.Connections = ["fis1/output1" "fis4/input1";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis2/output1" "fis4/input2";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis3/output1" "fis4/input3"];When evaluating fisT, use the same input data for the previously connected inputs. In this case, you repeat the same input data columns three times.output = evalfis(fisT,[input input input]);

Summary: The chirp function throws an error with negative frequencies
Description: The? chirp ?function produces an error message when the frequency  f1 , the third input argument, is specified as a?negative scalar.  For example:  y = chirp(-2:1/1e3:2,100,1,-200);? % Errors out

Summary: Compatibility check results in unclear error message for a model with Initialize-Reset-Terminate (IRT) Subsystem inside a Model Reference
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check throws an error when a model has an Initialize-Reset-Terminate  (IRT) Subsystem inside a Model Reference  block. The error does not show any specific reason for the incompatibility.Sample error message: Index exceeds the number of array elements(2)

Summary: Linker errors related to ara::log::CreateLogger API when AUTOSAR adaptive generated code is compiled using stub files and MathWorks ara cluster implementations
Description: Certain ara::log  header files use?ara::core::StringView , and due to MathWorks header file dependencies on boost, local boost files are used to compile the code. But the use of local boost files results in linking failures with MathWorks ara  shared libraries. The linking error is as follows: undefined reference to 'ara::log::CreateLogger(boost::basic_string_view<char, std::char_traits<char> >,boost::basic_string_view<char, std::char_traits<char> >, ara::log::LogLevel)'collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status

Summary: Compiling MATLAB Data API code with VS2019 Community Edition with Conformance Mode enabled generates an error with the message Error C2760 syntax error: unexpected token 'identifier', expected 'expression'
Description: In Microsoft Visual Studio 2019 Community Edition, the Conformance Mode option is set to /permissive-  (enabled) by default.? This causes the compiler to generate errors not generated by older versions of VS, or by other versions of Visual Studio 2019. In particular, it generates an error when a typedef  statement contains a dependent type name followed by an asterisk and parameter name, and the sequence is not preceded by the keyword typename . There are several such instances in the file ArrayFactory.hpp .
Workaround: You can use any one of these possible workarounds: Use mex or mbuild instead of Visual Studio to buildapplications. Under the Visual Studio project properties, under C/C++ -> Language, set Conformance Mode to No. Use a version of Visual Studio other than Visual Studio 2019 Community Edition.Edit extern/include/MatlabDataArray/ArrayFactory.hpp to insert the keyword typename before the dependent type names impl::ArrayFactoryImpl and impl::ArrayImpl in each of the four lines of source code for which the compiler generates errors.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b

Summary: Multiplier partitioning combined with sharing can result in a compilation error in the generated model
Description: When multiplier partitioning and sharing are both requested for a Product block, the generated model might result in a model compilation error. This model compilation error prevents validation model simulation, HDL testbench generation, and cosimulation model simulation.
Workaround: In the generated model, for the output of the signum block which model compilation throws an error, place a Data Type Conversion block and set the type to fixdt(1, 2, 0). This will allow validation model simulation and HDL testbench generation once HDL code generation has already been run.

Summary: Unable to export live script with more than 30 figures to PDF
Description: Exporting a live script to PDF fails if the live script has more than 30 figures. The following error will be thrown in MATLAB command window : [ERROR] FOUserAgent - Image not found.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when using y=f(y) style MATLAB function in a Simulink function inside a Stateflow chart
Description: Generated code using VAR_IN_OUT could compute an incorrect result when using a y=f(y) style MATLAB function block with identical input and output variables inside a Simulink function, within a Stateflow chart.??
Workaround: The y=f(y) style MATLAB function block with identical input and output variable?should be used directly in Stateflow chart instead of being inside Simulink Function in Stateflow chart.

Summary: Incorrect values of Last Modified On in Referenced Requirements imported from IBM Rational DOORS 9
Description: When items are imported from IBM Rational DOORS (versions 9.0+) as referenced requirements, the timestamps for the Last Modified On  property  are not captured correctly: the time part defaults to 00:00:00, which might also result in incorrect date part displayed. For example, after importing an item modified on Feb 2 14:15:25, the imported timestamp is Feb 2 00:00:00, and when working in EST zone, GMT Feb 2 00:00:00 will be displayed as Feb 1 17:00:00, which would look as if the requirement was updated on the previous day. This makes meaningful change impact analysis impossible because the displayed dates are incorrect.

Summary: Result status in Tool Qualification Report does not match status in Simulink Code Inspector Report
Description: In the Simulink Code Inspector report, the result status for the?DefaultTransition  model object is VERIFIED . However, in the tool qualification report, the Expected Result Details and Actual Result Details sections specify the result status for DefaultTransition  as FAILED_TO_VERIFY  in test cases Stateflow_neg1  and Stateflow_neg9 . This is a mismatch in results between the Tool Qualification Report and Simulink Code Inspector Report.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code may have missing initial values for tunable parameters in the model workspace
Description: Simulink PLC Coder support two forms of tunable parameters, namely Simulink.Parameter  object or?non-Simulink. Parameter  object which are marked as tunable (by using model configuration setting Optimization  > Signals and Parameters  > Configure  > Global(Tunable) parameters ). Using Simulink.Parameter  type tunable parameters in the model workspace may lead to uninitialized variables in the generated code. Non Simulink.Parameter  tunable objects in the model workspace may throw this error:  Error gathering tunable parameters: Undefined function or variable '<variable_name>'.
Workaround: Do not declare tunable parameters in the model workspace. Keep them in the base workspace.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for CODESYS target when using models with Shift Arithmetic blocks
Description: Behaviour of Shift Arithmetic  instructions on target CODESYS  is different from that in Simulink. There are two arguments to shift functions,(1) in : The number to be shifted, and(2) n :? The number of bits it should be shifted by. In Simulink, if n  is greater than word length of in , the answer will be zero. However, for such a case in CODESYS , the IDE will shift in  by n? MOD word_length(in )" bits?CODESYS SHL Operator  . This could lead to incorrect code generation.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Generated code for referenced models does not initialize shared local data stores to zero
Description: When you clear the parameter Remove internal data zero initialization,  the code generated for referenced models does not initialize shared local data stores to zero.
Workaround: Use global data store memory (which is also shared across model instances) instead of shared local data store memory.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019b Update 5, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated CUDA code produces incorrect results when greater than 3-D input image passed to imresize?function
Description: When generating CUDA? code for the imresize  function, the generated?code produces incorrect results when the input image to be resized is specified as? N-dimensional (N > 3). For example, the code generated for the following MATLAB entry-point function,?foo,? produces incorrect results: function output = foo()?  ? input = rand(640,480,3,4);? output = imresize(input,[224 224]); end
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Discrete-Time Integrator block?might produce incorrect results for unit gain and differing input and output data types
Description: The Discrete-Time Integrator?block might produce incorrect results when all of the following conditions are true:? The?Integrator method ?parameter is set to Accumulation: Forward Euler  or? Accumulation: Backward Euler .   The Gain value ?parameter is set to 1.0 .   The input data type is double or single. The output data type is different from the input data type.  Simulink might produce incorrect results in Accelerator, Rapid Accelerator, and?standalone code generation?modes under these conditions.

Summary: The?predict and classify functions of a?DAGNetwork?throw an unintended error?when the network has both image processing and recurrent layers.
Description: The predict  function of a DAGNetwork  object throws an unintended error when the network contains both image processing layers and recurrent layers from the following: Image-processing layers: convolution2dLayer  reluLayer  batchNormalizationLayer.?   Recurrent layers lstmLayer  bilstmLayer    For example, the predict function throws an error for the   following layer graph when trained. ?layers = [ . . .  ????sequenceInputLayer([32 32 3], 'Name', 'input', 'Normalization', 'None')????sequenceFoldingLayer('Name', 'fold')????convolution2dLayer(3, 32, 'Name', 'conv1')????batchNormalizationLayer('Name', 'bn1')????reluLayer('Name', 'relu1')????convolution2dLayer(3, 64, 'Name', 'conv2')????batchNormalizationLayer('Name', 'bn2')????reluLayer('Name', 'relu2')????sequenceUnfoldingLayer('Name', 'unfold')????flattenLayer('Name', 'flatten')????bilstmLayer(128, 'OutputMode', 'last', 'Name', 'bilstm')????fullyConnectedLayer(numClasses, ?'Name', 'fc')????softmaxLayer('Name', 'softmax')????classificationLayer('Name', 'classification')];lgraph = layerGraph(layers); lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph, 'fold/ miniBatchSize', 'unfold/ miniBatchSize');  After training the network, the predict function throws the following error  : ?>> Y = predict(net, X)  Index in position 2 exceeds array bounds (must not exceed 1).   Error in DAGNetwork/ predictRNN>iSlice (line 116)   Xslice = X(inds{:});?
Workaround: As a workaround, set the 'Acceleration' option of the predict function to 'none'. Y = predict(net, X, 'Acceleration', 'none');?Note that in most cases, setting?'Acceleration'?to?'none'?results in slower prediction.

Summary: Co-simulation test bench generation may fails for Bus that has more than99 elements or signals
Description: Co-simulation test bench generation for a model may fail when the model contains Bus that has more than 99 elements or signals. The following error message is generated: "Invalid Simulinkobject name: Caused by:? 'BusSel' is not a SubSytemblock"??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Function calls without trailing semicolons inside parfor loops might be discarded in the generated code
Description: If your MATLAB code has statements without trailing semicolons inside parfor  loops, these statements might be discarded in the generated C/C++ code.  For example, consider this MATLAB function: function exampleparfor k = 1:10    fprintf('Hello\n')end The code generator produces this warning:? Warning: Printing of expressions without semicolons is suppressed in PARFOR loops.?  In this case, the code generator deletes the call to fprintf  inside the  parfor  loop . ?This behavior causes any side effects from calling the function, such as printing or updating persistent variables, to not occur. As a result, the generated code behaves incorrectly.
Workaround: If you see the above warning, terminate the reported lines with semicolons. For example, in the previous example, change the code to:?function exampleparfor k = 1:10    fprintf('Hello\n');end
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Simulation 3D Fisheye Camera block image distortion center and relative rotation errors?
Description: The Simulation 3D Fisheye Camera block image distortion centers are always on the horizon, regardless of the camera rotation. Additionally, the block ignores the Relative rotation [Roll, Pitch, Yaw]  block parameter.

Summary: Analysis may terminate with an?Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end?error message.
Description: Analysis might terminate without producing the results. When you run the analysis with a user interface, it displays the following error message:Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end.
