Summary: Library saved in release prior to R2019b might cause Simulink to incompletely render the toolstrip
Description: When you open a library that was saved in a release prior to R2019b, the Simulink Toolstrip might not render completely.

Summary: Incorrect description of activities that can be used to satisfy DO-332 objective "Source code is accurate and consistent"
Description: In these artifacts, Polyspace Bug Finder Criteria-2 Tool Qualification Plan (qualkitdo_bugfinder_criteria2_tqp.docx/pdf) Polyspace Bug Finder Criteria-3 Tool Qualification Plan (qualkitdo_bugfinder_criteria3_tqp.docx/pdf)?  The description of activities that can be used to satisfy DO-332 objective "Source code isaccurate and consistent" (Tables OO.A-5 and OO.C-5, Reference OO.6.3.4.f)? incorrectly states that Polyspace Bug Finder and Polyspace Bug Finder Server:? Do not cover "Unused variables"?? Can be used to help identify "Data corruption due to tasks or interrupts conflicts"?  These tools do cover "unused variables", but do not help to identify "Data corruption due to tasks or interrupts conflicts".

Summary: MATLAB crashes on second simulation when changing a variable using the Simulink.SimulationInput object
Description: If a variable which depends on another variable (for example, a Simulink.Variant object using an enumeration in its conditional expression) is changed using the Simulink.SimulationInput  object, MATLAB crashes when you run a model that uses that object more than once in a MATLAB session.

Summary: Requirements imported from IBM DOORS Next missing "Updated on" Revision information
Description: Using Simulink Requirement IBM DOORS Next (also known as DNG or IBM DOORS Next Generation) direct import, you can either import whole DNG module or query a project. Requirements imported this way are missing "Updated on" Revision information. In the Requirements Editor, you will see "N/A". This makes Links involving these requirements flagged for change issue immediately.

Summary: AUTOSAR model build fails with "Unrecognized function or variable 'calPrmGraphicalName'"
Description: Code generation for an AUTOSAR top model might fail if both of the following conditions are met: The model contains parameters configured as AUTOSAR parameters. The model references a model that contains a Simulink.LookupTable  parameter with the storage class set to ExportedGlobal .  The model build might fail with the following error: The call to autosar_make_rtw_hook, ?during the after_tlc hook generated the following error:Unrecognized function or variable 'calPrmGraphicalName'.The build process will terminate as a result.Caused by:Unrecognized function or variable 'calPrmGraphicalName'.

Summary: MATLAB crashes if copyobj is called on a deleted SimBiology component
Description: MATLAB crashes if the?copyobj ?method is called on a deleted SimBiology component.

Summary: Model with more than 32 n-D Lookup Table blocks causes MATLAB to crash during code generation
Description: Generating code for a single model with more than 32 n-D Lookup Table blocks causes MATLAB to crash

Summary: innerjoin and outerjoin functions do not copy properties of nested tables and timetables
Description: The innerjoin  and outerjoin  functions do not copy properties from nested tables or timetables contained in the inputs into the corresponding nested tables or timetables contained in the outputs.?? Refer to the documentation for the table and timetable datatypes for a full list of metadata properties. For example, the following code does not copy the RowNames  property of the nested table from input table A  into the nested table of output table C . >> nestedTable = table([1;2;3],'RowNames',{'a','b','c'})nestedTable =  3ˇÁ1 table         Var1         ____    a     1      b     2      c     3  >> A = table([10;20;30],nestedTable)A =  3ˇÁ2 table    Var1    nestedTable                  Var1     ____    ___________     10      a     1        20      b     2        30      c     3   >> B = table([10;30;50])B =  3ˇÁ1 table    Var1    ____     10      30      50 >> C = innerjoin(A,B)C =  2ˇÁ2 table    Var1    nestedTable               Var1        ____    ___________     10         1           30         3
Workaround: When calling innerjoin or outerjoin on a table or timetable that contains a nested table or timetable, manually copy properties from the nested table or timetable contained in the input to the nested table or timetable contained in the output.For example, this call to innerjoin copies matching rows from A and its nested table to C. Manually copy the row names from the matching rows of the nested table in A to the nested table in C.?>> nestedTable = table([1;2;3],'RowNames',{'a','b','c'});>> A = table([10;20;30],nestedTable);>> B = table([10;30;50]);>> [C,ia,ib] = innerjoin(A,B)C =  2ˇÁ2 table    Var1    nestedTable               Var1        ____    ___________     10         1           30         3      ia =     1     3ib =     1     2>> C.nestedTable.Properties.RowNames = A.nestedTable.Properties.RowNames(ia)C =  2ˇÁ2 table    Var1    nestedTable                  Var1     ____    ___________     10      a     1        30      c     3

Summary: Special characters in DUT path may generate non-intuitive error messages during validation model generation
Description: When generating a validation model along with the HDL code for a DUT whose block path contains special characters (like "+"), ?the validation model generation fails with an unhelpful error message.

Summary: Viewing doc pages Installed locally does not work when disconnected from the Internet
Description: Viewing MATLAB doc pages in the Help Browser does not work when disconnected from the Internet, even if the MATLAB Help Preferences for the Documentation Location is set to Installed locally . The doc page either loads briefly in the Help Browser and disappears, or it does not load at all.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multirate sharing with adaptive pipelining generates incorrect HDL code
Description: The generated HDL code might be incorrect for a model that has multirate sharing and adaptive pipelining optimizations enabled for product, multiply add, and multiply accumulate blocks. In these cases, the validation model produces assertions due to the mismatch between the HDL code and Simulink simulation behavior.
Workaround: Either disable adaptive pipelining or use single-rate sharing when adaptive pipelining is enabled.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table n-D block or prelookup table block with non-power of 2 spacing generates incorrect HDL code
Description: Lookup Table n-D block or prelookup block might generate incorrect HDL code when the following conditions are true: The breakpoint is spaced with non-power of 2 values Inputs to the block are signed fixed point or integer type Saturation lower limit is a negative number.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when library links are broken
Description: MATLAB might crash when the model's library links are broken. This might occur when the library itself references another library.
Workaround: If a library references another library, break library links in the referenced library first, then the library link in the model.

Summary: Detaching renamed profile crashes MATLAB
Description: If you rename a profile, save the profile, and update the associated models, a copy of the profile that uses the old name remains. If you try to detach the profile using the old name from the model, MATLAB crashes.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assignment block with initialization signal within Iterator subsystem might generate incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all the following conditions are met:? Simulink model has an Assignment block. The Assignment block has the Initialize output (Y)  set to Initialize using input port <Y0> . The Assignment block is within a For Iterator or While Iterator subsystem. The Assignment block is used in a feedback loop. In the generated code, the buffer of the Assignment block is reused with the buffer of another block within this loop.
Workaround: Use any one of these workarounds:Add a Signal Conversion block at the output of the Assignment block (before a Outport block) and select Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter. This is a preferred workaround because this only excludes the Signal Conversion block from buffer reuse optimization.Disable buffer reuse by clearing Reuse local block outputs (BufferReuse) model configuration parameter. This workaround excludes other blocks from buffer reuse optimization.

Summary: transposedConv2dLayer in dlnetwork erroneously swaps top and bottom cropping
Description: When defining asymmetric cropping in a transposedConv2dLayer   that is used in a dlnetwork , the values for the top and the bottom cropping are swapped.
Workaround: To use a transposedConv2dLayer with the correct asymmetric cropping in a dlnetwork, create the layer with the top and bottom cropping values swapped around.For example, if your layer array contains a?transposedConv2dLayer?of the following form:layers = [? ? ...? ? transposedConv2dLayer(filterSize, numFilters, 'Name', 'transpConv', 'Weights', weights, 'Bias', bias, 'Cropping', [topCropping bottomCropping leftCropping rightCropping])? ? ... ];you can define the?transposedConv2dLayer?by swapping the top and bottom cropping values:layers_workaround = [? ? ...? ? transposedConv2dLayer(filterSize, numFilters, 'Name', 'transpConv', 'Weights', weights, 'Bias', bias, 'Cropping', [bottomCropping?topCropping?leftCropping rightCropping])? ? ... ];Use the?layers_workaround layer array to create your dlnetwork.lgraph_workaround = layerGraph(layers_workaround);dlnet_workaround = dlnetwork(lgraph_workaround);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code might be generated for a model referencing another model?containing Stateflow charts with similar graphical functions
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model references another model. The referenced model contains a Stateflow chart containing two graphical functions that have the same operations but reference different variables. At least one of the variables referenced within the graphical function is not used as the input or the output of the graphical function and is not defined within the local scope of the function.  When the above conditions are met, the generated code might have a graphical function call incorrectly replaced by a call to another graphical function. For the attached example g2121509.slx , the code generator generates the incorrect code in the function g2121509Ref  in g2121509Ref.c : *rty_Out1 = g2121509Ref_Fun1(tmp, rtu_Input1) + g2121509Ref_Fun1(tmp, rtu_Input1); ? ?The expected correct code is:? *rty_Out1 = g2121509Ref_Fun1(tmp, rtu_Input1) + g2121509Ref_Fun2(tmp, rtu_Input2);??
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Baseline tests always fail in Test Manager, if the outport signals have labels associated
Description: If you use the signal labels for the outports to generate the test cases from Simulink Design Verifier and run it in the Test Manager, the results fail.  The error shown :  Signal not aligned and not able to plot

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In a standalone chart in MATLAB, if a?state uses multiple temporal logic operators with different input events, only one operator is honored
Description: Suppose that a state S  in a standalone chart has on ?state actions and outgoing transitions triggered by temporal logic expressions based on different input events. The chart will only check one of the expressions?and?will ignore the others.?

Summary: Turning Bluetooth off after calling blelist in MATLAB on Mac causes MATLAB to crash
Description: Performing the following sequence of actions causes MATLAB to crash on a Mac computer: Call blelist  in MATLAB. Turn Bluetooth off. Turn Bluetooth on. Turn Bluetooth off again.  On a MacBook, Bluetooth can be configured to automatically turn off when you close the laptop. In this case, closing and opening the MacBook twice can cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, save your work and close MATLAB before turning Bluetooth off or closing your MacBook.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Dataflow execution domains using getCurrentTime function might produce incorrect results
Description: Simulation and code generation of dataflow execution domains might produce incorrect results when all the following conditions are true: The latency parameter of the dataflow execution domain is non-zero. The dataflow execution domain is partitioned into multiple threads as indicated in the Property Inspector. A System block in the dataflow domain is using the getCurrentTime ?function to output the current simulation time.
Workaround: Move the System block?using the getCurrentTime?function outside the dataflow domain.

Summary: Vivado IP Packager?reported as unsuccessful in Create Project task when the task log contains the "Error" string in file name or path
Description: Vivado IP Packager  in Create Project  task can be reported as unsuccessful when the "Error" string (case insensitive) is present in any of these cases:   Simulink model name, or Subsystem or block name.? HDL Project file name or path?   This error can be observed from the project log file.
Workaround: To continue running HDL Workflow Advisor, replace "Error"? strings from the above scenarios and re-run the workflow from the?Set Target Device and Synthesis Tool task.

Summary: Indexed deletion might cause unwanted changes to an array or cause MATLAB to crash
Description: During an indexed deletion operation of an array, if the remaining number of elements in the array is greater than intmax , then MATLAB might either crash or incorrectly change the value of the array elements. Consider the following example where A  is an array such that numel(A) > intmax : A = true(1,2^31+10); A([1 2^30]) = []; After deletion, the size of the remaining array is 2^31+8 . In this case, MATLAB either crashes or incorrectly changes the values of A .
Fixed In: R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 5, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Timerfind?and?Timerfindall?finds timer Object?with property values matching those passed as?Name,Value?pair arguments and the value part can be an empty array and gracefully handled.
Description: out = timerfind(Name,Value) out = timerfind(t,Name,Value) out = timerfind(S)? out = timerfindall(Name,Value) out = timerfindall(t,Name,Value) out = timerfindall(S) finds timer objects?with property values matching those passed as?Name,Value?pair arguments and returns an array,?out. The value part can be empty, and timerfind/timerfindall should not error. If a matching timer with the empty property is not found, an empty array out is provided. e.g. the following code and similar codes fail in R2020a (General Release), and it should no longer failing following R20201 (Update 1) t = timer('Tag','a') timerfind('Tag', [])

Summary: Comparison results for complex signals produce "Signals not aligned" warning
Description: In the Test Manager Equivalence Criteria table, when you select a complex signal, a 'Signals not aligned' warning occurs in the Comparison result if one of the simulations does not generate the signal. In the case where you do not select the complex signal, that unwanted signal is still included in the comparison result. These situations occur because the imaginary part of the complex signal is included in the comparison results.?

Summary: Enabling the Show partition index output port option of a For Each block causes HDL Code generation error
Description: Show partition index output port  option of a For Each block is not supported in HDL Coder. Enabling this option generates the following error message: Could not connect the blocks in the model.?Look for unconnected blocks or other warnings or errors and correct them to continue  In order to perform code generation, this option must be cleared.?

Summary: Code generation error for adaptive models that use complex data types (like structure of structure or array of structure) on model interface (I/Os)
Description: Duringcode generation, for AUTOSAR adaptive models, implementation files aregenerated (in a stub folder) for data types used in the model interface (I/Os).The data types can be POD (int , float , etc.), arrays, or structures. There isno error when POD, arrays, and structures of POD are used in the model interface. However, using complex data types like structure of structure?or array of structure?on the model interface results in a code generation error. The errorlooks like the following: The call to autosar_adaptive_make_rtw_hook, during the after_tlc hook generated the following error:Unrecognized method, property,? or field 'SwBaseType' for class 'Simulink.metamodel.types.Structure'.

Summary: MATLAB hangs while starting on Mac
Description: MATLAB might hang while starting on a Mac. You might encounter this issue if you enable one of the Mac system Accessibility Options.

Summary: MATLAB crashes during exit on Mac
Description: When you exit MATLAB on a Mac system, MATLAB might crash while it is shutting down, generating a stack trace.

Summary: Multi-valued indexed assignment with at least three trailing dot-index expressions might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash in an assignment of the form [a.b(idx).c(1).d] = x{:}  where b  is an instance of a MATLAB class.
Workaround: There are at least two possible methods for making such assignments.1) Remove the square brackets and loop over the values of idx.for ix = 1:numel(idx)    a.b(idx(ix)).c(1).d = x{ix};end2) Perform the assignment in multiple steps.T = a.b;[T(idx).c(1).d] = x{:};a.b = T;

Summary: MATLAB warns about the Connector not running when using UITools functions in MATLAB startup.m file
Description: If you use UITools functions, such as listdlg  or questdlg , in the MATLAB startup.m ?or matlabrc.m ?file, MATLAB displays a warning message: Warning: MATLAB Connector is not running.
Workaround: Add the following line of code before the call to UITools functions in the MATLAB startup.m or matlabrc.m?file:connector.ensureServiceOn;

Summary: C/C++ compiler throws an overflow warning when compiling an S-function
Description: When using GCC 9 or newer, the C/C++ compiler might throw an overflow error when compiling an S-function because the error message is too large for the buffer. The error message is similar to: ?simulink.c:101:5: warning: '</arg></arguments></diag></d...' directive writing 37 bytes into a region of size 14 [-Wformat-overflow=]
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Polyspace analysis stops in Software Safety Analysis Level 1 with a convergence failure message
Description: On a C application, a Polyspace Code Prover analysis can stop during Software Safety Analysis Level 1?with an error message like this: ****** Software Safety Analysis Level 1 - 3.1.1 (* analyzing myfunction)* Warning: convergence failure during loop. Degrading precision...exception SML_Pre_Basis.Fail("convergence failure (asserts enabled)")(Fail: convergence failure (asserts enabled)) raised. raised at: Raised by primitive operation at file "unix.ml" (inlined), line 126, characters 0-53 Note that even though the analysis stopped at level 1, the results from level 0 can point to potential run-time errors. You can start reviewing these results.

Summary: Streaming and sharing on same resource fails when clock-rate pipelining is enabled
Description: When generating HDL code for a model with clock-rate pipelining enabled, if a StreamingFactor  and SharingFactor  greater than 1  is specified on the same resource, streaming optimization is successful but sharing optimization fails.
Workaround: To perform Streaming and Sharing on the same resource, disable Clock Rate Pipelining.

Summary: Incorrect red Correctness condition check on a function pointer call
Description: Polyspace can incorrectly show a red Correctness condition  (COR) check on a function pointer call using a global variable if you do not provide a main  function. The red check indicates that the function pointer does not point to any function at all, but there might be cases where the function pointer does point to a function. For instance, Polyspace shows an incorrect red COR check on the function pointer call when using these options: -main-generator  -main-generator-writes-variables public   In the following code, the global variable g  has a function pointer pf  as a member. This function pointer is assigned to a function (see?init  function). Code Prover ignores this assignment and shows a red check when the function pointer is later used to invoke a function (see?bar  function). struct A {  void  (*pf)(void) ;};                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    struct A* g;void foo(void);                                                    struct A ga = { foo };                          void init(struct A* pa) {            if (pa == 0) {        g = &ga;    } else {        g = pa;    }}  void bar(void) {   if (g != 0) {      if (g->pf != 0) {          g->pf(); //Red check      }   }}

Summary: When deploying multiple shared libraries that use graphics, compiled applications might crash
Description: When deploying multiple shared libraries (or Python, .NET, or Java components) that use graphics, the compiled application might crash under certain circumstances.? For example, if each of two shared libraries creates its own graphics window, user interaction with one window followed by interaction with the second window might cause a crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect event data received in AUTOSAR adaptive model when Message Receive block specifies?Use initial value?for?Value source when queue is empty
Description: Incorrect code is generated for an AUTOSAR adaptive model if all  of the following conditions exist: The model is configured for system target file?autosar_adaptive.tlc . The model contains a root inport connected to a Message Receive block. The root inport is mapped to an event. Message Receive block parameter Value source when queue is empty ?is set to Use initial value .  Under these conditions, the generated code for receiving the event assigns an incorrect value to the message data variable.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code that uses ARM Compute library produces incorrect answer for rectangular input images
Description: Code generated for ARM Neon and ARM Mali targets (using GPU Coder) that uses the ARM Compute library produces incorrect results for networks that satisfy both these conditions: imageInputLayer  has the?'Normalization'  property set to 'zerocenter' , 'zscore'  ,'rescale-symmetric' , or 'rescale-zero-one' .  Shape of the input image is rectangular.? ?

Summary: MATLAB might crash during code generation when using TLC functions that return double  or float datatype
Description: MATLAB might crash during code generation under all the following conditions: The model uses custom .tlc  files. The custom .tlc  files include calls to TLC functions that return float  or double  data types.?

Summary: Internal assertion during HDL code generation for RAM mapping of double-typed persistent variables using MATLAB Function block in Native Floating Point mode
Description: If persistent variables are declared with double types, RAM mapping using the MATLAB Function block in?Native Floating Point  mode?generates? an assertion. Assertion failed: b:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\hdl_emitters\piremitter.cpp:2967:Call failed to slResolve block mRAMDoubleIV/Subsystem/Single Port RAM System for value 1:256  An issue in RAM initial value support exists when the value is non-zero and double.
Workaround: Change the initial value of the persistent variable to single precision and manually cast the value to double.

Summary: Dashboard Scope block sometimes does not display signal data during simulation
Description: Dashboard Scope blocks might not always display signal data during simulation

Summary: Aerospace Blockset Earth Orientation Parameters and Delta UT1?blocks?crash MATLAB?if the block is opened without a valid Aerospace Blockset license
Description: Aerospace Blockset Earth Orientation Parameters  and Delta UT1 ?blocks?crash MATLAB?if the block is opened without a valid Aerospace Blockset license.

Summary: Aerospace Blockset Earth Orientation Parameters block crashes MATLAB if OutputError property is programmatically set 'on' while the block dialog window is open
Description: Aerospace Blockset Earth Orientation Parameters block crashes MATLAB if OutputError  property  is programmatically set 'on' via set_param  while the block dialog window is open.?

Summary: Code generated for GoogLeNet can produce run-time error when deployed on embedded GPUs from Windows machine
Description: If you deploy GoogLeNet or networks that have inception modules for cuDNN library on NVIDIA GPUs by using the support package "GPU Coder Support Package for NVIDIA GPUs", you might get a run-time error. This issue occurs only when the networks are deployed from a Windows host machine.? The error might look like :? terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::runtime_error'  what():? Unable to find the cnn_googlenet_0_loss3-classifier_w.bin binary file in ..\ \ codegen\ \ lib\ \ m_predict\ \

Summary: Configuration Parameters dialog shows empty search results
Description: After you simulate a model that uses Simscape blocks, in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the search bar shows empty results.

Summary: Aerospace Blockset Flight Control Analysis template models produce an error message when Launch the Linear Analysis Trim Tool?in the model Analysis Workflow section is clicked
Description: Aerospace Blockset Flight Control Analysis template models created with?asbFlightControlAnalysis  produce an error message when the Launch the Linear Analysis Trim Tool ?link in the model Analysis Workflow  section?is clicked.

Summary: Increased build time when user clicks the?Build?icon?
Description: You can build the generated code using the Build  icon in Simulink. However, the build time is considerably high when the compiler warnings are greater than 5000.
Workaround: Build models from the MATLAB command prompt using rtwbuild

Summary: Changing the number of inputs for a Bus Creator block to 1?might generate an error
Description: Changing the number of inputs for a Bus Creator block to 1  generates an error when all of these conditions apply: You change the parameter value from the Block Parameters dialog box. Signals labeled?i ?and?j ?are connected to input ports of the Bus Creator block. One of these signals is connected to the first input port of the Bus Creator block.  The error you receive is one of these errors: Parameter must be real  Invalid entry specified for parameter <block path> of 'Inputs' to specify port information. The entry must be a single number, a vector, or a cell array

Summary: Incorrect red non-initialized variable check on a const member
Description: Polyspace can incorrectly show a red Non-initialized variable  (NIV) check on a const  variable member of a structure or class if you do not provide the constructor body. The red check indicates that the variable is not initialized at all. However, a constructor might initialize the member, and in the absence of the constructor body the check must be unproven (orange). In the following code, the initialization of variable member m_varMember  must remain unproven (orange NIV) because the constructor body of struct B  has not been provided. Instead, Code Prover shows a red NIV check on the return  statement when the member variable x  of struct S  is read. struct S {int x;};                                                                                                                        struct B {                                                                                                                                   B();        const S m_varMember ;                                                                                                                   };int main() {  B b;  return b.m_varMember.x; }

Summary: Generated model may fail to compile for filter blocks with mask variables whose values are not of the type double
Description: If the user model contains filter blocks that have mask variables that are not double datatype, then the generated model may fail to compile.

Summary: Code?generator does not honor data alignment?requirement for Simulink.Parameter with ImportedExtern storage class
Description: The code generator does not honor a data alignment requirement when all of the following conditions?are true: The model contains at least one block that directly uses a block parameter for computation, such as a Gain block. The block parameter is configured to a Simulink.Parameter  and setting its storage class to?ImportedExtern . The code generator uses a code replacement library (CRL) to replace an operation or function with a custom function. An operation or function on the parameter is replaced with a custom function. The corresponding?code replacement?entry specifies an alignment requirement with the?AlignmentBoundary? property. The Alignment setting on the Simulink.Parameter  is smaller than the specified alignment requirement from the entry.  If you are using a compiler on a target that uses instructions which produce a crash when data alignment requirement is not satisfied, then the compiled generated code from Simulink model (or MATLAB function)?can also?produce a crash.

Summary: Incorrect simulation results for models with Descriptor State Space block in Model reference block using Accelerator mode when top model uses explicit solver
Description: Results might be incorrect for simulation of models that contain a Descriptor State Space block inside a Model reference block that is set to use Accelerator mode. This issue occurs when the top model's solver is set to an explicit variable-step or fixed-step solver, such as ode45 or ode3.
Workaround: In the Solver pane of the top model's Configuration Parameters, set the?Solver in the Solver selection section to auto (Automatic Solver Selection).??Alternatively, set the?Solver?parameter for a top model named 'myModel' that is open or loaded using set_param?on the MATLAB command line as follows:For variable-step simulation:?set_param('myModel','Solver','VariableStepAuto');?For fixed-step simulation:?set_param('myModel','Solver','FixedStepAuto');??

Summary: Running test harnesses using Run with Stepper button on toolstrip is not supported
Description: When you run a test harness using the Run with Stepper button on the toolstrip, an error occurs.

Summary: Code inspection fails for a compatible model if thereis a mismatch between the names of a masked subsystem parameter and the lookuptable object assigned to it
Description: When a lookup table object initializes a masked subsystem parameter, code inspection fails if the names of the parameter and lookup table object do not match. However, Simulink Code Inspector does not detect this as an incompatibility but fails to inspect.
Workaround: Ensure that the names of the masked subsystem parameter and lookup table object are same.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code that uses row-major array layout might produce different results than MATLAB when using logical indexing into an explicitly reshaped variable
Description: If you choose to generate code that uses the row-major array layout, the code generated for a MATLAB function might produce incorrect results when all of the following conditions are true: The function reshapes a variable-sized array whose number of dimensions is greater than or equal to 3. The array is reshaped explicitly into an array? that has a smaller number of dimensions. Also, the reshaped array? has at least 2 dimensions. If the only effect of the reshape is to combine X rightmost dimensions of the array, this issue does not occur. So, the reshape must have an effect that is different from combining a few rightmost dimensions of the array.  The reshaped array is ?indexed by ? using logical indexing over the dimensions which the previous reshape combined.  The result of the reshape is used only once in the MATLAB code. The result of the reshape is used very close to where it is created in the MATLAB code. (For example, if the reshaped array is used in a function that is different from the function in which it was created, this issue does not occur).?   This issue affects the generated MEX, static library, dynamically linked library, or executable code. The values returned by the function are different between MATLAB and the generated C/C++ code. For example, for a function foo ?defined as:   function [loc] = foo(location, indices)tempLocation = reshape(location, [], 3);loc = tempLocation(indices, :);end  the following MATLAB script demonstrates the incorrect results:   % generate codecfg = coder.config('mex');cfg.RowMajor = 1;x = reshape(1:75,5,5,3);ind = true(25,1);codegen -config cfg foo -args {coder.typeof(0, [inf inf 3]),ind} % run the MATLAB simulation and generated MEXout = foo(x,ind);out_mex = foo_mex(x,ind); % compare the resultsisequal(out, out_mex)
Workaround: To work around this issue, either:In your MATLAB code, move the use of the reshaped array tempLocation to a function that is different from the function in which it was created. The function to which you move the use of?tempLocation?must contain coder.inline('never').Alternatively, in your MATLAB code, use the reshaped array tempLocation more than once.

Summary: Calling blelist or ble falsely returns error stating that Bluetooth is not turned on in macOS Catalina
Description: With Bluetooth turned on, calling  ble  or blelist ?from MATLAB in macOS Catalina (10.15) returns the following error: Bluetooth Low Energy is not supported on this computer or Bluetooth is not turned on

Summary: Aerospace Toolbox function aeroReadIERSData will produce an error when using default value "http://maia.usno.navy.mil/ser7/finals2000A.data" for input "url" until April 30, 2020
Description: The U.S. Naval Observatory website http://maia.usno.navy.mil is currently undergoing modernization efforts. The expected completion date is estimated as April 30, 2020.? Prior to completion, the Aerospace Toolbox function aeroReadIERSData  will produce the following error when using the default value for "url", "http ://maia.usno.navy.mil/ser7/finals2000A.data":?? >> aeroReadIERSData(pwd)  Error using urlreadwrite (line 98)  Error downloading URL. Your network connection may be down or your proxy settings improperly configured.   From the U.S. Naval Observatory website: "Notice of Pending Shutdown The U.S. Naval Observatory's Rapid/Service Prediction web/FTP sites (maia.usno.navy.mil and toshi.nofs.navy.mil) must undergo modernization and will be offline starting sometime Thursday, 24 October 2019. The expected completion of work and return of service is estimated as 30 April 2020."
Workaround: Provide an alternate web address to aeroReadIERSData or point directly to the NASA CDDIS FTP:?aeroReadIERSData(pwd, "url", "https://datacenter.iers.org/data/latestVersion/10_FINALS.DATA_IAU2000_V2013_0110.txt")?aeroReadIERSData(pwd, "url", "ftp://cddis.gsfc.nasa.gov/pub/products/iers/finals2000A.data")

Summary: When retraining an agent with a critic using parallel computing, Episode Q0 values are zero for all episodes
Description: When retraining an agent with a critic using parallel computing, the Episode Q0  values are zero for all episodes. While the Q0 values are incorrect, the agent is still properly training in parallel.

Summary: Assessments results corrupted when dynamic enumerated data type definition cannot be found
Description: In Simulink Test Manager, if a symbol is mapped to?a dynamic enumerated data type, the graphical explanation in assessment results is corrupted if the enumerated data type definition cannot be found.

Summary: AUTOSAR code compilation fails when inter-runnable variable code contains application data types rather than implementation data types
Description: An AUTOSAR model build might generate uncompilable code when all of the following conditions exist: The model uses function-call subsystems to model AUTOSAR runnables. A connection between subsystems is mapped to an AUTOSAR inter-runnable variable (IRV). The IRV application data type is mapped to an implementation data type with a different name. For example, AppType is mapped to ImpType.  In certain modeling conditions, the generated code refers to the application data type (AppType) rather than the corresponding implementation data type (ImpType).
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019b Update 8, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB crashes when a stereotype property contains invalid enumeration type
Description: MATLAB crashes when attempting to view a stereotype that contains an enumerated property with an invalid or missing enumeration type, for example, when the enumeration definition (MATLAB class file) cannot be found or has invalid syntax.

Summary: The faster R-CNN detect?function returns an error when using grayscale images
Description: Calling the? detect? function on the fasterRCNNObjectDetector  detector object, which was trained using grayscale inputs, returns an error.?  For example, the following code generates an error, when the input image,?I , is a grayscale image and the detector, fasterRCNN , is configured for grayscale inputs:? [bboxes,scores,labels] = detect(fasterRCNN,I);  Index exceeds the number of array elements (2).  Error in fasterRCNNObjectDetector/detect (line 557)    updateNetworkIfRequired(this,params.ImageSize(1:3),params);?

Summary: Streaming modes of Multiply-Accumulate block might show incorrect port connections when the block is in a library
Description: When the Multiply-Accumulate block is used in a library and the?Operation mode  block parameter is set to Streaming - using Start and End Ports  or Streaming -using Number of Samples and  Bias  block parameter is set to Input port, ?then it will show incorrect port connections.

Summary: Java Exception thrown during test results report generation
Description: If you select the option to include coverage results from a test and then generate a test results report, a?java exception might occur during report generation.

Summary: Test that overrides Signal Editor scenario and includes inputs in the results produces an error
Description: If you override the active scenario of a Signal Editor block using the Inputs or Table Iterations section of the Test Manager, and include input signal data in the test results, an error occurs.

Summary: Error when selecting signal to observe in model reference hierarchy two or more levels below top model
Description: If you navigate the model and select a signal to observe in a model reference hierarchy that is two or more levels below the top model, this error occurs: Dimensions of arrays being concatenated are not consistent
Workaround: Use the Manage Observer dialog box to select signals for observation, instead of selecting the signal by navigating in the Simulink diagram.

Summary: Warnings about Stateflow objects when running test cases with coverage recording enabled
Description: Running test cases from the Test Manager in Simulink Test with coverage recording enabled can produce the following repeated warnings and slow test case execution:  Warning: Stateflow.State object could not be loaded because of error: No matching constructor signature. > In sltest.testmanager/ ResultSet/ getReqMdlTestInfo (line 210)??In stm.internal.Coverage.addAndReturnFile?
Workaround: Disabling to record coverage will not produce the warnings.

Summary: Error opening AutoVrtlEnv.project after installing the Vehicle Dynamics Blockset??Interface for Unreal Engine??4 Projects support package
Description: After installing the Vehicle Dynamics Blockset??Interface for Unreal Engine??4 Projects and following the installation steps to set up the environment and Unreal Editor, you may see this error:? ? The following modules are missing or built with a different engine version:  UE4Editor-AutoVrtlEnv.dll  Would you like to rebuild them now? (Yes) (No)   If you do not have Visual Studio??installed and configured, rebuilding the module fails.
Workaround: Navigate to C:\Local\AutoVrtlEnv.Remove the Intermediate, Saved, and Source directories.Open AutoVrtlEnv.uproject in a text editor. Remove the "Modules" specification (lines 6 through 17).

Summary: Persistent global variable used within a Parallel for-Loops(parfor) present in a MATLAB Function block or MATLAB?System block may result in code that does not compile
Description: The generated code may not compile if the following conditions are met:A persistent global variable is used within a Parallel for-Loops(parfor) present in a MATLAB function block or MATLAB system block. In the Configuration Parameter dialog box, the Generate parallel for loops  parameter is enabled.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when running the generated MEX for a second time if your design has persistent deep learning objects
Description: For some versions of the GCC compiler on the Linux platform,? the generated MEX fails to unload if your entry-point design file has calls to gpucoder.sort .? If you make changes to the design function and regenerate MEX,? running the newer MEX may still continue to use older MEX, producing unexpected results.?The YOLOv2 and SSD object detectors from Computer Vision Toolbox internally use gpucoder.sort . A design using these object detectors may crash MATLAB if the MEX is run more than once. Using the clear mex ?command may also not resolve the issue.
Workaround: Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Reading MDF data can cause memory leak
Description: Reading MDF data, especially when the data size is large, can lead to excessive memory usage. When the available system memory is exhausted, the issue might cause MATLAB to become slow and unresponsive.

Summary: Bus Elements ports directly connected to a subsystem might generate incorrect HDL code
Description: Bus Element ports directly connected to a subsystem might generate incorrect HDL code. In some cases, when the TargetLanguage  is VHDL , HDL Coder generates an assertion.

Summary: Simulation plots in Simulink Test reports are incorrect
Description: When running multiple test cases with plots for each test case, the test report inserts the plots for the last test case in the report for all test cases.

Summary: Simulink might hang in edit-time operations related to buses
Description: Simulink might hang in edit-time operations when both of the following conditions apply: Multiple blocks have masks that use the get_param  function with the SignalHierarchy  parameter. These masked blocks are organized in a hierarchy such that one of these blocks contains another. Some of the signals selected by a Bus Selector block are not in the input bus.

Summary: ReqIF Import/Export doesn't include links for Jama
Description: When Jama Requirements are exported to ReqIF file, and there are links between requirements, corresponding SPEC-RELATIONS specifications are present in the generated .reqif file, but these are not honored by Simulink Requirements when importing from this .reqif file. Requiremets items are reproduced correctly, but there are no links. Similarly,?when links between the imported items are created in Simulink Requirements, these are not exported when modified requirements are exported to the .reqif file.?? After importing such a?.reqif file back into Jama, modified requirements receive updates from Simulink Requirements, but links between these requirements?are not visible?in Jama.

Summary: Wrong error message given by power for dlarray inputs
Description: When the power   function is called with a dlarray  input and encounters an out-of-memory condition, MATLAB produces the wrong error message. The software displays the following internal error: Unable to resolve the name err.identifier .  The correct error message for this situation is:?? Out of memory on device . ?  A wrong error is also displayed if the output of power  is complex (complex numbers are not supported for dlarray ) and one of the inputs is on the GPU. The software displays following internal error:? Unable to resolve the name err.identifier .  The correct error message for this situation is:?? Complex numbers are not supported .?

Summary: Simulink might crash when generating code for a model with a customized system target file
Description: Simulink might crash when generating code for a model that meets all the following conditions: The model contains a customized system target file, with its TargetType ?set to NRT . The model contains multiple rates and there is at least one block with multiple rates.? In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, under Solver Selection,? the Type  is set as Fixed-Step ?and the Treat each discrete rate as separate task   checkbox is selected.?

Summary: Generated audio plugin GUI controls may revert to their default states.
Description: When a generated audio plugin is loaded into a DAW, and the plugin GUI window is closed and then reopened, dropdowns, checkboxes, and switches may revert to their default states. The internal parameter values are not affected by this change in the GUI. This makes the GUI state inconsistent with the plugin behavior.
Workaround: Use the DAW's default GUI, if available.

Summary: Crash on model update when data type differs between source block and signal object
Description: On model update, MATLAB might crash when all of the following conditions are met: A Simulink.Signal object  is associated with the immediate output of a source block such as Constant or Input. The signal data type in the source block is set to an explicit type, instead of inherited:auto . The data type of the signal object is set to a different type than the source block. The initial value of the signal object is not empty.

Summary: A filter view with three or more conditions does not show the correct subset of the architecture model
Description: If you build a filter view with three or more conditions, the resulting view does not show the correct subset of your architecture model.

Summary: Error using SectionTitle reporter if OutlineLevel is not set
Description: You can use the getTitleReporter  method of a Section  reporter to customize the display of a section title. However, if you do not set the OutlineLevel  property of the SectionTitle  reporter, an error is thrown when adding the Section  reporter to a report. For example, the code: rpt = mlreportgen.report.Report('myReport');? section = mlreportgen.report.Section();? title = getTitleReporter(section);?title.Content = 'My Section'; section.Title = title; add(rpt, section);  produces the following error: Document does not contain a document part template named: SectionNumberedTitle  The OutlineLevel  is needed to find the correct template part for the title reporter.
Workaround: Set the OutlineLevel property of customized SectionTitle reporters. For example:import mlreportgen.report.* rpt = Report('myReport');?section = Section();?title = getTitleReporter(section);?title.Content = 'My Section';title.OutlineLevel = 1; % Outline level of top-level section is 1?section.Title = title;subSection = Section();title2 = getTitleReporter(subSection);?title2.Content = 'My Subsection';title2.OutlineLevel = 2; % Outline level of subsection is 2?subSection.Title = title2;add(section, subSection);?add(rpt, section);?Note: In releases before R2018b, the HoleId property of SectionTitle reporters must be set to "Title" before assigning the reporter to the Section object's Title property.

Summary: Polyspace analysis stops importing comments with exception option error
Description: When importing comments from one Polyspace results folder to another, the following error message can appear: exception Option(Option) raised.  If the message appears during an analysis, it means that the analysis completed successfully but comments from a previous analysis were not imported to the current results.
Workaround: If the previous results folder contains a file named ps_diff.db, remove this file and try to import comments again.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In generated code, linspace and randsample return empty matrices when more than 2147483647?points are requested
Description: In the generated code,?linspace(a,b,n)  and randsample(population,n,true)  return vectors of size 1x0  rather than 1xn  when n ?is greater than? 2147483647 .? These calls should produce error messages because code generation does not support arrays with sizes greater than?2147483647 , the maximum value for a 32-bit signed integer.? This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Using a third-party calibration tool to rapidly change model parameter values can cause target computer crash
Description: Using a third-party calibration tool to rapidly change model parameter values in the Simulink Real-Time application that is running on the target computer can cause the target computer to crash. An example of this issue can be observed by using the "Mouse Wheel Calibration" from CANape to calibrate a Simulink Real-Time application that is running as an XCP slave. This calibration tool lets you rapidly change the value of a slave parameter by scrolling their mouse wheel. Scrolling too rapidly causes the target computer to crash.

Summary: Edit View Properties dialog is incomplete and causes the Views Studio to freeze
Description: When opening the Edit View Properties dialog for a view there are fields that are missing from the dialog. Additionally, the entire studio becomes unresponsive until it is closed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When?input exceeds?last breakpoint, under certain conditions, Prelookup or n-D Lookup table blocks?might fail to return warning or error message
Description: Prelookup or n-D Lookup table blocks?might not return warning or error message under these conditions: Prelookup or n-D Lookup Table block is present in the model Diagnostic for out-of-range input? parameter is set to Warning  or  Error  Index search method? parameter is set to?Evenly spaced points ?or?Breakpoints specification? parameter is set to?Even spacing  Data type of Input or Breakpoints are integer or fixed-point data types Input value exceeds the last breakpoint value The difference between the input value and the last breakpoint value is small.  Note: The issue only occurs if the difference between the input and the last breakpoint is small. For larger differences, the warning or error is correctly returned.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer when the assigned variable of Complex type is being read more than once in a divide operation
Description: Incorrect code generation might occur for MATLAB code when the following conditions are true:A MATLAB function performs a complex typed divide operation. The variable being assigned the output of this operation is also read more than once on the right-hand side of the operation.  Consider the following example where the function complexDivideOp  performs a divide operation meeting the above conditions:  function out = complexDivideOp(in1,in2)??? out = in2;??? for idx = 1 : numel(out)??????? temp = 1 + in1;??????? temp = temp / abs(temp);??????? out(idx) = out(idx) * conj(temp);??? endend?  In the above MATLAB code, the divide operation inside the loop satisfies condition 1. The variable temp  appears twice in the divide operation and the output is again written to temp , which satisfies condition 2.  The generated C code shown below incorrectly reads the uninitialized variable br . The variable br  is defined after the if-else block.? void complexDivideOp(const emlrtStack *sp, const creal_T in1, const creal_T in2[100],???????????????? creal_T out[100]){? real_T d;? real_T d1;? int32_T idx;? creal_T temp;? real_T re;? real_T br;? real_T im;? real_T b_im;? memcpy(&out[0], &in2[0], 100U * sizeof(creal_T));? d = in1.re;? d1 = in1.im;? for (idx = 0; idx < 100; idx++) {??? temp.re = 1.0 + in1.re;??? temp.im = in1.im;??? if (d1 == 0.0) {????? re = (1.0 + d) / br;????? im = 0.0;??? } else if (1.0 + d == 0.0) {????? re = 0.0;????? im = d1 / br;??? } else {????? re = (1.0 + d) / br;????? im = d1 / br;??? }??? br = b_abs(temp);??? ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assignment block with Y0 port might produce incorrect results when inside nested iterator subsystem
Description: Simulation and code generation for models involving Assignment blocks inside nested iterator subsystems might produce incorrect results when all the following conditions are met: The Assignment block is contained in a For Each subsystem, which is directly or indirectly contained by a For or While Iterator subsystem. The initialization port (Y0 port) of the Assignment block is enabled.

Summary: Simulink.SimulationData.Dataset data saved in a version 7.3 MAT-file using R2019b does not load in earlier releases
Description: In R2019b, when you use the save  function to save a Simulink.SimulationData.Dataset  object to a version 7.3 MAT-file, the data loads correctly in R2019b but may not in earlier releases. Loading the file in an earlier release may issue this warning:? ? Warning: While loading an object of class 'Simulink.SimulationData.Dataset':?  ? To RESHAPE the number of elements must not change.?
Workaround: To load Simulink.SimulationData.Dataset data saved in R2019b in an earlier release:Use the save function to save the data to a MAT-file version other than v7.3. For example:>>save('my_file.mat','logsout','-v7')Use the?exportToPreviousRelease?function to save the data to any version MAT-file. For example:>>exportToPreviousRelease(logsout,'my_file.mat','logsout','-v7.3')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Poorly configured lookup tables with fixed-point output types may lead to incorrect results from generated C/C++ code
Description: If a lookup table is configured with all of the following criteria: The output is a fixed-point data type. The output type cannot represent all values in the lookup table data. The Extrapolation method  is set to?Clip  or Linear . The lookup table values are specified with storage class Auto,? that is, the values are either specified directly or with a Simulink.Parameter  object with the storage class set to Auto .  The output range of the lookup table may be miscalculated, causing incorrect code generation optimizations. These optimizations will produce incorrect results from the generated code only if the lookup table block is driven by inputs that cause the table algorithm to calculate an output value that cannot be represented by the output data type of the lookup table block.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash while starting on Mac systems
Description: MATLAB can crash while starting on Mac systems. This sometimes happens when mouse movement occurs during MATLAB startup.

Summary: Logical indexing with a large number of true?index values can produce out of memory error.
Description: Consider the following code: n = 2^31;A = ones(1, 2.5e9);idx = false(1, numel(A));idx(1:n) = true;B = A(idx);  The following error is thrown even if the system has sufficient memory to perform the above calculations: Out of memory. Type "help memory" for your options. ?
Workaround: For systems with sufficient memory, use numeric indices instead of logical indices. For example, you can do one of the following:1. Use the find function to convert the logical indices to numeric indices:?B = A(find(idx));2. Use numeric indices instead of logical indices:idx = 1:n;B = A(idx);

Summary: Generating code for a MATLAB function might cause MATLAB to crash with an assertion that starts with?MatrixType::isa(aExpr->type()
Description: Generating code from MATLAB code that meets the following conditions might cause MATLAB to crash: The MATLAB function contains an expression involving a binary operation such as a logical OR . The operands are variable size with one of the operands unbounded.  When MATLAB crashes, you get the following assertion message and stack trace: Assertion at util_cg/SanityCheckerImpl.cpp line 1823:  ValidCore violated: Failed assertion: MatrixType::isa(aExpr->type()) == (MatrixType::isa(aExpr->lhs()->type()) || MatrixType::isa(aExpr->rhs()->type()))    [SNIP]  Stack Trace (captured): [SNIP]  [? 5] 0x00007ff59f2d607e /local-ssd/inst_19a/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwemlcoder.so+02113662  [? 6] 0x00007ff59ee479d9 /local-ssd/inst_19a/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcg_ir.so+06466009  [SNIP]  [ 21] 0x00007ff59ec3b3df /local-ssd/inst_19a/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcg_ir.so+04318175 _ZNK2CG12InvariantSet8evaluateEPK6CG_ObjPKNS_17InvariantServicesE+00000063  [ 22] 0x00007ff59edec429 /local-ssd/inst_19a/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcg_ir.so+06091817 _ZN2CG13SanityChecker5doObjEP6CG_Obj+00000089  [ 23] 0x00007ff599bd2de5 /local-ssd/inst_19a/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmweml.so+04017637 _ZN3EML7Compile8doitImplEv+00001621  [ 24] 0x00007ff59b9edca7 /local-ssd/inst_19a/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_support.so+03681447 _ZNK2CG14ScopeTransform5applyEPNS_5ScopeERKNS_7ServiceE+00000167  [ 25] 0x00007ff599bc80d7 /local-ssd/inst_19a/bin/glnxa64/builtins/coder_core/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmweml.so+03973335 _ZN3EML7compileEPNS_3EnvEb+00000103

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Association results and corresponding output tracks might be incorrect in interpreted mode compared with generated code
Description: When using interpreted mode for a multi-object tracker update, if the measurement noise matrices differ significantly between the various detections, the calculated cost is inaccurate and may lead to a wrong assignment decision. As a result, detections may be incorrectly assigned to tracks, and tracks may be initialized, updated, or deleted incorrectly during that update step.? Generated code is not affected by this issue, resulting in a mismatch between simulation and generated code results. This issue affects the multiObjectTracker.
Workaround: Distinguish detentions using? the?MeasurementParameters?property in each input detection to avoid wrong assignments.?MeasurementParameters?is a property of?objectDetection. objectDetection is the detection input format accepted by trackers. The property is usually used to contain the parameters that describe how the detection is obtained. It is specified as a structure. In your program, if the tracker does not have the?MeasurementParameters?property, create this property for your tracker.? Then in the structure, create a field named "DetectionIndex". Make sure to set the value of this field for different detection inputs to a different integer value (for example, 1, 2, 3, ....). This prevents the tracker from neglecting the measurement noise differences between different detections and fixes the possible wrong assignments in the interpreted running mode.?The following code provides an example:tracker = multiObjectTracker;for time = 0:1? ?detections =repmat({objectDetection(time,[0;0;0])},2,1);? ?for i =1:numel(detections)? ? ?detections{i}.MeasurementNoise = i*eye(3);? ? ?detections{i}.MeasurementParameters= struct('DetectionIndex',i);? ?end? ?confirmedTracks = tracker(detections,time);end?

Summary: Convolution neural networks containing convolution layers with large padding can cause MATLAB to crash during training or inference
Description: MATLAB can crash during training or inference (prediction or classification) of convolution neural networks that contain convolution layers with large padding when the convolutional layers are followed by certain combinations of other layers.? This can happen when all of the following conditions are met:? The network contains a?convolution2dLayer ? or a?convolution3dLayer  whose? PaddingSize ? contains values greater than or equal to the?FilterSize ? of the layer in a corresponding dimension (for example, a?convolution2dLayer ? with?FilterSize ?equal to? [3 3] ?and?PaddingSize ?equal to?[3 0 0 0] ). The convolution layer is followed by any ReLU layer (reluLayer , clippedReluLayer , or leakyReluLayer )?or ?a batchNormalizationLayer  that is followed by any ReLU layer. The network is executed on the CPU.
Workaround: When training on the CPU, this issue can be avoided by inserting a?dropoutLayer?with a dropout probability of zero before the ReLU layer.??When calling any of the following functions on the trained network using the CPU, set the?'Acceleration'?name-value pair to?'none':predictclassifypredictAndUpdateStateclassifyAndUpdateStateactivations?

Summary: Simulation Data Inspector does not export all selected runs or signals when you export data using the Export dialog
Description: When you use the?Export ?dialog, the Simulation Data Inspector does not export?selected runs or signals that are not currently visible in the Simulation Data Inspector archive or work area without scrolling.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing a Power block or for a MATLAB code with a power function and Standard Math Library as C99 (ISO)
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for MATLAB code containing a power  function or for a model with a Power block if the following conditions are met:The input datatype to the power? function or Power block is single . If generating C code from a model, in the Configuration Parameters dialog box the Standard math library ?parameter is set to C99 (ISO).  If generating C code from MATLAB code using the MATLAB Coder app, on the? Custom Code tab,? ?the Standard math library ?parameter is set to?C99 (ISO).  The input to the Power block or power  function is either 1  and NaN  or NaN  and 0 . The generated code contains powf  functions.
Workaround: Select C89/C90(ANSI) as the Standard Math Library when using power function in MATLAB or when using Power block in Simulink
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Spurious errors and crashes in fast-analysis while analyzing C++ code
Description: If you are running Bug Finder on C++ code in the fast-analysis mode (using the option -fast-analysis ), the analysis might stop with spurious errors.

Summary: ExportToFile property of?sltest.plugins.TestManagerResultPlugin exports both current test run and all previous runs
Description: If you leverage?matlab.unittest.TestRunner ?and use ExportToFile  in sltest.plugins.TestManagerResultsPlugin  to export Simulink Test Manager results, the results for all test runs during that session are exported if you use the same TestRunner instance. Only the results for one run should be exported.

Summary: Many jcef_helper processes remain open on Mac
Description: Multiple jcef_helper  processes can be seen in the Mac activity monitor even after closing Live Editor and the Help browser. Only exiting MATLAB closes all of them.

Summary: Incorrect result or uncompilable code when calling Simulink function inside Simulink based state without using fully qualified name
Description: During simulation, you might get an incorrect result when the following conditions are met: A Stateflow chart calls a Simulink function. The chart uses C action language. The Simulink function is located inside a Simulink based state. The function call does not use a fully qualified name when referring to the function. For example, suppose that function f is located in Simulink based state B with parent state A. Function calls B.f() or f() are not fully qualified names. If A's parent is the chart, then A.B.f() is a fully qualified name.  If you generate code from a model impacted by this bug, the generated code will not compile.

Summary: Crash while exiting MATLAB after simulating a model
Description: After simulating a model, and while exiting MATLAB, the program might exit with a nonzero exit code and users might notice an additional crash log generated in MATLAB's temp directory. If the stack trace from the crash log resembles the following, please see this?Bug Report ?for more information and possible workaround: [? 0] 0x000000010b09815c? ? bin\win64\libmwsl_services.dll+00622940 SlrtwFltToAscii::~SlrtwFltToAscii+00000796 [? 1] 0x000000010b090d62? ? bin\win64\libmwsl_services.dll+00593250 SlTracer_Simple::getCurrentTime+00003682 [? 2] 0x0000000011c78801? ? bin\win64\libmwfoundation_usm.dll+00100353 foundation::usm::Detail<foundation::usm::scope::Process>::erase+00000161 [? 3] 0x000000010020b3bb? ? bin\win64\libmwservices.dll+02208699 services::graphics::MCRContext::SetMCRHooks+00015835 [? 4] 0x0000000015d7956f? ? bin\win64\mcr.dll+01086831 QueryMLFcnTable_mcr+00016767

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Function block or MATLAB Function in Stateflow might read stale data from a MAT-file when using coder.load()
Description: After the initial build of a Simulink model or a Stateflow chart, any changes made to a MAT-file that is loaded by using coder.load  might not be used in subsequent simulations. Data from the updated MAT-file might not be used during simulation if all the following conditions are true: The MAT-file is located in a folder on the MATLAB path which is different from the folder that contains the Simulink model or the Stateflow chart. The model or the chart loads data from the MAT-file by using coder.load . After the first build, you update or replace the MAT-file by another MAT-file with the same name.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:Do not use coder.load to load MAT-files that are not located in the folder that contains the model or the chart.After updating the MAT-file and prior to simulation, either modify the block contents or force a full rebuild of the block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink might not throw run-time error during simulation of model hierarchy
Description: During simulation, Simulink might fail to report run-time errors and fail to terminate the simulation when all of the following conditions apply: A model contains at least one Model block set to simulate in normal mode. The model hierarchy uses function-call subsystems, Stateflow charts, or MATLAB Function blocks.  The failure to report run-time errors and terminate the simulation might result in incorrect answers. Any blocks downstream of the source of the run-time error will continue to execute with the invalid upstream signal data. Visual debugging tools, like scopes, display blocks, and the Simulation Data Inspector will show incorrect data.  Therefore, this bug causes simulation to behave differently than code generated from the model.
Workaround: Run the attached script after you simulate any potentially impacted model. The script will output true or false, indicating whether a run-time error occurred without being reported.If the script returns true:Resolve the error returned by the script.Restart MATLAB. The script cannot clear previously captured diagnostics within a MATLAB session.

Summary: The linuxvideo adaptor is unable to detect webcams in certain Linux operating systems
Description: Webcams are not detected by linuxvideo  adaptor in the following Linux operating systems: Ubuntu 19.04 Ubuntu 18.04 LTS Debian 10 Debian 9?  This includes using the linuxvideo  adaptor with videoinput  function, Image Acquisition Tool(imaqtool ), and From Video Device block.
Workaround: For the following MATLAB releases, a fix is provided asan update to the "OS Generic Video Interface" support package.To install the updated version, start Add-On Explorer:On the MATLAB Home tab, in the Resources section, clickAdd-Ons > Get Hardware Support Packages , orExecute supportPackageInstaller command in MATLAB commandwindow.MATLAB R2019b,Open Support Package Installer and update the "OSGeneric Video Interface" support package to version 19.2.1 by uninstallingthe support package and reinstalling it.MATLAB R2019a,Open Support Package Installer and update the "OSGeneric Video Interface" support package to version 19.1.1 by uninstallingthe support package and reinstalling it.MATLAB R2018b,Open Support Package Installer and update the "OSGeneric Video Interface" support package to version 18.2.1 by uninstallingthe support package and reinstalling it.

Summary: Using the?applyToModel method on SimulationInput object to apply variables, does not apply variables in data dictionary of model references.
Description: Simulink.SimulationInput  object is used to change the value of variables in the global workspace, which includes the variables specified in the data dictionary of model references. Using the applyToModel  method on the SimulationInput  object, the specified values can be applied  to the global workspace (that is, the value of the corresponding variable in the global workspace is set to the value specified on the SimulationInput  object). Due to this bug, the value of variables specified in a data dictionary of model references are not updated when using applyToModel  .

Summary: MATLAB might silently quit during startup on macOS Catalina
Description: When you launch MATLAB on macos 10.15 (Catalina) or later, if an invalid license file is detected, MATLAB fails to start but does not display an error message.  The issue can be seen by launching MATLAB from the command line. If MATLAB is failing to launch because of a licensing issue but no dialog box is shown, text similar to the following will be output to the terminal window: License checkout failed.License Manager Error -2Make sure that the formatting of the license file is correct.?When editing the license file use a basic text editor.

Summary: IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation (NG) cannot import ReqIF file exported by Simulink Requirements
Description: If requirements are authored in Simulink Requirements and exported as ReqIF, IBM Rational Doors NG is not able to import the ReqIF file.

Summary: Incorrect format for anchor box size in yolov2OutputLayer and yolov2Layers functions
Description: The functions yolov2OutputLayer  and yolov2Layers  are intended to accept the anchor box size in [height, width] form, but instead the anchor box size must be specified in [width, height] form. If you specify the anchor box size in?[height, width] form, the YOLO v2 object detection network created using the yolov2OutputLayer  or yolov2Layers  function produces incorrect results.
Workaround: To avoid the issue, specify the size of anchor box in [width, height] form for the yolov2OutputLayer and yolov2Layers functions.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Integer constants with types int64 or uint64 can generate incorrect VHDL Code
Description: Integer constants with types?int64? or?uint64 ?can get truncated to 32  bit integers when the TargetLanguage  is VHDL .?
Workaround: Use fixed-point constants fi(value, 0, 64, 0) or fi(value, 1, 64, 0).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results for S-Function block with conditional function call output
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a user written S-Function block with a function call output. The output function call is called conditionally. The function call signal is connected to a reusable subsystem block.  The generated code might incorrectly contain local variables for the reusable subsystem output, instead of global variables. Thus, the reusable subsystem output does not hold its value between calls.
Workaround: Change the code generation option of the reusable subsystem to inlined or non-reusable.

Summary: Incorrect VHDL test bench generated for large vector constants of type uint64 or int64
Description: Incorrect test bench code is generated when the following conditions are met:  The TargetLanguage  is VHDL  for makehdltb . One of the outputs to DUT need to hold its value as a constant vector throughout the simulation time. Scalar outputs give the correct test bench. One of the constant vector elements have to be a value that cannot be represented using the double  datatype (for e.g. X"FFFFFFFF00000001 "). The constant vector must not be a fixed point data type (fixdt or fi).
Workaround: Use fixed point data types when working with int64 or uint64 types.

Summary: Crash on update diagram when bus object in data dictionary uses enumerated type not visible to all models in hierarchy
Description: MATLAB will crash when you perform an update diagram if the following conditions are true: You have a model hierarchy with at least 3 levels (e.g. top.slx->mid.slx->bot.slx). There are two or more sub-models that link to data dictionaries. You have an enumerated data type defined in a data dictionary and this type is not visible to all sub-models. A sub-model uses a bus object in a data dictionary and this bus refers to the enumerated data type.
Workaround: Create a new data dictionary to share across the sub-models.Move the enumerated type definition to the dictionary.For each sub-model that links to a data dictionary, create a reference from the model's data dictionary to this new data dictionary.?

Summary: Phased Array System Toolbox app?Sensor Array Analyzer errors on?launching
Description: MATLAB might throw an error and become unresponsive when you try to launch the Sensor Array Analyzer app if you do not have certain products installed in addition to Phased Array System Toolbox. Unable to resolve the name matlabshared.application.layout.ScrollableGridBagLayout.?
Workaround: You can work around this issue by installing the Antenna Toolbox, RF Toolbox, or Communication Toolbox in addition to the Phased Array System Toolbox.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash while extending existing test cases with root-level Bus inports
Description: MATLAB crash occurs when extending the existing test cases during the test-generation analysis in the following scenario:The model has at least one root-level Bus inport. The bus contains at least one bus element with Complexity  as complex .  Stack Trace (from fault): [? 0] 0x00007fafe104eb02 std::vector<DV::AnalysisInterface, std::allocator<DV::AnalysisInterface> >::size() const  [? 1] 0x00007fafe104f873 DV::MX::CGTestCaseFromMx::initFromMxArray(DV::Results&, mxArray_tag*, mxArray_tag*, mxArray_tag*)  [? 2] 0x00007fafe1049c44 DV::ExternalLoader::Analyzer::load()?  [? 3] 0x00007fafe101c52c DV::DvInternal::loadTestCases(mxArray_tag*)  [? 4] 0x00007fafe11a6b5e TestComponent::loadTestCases(mxArray_tag*)  [? 5] 0x00007fafe119878a TestComponentloadTestCasesMI::invokeMethod(UDMethodSignature*, int*, void**, int, void**)

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may return the status asObjectives Unsatisfiable or Objectives Valid when a runtime error occurs.
Description: SimulinkDesign Verifier inserts assumptions to generate test cases and counterexamplesthat avoid unrelated runtime errors. Whenthe only test case or counterexample encounters one of these runtime errors,Simulink Design Verifier uses these assumptions in rare instances?to prove that the objective is unsatisfiableor valid. In?TestGeneration ?mode, the following incorrect Objective Statuses arepossible when the only test case or counterexample encounters a runtime error: 1)Objectives Unsatisfiable 2)Objectives Unsatisfiable under Approximation In?PropertyProving ?mode, the following incorrect Proof Objective Statuses arepossible when the only test case or counterexample encounters a runtime error: 1)Objectives Valid 2)Objectives Valid under Approximation

Summary: MATLAB crashes when loading and starting a model that uses dashboard blocks on a real-time target
Description: MATLAB crashes when loading and starting a Simulink model that has connected dashboard blocks on a Simulink Real-Time target. The crash stack includes:? sl_hmi.dll+00985532 sl_hmi::DashboardBlock::handleModelStart+00002108   Simulink might issue this warning prior to the crash: Warning: Dashboard blocks are only supported in External mode.?
Workaround: Ensure the model window is closed on the host machine before starting the model on the Simulink Real-Time target.?

Summary: Using grayscale images to train a Yolo V2 or R-CNN object detector or calling the detect function on a Yolo V2 or R-CNN detector object with grayscale images, returns an error.
Description: Training a YOLO v2 (trainYOLOv2ObjectDetector ), R-CNN (trainRCNNObjectDetector ), Fast R-CNN (trainFastRCNNObjectDetector ), or Faster R-CNN (trainFasterRCNNObjectDetector ) object detector with grayscale inputs, returns an error. The error also occurs when you call the detect  function on any of the related detector objects, yolov2ObjectDetector , rcnnObjectDetector , fastRCNNObjectDetector , or fasterRCNNObjectDetector ). For example, the following code generates an error, when the input datastore, ds , provides grayscale images.and the network layer graph, lgraph , is configured for grayscale inputs:? ? ? ? ? ?[detector,info] = trainYOLOv2ObjectDetector(ds,lgraph,options);  ? ? Error in trainYOLOv2ObjectDetector (line 187)  ? ? Caused by:  ? ? ? ? Error using vision.internal.cnn.validation.checkImageAndNetworkChannelSizes (line 8)  ? ? ? ? Invalid input image channel size: 1.  ? ? ? ? The input image channel size (1) must be the same as the network's input channel size (1).?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The cordictanh function returns incorrect values for certain unsigned fixed-point inputs.
Description: For fixed-point inputs, the cordictanh  function uses the data type of the first input argument to determine the data type of intermediate variables. Because some of these variables undergo repeated subtraction,? they can become negative, resulting in incorrect outputs.

Summary: Crossing point of two linearly interpolated signals might not be detected correctly when evaluating temporal assessments
Description: When comparing linearly interpolated signals in a temporal assessment, the time point when the signal becomes greater (or less) than the other signal might not be detected properly. When this happens the overall result of the assessment might silently pass (or fail) causing a test case to silently pass (or fail). This behavior is specific to linearly interpolated signals and does not occur when comparing signals with 'zoh' interpolation.

Summary: MATLAB might crash with masked library blocks having parameter promotion
Description: A Simulink block linked to a masked library block having promoted parameters might cause MATLAB to crash. This behavior is common with promoted parameters having datatype mismatch.  Stack trace snippet:?  Assertion in void __cdecl PromotedParamMgr::add(const class SLBlock *,const class SLPrmDesc *) at l:\bmasklib\matlab\src\simulinkmask\engine\slpromoteprmdesc.cpp line 1143:  true == bIsStyleSame || m_PromotedParamPtrMap.empty()?
