Summary: Inactive signal properties for Signal Editor block inside?subsystem are reset to default after?model is closed and reopened
Description: For Signal Editor blocks, you can set different properties for different signals. When this block is?in a subsystem, the signal properties of an inactive signal are reset to default if the model is saved, closed, and reopened.
Workaround: To recover properties of all signals of a Signal Editor block inside?a subsystem:Unzip the attachment and save the?workaroundForBugReport2068446.p?file to a folder on your MATLAB path.Run this script each time the model containing Signal Editor block is loaded or opened.Input to this script is a cell array of Signal Editor block paths or a vector of Signal Editor block handles.

Summary: Unexpected behavior when editing Custom Gauge blocks or panels after navigating a model hierarchy
Description: After you navigate through a model hierarchy, unexpected behavior can occur when you try to add a block to a panel or edit a Custom Gauge block in a panel. Unexpected behaviors include: When you drag and drop a block into a panel, the block might appear to vanish. When you enter edit mode for a Custom Gauge block in a panel, the block might not enter edit mode or a Custom Gauge block outside of the panel might appear to vanish.
Workaround: To resolve these issues, open the component with the issue in a new window. In the new window, blocks that appeared to vanish now display correctly, and you can enter edit mode for Custom Gauge blocks.To enter edit mode for a Custom Gauge block in a panel, you can also:Add a Custom Gauge block to the component with the issue.Enter edit mode for the added Custom Gauge block.Select the Custom Gauge block in the panel.

Summary: Variant Subsystems not extracted as Variant Components in an architecture model
Description: When you extract an architecture model from a Simulink model, Variant Subsystems are transformed to Components instead of Variant Components.

Summary: Unable to open variant using Simulink Toolstrip
Description: When you navigate inside a locked system, such as a locked library, a library linked subsystem, or a write protected model, the menu items on the?Variant Subsystem > Open  tab of the Simulink Toolstrip are disabled. This prevents opening the variants from this menu.
Workaround: Context-click on the block in the editor, then choose Variant > Open to open the desired variant.

Summary: CAN Unpack block generates unoptimized code block
Description: The CAN Unpack block generates unoptimized code, which has the floor  and ceil  functions. These functions cause implicit data conversion between single and double data type in the generated code.

Summary: Trying to get block handle from block SID inside Subsystem Reference fails
Description: After converting a Subsystem to Subsystem Reference, trying to get a block handle from Simulink Identifier (SID) for a block which is inside a subsystem in Subsystem Reference fails.  The diagnostic viewer shows the below error message:  Object No Longer Exists

Summary: Code generator might crash if the MATLAB code passes a vector input to a method that returns multiple outputs
Description: The code generator might crash if a call to a method in your MATLAB code satisfies both of these conditions: The call passes a vector (for example, 1:n  or a:b:c ) to the method. The method returns multiple outputs to the caller function.  The crash report contains an assertion?at CallExpr.hpp line 16 . For example, suppose that you define a MATLAB class A_class :  classdef A_Class    methods        function [a,b] = a_method(obj, input)            a = 0;            b = input;        end    endend If you attempt to generate code for this function, the code generator might crash: function for_codegen(n)obj = A_Class;[a,b] = obj.a_method(1:n);end
Workaround: In your MATLAB code, save the vector input into a temporary variable and pass this temporary variable to the method.For example, modify the for_codegen?function in the previous section by introducing a temporary variable temp.?function for_codegen(n)obj = A_Class;temp = 1:n;[a,b] = obj.a_method(temp);end
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 6, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: In the generated code,?isoutlier, filloutliers, and rmoutliers functions might index out of range
Description: The generated code for the isoutlier , filloutliers , and rmoutliers  functions might attempt to index out of range (at index 0) when all  of the following conditions are true: The 'gesd'  or 'grubbs'   method is selected. The input data contains at least two elements. ?All the input data elements are equal.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB functions performing subtractions with signed integers typecasted as unsigned integers might generate incorrect HDL code
Description: Incorrect HDL code can be generated from MATLAB functions that performs subtractions with signed integers are typecasted as unsigned integers. For example: % in32 is of type int32?  function y_uint32?= foo (in32)  ????uint32_extreme = uint32([0, 4294967295]);  ? ? y_uint32 = uint32_extreme??- uint32(in32);??  end??   The generated code in such cases does not perform saturation of negative values of the signed integer to zero, which results in testbench simulation mismatches.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening a bus block dialog or using get_param
Description: MATLAB might crash or appear to hang when you open a Bus Selector, Bus Creator, or Bus Assignment block dialog or invoke get_param(portHandle, 'SignalHierarchy')  on one of those blocks. The generated stack trace contains lines similar to the following: [  0] 0x00000000f05eb4b6                        bin\ win64\ sl_graphical_classes.dll+02405558 SlSigHierInfo::isHierarchyPartiallyKnown+00000006[  1] 0x00000002e0812caa                        bin\ win64\ libmwsimulink.dll+08334506 RTWCGBusParameterBlock::getRTWBlock+00108490[  2] 0x00000002e0812f7f                        bin\ win64\ libmwsimulink.dll+08335231 RTWCGBusParameterBlock::getRTWBlock+00109215

Summary: ARXML importer function updateModel error for AUTOSAR composition model that uses error status ports
Description: Using the ARXML importer function updateModel  to update an AUTOSAR composition model that contains error status ports generates this error message: No appropriate method, property,?or field 'isErrorStatusPortElementMapped' for class 'autosar.updater.SLCompositionMatcher'
Workaround: First run the updateModel function on each component model in the composition model.Run the ARXML importer function createCompositionAsModel and use the ComponentModels parameter to specify the component models updated in step 1.

Summary: Using a webcam with the?macvideo adaptor on macOS Mojave or macOS Catalina might crash MATLAB
Description: When using a webcam with the?macvideo  adaptor on macOS Mojave or macOS Catalina, MATLAB might crash. This can happen when using the Image Acquisition Tool or the command line.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following steps to start MATLAB from the command line in macOS and allow access to the webcam when prompted.1) Open the Terminal app from the Utilities folder in Applications.2) Navigate to the <MATLAB>/bin folder in Applications, using the following command in the Terminal app:/Applications/MATLAB_R2019b.app/bin3) Start MATLAB, using the following command:./matlab4) At the MATLAB command line, run the following command: imaqhwinfo('macvideo')5) Grant the Terminal application access to the camera when prompted by an alert from macOS. This provides MATLAB access to the camera.?Notes:Subsequent uses of the capture system do not cause the alert to appear again because macOS remembers the permission settings. These settings can be changed in System Preferences > Security & Privacy > Privacy > Camera.Restricted users cannot access media devices in macOS Mojave or macOS Catalina. For such users, no device is detected.

Summary: Using a USB webcam on macOS Mojave causes MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when you create a webcam object on macOS Mojave.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, use the following steps to grant camera access to the Terminal application:1) Open the Terminal app from the Utilities folder in Applications.2) Navigate to the <MATLAB>/bin folder in Applications using the following command in the Terminal app.? ? ? ? /Applications/MATLAB_R2019b.app/bin3) Start MATLAB using the following command.? ? ? ? ./matlab? ?4) Create the wecam object in MATLAB with the following command:? ? ? ? ?w = webcam5)?Authorize camera access to the Terminal application when prompted. Granting access allows MATLAB and the webcam to work as expected.Notes:Once you grant/deny the camera access to the Terminal, the system prompt will not appear for subsequent launches of MATLAB from the Terminal. You can change the permission settings in System Preferences > Security and Privacy > Privacy > Camera.Restricted users cannot access media devices in macOS Mojave. For such users, no device will be detected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: While loop with empty body incorrectly omitted in generated code
Description: For MATLAB code that satisfies the following conditions:? There exists a while  loop which does not contain any statements inside its body.?? The condition of the while  loop is a compound logical expression involving logical and '&& '. At least one of the logical subexpressions is a function-call with a side effect.  The code generator might incorrectly omit the while  loop in generated code.? For an example, see the file issue_example_2067108.m in the attached issue_2067108.zip.? function out = issue_example_2067108(idx)    global globalState;    globalState = 0;    while(globalState < idx  && ...          updateGlobalState())    end     out = globalState;end function out = updateGlobalState()    coder.inline('never');    global globalState;    globalState = globalState + 1;    if(globalState > 100)        out = false;        return;    end    out = true;end  The call to updateGlobalState  in the while  loop condition has a side-effect of incrementing globalState . In the generated code, the while  loop and the call to this function are incorrectly omitted. >> codegen issue_example_2067108 -args {0} -globals {'globalState',0}real_T issue_example_2067108(const emlrtStack *sp, real_T idx){  (void)sp;  (void)idx;  globalState = 0.0;  globalState_dirty |= 1U;  return globalState;} Instead the code generator should have produced real_T issue_example_2067108(const emlrtStack *sp, real_T idx){  boolean_T exitg1;  globalState = 0.0;  globalState_dirty |= 1U;  exitg1 = false;  while ((!exitg1) && (globalState < idx)) {    if (*emlrtBreakCheckR2012bFlagVar != 0) {      emlrtBreakCheckR2012b(sp);    }    if (!updateGlobalState()) {      exitg1 = true;    }  }  return globalState;} This issue can affect MATLAB Coder only code generation Simulink Coder code generation of models containing a MATLAB Function block, a MATLAB System block, or a MATLAB function in Stateflow?  See also Bug Report ID 2093701
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code when the output of a Rate Transition block uses a? Reusable storage class
Description: The behavior of the generated code for a model may differ from its simulation behavior when it contains a Rate Transition block whose output uses a Reusable  custom storage class.
Workaround: Do not use the Reusable storage class for the output of a Rate Transition block

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: circshift is not supported for code generation with table inputs and does not issue an error
Description: circshift ?is not supported for code generation? with table inputs. But if you attempt to generate code for a MATLAB function that passes table inputs to? circshift , the code generator does not produce an error and proceeds with code generation. In the generated code, ?the?circshift  function incorrectly returns table inputs unchanged.

Summary: Reordering lifelines in the Sequence Viewer block causes MATLAB to hang
Description: In the Sequence Viewer block,?you can reorder lifelines by dragging them. To change the order of a lifeline within its current hierarchical level, you can drag its header symbol so that it occupies a new horizontal position in the header pane.? If you reorder any lifeline which is not at the top level of the lifeline hierarchy, this causes MATLAB to hang.

Summary: Incorrect geometry visualization and meshing with polygonal PCB board shape in pcbStack
Description: Using pcbStack? to create a single layer of metal on top of a dielectric substrate with the BoardShape  property assigned to an antenna.Polygon  object, shows the following inaccuracies in:  The geometry visualized if the board shape specified as a polygon is not centered at the origin of the coordinate system. The results from an analysis because the mesh constructed is incorrect. In particular, the bottom face of the substrate is also covered with metal when only the top should have metal on it.?

Summary: Incorrect table formatting in Simulink Requirements report
Description: In Simulink Requirements, you can use tables in a requirement Description  or Rationale  field. Depending on the table style, table borders might not appear in a report, resulting in inconsistent table formatting between the report and the Requirements Editor.

Summary: Placing a table UI component in a grid layout manager causes App Designer .mlapp file corruption
Description: When an App Designer app has a table UI component in a grid layout manager, the app becomes corrupted.? The file corruption occurs in either of the following situations: 1.? Loading an app that contains a table UI component in a grid layout manager.? The following error is shown:  "Unable to open <path to MLAPP file> because it is corrupt, or has an unrecoverable issue. "    2.? Designing an app that contains a table UI component in a grid layout manager.? This impacts interactive gestures, including re-positioning of the table component, copy/paste, etc.? When designing the app, no errors or warnings are shown.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch between simulation and code generation results for bit operations in variant conditions having normal MATLAB variables
Description: When variant condition expressions contain  bitcmp  or  bitshift  operations with normal MATLAB variables whose data type is not specified (like A = 4 etc.), simulation?uses the assumed type  uint64  for the operand whereas C preprocessor?uses the largest available integer type (example  int64 ). This causes mismatch between simulation and code generation results.

Summary: Favorited gallery buttons persist when switching between Simulink and Stateflow.
Description: When you favorite a gallery button in Simulink, the button appears in the Favorites category at the top of the gallery. You can then favorite the Stateflow gallery button that has the same name, and the two identically named buttons appear side-by-side.?Both buttons are functional, but the expected behavior is that only one should be present.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Low disk space causes MATLAB crash during large Simulink Real-Time model builds
Description: When disk space is low, MATLAB can crash during large Simulink Real-Time model builds. This crash occurs when there is insufficient disk space to write the large real-time application MLDATX?file that the build generates. The stack trace display includes the line: slrt::slrtApplication::finalizeFile
Workaround: To avoid this issue, provide sufficient disk space available on the drive where the model build is writing the MLDATX file.

Summary: Aerospace Blockset Turn Coordinator and Artificial Horizon flight instrument blocks may display incorrect values when connected to discrete signals
Description: Aerospace Blockset Turn Coordinator and Artificial Horizon flight instrument blocks sporadically exchange first and second input values in the block displays when connected to discrete signals.

Summary: MATLAB crash during code generation for AUTOSAR model with multiple bus inports
Description: MATLAB crashes during code generation for an AUTOSAR model if all of the following conditions exist: In the model, a Simulink bus inport connects to more than one block, either at the top level or inside a subsystem. In the Code Mappings editor, the Simulink bus inport is mapped to AUTOSAR data access mode ExplicitReceive . The Simulink bus inport is one of multiple bus inports mapped to the same AUTOSAR receiver port. The bus inport is not the first bus inport in the AUTOSAR receiver port mapping.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for a model behaves differently from normal mode simulation when Block reduction parameter is selected
Description: The code generated for a model might behave differently from normal mode simulation when the model meets the following conditions:?In the model configuration parameter dialog box, the Block Simulation Target > Advanced parameters > Block reduction ?parameter is selected.? An atomic subsystem has no "direct functional connection" between any of its Inport and Outport blocks during normal mode simulation, but block reduction leads to a direct functional connection between a pair of its Inport and Outport blocks when code is generated. This typically occurs when blocks that do not reduce during simulation do reduce during code generation (e.g. the Reshape block).  Turn on display of execution order to determine whether a direct functional connection exists between a pair of Inport and Outport blocks. If no blocks along a signal path from an Inport to an Outport displays their execution order, then all blocks on that path are either inherently virtual or reduced during code generation, and a direct functional connection between a pair of Inport and Outport blocks exists via that path.? If any blocks along that path display their execution order, then that path does not provide a direct functional connection between the Inport and Outport blocks.
Workaround: Clear the model configuration parameter Simulation Target > Advanced parameters > Block reduction.

Summary: The nrCDLChannel?channel model incorrectly models the?orientation of the antenna arrays.
Description: The?TransmitAntennaArray.Orientation ?and?ReceiveAntennaArray.Orientation ?properties of the?nrCDLChannel ?channel model control the mechanical orientation of the transmit and receive antenna arrays, respectively. However, changing these property values does not change the orientation of the antenna arrays correctly and may lead to silent wrong conclusions.?

Summary: Active toolstrip tab is not set correctly upon first model opening
Description: When you save a model, the currently active toolstrip tab is restored as the active tab the next time you open the model. However, the Simulink toolstrip will set the Simulation tab active when the following conditions are true: The model is the first model you open after restarting MATLAB. The model has been configured via a toolstrip app to target a hardware board.   ?For example, the following apps configure the hardware board: Simulink Real-time app Desktop Real-time app "Run on hardware board" app "Run on custom hardware" app Robot Operating System (ROS) app

Summary: Model Advisor check: Comparison operation in Stateflow does not resolve data types of macros defined in a header file
Description: When there is a mismatch in the data types of operands used in a comparison operation in Stateflow, the Model Advisor check: Comparison operation in Stateflow (ID:mathworks.maab.na_0013) flags the data type mismatch. For operands that are macros which are defined in header files, the Model Advisor Engine is unable to resolve the data type as it is outside the scope of static analysis. In this case, the Model Advisor check flags the usage of macros defined in header files even when they have the same datatype.

Summary: Unnecessary global variables might be generated for a model containing a For or a While Iterator subsystems when the States when starting parameter?is set to reset
Description: The generated code may contain unnecessary global variables ending in _IterationMarker  when the following conditions are met: The top-level model references another model containing a For or While Iterator subsystem. The block dialog parameter States when starting  of the For or While Iterator subsystem is set to reset .

Summary: On the App Designer Start Page, clicking on the Interactive Tutorial from Examples: General does not open the tutorial.
Description: On the App Designer Start Page, clicking on the Interactive Tutorial  from Examples: General  does not open the tutorial.There is no error or warning that is displayed.
Workaround: To open the interactive tutorial, use either of the following options:?On the first slide at the top of the App Designer Start Page, click the Start Tutorial button.From the Command Window, run this command and navigate to the documentation page called "Create and Run a Simple App Using App Designer":?docsearch('Create and Run a Simple App Using App Designer')Then, click Open Example from the documentation page.

Summary: Validation model generation from a non-top level DUT generates ambiguous error message
Description: Validation model generation from a non-top level DUT which is present under a masked subsystem generates ambiguous error message as follows: Index exceeds the number of array elements (0)

Summary: Packet-Based ADSB Transceiver example RF mode support in hardware.
Description: The Packet-Based ADS-B Transceiver example in theSoC Blockset produces incorrect results when deployed to the supported hardwareplatforms, Xilinx? Zynq? ZC706 evaluation kit andZedBoard?. In simulation, the model correctly simulates two planes with locationinformation and correctly displays the expected results. When deployed to ahardware platform, the model shows multiple incorrect planes without locationinformation. Only the simulation half of this example can be used successfully.     ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: PLC Coder generates wrong code for the Discrete-time Integrator block using unsupported integrator methods
Description: PLC Code generation does not support the following integrator methods of the Discrete-time Integrator block: 'Integration: Backward Euler',? 'Integration: Trapezoidal', 'Accumulation: Backward Euler', 'Accumulation: Trapezoidal'

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results when building a referenced model that contains a Bus Creator block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect when building a referenced model that meets all of these conditions:The referenced model contains a Bus Creator block that directly connects to a referenced model output.? The signal that connects the Bus Creator block to the referenced model output contains a signal object.? In the Bus Creator Block Parameters dialog box, the Output as nonvirtual bus  parameter is selected.?
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Bus Creator block and the referenced model output. In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, set the Output parameter to Signal copy.

Summary: Signal names specified on a signal line may not appear in the generated C-API file
Description: When generating C-API files, a signal name specified on a signal line from a subsystem may be discarded and not appear in the list of rtwCAPI_Signals  structure when the following conditions are met: The output signal line coming out of the Subsystem is named. A test point is set on the signal line.? The Subsystem's Function packaging  property is marked as Auto .

Summary: MATLAB performance slows down from jcef_helper memory consumption
Description: The MATLAB  jcef_helper  processes increase memory consumption over time, slowing down MATLAB responsiveness.

Summary: Models exported from R2019b to previous versions generate an error when re-opened in R2019b
Description: When you export a model from R2019b to a previous version, re-opening that exported model in R2019b generates an error. This occurs for models with any of the following blocks: CAN Pack  CAN Unpack  CAN FD Pack  CAN FD Unpack   The model opens correctly in earlier versions. Also, the exported model opened and saved in the earlier version will open correctly in R2019b.
Workaround: To work around this issue, open and save the exported model in the earlier version of Simulink. Then you can open that resaved model in R2019b correctly.

Summary: macOS Mission Control windows do not display if Property Inspector is open
Description: If you open the Property Inspector on macOS platforms and then open Mission Control, no application windows display.

Summary: PID block throws non-intuitive?error message when Use filtered derivative?setting is?disabled
Description: Discrete PID Controller block throws a cryptic error message during HDL code generation  when Use filtered derivative  setting is? disabled. "Block 'Discrete PID Controller/Filter/Differentiator/TSamp' is not supported for HDL code generation. To continue with code generation, comment out this block." Disabling the filtered derivative setting is not supported for HDL code generation.

Summary: matlab -r does not support?non-ascii command line arguments
Description: MATLAB does not process non-ascii command line arguments in the  -r  switch. For example,  % matlab -r?"disp('?')"  displays the following character in MATLAB:    >>?

Summary: Generated code might not compile for non-scalar input?Initialize Function, Reset Function, and Terminate Function blocks?in referenced model
Description: Generated code might not compile if all these conditions are true:? The model contains a model reference. The referenced model contains Initialize Function, Reset Function, and Terminate Function blocks. The Initialize Function, Reset Function, and Terminate Function blocks have non-scalar input.?

Summary: Simulink Coder might generate code with an unused local variable
Description: Simulink Coder migh generate code with an unused local variable under the following conditions: The Simulink model contains a Model block. The input to the Model block is a local variable. The referenced model has a Initialize Function block.  Simulink Coder might generate a unused local variable in the parent model's start or initialization function, if this local variable is not used by any other code in that function.

Summary: StartTime and StopTime for test case results incorrect after importing result set
Description: The StartTime  and StopTime  properties for test case results both display as 31-Dec-1969  after importing a result set. This incorrect date might impact the Simulink Requirements verification status, which depends on these properties.

Summary: equilibrate errors for complex sparse arguments
Description: The?equilibrate  function errors when called with a complex sparse matrix.
Workaround: Call equilibrate on the absolute value of a sparse complex matrix instead. The result is equal to that called on the original matrix.?For example, instead of calling equilibrate(A), call equilibrate(abs(A)).

Summary: MEXCUDA does not support Visual Studio 2019 as a host compiler
Description: ?Attempting to use Visual Studio 2019 as a host compiler to compile?MEX-functions for GPU computations results in the following warning: ?Warning: The selected C++ compiler is not supported for CUDA compilation. Searching for a supported compiler.  MEXCUDA then attempts to compile using an available supported compiler.??
Workaround: To use Visual Studio 2019 as a host compiler for MEXCUDA, use the attached custom configuration files. Specify the filepath to the configuration file using the option -f and the name of the file.To compile a MEX-function from a CUDA C++ file, use the following steps:      Download the attached zip file    Unzip the downloaded file into the same location as the source file to be compiled?    Execute the following command, replacing mexGPUExample.cu with the name of the file to be compiled:      mexcuda -f 'nvcc_msvcpp2019.xml' mexGPUExample.cu       If you are compiling with dynamic parallelism (the -dynamic option) use the alternative options file (nvcc_msvcpp2019_dynamic.xml) as follows:        mexcuda -dynamic -f 'nvcc_msvcpp2019_dynamic.xml' mexGPUExample.cu        Change log :November 20, 2019: attachment_2059103_19b_win64_2019_11_27.zip: initial version

Summary: readcell and readmatrix error on Windows for XLSB-file and ODS-file formats
Description: Using readcell  and readmatrix  with XLSB-files and ODS-files on Windows results in an error. For example: C = readcell('sample.xlsb');C = readcell('sample.ods','UseExcel',false);Error using readcell (line 139)Unable to determine range. Range must be of the form 'A1' (cell), 'A:B' (column-select), '1:5' (row-select), 'A1:B5' (rectangle-select), or a valid named range in the sheet.M = readmatrix('sample.ods');M = readmatrix('sample.xlsb','UseExcel',false);Error using readmatrix (line 148)Unable to determine range. Range must be of the form 'A1' (cell), 'A:B' (column-select), '1:5' (row-select), 'A1:B5' (rectangle-select), or a valid named range in the sheet.
Workaround: To avoid this issue on a Windows system with Excel installed, use the 'UseExcel' name-value pair argument with a true value. For example:C = readcell('sample.xlsb','UseExcel',true);M = readmatrix('sample.ods','UseExcel',true);

Summary: Passing an empty GraphicsPlaceholder array to gca crashes MATLAB
Description: If you pass an empty GraphicsPlaceholder  array to the?gca ?function, MATLAB crashes.For example, this command creates an empty GraphicsPlaceholder  array arr .arr = findobj('Type','UndefinedType')  Passing arr  to the gca  function crashes MATLAB.ax = gca(arr);

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you programmatically plot or import data that includes an empty timeseries or timetable?object
Description: When you use the plot ?function to plot a  Simulink.SimulationData.Dataset  or  Simulink.SimulationOutput  object in the Simulation Data Inspector, MATLAB crashes if the object contains a  timeseries ? or timetable ?object with no data. ?The same crash occurs if you use the Simulink.sdi.createRun ?function to import the data into the Simulation Data Inspector.

Summary: MATLAB may crash or assert with messages "Critical Section Interleaved" or "Critical Section Statements out of order"
Description: MATLAB may crash or assert when simulating or generating code from a Simulink model that meets any of the following conditions:? ?The model contains one or more Rate Transition blocks (might be hidden). ?The model configuration parameter Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is set to on.  ?The model simulation mode is either Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator.  Following two stack trace messages might be seen:"Critical Section Interleaved " "Critical Section Statements out of order "

Summary: Incorrect address of model parameter argument in C-API
Description: When using a model parameter argumentin a model, the access to the parameter in the model_InitializeDataAddr? methodof C-API may be incorrect.  Insteadof: / * Initialize Data Address */  static void model_InitializeDataAddr(void* dataAddr[], ?InstP_model_T* model_InstP) {  ? dataAddr[0] = (void*) (&top_17b_P->p1);?  }?The generatedcode might incorrectly access the parameteras:  dataAddr[0] = (void*) (&model_M->model_InstP_ref->p1);  Thiscode fails to compile as the model_M  variable isn't passed intothe?InitializeDataAddr  function.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when creating an atomic subchart around an existing atomic subchart with input events
Description: MATLAB crashes when you create an atomic subchart that contains an existing linked atomic subchart if the linked subchart has input events.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Sum block generates incorrect HDL Code when the List of signs parameter has the value minus (-)
Description: When a Sum block has the?List of signs ?parameter set to the value minus (-) , the generated HDL Code uses sum of elements followed by unary minus.?This results in a mismatch from the Simulink block behavior.

Summary: The Model Advisor check Check fundamental logical and numerical operations?does not consider the base data type of the input signals when the data type of input signals is defined as "Simulink.AliasType"
Description: The Model Advisor check Check fundamental logical and numerical operations ?(ID:mathworks.maab.na_0002 ) checks for appropriate usage of basic logical and numerical operations. Signals with Boolean alias type (Simulink.AliasType) connected to logical operator block is flagged by the check even though their base type is Boolean. For a similar issue see the following?bug report .

Summary: Detailed code inspection report incorrectly marks the false branch of an if-else statement as optimized
Description: The detailed code inspection report incorrectly marks the false branch of an if-else statement as optimized when it should be marked as write after write. The code inspection report summary marks the branch as write after write.

Summary: Code generator produces an assertion if a cell array is grown by using concatenation
Description: If you grow a cell array variable by using concatenation in your MATLAB code, the code generator might produce this assertion: Assertion at mclass/eml_mclass.cpp line 1167: In with_size: Assertion failed: !isCell()?  This is an example of the MATLAB code pattern that might cause this assertion: x = [];for i = 1:n    x = [x {1 2 3}];  % <-- this line causes the assertionend This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink and MATLAB functions in Stateflow charts.
Workaround: If var is a cell array, avoid using the?var = [...var...] syntax. Consider preallocating the cell array explicitly instead of growing it in a loop.

Summary: Code generation fails when specifying a variable-size table input argument with variables having the categorical, duration, or table data types
Description: When generating code, using coder.typeof  to specify a variable-size table with variable number of rows might fail. In particular, if the table contains variables having the categorical  or duration  data types, or if the table is a nested table, then the following error message is returned: coder.resize is not supported for a coder.ClassType object.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model build of Simulink Real-Time model that includes vector or matrix root inport data may generate wrong answer
Description: When you build a Simulink Real-Time model that receives vector or matrix data --multiple entries per time step-- through root level inports, the generated code produces a? wrong answer. The wrong answer is that the data is not arranged properly. This issue can be difficult to locate and diagnose.

Summary: Model build of Simulink Real-Time model that includes padded bus root inport data fails during code generation
Description: When you build a Simulink Real-Time model that includes padded bus root inport data, the model build fails during code generation. The build error message is: Unable to perform assignment because the left and right sides have a different number of elements.   In generated code, a Simulink Bus appears as a C struct. When the Bus contains different data types, the code generator forms a padded struct in generated code. For example, a Bus that contains an int8 ?data type and a double ?data type has byte padding in the generated code and will encounter this bug.

Summary: Pedestrian model outputs incorrect velocity estimation when the sampling interval changes
Description: When calling the move  method on phased.BackscatterPedestrian  or backscatterPedestrian , the resulting velocity of body segments will be incorrect if the duration of the next walking interval changes in consecutive calls. For example: [bppos,bpvel,bpax] = move(pedestrian,t1,0);??[bppos,bpvel,bpax] = move(pedestrian,t2,0);

Summary: Exception thrown when using C Caller block inside a Function-Call subsystem
Description: ?If a Simulink model has a Function-Call subsystem containing a C Caller block, an exception is thrown during model update if the block dialog of the C Caller block is open and the selected function is changed.
Workaround: Save and reopen the model.

Summary: In Signal Analyzer, fitting spectrum plots to view for complex-valued signals results in inconsistent limits between the spectrum view and the spectrogram view
Description: In Signal Analyzer, if you zoom into a spectrum plot of a complex-valued signal and then reset the view by clicking?Fit to View , the resulting spectrogram and spectrum views in the app have different frequency-axis limits.

Summary: Code generated from MATLAB enumerations in packages can fail to compile
Description: When generating C/C++ code from a MATLAB enumeration class that is contained in a MATLAB package, say pkg.AnEnum , the code generator can produce code that fails to compile. This error happens because the dots " . " in the MATLAB class name are printed in the generated code, which produces invalid code.   Specifically, the invalid C/C++ code is produced from a dynamically sized array whose base type is such an enumeration, or from a dynamically sized array of structures where one of the struct fields is such an enumeration. This is an example C/C++ compiler error that this behavior causes: codegen/dll/foo/foo_emxAPI.h:26:46: error: expected ˇ®=ˇŻ, ˇ®,ˇŻ, ˇ®;ˇŻ, ˇ®asmˇŻ or ˇ®__attribute__ˇŻ before ˇ®.ˇŻ token  ? ?extern emxArray_pkg_AnEnum *emxCreateND_pkg.AnEnum(int numDimensions, const  The dot ". " in the name emxCreateND_pkg.AnEnum ?causes this C/C++ compiler error.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code for Multiply-Accumulate block with matrix inputs
Description: MATLAB might crash when generating HDL code for a Multiply-Accumulate block with: HDL Architecture  set to Parallel  in the HDL Block Properties dialog box? Operation Mode  is set to Vector  in Block Parameters dialog box? Inputs to the block are matrices

Summary: BridgeMode property cannot be set to 'Half' or 'Full'
Description: Setting a bridge channel BridgeMode  property value to 'Half'  or 'Full'  silently fails, and its value remains at the default value of 'Quarter' , even though the display reflects the requested change. The issue affects the following example: s = daq.createSession('ni');ch = addAnalogInputChannel(s, "cDAQ1Mod2","ai0","Bridge")ch =Data acquisition analog input channel 'ai0' on device 'cDAQ1Mod4':             BridgeMode: Unknown       ExcitationSource: Internal      ExcitationVoltage: 2.5NominalBridgeResistance: 'Unknown'                  Range: -0.025 to +0.025 VoltsPerVolt                   Name: ''                     ID: 'ai0'                 Device: [1x1 daq.ni.CompactDAQModule]        MeasurementType: 'Bridge'          ADCTimingMode: '' s.Channels(1).BridgeMode = 'Half'ch =Data acquisition analog input channel 'ai0' on device 'cDAQ1Mod4':             BridgeMode: Half       ExcitationSource: Internal      ExcitationVoltage: 2.5NominalBridgeResistance: 'Unknown'                  Range: -0.025 to +0.025 VoltsPerVolt                   Name: ''                     ID: 'ai0'                 Device: [1x1 daq.ni.CompactDAQModule]        MeasurementType: 'Bridge'          ADCTimingMode: ''  Despite the fact that the BridgeMode  property value is reported as 'Half' , its actual value is 'Quarter' .
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 5, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Chapter reporter ignores page number format from custom template
Description: For chapters other than the first chapter of a PDF report, the Report API Chapter  reporter ignores the page number format from a custom template.
Workaround: Customize the page number format for each chapter by using the?Chapter?reporter's?Layout property. For example:ch = mlreportgen.report.Chapter("MyChapter"); ch.Layout.PageNumberFormat = "A";

Summary: Create test for model component wizard errors if the model uses Simulink.Signal' objects
Description: When the Create test for model component wizard generates tests for a subsystem containing Simulink.Signal elements, this error message occurs : Subsystem1: The Simulink signal object 'sigObj1' for output port 1 of 'mSigObjects/? Subsystem1/? Gain' has logging information set. The LoggingInfo field of the Signal object is only supported for Data Store Memory and must not be set for signals.

Summary: Code inspection report provides incorrect result for a function call in an if condition
Description: The code inspection report summary incorrectly states that a function call in an if  condition has a write after write pattern. In the detailed report section, the report states that it has a write with no subsequential read.The code inspection report summary should state lines of code inconsequential and in the detailed report section, unexpected function call.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for coding pattern with the same structure read and written in the same assignment
Description: Simulink might generate code with incorrect read/write ordering for a MATLAB statement containing: A structure variable written as the destination of an assignment A field of the same structure read in the same statement The structure contains multiple fields  For example, see the attached model issue_2054062_model in attached issue_2054062.zip. This model contains a MATLAB function with the statement: s1 = A(s1.f2);? In this statement s1.f2 is read before writing to s1. But the generated code is  s1_f1 = A[s1_f2].f1;s1_f2 = A[s1_f2].f2;s1_f3 = A[s1_f2].f3;s1_f4 = A[s1_f2].f4; Note that the last two statements read s1_f2 after it has been written. Instead the value of s1_f2 must be stored in a temporary like the following: temp = s1_f2 s1_f1 = A[temp].f1;s1_f2 = A[temp].f2;s1_f3 = A[temp].f3;s1_f4 = A[temp].f4; This issue can affect a model containing:?  MATLAB Function Block MATLAB Function in Stateflow MATLAB System Block   When generating code or running rapid accelerator simulation.
Workaround: This issue is due to an optimization within the code generator that explodes structure access into multiple variables representing individual structure fields. This optimization is not properly accounting for the nested structure access in the assignment:s1 = A(s1.f2); As a workaround, this optimization can be disabled using coder.ceval:s1 = A(s1.f2); coder.ceval('(void)',s1);In the generated code, the structure s1 will not be exploded into its fields and the issue does not occur.s1 = A[s1.f2];(void)s1;

Summary: Double-clicking a commented Bus Creator block might crash Simulink
Description: Double-clicking?a commented Bus Creator block might crash Simulink.

Summary: After saving or running an existing .mlapp file in App Designer, recent code changes cannot be undone by pressing?Ctrl+Z.
Description: After saving or running an existing .mlapp  file in App Designer, recent code changes cannot be undone by pressing?Ctrl+Z .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model containing Enabled Synchronous Subsystem or Resettable Synchronous Subsystem and?Single output/update function?parameter set for referenced model with states might generate incorrect C code
Description: Embedded Coder might generate numerically incorrect code when a model meets all the following conditions: In the referenced model, Model configuration parameter Single output/update function ?parameter is selected. The model contains synchronous semantics. The model has states.  AND  In the top model, Model configuration parameter Single output/update function ?parameter is selected. The referenced model is inside an Enabled Synchronous Subsystem or a Resettable Synchronous Subsystem.  The results obtained from running this C code might differ from simulation results.??

Summary: Adding tests for missing coverage from test case results produces aggregated results only for added tests
Description: From test case results, if you auto generate tests using the Add tests for missing coverage button , when you run the test case, the?aggregated coverage results only show the coverage for the newly added tests.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might have wrong results due to incorrect ordering of initialization statements
Description: In some cases involving execution order with "constant tasks", the ordering of initialization statements might be incorrect which could result in the generated code producing the wrong output. For example, prior to R2019b:? ? ? ? ? ?product := 1;?  ? ? ? ? ?Out1 := product;  In R2019b:? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?Out1 := product; ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?product := 1;?? This incorrect ordering of initialization statements might produce the wrong output. ?This happens when a model execution order with constant tasks. In the above example, the product is computed in a constant task and its order got affected. https://www.mathworks.com/help/releases/R2019b/simulink/ug/controlling-and-displaying-the-sorted-order.html ?

Summary: App Designer message panel in GUIDE causes high idle CPU usage and might cause MATLAB to become unresponsive
Description: When GUIDE is open and the App Designer message panel is displayed, the following issues can occur: The computer's idle CPU usage increases by 10 - 30% potentially impacting the performance of all running applications on the system. Some charting interactions, such as trying to add a data tip, can cause MATLAB to become unresponsive or the action to not take place.
Workaround: Clear the "Show App Designer message panel" check box in the MATLAB GUIDE preferences.

Summary: Polyspace analysis stops in pre-analysis phase with error: Internal error: *** Assertion failure ***
Description: When verifying a complex C application, Polyspace can stop during the pre-analysis phase with this error message: **** Bug-finder - Pre-analysis - 4 (P_MA) - timeout - **** Bug-finder - Pre-analysis - 4 (P_MA) took xxxx.yyreal,? ?xxxx.yyu + zs (xxx.yygc)**** Bug-finder - Pre-analysis - 5 (P_G)Internal error: *** Assertion failure ***
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: The Simulink Toolstrip does not render correctly when using a per-application DPI override on Windows
Description: The Simulink Toolstrip might fail to render as expected when moved between monitors on Windows 10.? When encountering this problem, the Simulink Toolstrip renders at an inappropriate size--for example, it might only cover a quarter of the expected area. ?The problem occurs when the following conditions are true:  The affected system has two or more monitors The monitors have differing DPI scaling values A per-application DPI scaling override for MATLAB is applied
Workaround: Open Windows Explorer and navigate to your MATLAB install directory.Right click "matlab.exe" and select "Properties".View the "Compatibility" tab.Click the button labeled "Change high DPI settings".Deselect the checkbox labeled "Override high DPI scaling behavior."Click "OK" to close this dialog.Click "OK" to close the matlab.exe Properties dialog.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect?Non-initialized variable in C++ on member variable?that is constant
Description: When a class member is declared const , it must be initialized at the time of object construction and cannot be non-initialized when read later if your code compiles with a C++ compiler. However, Bug Finder might show an incorrect?Non-initialized variable ?defect on read operations on a const  data member. This example shows a possible scenario: struct A { ? A(bool isOk):ok(isOk) {}? void f() const {? ? ? ? if (ok) {}? ?// "Non-initialized variable" defect? }? const bool ok; };   void foo(bool x) {? ? A a(x); ? ? a.f(); }?  In foo() , the member variable?a.ok  is initialized when the object a  is created. Therefore,?a.ok ?cannot be non-initialized in the function?a.f()  later.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR generated code might write uninitialized value if IRV feeds back to runnable
Description: AUTOSAR generated code might write an uninitialized value to an IRV if all of the following conditions exist: The model contains a subsystem that is mapped to an AUTOSAR runnable. The subsystem writes to a signal that is mapped to an AUTOSAR IRV. The subsystem reads from the IRV signal that it writes.  Under these conditions, the generated code might write an uninitialized variable.

Summary: Polyspace analysis stops after compilation with error: A segmentation fault occurred in "ps_cxx_fe_ia64.exe"
Description: When verifying a complex C++ application, Polyspace can stop during the compilation phase with an error messages like one of the following: Internal error: assertion failed at: ".../src/expr.c", line 5541  Error: A segmentation fault occurred in "ps_cxx_fe_ia64.exe"

Summary: Unnecessary warning message generated by n-D Lookup Table block during HDL code generation when using linear interpolation
Description: The LUT block configured with linear interpolation throws the following warning message when generating HDL code: "Using linear interpolation on the Lookup Table (n-D) block, ?may require, ?using a divide operator in the generated HDL, ?which may not be synthesizable."  This warning is not relevant starting in R2017b as a divison operator is no longer generated.?

Summary: Broken traceability linkfor Delay block with Reset type?set to None?following a Lookup Table
Description: Delay block with?Reset type ?set to?None ?following a Lookup Table maps to a ROM in thegenerated HDL code, which causes a broken link in the traceability report.?

Summary: Code inspection fails for a model containing Boolean datatype that is imported from C99 math library
Description: A Simulink model that is configured to use C99 math library fails code inspection if the model uses a Boolean datatype that is imported from stdbool.h.

Summary: Observer Port blocks observing non-virtual bus with different data type elements might produce incorrect output
Description: If a non-virtual bus being observed by an Observer Port block has bus elements of different data types, the Observer Port block output might not match the signal being observed. In the non-virtual bus output of the Observer Port block, one or more bus elements near the end of the bus object structure will be constantly zero, regardless of their actual value in the system model. No error or warning is issued during model compile or simulation.?

Summary: Parallel build generates makefile with wrong optimization flags when 'BuildConfiguration' for referenced model is 'Faster Runs'
Description: If your model contains a model reference hierarchy and the?'BuildConfiguration' ?parameter for a referenced model is set to?'Faster Runs' , then building the referenced models in parallel generates a makefile with the wrong optimization flags. The generated makefile contains the optimization flags for 'Faster Builds'  instead of the flags for?'Faster Runs' .  The parallel worker uses the correct optimization flags to build the referenced model binaries. However, if you build the referenced model binaries with the generated makefile, the incorrect optimization flags are used.  ?
Workaround: Insert the correct optimization flags by manually modifying the generated makefile.

Summary: Signal Analyzer does not plot spectra of complex-valued signals generated by extracting regions of interest from timetables
Description: In Signal Analyzer, if you import a timetable with complex-valued and then generate a new signal by extracting a region of interest, the app produces an empty spectrum plot.

Summary: HDL coding standard report shows violation for Check for non-integer constants when floating-point library is specified
Description: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the HDL Code Generation > Global Settings > Coding standards  tab, when you set HDL coding standard  to Industry  and generate code, the HDL coding standard report shows a violation for the check Check for non-integer constants  even when?Floating Point IP Library  is set to Native Floating Point  or other target floating-point libraries.

Summary: Error "Undefined function 'isvalid'" for code that calls uifigure and axes in debugger
Description: MATLAB returns an error when both of the following conditions are true: You run the debugger using dbstop if error , or you use the Pause on Error  option Your code creates an axes either in App Designer, or in a figure created with the uifigure  function  The error returns the following message: Undefined function 'isvalid' for input arguments of type 'double'.  Error in matlab.graphics.interaction.graphicscontrol.ControlManager/removeControl (line 62)  if(isvalid(this)) %Makes sure that this object is still valid and not destroyed ealier.  Error in matlab.graphics.shape.internal.ScribeStackManager/createLayer (line 62)  ? ? ? ? ? ? set(hOverlay,'Parent',matlab.graphics.primitive.world.Group.empty);  Error in matlab.graphics.shape.internal.ScribeStackManager/getLayer (line 81)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? hLayer = matlab.graphics.shape.internal.ScribeStackManager.createLayer(hViewer,layerName,[]);  Error in matlab.graphics.internal.CanvasSetup.createScribeLayers (line 13)  ? ? ? ? ? ? if? isempty(cM.getLayer(canvas,'overlay'))  Error in matlab.graphics.controls.HTMLCanvasPlugin/createCanvas (line 14)  ? ? ? ? ? ? can = hObj.Factory.createCanvas();
Workaround: Refrain from either the dbstop if error option or the Pause on Error option in the debugger. Instead, set a standard break point in the debugger to step through your code.

Summary: Linearization error when creating linearization advisor object for Simulink model that includes model reference with bus inport or outport blocks
Description: When your Simulink model includes a model reference that uses a?Bus ?data type for any of its Inport or Outport blocks, creating a linearization advisor object during linearization produces the following error message: Error using linearize (line 353)  Index exceeds the number of array elements (0).  This error occurs when you create a linearization advisor: At the command line by setting the? StoreAdvisor   linearization option to true  In the Linear Analysis Tool  by selecting the Linearization Advisor  option on the Linear Analysis  tab
Workaround: Configure any Inport and Outport blocks that use a?Bus?data type to output a nonvirtual bus signal. To do so for:?Inport blocks, select the Output as nonvirtual bus?parameterOutput blocks, select the Output?as nonvirtual bus in parent model parameter

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify a Lookup Table block that uses a Simulink.LookupTable object, when the block is in a masked subsystem
Description: A model containing a Lookup Table block which uses a?Simulink.LookupTable ?object,?fails code inspection when the following conditions are met: The Lookup Table block is in a masked subsystem. The mask parameter name for the?Simulink.LookupTable ?object is different from the actual name of the?Simulink.LookupTable ?object.

Summary: Schedule Editor comparison in Model Comparison tool is missing.
Description: Contents of Schedule Editor are not compared in the Model Comparison tool. This does not affect the comparison of other features of the model.?

Summary: MATLAB Job Scheduler version R2019a or newer is incompatible with MATLAB workers version R2017a Update 3 or older when using security level 2 or 3, or when enabling secure communication
Description: MATLABJob Scheduler supports scheduling jobs on MATLAB workers running olderreleases. If the MATLAB Job Scheduler is configured to use security level 2 or 3, then any credentials communicated between the MATLAB Job Scheduler and the workers are encrypted using SSL. If the MATLAB Job Scheduler is configured to use secure communication, which is required when it runs with security level 3, then all communication between the MATLAB Job Scheduler and the workers is encrypted using SSL. If the version ofthe MATLAB Job Scheduler is R2019a or newer and the version of the MATLABworker is R2017a Update 3 or older, then the handshake required to establish SSLcommunication fails. Any task that you submit to the scheduler from a R2017a orolder MATLAB client will finish with the following error:An error occurred during execution of Task with ID 1.  Caused by:  ? ? Cannot rerun task because there are no rerun attempts left (The task has no rerun attempts left.).  ? ? Original cancel message:  ? ? java.lang.Exception: Job setup failed - MATLAB will now exit and restart.  To confirm the causeof this exception, you can check the job manager logs that are located inthe?LOGBASE folder. The? LOGBASEfolder is defined in the mjs_def  file of the MATLAB Job Scheduler. The failureto establish SSL communication is indicated by the following log statement:"Exception: javax.net.ssl.SSLHandshakeException: DHPublicKey does not comply to algorithm constraints"  This problem does not affect MATLAB workers whose version is R2017a Update 4 or newer.
Workaround: To fix the problem, edit any worker MATLAB install which is R2017a Update 3 or older as follows:Edit the file <matlabroot>/toolbox/distcomp/distcomp_evaluate_task.m to replace the existing line:"jobManagerProxy.forceVanillaRmi(true);"?with?"jobManagerProxy.forceVanillaRmi(false);"?where <matlabroot> is the location of the MATLAB install on the worker host.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing an?output port of a reusable MATLAB Function block connected to a Terminator block
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model contains a MATLAB Function block. The MATLAB Function block has the block parameter Treat as atomic unit ?enabled and Function packaging ?parameter is set to Reusable function . The MATLAB Function block has more than one input and output ports.? The same variable is used for both an input and an output of the MATLAB Function block and the output port is connected to a Terminator block.??  In the generated code the input port data is not passed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing a?Unit Delay block inside a?For Each Subsystem block
Description: The generated code contains an incorrect initialization of a Unit Delay block state for a model that meets all of these conditions: The Unit Delay block is inside a For Each subsystem. The Initial Condition  of the Unit Delay block initialization is specified by a parameter instead of a fixed value. In the For Each Subsystem block parameters dialog box, the?Parameter Partition ?is selected .
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 6, R2019b Update 2, R2020a

Summary: Assertion error during code generation when assigning a Stateflow variable with custom code variable
Description: Stateflow might throw an assertion error during code generation when all of the following conditions are true: A Stateflow chart contains two transitions originating from one junction and ending on the another junction Both transitions assign the same Stateflow variable The first transition assigns the Stateflow variable with a custom code variable, for example, with the transition label [in > 0] {out = customCodeVar;}  The second transition assigns the Stateflow variable with a non custom code variable or a constant, for example with the transition label {out = 1.1;}  The custom code variable is defined with a type alias, for example typedef float MyReal32_T  The option Import custom code  is selected on the Simulation Target pane of configuration options  The assertion error is similar to: CGIR assertion 'SaneConditionalExprs violated: conditional expr output type and false expr type must match' failed in 'invariant/SaneConditionalExprs.cpp:71'?

Summary: slreq.createLink(src, dest)?errors if one input is?slreq.Reference, or when?performing Clear Issue action
Description: When importing Requirements from .reqif file with the default options, you get a hierarchical collection of slreq.Reference objects. If you later want to create a link from or to?such an?object, an error might display at the MATLAB?command prompt: "Invalid argument in a call to slreq.createLink", or other similar error to do with invalid input argument type. Additionally, when using "Clear Issue" action for the link flagged with a change issue (due to changes with the linked requirement), a dialog box might appear which says "DATESTR failed converting date number to date vector".

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reciprocal block with ShiftAdd architecture can generate incorrect HDL code for all rounding modes with saturation mode cleared
Description: For the Reciprocal block with ShiftAdd  specified as the Architecture  in the HDL Block Properties dialog box, if you clear the Saturate on Integer Overflow  check box, all?Integer rounding mode  options can generate incorrect HDL code.?
Workaround: Either use Linear architecture or use Zero as the Integer Rounding?mode?and?Saturate on Integer Overflow?check box selected when using the?ShiftAdd?architecture.

Summary: Data store logging using Data Store Memory block and Discrete-Event System block might produce incorrect results
Description: Data store logging using a Data Store Memory block might produce incorrectresults when both of the following conditions are met: ?A custom MATLAB Discrete-Event System block exchanges data with a Data Store Memory block. The MATLAB Discrete-EventSystem block is configured to simulate through generated code

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a model with MATLAB System and Data Store Memory Read blocks
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a MATLAB System block under all the following conditions: The MATLAB System block has a?updateImpl  method. The MATLAB System block writes to a globlal MATLAB variable in the?updateImpl  method. The global MATLAB variable is read by a Data Store Memory Read block.  The value read by the Data Store Memory Read block might be off by one timestep when compared to the simulation result.

Summary: Reading corrupted multiframe GIF files using imread throws error: Unable to perform assignment
Description: Reading certain corrupted multiframe GIF files using?imread  might throw the following?error in MATLAB: Unable to perform assignment because the size of the left side is 255-by-636 and the size of the right side is 181-by-542.

Summary: MATLAB might crash while generating HDL code for Product of Elements with adaptive pipelining
Description: MATLAB might crash while generating HDL code for vector inputs to Product of Elements block when Adaptive pipelining  is enabled in?HDL Code Generation > Optimization > Pipelining  pane of Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Unable to view or edit properties of objects using Property Inspector, or unable to open multiple Property Inspector windows
Description: When you double-click on some objects in the Workspace Browser, the Property Inspector opens instead of the Variables Editor. In the Property Inspector, you may not be able to view or edit property values that are arrays or objects. Only one Property Inspector window can be open at a time, so you cannot see the properties of multiple objects at once using the Property Inspector.
Workaround: View or edit property values in the Variables Editor instead of the Property Inspector by using the openvar?function. For example, create a ParquetInfo object. Then, view its properties using the Variables Editor.info = parquetinfo('outages.parquet');openvar('info')

Summary: Compilation time may be slow in models that log output signals of referenced subsystems.
Description: Slow compilation time can result when a model has duplicate logged signals in the InstrumentedSignals  model parameter. Duplication in the InstrumentedSignals  property can occur when the model logs output signals of a referenced subsystem. The increased compilation time is spent in the HMI compile settings initialization  phase. You can see the compilation phase in the status bar of the Simulink Editor during compilation.  ?To confirm that the increased compilation time is caused by duplicates in the InstrumentedSignals ?property:? Access the InstrumentedSignals property for the model:instSigs = get_param(bdroot,'InstrumentedSignals');  Check the Count property of the InstrumentedSignals object: instSigs.Count  Check the number of signals that appear in the Simulation Data Inspector or in the logged signals  Dataset  ( logsout ) in the workspace.   If the signal count in the InstrumentedSignals ? object is larger than the number of signals in the logged signals workspace variable or the count of signals in the Simulation Data Inspector, the InstrumentedSignals ? property for the model contains duplicates.?
Workaround: Possible workarounds:Copy model contents into a new .slx file.?Stop logging signals by clearing the InstrumentedSignals property for the model:set_param(bdroot,'InstrumentedSignals',[]);Run a clean-up script.?To run the clean-up script, unzip the attachment and save the cleanupInstrumentedSignals.p file in a folder on the MATLAB path. Then, open the model and enter cleanupInstrumentedSignals(bdroot) into the MATLAB Command Window.

Summary: Dependency Analyzer reports a dependency on Simulink when using some functions in Optimization Toolbox
Description: When a MATLAB file calls certain functions in Optimization Toolbox, for example?lsqcurvefit ?or fmincon , the Dependency Analyzer reports that your file requires that Simulink be installed.

Summary: Polyspace analysis of a C++ function fails with a stack overflow error message
Description: When you analyze a complex C++ function (> 40000 lines of code), the analysis fails with a stack overflow error message similar to this: ... **** Bug-finder - Function Analysis - 736 (Verified functions #73501 to #73600 on 104249) took 26.4realexception Stack overflow(Stack overflow) raised.raised at: Raised by primitive operation at file "../verifier/kernel_api/IL/simplify.sml", line 1698, characters 79-105Called from file "../verifier/kernel_api/IL/simplify.sml", line 1698, characters 79-105Called from file "../verifier/kernel_api/IL/simplify.sml", line 1905, characters 29-48Called from file "../verifier/kernel_api/IL/simplify.sml", line 1905, characters 29-48 ...
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Deep learning network that uses MKL-DNN v0.14 on AVX-512 CPUs can generate wrong output for certain convolution operations
Description: Generated code for deep learning networks that uses the MKL-DNN target library version 0.14 can generate incorrect output if all of the conditions below are met:The target processor uses Intel AVX-512 ISA extensions. The input tensors to a convolution layer have height and width that are multiples of 224. The padding right parameter to the convolution layer has a non-zero value. The output filters are multiples of 16 to the convolution layer.  For example, if the input tensor dimensions are 3-by-224-by-224 (c,h,w), the padding parameters are [0 0 0 1] (top, bottom, left, right), and the filter dimensions are 3-by-3-by-3-by-16 (h,w,i,o), then the convolution layer produces incorrect output.

Summary: Various cases of growing a gpuArray using linear indexing assignment results in an error
Description: Using a linear index to assign a value to an empty gpuArray, or increasing the size of a gpuArray by assignment using linear indexing results in the following error:? Error using gpuArray/subsasgn  Attempt to grow array along ambiguous dimension.?   The error occurs in various cases where MATLAB cannot determine the dimension along which to grow the array. The following representative examples produce the error:?? Example 1:  g  is a 0-by-0 array.? g = gpuArray([]);  g(2) = 3;  Example 2:  ?g ?is a non-square, non-vector array with an empty dimension. g = gpuArray.zeros(2, 0);  g(2) = 3;  Example 3:  g ?is a 1-by-1-by...by-N array.? g = gpuArray.zeros(1, 1, 1, 2);?  g(3) = 3  Example 4:  g  is a gpuArray inside a dlarray. Error occurs during assignment inside a call to dlfeval.  a = dlarray(randn(5,'gpuArray'));  loss = dlfeval(@modelGradients,a);  function g = modelGradients(a)  ? ? for i = 1:3  ? ? ? ? g(i) = mean(a, 'all') + i;?  ? ? end  end ?
Workaround: To avoid this error, either preallocate the gpuArray or construct the gpuArray in advance.For? example:g = zeros(1,'gpuArray');g(2) = 3;If you encounter this error in the example?Sequence-to-Sequence Classification Using 1-D Convolutions, replace the maskedCrossEntropyLoss function with the following function:function loss = maskedCrossEntropyLoss(dlY, dlT, numTimeSteps)numObservations = size(dlY,2);loss = zeros([1, 1], 'like', dlY)for i = 1:numObservations    idx = 1:numTimeSteps(i);    loss(i) = crossentropy(dlY(:,i,idx),dlT(:,i,idx),'DataFormat','CBT');?end?end

Summary: Code inspection fails for arithmetic shift operations when the Casting Modes model configuration parameter set to Standards Compliant
Description: When the Casting Modes  model configuration parameter is set to Standards Compliant , code inspection fails to verify the explicit type cast in the generated code for arithmetic shift operations.

Summary: Setting session Rate after a single scanmeasurement causes later single scans to return stale values
Description: While using the session interfacewith National Instruments hardware (e.g., NI USB-6218), setting the session Rate  property after calling inputSingleScan  will result in future calls to inputSingleScan returning stale values. For example, s = daq.createSession('ni');addAnalogInputChannel(s,'Dev1','ai0','Voltage');s.Rate = 2000;% The following measurement value is correct:data1 = inputSingleScan(s);% After changing the session Rate, subsequent inputSingleScan measurements will return buffered (stale) values.s.Rate = 1000;dataStale = inputSingleScan(s);

Summary: False "Invalid root Inport/Outport block connection"?warning or error?for referenced models with inactive variants
Description: When the Invalid root Inport/Outport block connection ?configuration parameter is set to warning  or error  in a referenced model and one of the root output ports is in an inactive variant, a warning or error will always be reported.

Summary: Enumeration class errors for AUTOSAR component model when enumerated type in data dictionary types outports connected to Signal Invalidation blocks
Description: An AUTOSAR component model generates enumeration class errors if the model uses enumerated types stored in a Simulink data dictionary to type Outport blocks connected to Signal Invalidation blocks.?The following errors might occur when viewing the outport Communication  attributes in the Property Inspector view for the Code Mappings editor, validating the AUTOSAR mapping of the model, or generating code: Cannot define enumeration class 'myEnumeratedType' because it was previously defined by a data dictionary.Cannot clear enumeration class 'myEnumeratedType' because it was created by a data dictionary.
Workaround: Move the definition of the enumerated type out of the data dictionary and into the base or model workspace.

Summary: Collecting model metrics produces an error with Diagnostic Event Manager caller blocks
Description: Collecting model metrics with Diagnostic Event Manager blocks such as DiagnosticInfoCaller, DiagnosticMonitorCaller, and DiagnosticEventAvailableCaller might produce the following metrics calculation error: Error during compilation of Simulink model 'autosar_swc_expfcns'.Caused by:Error due to multiple causes.A definition for the function 'ExampleGet_GetEventStatus' called by Function Caller block 'autosar_swc_expfcns Runnable2_subsystem DiagnosticInfoCaller',?could not be found.
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check

Summary: Validation of AUTOSAR mapping might error out unnecessarily with mapped signals
Description: The mapping validation for an AUTOSAR model might unnecessarily error out if all of the following conditions exist: A subsystem output signal is mapped for AUTOSAR, as displayed in the Code Mappings editor,?Signals/States ?tab. The underlying signal within the subsystem, which is connected to the outport of the subsystem output signal, is also mapped for AUTOSAR. One of the two signals is mapped to?Auto , while the other is mapped to AUTOSAR variable type?ArTypedPerInstanceMemory ?or?StaticMemory .  The error message reports a signal configuration conflict. For example: Conflicting signal configuration. The code generation properties in code perspective for signal on output port 1 of 'autosar_swc/SS1/Sum' conflicts with signal on output port 1 of 'autosar_swc/SS1' since both outputs map to the same signal.
Workaround: Remove the AUTOSAR mapping for the signal that is mapped to?Auto.

Summary: Static code metrics report is not generated when using a MinGW compiler for MATLAB code
Description: When MinGW  is set as the default compiler, errors occur and a Static Code Metrics Report is not generated for MATLAB code.
Workaround: If Microsoft Visual Studio is available on your machine, set it as the default compiler using this command: mex -setup

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsystem with multirate input signals generates incorrect PLC code when systemSampleTime is set
Description: When a Subsystem with multirate input signals has the systemSampleTime   parameter set, the generated PLC code is multirate instead of single rate.
Workaround: Set the systemSampleTime parameter?to -1 when using multirate signalsUse single rate signals in the entire model to generate single rate code

Summary: App Designer components are incorrectly positioned
Description: When loading a saved app in App Designer, components inside Panel  objects that are parented to GridLayout  objects are incorrectly placed. They might be shifted vertically in either direction. However, when you run the app, the components are positioned correctly. Repositioning the components in App Designer and re-saving the app does not fix the issue.
Workaround: To work around the issue, use the following steps to replace the panel with a tab group and a tab:1. In App Designer, move the panel for which the content is incorrectly positioned, to a temporary location, for example below the uifigure.2. Add a tab group and tab where the panel was previously positioned.3. Move the content of the panel to the tab. To do so:a. In the Component Browser, right-click on any component.b. Check Include component labels in Component Browser.c. Locate the panel in the Component Browser and multi-select all the components inside the panel by holding the Shift key.d. Place the mouse over the selected components in the canvas, and drag them into the tab. If you are unable to drag them directly into the tab, first drag them outside the panel to a temporary location, then drag them into the tab.4. Delete the panel.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing multiple?Reusable?custom storage class with a?branched root Inport
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: A pair of root-level Inport and Outport signals contain the same?Reusable ?custom storage class specification. The root Inport signal is branched and feeds into an atomic subsystem. The Function Packaging  parameter in the Block Parameters of a subsystem is set to Auto  or Reusable function . The subsystem is reused in the generated code.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a Simulink Function block that modifies a Data Store Memory block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:A Simulink Function block modifies a Data Store Memory block. A Function Caller block, a MATLAB System block or a MATLAB Function block reads from or writes to the Data Store Memory block before and after the Simulink Function block modifies the Data Store Memory block.
