Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a MATLAB System block
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a MATLAB System block under all the following conditions: The MATLAB System block has a?updateImpl  method. The MATLAB System block inherits from?matlab.system.mixin.Nondirect  class The MATLAB System block has a?isInputDirectFeedthroughImpl  method that returns false.  The output of the MATLAB System block might be off by one timestep when compared to the simulation result.

Summary: MATLAB crashes during simulation?when string output?from a referenced model feeds a Terminator block
Description: MATLAB crashes when simulating a top?model with the following characteristics:? The top model references a model (A), which references another model (B). Referenced model B has an output signal with a string data type. The output string signal connects to only a Terminator block in model A. In the top model, the Model block that references model A is configured to simulate in accelerator mode.

Summary: HTML to DOM conversion ignores style sheet styles for HTML pre elements
Description:
If a style for pre elements is defined in a style sheet of HTML text or an HTML file, the style is ignored, with a warning, when the HTML is converted to DOM representation by using mlreportgen.dom.HTML or mlreportgen.dom.HTMLFile . For example, suppose that the file example.html contains the following HTML: ??? ??? 
Hello world!
? If you convert the file to DOM representation and add it to a document, the "Hello world!" text is not green? and a warning is produced: >> mlreportgen.dom.HTMLFile('example.html'); Warning: Unknown element name parsing the stylesheet: 'pre'??
Workaround:
Set styles in the style attribute of pre elements. For example:
Hello world!
?Alternatively, define a paragraph style with similar attributes to the pre style and use p elements instead of pre elements. pre?elements are usually formatted with a monospace font and white spaces preserved. For example:??? ?

Hello world!

??

Summary: Specifying an invalid DBC-file in set_param for the CANdbFile parameter on pack and unpack message blocks does not throw an error
Description: Specifying an invalid DBC-file when using the command-line function set_param ?for the CANdbFile parameter on any of the following blocks should throw an error, but does not. CAN Pack CAN Unpack CAN FD Pack CAN FD Unpack  For example, the following command should error when filename.dbc  is invalid: set_param(gcb,'CANdbFile','filename.dbc')

Summary: Compatibility check may fail for models containing Data Store Memory blocks
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails? with the message  Checking Compatibility Failed ? when all the following conditions are met: The model contains?Data Store Memory blocks  The model contains blocks having?a constant sample time and the?BlockType ?equal to?S-Function  A replacement model gets generated   Additionally,? when these conditions are met,?you will encounter the following error?while executing? sldvblockreplacement :  $PRODUCT$ has detected an S-Function with a constant sample time. $PRODUCT$ does not support S-Functions with this configuration.

Summary: Error when extracting the architecture of a Simulink model with inactive variants
Description: Exporting a Simulink model to a System Composer architecture model returns an error if the Simulink model has Variant Subsystem blocks with inactive variant choices. The error has the following form: Warning: File '<model name>_tempExportWorkflow.slx' not found.  > In systemcomposer.internal.arch.exportToArch (line 139)  ? In systemcomposer.extractArchitectureFromSimulink (line 12)  Error using systemcomposer.extractArchitectureFromSimulink (line 14)  Invalid Simulink object name: <block path>  Caused by:  ? ? Error using systemcomposer.internal.arch.exportToArch>addInterfaceToPort (line 583)  ? ? <block name>?is not a SubSystem block. - Show complete stack trace?

Summary: Assertion error when calling a custom code function that has an unknown sized array argument
Description: Stateflow might throw an assertion error when you update diagram for a model when all of the following conditions are true: There is a call in a Stateflow chart to a custom code function? The signature of the custom code function contains an unknown sized array argument (e.g. void ccfcn(double in[ ]) The option "Import custom code" is selected on the Simulation Target pane of Configuration Parameters  The assertion message is similar to: failed assertion in b:\matlab\src\cgir_xform\dom_core\lowering\reshapetransform.cpp at line 124:?  fWorkCtx.numElement(tCast) == fWorkCtx.numElement(tCastee)?
Workaround: There are two workarounds available:Change the custom code function signature from taking an unknown size array to taking a pointer. For example, change "void ccfcn(double in[ ])" to "void ccfcn(double * in)"Deselect "Import custom code" from the Simulation Target pane on Configuration Parameters

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR generated code might write uninitialized value if Bus Creator block output writes to root outport
Description: AUTOSAR generated code writes a partially initialized struct  value to a root outport if all of the following conditions exist: The model contains a root outport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to either ExplicitSend  or ImplicitSend . The model contains a Stateflow chart that outputs multiple signals. The signals are fed into a Bus Creator block. The bus signal output of the Bus Creator block is written to a root outport. The chart logic partially writes the bus signal in different states. The chart is not configured as Treat as atomic unit  with Function packaging  set to Reusable function .  Under these conditions, the generated code writes a partially initialized variable to the root outport.
Workaround: In the Stateflow chart block parameters:On the Main tab, select Treat as atomic unit.On the Code Generation tab, set Function packaging?to Reusable function.

Summary: Unclear error when creating a project in a folder containing a Resources folder.
Description: Unable to create a project in a folder that contains a resources folder with uppercase characters. Users encounter an unclear error: "The category with uuid FileClassCategory does not exist in the project".
Workaround: To avoid this issue,rename the folder to resources all lowercase.rename or move the folder.create the project in a parent folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for consecutive parfor loops that iterate over the same range and do not have reduction variables might contain incorrect OpenMP variable classifications
Description: The generared code might contain incorrect OpenMP variable classifications, resulting in sporadic wrong answers, if your MATLAB code satisfies all of these conditions: Your MATLAB code has two or more consecutive?parfor ?loops. These consecutive?parfor  loops do not use reduction variables. For more information on reduction variables, see?https://www.mathworks.com/help/parallel-computing/reduction-variable.html . These consecutive parfor  loops share the same iteration range.
Workaround: Use any one of these three workarounds:Minimize the occurrence of consecutive parfor loops with the same iteration range. You can achieve this by using interleaving statements or by moving consecutive parfor loops to separate, auxiliary functions until no consecutive?parfor?loops remain.Make sure that at least one of the consecutive parfor loops contains the use of a reduction variable.Make sure that consecutive parfor loops do not iterate over the same range.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Difference between simulation and code generation results when a model contains an Atomic subsystem with reusable function packaging
Description: Simulation and code generation results may be different when all the following conditions are met: The model contains an Atomic subsystem with the Function Packaging  option set to Reusable function . An Outport block, Signal Specification block, or Inport block with a specified sample time is directly connected to an output port of the Atomic subsystem, or through virtual blocks. The sample time specified on the Outport block, Signal Specification block, or Inport block is different from the compiled sample time without the specification.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block outside the Atomic subsystem. The Signal Conversion block is only required on output ports matching the problem description.

Summary: Assertion during code generation when Stateflow chart and MATLAB Function block call the same external function
Description: An assertion error occurs during code generation when all the following conditions are met: The model contains a Stateflow chart with C as the action language. The model contains a MATLAB Function block. The model has the setting Import custom code  enabled. The Stateflow chart and the MATLAB Function block both call the same external function. The Stateflow chart is from a library model with the setting Use local custom code settings (do not inherit from main model)  disabled.  The assertion is similar to the following:? ?failed assertion in b:\matlab\src\rtwcg\rtwcg_utils\managedcustomcodescope.cpp at line 243:?  ? ? In getSpecialScopeImpl: Assertion failed: scope->parent() == fCCScope?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Unrecognized function of variable 'cmdToUrl' error when exporting to ReqIF
Description: When exporting Simulink Requirement Set to ReqIF, export may fail with the message: Unrecognized function or variable 'cmdToUrl'  when the following conditions are met: The export links  option is enabled. There are links with Simulink Test Cases.

Summary: MATLAB hangs on some Linux distributions when opening certain user interfaces
Description: On some Linux distributions, opening a user interface that uses an embedded browser can cause MATLAB to hang. This generally occurs when the same user interface was previously opened and closed in the same MATLAB session.
Workaround: Keep a user interface that uses an embedded browser open for the duration of your MATLAB session. For example, keep the MATLAB Web Browser open during your MATLAB session. You can open the MATLAB Web Browser by running:?>> web?

Summary: Non descriptive error message from Variant Subsystem related to ExclusiveSignalOnInport
Description: Variant Subsystem choices having same signal names specified inside variant choices may result in following error:Signals with the same identifier used in mutually exclusive choices of a variant subsystem cannot be specified on the Inport block of a choice. Consider adding a signal conversion block and specifying the signal property on the output port of the signal conversion block.  This error can happen when all the below conditions are met.  Variant Subsystem configured to generate code through code compile activation. Variant choices have inports with signal name specified and have valid storage class associated with those signals.

Summary: Simulink.Signal object property InitialValue on root outport of referenced model is ignored in certain cases
Description: Simulink can produce incorrect numeric results when each of the following conditions are true: Simulink.Signal  object is resolved for signal feeding root outport of referenced model. Simulink.Signal  object has property InitialValue  and a custom storage class specified. Referenced model is conditionally executed. For example, the model is triggered or enabled.? Top model is set to accelerator or rapid accelerator simulation mode, or the parent model is a referenced model that uses an accelerated simulation mode.
Workaround: There are 2 ways to work around this problem:Specify the?InitialOutput property?on the outport block of the referenced model instead of specifying the InitialValue property on the?signal object.Simulate the top model and referenced models in normal simulation mode.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect proven out of bound array index on read of a C extern array field
Description: Code Prover incorrectly shows a proven (green) Out of bound array index  (OBAI) check when a C?extern  array field is read. If the size of the array is unspecified, the check should be unproven (orange). The following code demonstrates the issue: extern unsigned int array[];  unsigned int foo(int value) {  ? ? ? ? ?return array[value]; // OBAI check is green and should be orange  }?

Summary: esfrChart errors out on certain valid test charts
Description: The esfrChart  function errors out with the following error message for certain valid chart images:   Error using images.internal.testchart.verify_esfrChart (line 40)  Detected alignment points may not be correct. Check if image is valid and consider changing detection sensitivity in case no registration points are provided.  Error in esfrChart (line 325)  images.internal.testchart.verify_esfrChart(chart); This function supports only the 16:9 aspect ratio of the Extended version of the test charts. Other aspect ratios and formats are not supported and will result in this error.

Summary: Template makefile build process generates file-specific makefile rules that use absolute paths
Description: If you use the template makefile approach, the build process compiles source files by using file-specific makefile rules. The rules use absolute paths instead of tokenized paths, e.g.?$(START_DIR)/path  .  The use of absolute paths might produce build errors if:  Your workflow involves a makefile relocation that causes the absolute path to become invalid.  An absolute path contains a space.
Workaround: If a space in a source file path causes a build error, move the file to a folder on a file path that does not contain a space.If your build folder name contains a space, then change your build folder to a folder that does not have a space in its file path.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during HDL code generation in the presence of unused ports in the model
Description: For certain models that contain unused ports, HDL code generation might result in a MATLAB crash with a stack trace containing pir::UnusedPortDeleter::run : Typical stack trace may contain:?  [? 0] 0x0000000500000000 [unknown function] at [unknown module] (no module specified)?  [? 1] 0x00000002e8ea63df pir::CtxIterator::advance at ...\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\dom_pir_core\iterator.cpp:82 (in ...\matlab\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll)  [? 2] 0x00000002e97bfe33 pir::UnusedPortDeleter::run at ...\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_transforms\unusedportdeleter.cpp:384 (in ...\matlab\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll)?
Workaround: If your stack trace consists of? the text 'unusedportdeleter',?run the attached P-coded file. The syntax for running the P-coded function is as follows:?>>?fixUnusedPortDeleter('<modelName>');

Summary: Training a convolutional neural network with large 3D input data results in a crash
Description: MATLAB crashes when training a network with a large 3D input data size or a huge number of observations (for example, of the order of 1e+03). You might see an error like the following:An unexpected error occurred during CUDA execution. The CUDA error was:an illegal memory access was encountered

Summary: Inconsistent behavior of Simulink.BlockDiagram.getChecksum between base workspace variable and model workspace variable in Accelerator mode
Description: When a model is set to Accelerator mode, the behavior of Simulink.BlockDiagram.getChecksum  is inconsistent for modifications on base workspace variable and model workspace variable. Modification of a base workspace variable does not change this checksum of the model, while modification of a model workspace variable changes this checksum. Accelerator target will be rebuilt unexpectedly because of the change in checksum, caused by the modification in model workspace variable.

Summary: Compiling or simulating an unmodified model with mismatched bus elements might dirty the model
Description: Compiling or simulating an unmodified model dirties the model when all of the following conditions apply: You open the model in the Simulink Editor. The model contains a Bus Selector, Bus Assignment, or In Bus Element block that links to a library. The linked block expects different signals than it receives. You receive one or more warnings for the linked block, such as this one: Remapping selected signal 'x.a' to 'y.a' in 'myModel/mySubsystem/Bus Selector1 ' to match the bus hierarchy. Please save your model to make this change permanent.    Saving the model removes the dirty flag and warning until you close, reopen, and compile or simulate the model again.
Workaround: Rename the signals in the model or specified by the parent library block so that they match.

Summary: Error in validation model generated from model containing Complex to Magnitude-Angle HDL Optimized
Description: When you generate a validation model from a model containing the Complex to Magnitude-Angle HDL Optimized block, located in DSP System Toolbox > Math Functions library, and you enable the "Generate high-level timing critical path report" option, running the validation model returns errors about ufix1 vs boolean data type mismatches.
Workaround: Insert a Data-Type Conversion block on the data path highlighted by the error message.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Variant block output value when fed from a Model block's output with constant rate
Description: Consider a scenario?in which?a Model block has non-constant rate while one of its outports has a constant rate.Then the output of the Variant Subsystem or a Variant Source may produce incorrect results in these cases:  Model block is a choice within a Variant Subsystem.  Model block's output with constant rate is connected to multiple input Variant Source block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Absolute-time temporal logic can interrupt state during actions in standalone charts
Description: Because standalone charts in MATLAB define temporal logic in terms of wall-clock time, absolute-time temporal logic operators can interrupt a state during  action. For example, suppose that a during  action takes several seconds of wall-clock time to complete. In this case, the temporal logic expression after(1,sec)  can trigger a transition to another state before the during  action finishes executing. The chart takes the transition and executes the entry  action for the new state before resuming the during  action for the previous state.

Summary: C/C++ code generation for edge function fails for uint32 and uint64 datatypes.
Description: C/C++ code generation for the?edge   function?using a shared library?fails for?uint32? and?uint64? datatypes becausethe shared library is missing symbols for these datatypes.?
Workaround: To workaround this problem, convert an input matrix in uint32?and?uint64?datatypes to the?single datatype before?passing it to the?edge function.

Summary: Debugging standalone Stateflow charts that contain?absolute-time temporal logic might not work as expected
Description: In standalone charts, absolute-time temporal logic operators can become valid while the chart is paused at a breakpoint. In this case, the chart unexpectedly exits debugging mode and the execution continues.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In a standalone chart in MATLAB, nested event calls can lead to multiple active events
Description: In a standalone chart in MATLAB, if one event results in a call to a second event, the chart incorrectly sets both events as active. This behavior can lead to unexpected results.

Summary: Incorrect ARXML data type tag?'IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE' generated for AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform
Description: When generating code for a model configured for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform, data types are exported to ARXML files using the tag 'IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE'. This tag is used in the AUTOSAR Classic Platform?to export data types. For the?AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform, the correct tag is 'CPP-IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE'.? ?See also?2073966 ?for a related issue.?

Summary: Search results appear in parent instead of current Stateflow window
Description: When you open a Stateflow chart in a new window and search for results using model find (Ctrl+F), the results display in the parent window instead of the current window.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for subsystems might use a single rate for multirate virtual buses defined by?Simulink.Bus objects
Description: Generated code for a subsystem might use a single rate for multirate signals when all of the following conditions apply: A virtual bus contains the multirate signals. A?Simulink.Bus ?object defines the virtual bus. The virtual bus?connects to a Subsystem block. To generate the code, you right-click the Subsystem block and select?C/C++ Code > Build This Subsystem .

Summary: Merging data dictionary files within a GitHub project sets property HasAccessToBaseWorkspace to true
Description: The property HasAccessToBaseWorkspace? for a Simulink data dictionary ?is automatically set to true ?when the following conditions are true: You are working in a Windows environment. You use GitHub to manage your Simulink data dictionaries (.sldd  files). You merge .sldd  files from different GitHub branches within MATLAB.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: The readFrame?method of?VideoReader?returns different total number of frames than the?NumberOfFrames?property
Description: For certain AVI, MOV, or MP4 files on Windows?, using the readFrame  method of?VideoReader  returns different total number of frames than the NumberOfFrames  property. For example: vidObj = VideoReader('myTestfile.avi');vidObj.NumberOfFrames   298 numFrames = 0;while (hasFrame(vidObj))    readFrame(vidObj);     numFrames = numFrames + 1; end numFrames   930

Summary: Changes to DC Gain, Peaking Gain, and GPZ Matrix parameters in CTLE?block are not reflected in IBIS-AMI Manager.
Description: If you change the DC Gain , Peaking Gain , and GPX Matrix  parameters in the SerDes Datapath CTLE block, it can change the number of possible CTLE configurations, as shown by the Configuration select  drop-down menu. But, the associated?ConfigSelect ?parameter under the Model Specific  > CTLE  menu in the AMI-Tx  and/or AMI/Rx  tabs of the SerDes IBIS-AMI Manager dialog box may not reflect the changes. Also, an error message appears when attempting to set Configuration select  parameter to a number that was not allowed before the changes.
Workaround: Open the SerDes IBIS-AMI Manager and select the AMI-Rx tab. In the SerDes IBIS-AMI Manager dialog box select the ConfigSelect parameter for your CTLE under Model_Specific and click the Edit...?button.?In the newly opened SerDes IBIS-AMI Manager - Add/Edit AMI Parameter dialog box edit the List values array to include the range of configuration numbers supported by the CTLE, reflecting the changes made in the CTLE block mask.

Summary: The code generator produces an assertion that contains: In x_binary: Assertion failed: !r1.isDimMatrix()?
Description: The code generator can produce this assertion during code generation: Assertion at b:\ matlab\ src\ cgir_support\ dom_core\ analysis\ range\ rangevaluefactory.cpp line 725:? In x_binary: Assertion failed: !r1.isDimMatrix()?  One situation that can trigger this assertion is when your MATLAB code satisfies all of these conditions: There are multiple assignments to a variable x  and these assignments have different sizes. After these assignments, you again assign x  to the output of a function that is declared as extrinsic.  For example, this MATLAB code can trigger the above assertion: coder.extrinsic('magic');if (n < 0)    x = 1;else    assert(n < 10);    x = ones(n,n);endx = magic(3); Here,?the output of the extrinsic function magic ?is assigned to the variable?x . There are two previous assignments to x , and these assignments have different sizes (1 -by- 1 ?and n -by- n ?respectively). The presence of these pre-assignments can trigger the assertion. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, GPU Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Workaround: In your MATLAB code, insert a single pre-assignment before assigning the output of the extrinsic function.?For example, in the MATLAB code in the previous section, introduce a variable temp as follows:?coder.extrinsic('magic');if (n < 0)    x = 1;else    assert(n < 10);    x = ones(n,n);end temp = x;temp = magic(3);x = temp;For more information, see?Extrinsic Functions.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Coder might generate incorrect code for parameter partitions in For Each subsystem
Description: Simulink Coder might generate incorrect code under the following scenario:The model contains a masked For Each subsystem block. The For Each block configures a parameter partition for a multi-dimensional mask parameter. The parameter partition is used in a Lookup, Prelookup or any other block that would use the starting address of the parameter partition.  Simulink Coder will use the base address of the mask parameter instead of the parameter partition as the starting address.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a

Summary: Generated C++ code might contain an extra unnecessary set of parentheses for a static_cast
Description: The generated C++ code for a static_cast  might contain an extra unnecessary set of parentheses as follows: ?static_cast<uint16_T>((rtDWork.temporalCounter + 1U));  With this fix, the generated code is as follows:?  static_cast<uint16_T>(rtDWork.temporalCounter + 1U);

Summary: Check model reference parallel build in Performance Advisor might incorrectly detect less than two referenced models
Description: When both of the following conditions are true, Check model reference parallel build? in Performance Advisor?passes and detects less than two referenced models: There are more than two referenced models There are less than two referenced models in Accelerator Mode
Workaround: Change the simulation mode of the top model to Accelerator mode.

Summary: Compatibility check fails when inactive variant choice contains a referenced model with configuration reference
Description: When an inactive variant choice in a model consists of?referenced model?and the referenced model has a configuration?reference, Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check may fail with this error:  Changing property UpdateModelReferenceTargets is not allowed
Workaround: Reduce the model by using Variant Reducer before performing Simulink Design Verifier analysis.

Summary: Incompatible Output when disabled?setting for referenced function-call model produces warning during simulation
Description: ?Simulating a model hierarchy produces a warning instead of an error when all of the following conditions apply: An enabled subsystem references a function-call model. An Outport block at the top-level of the referenced function-call model has?Output when disabled ?set to reset . An Outport block at the top-level of the enabled subsystem has Output when disabled ?set to held .

Summary: Start method on?System.Diagnostics.Process .NET class might throw no matching signature error
Description: MATLAB might throw an error when you call the Start method for System.Diagnostics.Process objects on Windows 10 version 1903(18353) or later.? For example:? p = System.Diagnostics.Process();p.StartInfo.FileName = 'C:\ WINDOWS\ system32\ notepad.exe';p.Start();  Error: No method 'Start' with matching signature found for class 'System.Diagnostics.Process'
Workaround: Use Start(p) instead of p.Start()

Summary: Deployed MATLAB functions might error or crash on originating system
Description: If you create a deployable archive on a system with any MATLAB add-on installed using the MATLAB Compiler or MATLAB Compiler SDK, the CTF-file produced can cause errors when run on the system that it was created on. This behavior is isolated to the machine that the CTF-file was created on, and does not occur if the file is run on a different system.

Summary: Error while using the smexportonshape function before you can login to your Onshape account
Description: If, in your MATLAB Preferences, System Web Browser  is set to? Use system web browser when opening links to external sites , then when you use the?smexportonshape ?function to export an Onshape assembly to Simscape Multibody, before you can login to your Onshape account, MATLAB stops and generates this error: ?"Failed to complete user authentication" .?
Workaround: In the MATLAB toolstrip, on the Home tab, in the Environment settings, click Preferences. In the left pane of the Preferences settings, click Web. In the MATLAB Web Preferences settings, clear the Use system web browser when opening links to external sites (recommended) check box

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Race condition possible with EnableMultiTasking and low value for MaxStackSize
Description: If the MaxStackSize ?model configuration parameter is set to a low value, some local function variables may be promoted to be global if their size (in bytes) exceeds the MaxStackSize  value specified.  If EnableMultiTasking ?model configuration parameter is also checked, then the code generator generates different step functions for each task, for example, model_step0()  and model_step1() .  When the same global (that was promoted from a local) is referred in (written to and read from) different task functions, this might lead to a race condition on the global and might lead to unpredictable results.?

Summary: Input design range when specified for enumeration type signals results in assertion failure
Description: When you specify a design range for the signals of enumeration type, Simulink Design Verifier results in assertion failure instead of ignoring the design ranges during the analysis.   The analysis reports this error in the Diagnostic Viewer window:  Building model representation failed: Unexpected unknown exception from MEX file.

Summary: Incorrect animation highlighting for standalone Stateflow Charts when using absolute-time temporal logic operators
Description: If you use absolute-time temporal logic operators with very short time arguments, the Stateflow animation highlighting of a state may not properly clear when the chart exits the state.?
Workaround: If the highlighting persists, right-click the chart and select Remove Highlighting.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check reports Unexpected unknown exception from MEX file
Description: Simulink Design Verifier reports Building model representation failed:?Unexpected unknown exception from MEX file  while performing compatibility check on a model that:  Contains Linked Atomic Subchart The Linked Atomic Subchart has machine-parented data

Summary: Generating HDL Code from a model with MATLAB System objects having buses at its interface might lead to assertion
Description: When trying to generate HDL Code from a Simulink model containing a MATLAB System object with buses at its interface, you might encounter an assertion that looks like: ?Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/runElabOptimizations  Assertion failed:  b:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\emlinterface\emlinterfaceforhdl.cpp:335: CgirComp: <Component CtxName="matlabSystemBusExample" RefNum="c0" UserName="MATLAB System1" RTTIClass ="class pir::EMLComp" Network="n2"/>  Mismatch of inferred output type at Port 0  Block Output Type ---> struct { Fields: { { Name: elementA Type: logical}{ Name: elementB Type: ufix4}{ Name: elementC Type: uint8} } }  MATLAB Inferred Output Type ---> struct { Fields: { { Name: elementA Type: logical}{ Name: elementB Type: ufix4}{ Name: elementC Type: uint8} } }  In the error message, the "Block Output Type"?and the "MATLAB? Inferred Output Type" lists the same set of elements.

Summary: sldvruncgvtest results in error for models when EnableAccessToBaseWorkspace parameter set to off
Description: If a model has a data dictionary file attached to it and have no access to the base workspace, the  sldvruncgvtest? function results in error with the following error message:  The values for one or more parameters specified through the Configuration Parameters dialog box are not present in the data dictionary associated with the model <dataDictiionaryFileName>. To avoid this error, add the missing parameters to the data dictionary for the model. Alternatively, if you do not specify a data dictionary, you can use the addInputData method to specify configuration parameters for your model.
Workaround: Set the parameter EnableAccessToBaseWorkspace of the model to on.Command line :set_param(<modelName>,"EnableAccessToBaseWorkspace","on");

Summary: Sample time colors overwrite the block foreground colors
Description: If a model has Sample Time coloring on, the sample time colors overwrite the block foreground colors when the model is saved.
Workaround: Select all blocks and set the foreground color to black.Turn off the sample time colors display in one of the following ways and re-save the modelIn the Simulink model window, on the?Debug?tab, deselect?Information Overlays?>?Colors.- or -From the MATLAB terminal, type: set_param(model, 'SampleTimeColors', 'off');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink ignores the runtime value of a model argument when used as a Model Workspace data object's value
Description: For a model workspace data object, if you specify value with an expression that includes a model argument, definition value of the model argument is used during simulation. Simulink does not use the model argument's updated value from parent model during simulation.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated HDL code for Sqrt block might be incorrect when fraction length is greater than word length
Description: The overflow logic in the HDL code generated for Sqrt block might be incorrect when the following conditions are true: Input datatype is fixed point Input data has fractional length greater than word length The UseMultiplier ?property in the HDL Block properties is cleared
Workaround: For SqrtbitSet? or SqrtFunction? architecture select multiplier option to avoid the ModelSim simulations mismatches for the? inputs in which fractional part is greater than word length.

Summary: Update diagram fails for Simulink model with multiple Simulink.data.dictionary.EnumTypeDefinition objects with same name in open data dictionaries
Description: Updating the diagram can incorrectly error out for a Simulink model when the model has multiple open data dictionaries and those dictionaries contain?Simulink.data.dictionary.EnumTypeDefinition?objects with duplicate entry names. This error reports that an enumerated type has more than one definition, even when those definitions are identical or are not used by any model.?
Workaround: No workaround available

Summary: Phased Array System Toolbox app?Radar Waveform Analyzer errors on?launching
Description: MATLAB might throw an error and become unresponsive when you try to launch the Radar Waveform Analyzer app if you do not have certain products installed in addition to Phased Array System Toolbox. Undefined variable "matlabshared" or class "matlabshared.application.layout.GridBagLayout".?
Workaround: You can work around this issue by installing the Antenna Toolbox, RF Toolbox, or Communication Toolbox in addition to the Phased Array System Toolbox.

Summary: The serdes.DFECDR tap weight adaptation algorithm converges to nonideal values.
Description: The serdes.DFECDR tap weight adaptation algorithm previously used the MATLAB function fix ?instead of round ?which leads to less accurate tap values.  The issue impacts all DFE tap weights regardless of the magnitude. But it is most noticeable for DFE tap weights that are less than 50 mV since the deviation is a significant portion of the true tap value.
Workaround: To avoid the issue, set theresolution of the DFE tap step size to the ideal value of zero.? If you are using a serdes.DFECDR System objectTM, set the property EqualizationStep? to 0. If you are using a DFECDRlibrary block, set the parameter Adaptive step size (V) to 0.

Summary: Model block SIL or PIL simulation produces error for AUTOSAR software component with model workspace parameters mapped to SharedParameter
Description: If you run a Model block software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation for an AUTOSAR software component that has model workspace parameters mapped to AUTOSAR parameters of type?SharedParameter , the simulation produces an error. For example:  SIL or PIL component "mymodel" references AUTOSAR calibration parameter "Model workspace parameter 'myparam'" which cannot be initialized. The parameter is not initialized in the generated code and is not supported for SIL/PIL dynamic parameter initialization. To avoid this error, only use parameters that SIL/PIL can dynamically initialize (see SIL and PIL tunable parameter support documentation).
Workaround: To test the generated code for the AUTOSAR software component, you can run a top-model SIL or PIL simulation, which does not produce the error.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 6, R2020a

Summary: No permissions in the destination folder when importing comments
Description: When you import comments from a previous set of results using the Polyspace UI, you get a pop-up error message similar to the following:? Error: You do not have permissions in the destination folder: F:\MyFolder\CP_Results_1 | (and file F:\MyFolder\CP_Results_1\ps_diff.db)
Workaround: Close the Polyspace UI and delete the file indicated in the error message, for instance F:\MyFolder\CP_Results_1\ps_diff.db.
Products: Polyspace Code Prover, Polyspace Code Prover Server

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation using Initialize, Reset, and Terminate Functions with Variant Subsystems
Description: Consider an Event Listener ( Initialize/Reset/Terminate ) Subsystem containing a Variant Subsystem. Code corresponding to the Variant Subsystem is not generated when all the following conditions are met:  The Variant choices within the Variant Subsystem inside the Event listener block includes the StateWriter   The Variant choice blocks have Function Packaging set to Auto  or Inline   The State owner of the StateWriter  block resides within a choice of another Variant Subsystem

Summary: Model fails to generate code due to branched signal driven by a Constant block
Description: A model fails to generate code when all of the following are true: The model generates code when?Code Generation > Optimization >?Default parameter behavior ?configuration parameter is set to Tunable.  The model has a model reference block. The referenced model includes a Constant block. In the Constant block, Sample time  is set to?[inf?0] . At least one signal with sample time of?[inf 0]  is branched and drives different destinations. The?destinations of the?signal with sample time of?[inf 0] ?have different sample times. GCD of?the?destination sample times?does NOT exist in?the referenced model.?
Workaround: When?the output signal from a Constant block needs to be branched, change Sample time of the Constant block to -1 if its?destinations?have different rates.

Summary: Scatter plots in apps that have large numbers of points render and respond slowly
Description: If you create a scatter plot that has thousands of points, it might become unresponsive if you display it in an App Designer app or in a figure created with the uifigure  function.
Workaround: Modify your code in any of the following ways:Display fewer points in the plot.Use the plot function instead of the scatter function to visualize the data.If the uifigure function was explicitly called while creating the plot, use the figure?function instead. Additionally, if the uiaxes function was used, use the axes function instead.?

Summary: Degraded performance when running large loops with many iterations in live scripts
Description: Code in the Live Editor runsslower than code at the command prompt. In most cases, the performance change is not significant. However, you may notice a difference when running large loops with many iterations.??

Summary: Test Sequence block or Stateflow chart breakpoint prevents resuming test case execution
Description: If you set a breakpoint in a Test Sequence block or Stateflow chart in a model, running the model from a test case in Simulink Test Manager pauses at the breakpoint. However, all the debugging buttons (Continue, Step, Stop, etc.) are disabled in the toolbar, making it impossible to continue or terminate the simulation.

Summary: OPC UA node with large list of children might not display all child nodes
Description: If an OPC UA node contains a large number of child nodes, OPC Toolbox might not retrieve or display all child nodes. Only a partial list of nodes is returned. The exact number of nodes depends on the server configuration.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using a DBC-file that contains inconsistencies
Description: When trying to open a DBC-file that contains inconsistencies, MATLAB might crash without generating any crash log. The impacted workflows include:? Calling the function canDatabase  with such a DBC-file. Specifying such a DBC-file to use with the following blocks: CAN Pack, CAN Unpack, CAN FD Pack, CAN FD Unpack, J1939 Network Configuration.
Workaround: If accessing a DBC-file causes MATLAB to crash, you can use Vector CANdb++ to perform a consistency check for the file in question. Fixing the reported inconsistencies should prevent the crash.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect dead logic reported for multiport switch having constant array as control input
Description: Simulink Design Verifier reports incorrect dead logic when model has multiport switch with constant array/vector as control input.Example: Consider a multiport switch with 4 inputs. For such a multiport switch if the control input is connected to [1 2 ] i.e a constant array/vector, then Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports truncated input(2) value as unreachable .

Summary: Double clicking a .m file in a non-ascii folder in Windows results in error
Description: In Windows Explorer, if you try to open a .m file from a directory containing non-ascii characters in the name, then MATLAB throws an error   For example, from directory:  C:\ Users\user1\ ????  MATLAB displays:?  Error using cd  Cannot CD to C:\ Users\user1\ ???? (Name is nonexistent or not a directory).

Summary: Model with Variants and Stateflow charts generate uncompilable code
Description: The following modelling semantic generates uncompilable model_initialize  method: Model has inlined Stateflow charts that produce local block output variables. These Stateflow charts?act as variant choices in a Variant Subsystem.
Workaround: Move each variant choice chart into an atomic subsystem and connect the output of the chart to a Signal Conversion Block.

Summary: Unguarded local block variable in the generated code with Variant Subsystem
Description: Unguarded local block variable in the generated code with variant subsystem when all of the below conditions are TRUE:??  The checkbox?Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  is selected.  The checkbox Propagate conditions outside of variant subsystem  is?selected. The Variant Subsystem is connected directly or indirectly to a?block which produces local block variable.  The generated code will have an unguarded local variable?rtb_Local:  void UnusedLocalVar_10ms(void)  {? ?  /* local block i/o variables */  real_T rtb_Local ;  Var_Y.msg4 = Var_B.msg2;  #if Variant1 <= 0 && Variant2 > 0  if (Var_Y.msg4 >= 0.0)  ? ? {  ? ? rtb_Local = 10.0;  ? ? }  else{? ?  ? ? rtb_Local = 20.0;  ? ? }  #endif  }  Note:?A warning of  unreferenced local variable ?during? rtwbuild  ?of?the model can also be seen in the above scenario.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code  generator may not honor data alignment?requirement in the  generated code for Simulink.Bus object
Description: Code  generator might not honor the data alignment?requirement in generated code for a Simulink.Bus  object when all of the following conditions are true:?  The Simulink.Bus  object is used multiple times within?a model or?across multiple?models.  At least two usages of the Simulink.Bus  object have different data alignment requirements.  The alignment specification on Simulink.Bus object   is less than the required alignment for at least one of the usages.?     If the usages of the  Simulink.Bus  object are in the model reference and the bottom model requires higher alignment than the top model, then the generated code may have incorrect numeric results or result in a linking error.??  If the compiled code from your compiler for the target processor would result in different numeric results or crash when using an instruction?that requires specific data alignments but the data is not aligned properly,?the generated code can produce numeric results that differ from simulation results or result in crashes when all of the conditions above are met.

Summary: During code generation, the Simulink Scope block creates?a PWORK struct for the LoggedData regardless of the status of the Log data to workspace?property in the Scope.
Description: When generating code, the Scope block always generates this code for the logged data: ?struct {  ? ?void *LoggedData;  ? } StickInput_PWORK;? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? // '<Root>/Stick Input'  } DW_f14_T;   ?This code is unnecessary unless the?Log data to workspace ?property is enabled in the Scope. The?Log data to workspace? property can be found in the?Configuration Properties? of the Scope, on the?Logging ?tab.?
Workaround: To remove the unnecessary code, turn on the?Block Reduction?setting. In the model Configuration Parameters, in the Simulation?Target?section, select Block Reduction.

Summary: Assigning a default value to a class property using nested anonymous functions results in a error
Description: Assigning a default value to a class property using an expression that contains nested?anonymous functions results in a error. For example, consider the property Z  in the properties ?block of?MyClass . classdef MyClass ? ? properties?? ? ? ? Z = @(q)arrayfun(@(p)sin(p),q) ? ? end end  A call to the class constructor?produces an error. >> obj = MyClassInvalid default value for property 'Z' in class 'MyClass':Error: Invalid use of operator.
Workaround: Define a helper function that returns a handle to the outermost anonymous function. Assign a default value to the property by invoking the helper function. For example:classdef MyClass ? ? properties?? ? ? ? Z = getZproperty();? ? end endfunction fcnHandle = getZproperty()fcnHandle = @(q)arrayfun(@(p)sin(p),q);end>> obj = MyClassans =?? MyClass with properties:? ? Z: @(q)arrayfun(@(p)sin(p),q)
